howtocook:aquatic/咖喱炒蟹.name: Curry Fried Crab
howtocook:aquatic/咖喱炒蟹.description: This is a home-style seafood dish with Thai flavors. Rich curry coats the full crab meat, with the aroma of crab roe and the smooth texture of coconut milk. Crab is rich in high-quality protein and various trace elements, helping to replenish energy. The steps are clear; just pay attention to sealing the crab roe and stir-frying the sauce off the heat at the end. Suitable for those with some cooking experience. It takes about 30 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Open the crab by lifting the lid, cut it in half. Lightly crack the claws with the back of a knife. Dip the cut surfaces and claws in some cornstarch, not too much. Sprinkle 5g cornstarch into the crab lid to cover the roe, set aside.
Finely chop the onion, set aside.
Finely chop the garlic, set aside.
Boil a pot of water, set aside.
Heat oil in a pan, add about 20ml cooking oil, wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Place the crab cut-side down into the pan gently, fry for 20 seconds (this seals the roe and meat). Then flip, fry each side for 10 seconds. Remove crab and set aside.
Put the crab lid in the pan, use a spoon to ladle hot oil over the lid to seal the roe inside, fry for 20 seconds, then remove and set aside.
Without cleaning the pan, add another 10ml oil. Heat on high until lightly smoking, then add minced garlic and chopped onion, stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add curry cubes and stir until melted (10 seconds), then add the fried crab and stir to coat evenly.
Pour in 300ml boiling water, cover and simmer for 3 minutes.
After simmering, add coconut milk and egg white, turn off the heat. Keep stirring until the sauce thickens.
Serve.

howtocook:aquatic/响油鳝丝.name: Sizzling Eel Shreds
howtocook:aquatic/响油鳝丝.description: Sizzling Eel Shreds is a classic home-style dish from Jiangsu, Zhejiang, and Shanghai. The eel shreds are tender and smooth, with rich garlic flavor and a slightly sweet sauce. Drizzling hot lard over it releases an irresistible aroma, making it a perfect accompaniment to rice. Eel is rich in high-quality protein and DHA, offering great nutrition. This dish requires some knife skills and heat control, so it's moderately challenging. Beginners may need a bit of practice. From prep to serving, it usually takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Cut the eel into three sections, then shred into thin strips.
Add 0.5 g white pepper, 3 g cooking wine, mix well. Then add 5 g sesame oil and marinate.
Heat oil and coat the wok.
Add vegetable oil and heat to 60% (medium-high heat).
Add half the minced garlic and all the minced ginger, stir-fry a few times.
Add the eel shreds, stir-fry over medium-high heat for 30 seconds.
Drizzle 10 g cooking wine along the edge, stir-fry a few times.
Add light soy sauce, stir well.
Add oyster sauce and dark soy sauce, stir-fry a few times.
Add salt, sugar, and 3 g white pepper, stir well.
Mix starch with water to make a slurry, pour into the wok, and cook until the sauce thickens.
Plate and sprinkle with minced garlic and chopped green onions.
Heat another wok with lard until 70% hot, then pour it over the eel shreds.

howtocook:aquatic/小龙虾/小龙虾.name: Crayfish
howtocook:aquatic/小龙虾/小龙虾.description: This dish is spicy and fragrant, with tender and juicy meat. It is a classic Sichuan-Hunan style appetizer for drinks. Making it at home is clean and economical. Crayfish is rich in high-quality protein and various trace elements, but the broth contains a lot of oil and salt, so it is suitable for occasional enjoyment. Handling live shrimp and a variety of spices is somewhat challenging for beginners, but skipping the deveining step can significantly reduce difficulty. From preparation to serving, it takes about 45 minutes.
steps: Wash the crayfish and remove the vein (devein). Cut green onions into 2cm sections, mince ginger and garlic.
Heat oil until slightly warm, then add bay leaves, star anise, cinnamon, Sichuan green peppercorns, Sichuan peppercorns, and bullet chili peppers.
Once the spices are fragrant, add the green onions, ginger, and garlic.
After the aromatics are fragrant, add Pixian bean paste and yellow soybean paste, stir-fry until red oil appears.
Add the crayfish and stir-fry until they change color.
Add beer, and after it comes to a boil, add light soy sauce and salt.
Cook until the crayfish are fully cooked, then remove from heat.

howtocook:aquatic/干煎阿根廷红虾/干煎阿根廷红虾.name: Pan-fried Argentine Red Shrimp
howtocook:aquatic/干煎阿根廷红虾/干煎阿根廷红虾.description: A delicious and tempting quick seafood dish. After pan-frying, the Argentine red shrimp has tender white meat, sweet and rich flavor, and crispy shells. Rich in trace elements like iodine, phosphorus, and astaxanthin, it helps boost immunity, protect the heart, and lower cholesterol. Moderate difficulty; requires mastering heat and flipping techniques, but steps are clear so beginners can succeed. Takes about 20 minutes to complete.
steps: Thaw the Argentine red shrimp, preferably by transferring from freezer to fridge a day in advance for natural thawing to better preserve flavor and texture. You can buy ones that are already butterflied and deveined to save time.
Wash the thawed red shrimp, pat dry, and set aside. Make sure to drain well; if in a rush, use paper towels to absorb moisture.
Slice ginger, dice onion into small cubes. Wash cilantro, separate leaves from stems, chop cilantro leaves. Crush garlic and mince into small pieces.
Heat the pan over high heat. Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil. When oil is hot, add ginger slices, onion cubes, and cilantro stems; stir-fry.
After about 1 minute, remove ginger, onion, and cilantro stems; discard.
Adjust to medium-high heat. Place the red shrimp in the pan, making sure each shrimp's one side fully contacts the pan. Fry for about 2 minutes, while brushing each shrimp with a layer of oil.
When the bottom shell is slightly browned, flip the shrimp. Sprinkle garlic mince, and gently shake the pan to distribute heat evenly.
After about 1 minute, add 20ml of white wine.
Fry for another minute, then reduce to medium-low heat. Sprinkle a layer of salt and black pepper evenly.
Add a drop of light soy sauce to each shrimp.
Sprinkle cilantro leaves and plate.
Slice lemon into wedges and arrange on the side of the plate.

howtocook:aquatic/微波葱姜黑鳕鱼.name: Microwave Black Cod with Scallion and Ginger
howtocook:aquatic/微波葱姜黑鳕鱼.description: Microwave cooking for fish, scallion and ginger enhance freshness, hot oil drizzle makes it fragrant. This is a home-style steamed fish dish with tender meat and savory flavor, a modified Cantonese method. Black cod is rich in high-quality protein and omega-3 fatty acids, beneficial for heart and brain health. The operation is not complicated, but requires control of heat and oil temperature, better for those with some cooking experience. It takes about 20 minutes from preparation to table.
steps: Place each fish fillet into a separate sealed bag, skin side down on a plate.
Take 25g scallion (white part), cut into strips. Peel and cut 10g ginger into strips. Mix together, then divide into two halves, and place each half on the fish fillet in each bag.
Pour 2.5mL cooking wine into each bag.
Seal the bags, place in microwave, and microwave on medium (800W) until opaque and easily flaked (about 3.5-5 minutes). Remove the fish fillets from the bags.
Discard the scallion and ginger.
Mix 25mL soy sauce and 2mL sesame oil, then drizzle evenly over the two fish fillets.
Take 10g scallion (green part), cut into fine strips. Peel and cut 3g ginger into strips. Mix together, divide into two portions, and sprinkle over the fish fillets.
Heat 50mL peanut oil in a small saucepan to 190°C.
Drizzle the hot oil over the fish fillets topped with scallion greens. Serve immediately.

howtocook:aquatic/水煮鱼.name: Sichuan Boiled Fish (Shui Zhu Yu)
howtocook:aquatic/水煮鱼.description: Sichuan boiled fish is a classic Sichuan dish known for its spicy and numbing flavor and tender fish slices. Basa fish is rich in high-quality protein and low in fat, making it a balanced meal when paired with seasonal vegetables. The difficulty is moderate, challenging for beginners, but following the steps carefully will lead to success. Estimated cooking time: about 2 hours.
steps: Prep: If the basa fish is frozen, let it thaw at room temperature for 5 hours before slicing.
Slice: Slice the basa fish into thin pieces, about 5 cm long and 3 cm wide.
Marinate: Place the sliced fish in a large stainless steel bowl.
Add 30g chili bean paste, 3g salt, 10ml green Sichuan pepper oil, and 3g white pepper powder.
Mix well with your hands, then add 5ml rapeseed oil to seal in the flavors.
Let it marinate at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.
Prep vegetables: Mince the garlic cloves. For example, use 300g cauliflower and 200g lettuce. Wash the cauliflower and lettuce.
Blanch and stir-fry: Blanch the cauliflower in boiling water and set aside. Wash the lettuce, drain, and stir-fry until cooked (no oil needed).
Fry the chili bean paste: Heat the wok with cold oil (20ml rapeseed oil), add 10g chili bean paste, 10g fermented black beans (optional), and the minced garlic. Stir-fry over medium heat.

Quick-boil the fish slices: Add 150ml hot water. Once it boils quickly, add the marinated fish slices. Gently stir to separate the slices. Add 2g salt and 2g sugar to season (adjust salt to taste). When the water boils again, remove from heat.
Plate: First, place the cooked vegetables in a large bowl. Then, place the hot fish slices on top of the vegetables. Pour the remaining hot broth from the wok over everything. Serve immediately!

ingredients.name: Vegetables (e.g., potato slices, bean sprouts, cauliflower, lettuce, etc.)

howtocook:aquatic/油焖大虾/油焖大虾.name: Oil-Braised Prawns
howtocook:aquatic/油焖大虾/油焖大虾.description: This classic Shandong dish wraps whole prawns in a sweet and savory sauce, resulting in bright red, glossy prawns with firm and tender meat. Prawns are rich in high-quality protein and astaxanthin, low in fat, making them a nutritious ingredient that won't burden the body. The cooking process requires making scallion oil, trimming prawns, braising, and reducing the sauce, demanding a good grasp of heat and seasoning. It's better for experienced cooks. The whole process takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Cut the prawn's spear to the root, trim the antennae and legs, remove the sand sac, butterfly the back and remove the vein, wash and set aside.
Fry the flavored oil.

When the oil reaches 30% heat, add Sichuan peppercorns. Once the oil is hot, remove from heat, add scallions and ginger (best not to let the oil discolor). When the scallions turn slightly yellow, drain the oil (if you have extra scallion oil, you can save it for noodles).
Add oil to the pan, arrange the prawns neatly. Once both sides change color, gently press the prawn heads.
Add minced ginger (finely diced ginger).
30g cooking wine.
Two small bowls of water.
3g salt.
10g rock sugar.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then turn to low heat, cover and braise (do not add any more liquid during cooking, do not uncover).
When the shell is glossy and the prawns are curled, remove from the pan and arrange on a plate.
Reduce the sauce (strain it back into the pan and thicken, add scallion oil) until about 1/4 of the liquid remains.
Pour the sauce over the prawns.
Done.
Oil-Braised Prawns - Preview Image 1
Start eating ✅

howtocook:aquatic/混合烤鱼/烤鱼.name: Grilled Fish
howtocook:aquatic/混合烤鱼/烤鱼.description: This Sichuan-style grilled fish is spicy and numbing, with crispy skin and tender meat. The vegetables soak up the broth, making it extra flavorful with rice. Grass carp is rich in high-quality protein, and the mix of vegetables provides balanced nutrition. The process may look complicated, but following the steps makes it manageable—perfect for beginners wanting a challenge. From prep to table takes about an hour.
steps: Cut the grass carp (about 3 jin) open from the back. Score both sides along the back, but don't cut through the belly, or it won't hold its shape.
You can rinse the fish with hot water to remove slime, or scrub it under running water with a brush until it's no longer sticky.
Place the fish in a container. Add cooking wine, 10g white pepper, and 5g salt. Rub evenly and marinate for 20 minutes.
Cut half a stalk of scallion into chunks. Halve the garlic cloves crosswise. Put the scallion, garlic, star anise, bay leaves, and cinnamon together in one bowl.
Split the dried chili segments in half and place them in a bowl with the lantern chilies.
Cut the celery into small sections.
Blanch the bean sprouts.
Blanch the tofu skin sheets and cut into shreds.
Slice the onion into strips.
Prepare to cook the fish.
If you have an oven, brush the baking tray with oil, place the fish skin side down, and roast until both sides are golden. Then sprinkle with cumin powder.

If you don't have an oven, heat oil in a pan. Sprinkle 2g salt on both sides of the pan, add the grass carp, and fry without moving until the first side sets. Flip and fry until golden on both sides. Sprinkle with cumin powder and remove to a plate.
Add 20ml cooking oil to the pan. Once hot, stir-fry the scallion, garlic, star anise, and bay leaves until fragrant.
Add half a packet of hot pot base and 15-20g doubanjiang (fermented broad bean paste). Stir-fry until the oil turns red.
Add 5g sugar, 10g salt, and 5ml light soy sauce. Pour in enough water to just cover the ingredients and bring to a boil.
Add the celery, bean sprouts, and tofu skin shreds in order. Don't cook them through; just blanch briefly. Then lay the onion strips on top and place the grilled fish on them.
Add the dried chili segments, lantern chilies, and green Sichuan peppercorns.
Heat oil in another pan. When hot, pour it over the chilies just added to release their aroma.
Finally, sprinkle with roasted peanuts, chopped scallions, white sesame seeds, and cilantro.
Cook for 5-6 minutes and it's ready to serve.

howtocook:aquatic/清蒸生蚝.name: Steamed Oysters
howtocook:aquatic/清蒸生蚝.description: Steamed oysters are a coastal home-style dish that preserves the original flavor. The oyster meat is tender and juicy with a hint of sea saltiness. Minced garlic and shredded ginger bring out a refreshing sweetness. Oysters are rich in high-quality protein and zinc, which can help boost immunity. The process is not complicated, but opening the hot lid and handling the oyster shells require some care, and beginners can easily do it. From washing to serving, it takes about twenty minutes.
steps: Scrub the oysters clean with a brush (or a toothbrush if no brush).
Add water to the steamer, place the steamer rack, then arrange 6 oysters flat on the rack. Steam at 50% power for 3 minutes.

Use a wet cloth in your right hand to open the hot lid. For each oyster, open half of the shell and remove it, placing the oyster with the curved side down and flat side up. Put 1 shred of ginger and 10g minced garlic on each oyster.
Close the hot lid and steam at 100% power for 3.5 minutes.
Turn off the heat. Use a cloth in your right hand to open the hot lid. Add 5ml soy sauce to each oyster.
Plate and serve.

howtocook:aquatic/清蒸鲈鱼/清蒸鲈鱼.name: Steamed Sea Bass
howtocook:aquatic/清蒸鲈鱼/清蒸鲈鱼.description: Steamed sea bass is a classic Cantonese steamed dish. The fish meat is tender and smooth, light and fresh, retaining the original flavor of the sea bass to the greatest extent. Sea bass is rich in high-quality protein and DHA, nutritious and easy to digest. This dish is simple to prepare and very beginner-friendly, taking about 30 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Slice the ginger into pieces and then into thin strips. Cut the white parts of the scallions into sections, and slice the green parts into thin strips. Soak the scallion strips in cold water and set aside.

After cleaning the sea bass, pat dry with paper towels. Make a few cuts on both sides. Rub the fish with salt to remove the slime, then rub 10g of salt all over the inside and outside of the fish. Marinate for at least 10 minutes.
Here's a tip for cutting and plating the sea bass: after scoring, the fish can stand upright while steaming, ensuring even heating and making it easier to eat without flipping.
Score the fish
清蒸鲈鱼-预览图-2
Stuff the fish belly with ginger and scallion whites, and also sprinkle some on top. Use half of the prepared amount. Place chopsticks under the fish in the steaming plate to lift it off the bottom.
Heat the water until it's warm to the touch, then place the fish in the steamer.
Steam over high heat for 10 minutes.
Transfer the steamed fish to a clean plate and remove the ginger and scallions from the fish.
Drizzle 15ml of steamed fish soy sauce over the fish.
Sprinkle fresh ginger and scallion strips on the fish. Heat 10ml of cooking oil in a pan until hot, then pour the hot oil over the fish. Serve.

howtocook:aquatic/白灼虾/白灼虾.name: Boiled Shrimp
howtocook:aquatic/白灼虾/白灼虾.description: Boiled shrimp is a classic quick Cantonese dish. The shrimp meat is sweet, bouncy, and tender, preserving its original flavor. Shrimp is rich in high-quality protein and various minerals. Paired with dipping sauce, it's appetizing and not greasy. The method is simple and forgiving, very beginner-friendly. From prep to serving, it takes only about 15 minutes.
steps: Cut the onion into small pieces, slice the ginger, and spread them evenly in a flat-bottomed pan.
Rinse the live shrimp (optional: devein, snip off legs and antennae), drain, and lay them on top of the onion and ginger in the pan.
Pour in cooking wine, cover the lid. Cook on medium heat for 1 minute, then low heat for 5 minutes. Turn off the heat and let sit for 5 minutes.
Concurrently with the previous step, prepare the dipping sauce:
Chop the scallion into small pieces, mince the garlic, then add soy sauce, sesame, and vinegar. Stir to combine.
Heat the oil until hot, then pour it over the dipping sauce.
Remove the shrimp from the pan and plate on a clean dish.

howtocook:aquatic/糖醋鲤鱼/糖醋鲤鱼.name: Sweet and Sour Carp
howtocook:aquatic/糖醋鲤鱼/糖醋鲤鱼.description: Sweet and Sour Carp is a classic Shandong dish, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with a perfect balance of sour and sweet. The fish is deep-fried until golden and then topped with a bright red sweet and sour sauce, making it very appetizing. The fish is rich in high-quality protein and various minerals, easy to digest and absorb. This dish is not very beginner-friendly; it's medium to high difficulty, requiring mastery of cutting techniques, battering, and frying temperature. From prep to serving, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Clean the fish thoroughly, ensuring no scales or foreign matter.

Place the fish with head to the left, belly down, and hold the knife in your right hand. Cut vertically 1 cm deep, then while holding the fish firmly, slice horizontally to the left 3-4 cm. Then lightly score the middle of the fish slice.
Put the fish in a basin. Slice the ginger into thick slices and cut the scallion into sections (just roughly chop, mainly for removing fishy smell).
Squeeze the juice from the scallion and ginger into the basin with all your might.
Add 20g salt and 25g cooking wine, then give the carp a bath by rubbing evenly.
Marinate.

In a clean basin, add 100g flour, 200g cornstarch, 180g water, and 5g salt. Mix evenly by hand until the batter is thick and can be pulled into strings. Then crack in one egg and mix again until smooth.
Wait 30 minutes.
Place the fish on a cutting board and dry the body with a dry towel (this helps the batter adhere better).
Rinse the basin clean and dry with a dry towel.
Heat oil in a wok, add about 1L of oil, and heat to 70% hot, about 200-240°C.

Hold the fish by the tail and lower the head into the oil. Use a ladle to pour hot oil over the fish's body. Once the batter sets, slowly lower the whole fish into the oil. Gently lift the head with a spatula and place a strainer underneath to prevent the bottom from burning.
Prepare a plate for the fish and place it next to the wok.

Gently slide the spatula under the fish's body and flip it over with the help of another tool. Fry for another two minutes. Then, using the same method (strainer supporting the head, spatula supporting the body), lift the fish onto the plate.
Pour the oil from the wok into a dry basin and set aside. Clean the wok thoroughly.
In a small bowl, mix 50g water, 40g ketchup, 20g white sugar, and 10g white vinegar until well combined.
In another small bowl, mix 10g cornstarch and 10g water to make a slurry.
Heat the wok over high heat, then pour in the sauce mixture. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat.
Add the cornstarch slurry, stirring constantly. After 20 seconds, turn off the heat.
Pour the sweet and sour sauce evenly over the fish using a ladle. Garnish with a little cilantro or chopped scallion. Sweet and Sour Carp is ready.
Sweet and Sour Carp - Preview Image 2

howtocook:aquatic/红烧鱼.name: Braised Fish
howtocook:aquatic/红烧鱼.description: Braised fish is a classic home-style dish with rich flavors, savory with a hint of sweetness, tender and flavorful fish meat. It is a popular Chinese braised dish. Fish is rich in high-quality protein and unsaturated fatty acids, easy to absorb. The process involves handling raw fish and pan-frying, with several steps, which may be challenging for beginners, but following the recipe can still succeed. From prep to serving, it takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Prepare ginger and garlic, chop finely
Chop dried chili peppers, combine with ginger and garlic
Add 30-50ml oil, wait for the pan to heat up...
Place the dried fish (to avoid oil splatters), then shake the pan to fry the fish in hot oil, ensure to use low heat
Flip the fish, repeat the frying process
Add ginger, garlic, and chili, stir-fry until fragrant
Pour in cooking wine, a bit more, be careful as it will create a lot of smoke
Pour in vinegar (add more if you like vinegar)
Then add white sugar and soy sauce (dark soy sauce)
Add cold water, just enough to submerge the fish, then turn to medium heat, cover the lid, after about 1 minute flip the fish, continue covering
After 3-4 minutes, add salt, small chili peppers, oyster sauce (or MSG, chicken powder), then continue covering, flip again later

When the sauce has reduced to just below the fin on the fish's back (or when there's not much sauce left), turn to low heat, add cilantro and chopped green onions, then cover for 20 seconds, turn off heat
Serve

howtocook:aquatic/红烧鱼头.name: Braised Fish Head
howtocook:aquatic/红烧鱼头.description: Braised fish head is a classic home-cooked dish. The fish head is rich in collagen, with tender and smooth meat. The sauce is savory with a slight spiciness, perfect with rice. Fish head is rich in high-quality protein, lecithin, and unsaturated fatty acids, beneficial for skin and brain health. Since it involves handling raw fish and chopping bones, it is slightly challenging and not recommended for absolute beginners. The whole process (including marinating) takes about 2–2.5 hours.
steps: Wash scallions, ginger, garlic, cilantro, and fairy peppers separately.
Rinse dried chilies and star anise briefly.
Cut the scallion in half. For the back half (white part), cut into sections about 4 cm each. For the front half (green part), first cut into sections, then split each section into four pieces.
Slice ginger into pieces about 3 mm thick.
Crush the garlic cloves.
Take two cilantro stalks, remove the roots, and cut into 1.5 cm pieces.
Slice fairy peppers into rings about 3 mm thick.
Cut dried chilies into four sections each.
Note: The fish body mentioned below refers to the meat attached to the fish head when purchased.
Scale the fish head and clean any remaining internal organs from the head area.
Chop off the fins and clean out the gills.
Separate the lower jaw from the body, chop the body into pieces, and cut the head into four or six pieces.
Note: Handling the fish is hard to describe fully in text; search for fish head preparation videos.
Rinse the chopped fish pieces, ideally washing off any blood residue.

Place the cleaned fish pieces in a bowl. Add 5g salt, 10g light soy sauce, 10g cooking wine. Add the chopped green part of the scallion and 1/3 of the ginger slices. Mix well and let sit for 1–2 hours.
Add 30ml oil and wait for the pot to heat...
When the oil is hot, turn the heat to low.
If you don't understand why, please refer to the section on stir-frying aromatics in 'Learning to Stir-fry and Pan-fry'.
Add ginger slices and stir-fry slowly until most of the ginger juice is released and they turn golden brown.
Add scallion sections and stir-fry until they become slightly white.
Add crushed garlic, star anise, and dried chilies; stir-fry for 5 seconds.
Pour the marinated fish head into the pot and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes.
Add 500ml water, then 2g salt, 3g chicken bouillon, 5g light soy sauce, 3g dark soy sauce, 5g cooking wine, 2g black pepper, and 3g aged vinegar.
Add the two cilantro stalks into the pot and cover with a lid.
Turn to high heat and bring the water to a boil.
Turn to medium heat and let it simmer slowly to infuse flavors.
When the liquid has reduced by half, uncover the pot.
Turn to high heat to reduce the sauce. When about 1/3 of the liquid remains, turn off the heat and transfer to a small bowl.
Note: Evenly pour the sauce in the pot over the fish head. When serving, you can place the cooked cilantro at the bottom of the bowl for a better presentation and taste.
Place cilantro on top of the fish head, then arrange the fairy pepper rings over the cilantro.
Your aromatic and flavorful braised fish head is ready!

howtocook:aquatic/红烧鲤鱼.name: Braised Carp
howtocook:aquatic/红烧鲤鱼.description: Braised carp is a homestyle Shandong dish. The fish is first fried then braised, with a slightly chewy skin and tender flesh. The savory-sweet sauce carries the rich flavor of pork belly, and the thick gravy is perfect over rice. Rich in high-quality protein and minerals like calcium and phosphorus, it's nourishing. Frying the fish and balancing the seasonings require a bit of patience, but the steps are clear, even beginners can follow. From prep to finish, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Wash the scallions, ginger, garlic, and dried chili peppers separately.
Cut the white part of the scallions into segments about 4 cm long, then split each segment into four strips.
Slice the ginger into pieces about 3 mm thick.
Smash and mince one garlic clove, cut the remaining cloves in half.
Cut the dried chili peppers into four pieces each.
Slice the pork belly into pieces about 4 cm by 4 cm.
Clean the fish.
Make several diagonal slits on the thick part of the fish's back to help it absorb flavors.

Pour enough oil into the pan, heat to 70% hot (just starting to smoke), then fry the fish for 1 minute until the skin firms up slightly. Remove and set aside (do not disturb the fish immediately; wait a bit before turning). Pour out the frying oil, leaving a little base oil in the pan.
Heat the remaining oil in the pan, add the pork belly, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the dried chili peppers, scallions, ginger, and garlic cloves, and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Place the fried fish back into the pan.
Pour along the edge of the pan:
50 ml cooking wine
50 ml aged vinegar
50 ml premium light soy sauce
20 ml dark soy sauce for color
5 ml oyster sauce for umami
5 g salt
50 g sugar
Add water until it just covers the fish.
Turn the heat to medium and bring to a boil.
Reduce to low heat and simmer gently to infuse flavors.
After 15 minutes, uncover the pot, and pick out the scallions, ginger, garlic, and dried chili peppers.
Turn the heat to high to reduce the sauce. When about 1/4 of the sauce remains, sprinkle some minced garlic, turn off the heat, and serve.
Braised carp is ready!

howtocook:aquatic/肉蟹煲.name: Meat Crab Pot
howtocook:aquatic/肉蟹煲.description: This dish has a rich sauce flavor, with sweet and slightly spicy crab meat, soft and sticky potatoes, chewy rice cakes, and the sauce is especially satisfying when mixed with rice. It's a hearty dish suitable for gatherings. It is rich in high-quality protein and various trace elements, nutritious. It is moderately difficult to make, requiring handling live crabs, controlling heat and seasoning ratios, somewhat challenging for beginners, and takes about 1 hour in total.
steps: Cut potatoes into 3 cm cubes, green and red bell peppers into 4 cm diamond-shaped slices, onions into 3 cm wide crescent moons, and rice cakes into 1 cm thick slices.
Cut crabs into 50-80 g pieces, coat lightly with starch, deep-fry at 180°C for 1 minute, then remove.
Heat oil in a wok, sauté garlic, ginger, and dried chili until fragrant. Add all sauces and rock sugar, stir-fry over low heat until red oil appears, being careful not to burn.
Add crabs, potatoes, beer, and water. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 12 minutes.
Add rice cakes and green/red bell peppers. Turn up the heat to reduce the sauce until it coats the ingredients. Finally, sprinkle white pepper powder.

howtocook:aquatic/芥末黄油罗氏虾/芥末黄油罗氏虾.name: Wasabi Butter Prawns
howtocook:aquatic/芥末黄油罗氏虾/芥末黄油罗氏虾.description: This dish features the wonderful pairing of butter and wasabi. The prawn meat is firm and bouncy, with a rich and spicy sauce. It's a modern seafood dish. Prawns are rich in high-quality protein and various trace elements, making it nutritious. The difficulty is moderate; with proper preparation and heat control, even beginners can succeed. From preparing live prawns to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Cut off the sharp spikes, antennae, and legs of the prawns. Use scissors to butterfly the backs and remove the digestive tracts.
Mix the wasabi sauce in advance: soy sauce, oyster sauce, wasabi, salt, sugar. Stir well!
Wash the cilantro and cut into sections. Set aside.
Drain the prawns. Add oil to the pan, place the prawns in, and cook over medium heat until the surface turns golden. Remove.

Add minced garlic, turn to high heat, and stir-fry in the remaining oil. When white smoke appears and the garlic is fragrant, add back the prawns and butter. Let the prawns fully absorb the butter aroma.
Add the prepared sauce, continue boiling over high heat, and stir-fry the prawns until the sauce thickens. Add wine (30g of cooking wine or beer, or 15g of rum for a stronger flavor).
When the sauce is slightly reduced, add cilantro, stir-fry a couple of times, and serve.

howtocook:aquatic/葱油桂鱼/葱油桂鱼.name: Steamed Mandarin Fish with Scallion Oil
howtocook:aquatic/葱油桂鱼/葱油桂鱼.description: Steamed Mandarin Fish with Scallion Oil is a home-style steamed dish with rich scallion aroma and tender fish. It belongs to light Chinese flavors and suits all palates. Mandarin fish is rich in high-quality protein and unsaturated fatty acids. Through careful deodorization, it retains the fish's freshness and nutrition. The recipe is beginner-friendly and tolerant of mistakes; as long as you follow the steps, it's basically foolproof. From prep to serving, it takes about 45 minutes in total.
steps: Buy a pre-cleaned fish from the market (if cleaning yourself, remove the innards). Scrape off all scales from the fish skin.
Use a paper towel to wipe away the blood and black membrane inside the belly (blood affects texture; black membrane is the source of fishy smell).
Scrape the fish skin back and forth with a knife to remove the slime, further removing fishy smell. Then rinse the fish inside and out with water.

Place the fish flat on a cutting board. Pat dry inside and out with paper towels. With the head on the left and tail on the right, make vertical cuts every 3 cm starting from the gill, cutting down to the backbone. Repeat on the other side.
Place the fish flat in a basin, ensuring there is no excess water in the basin.
Take 50g of ginger (about the size of an egg). Peel the skin with a peeler and wash it. Slice into 3 mm thick slices.
Wash the bird's eye chilies, remove the stems, and cut into 2 mm thick rounds (or 1 mm wide strips).
Wash the scallions, remove the roots, and cut into 3 cm pieces. For thicker scallions, split them lengthwise down the middle.
Add 8g salt and 25g cooking wine to the basin. Put on disposable gloves and massage the fish all over for 1 minute, ensuring every part is evenly coated with salt and wine.
After massaging, insert one ginger slice into each cut on the fish, and put 3 ginger slices inside the belly. Marinate for 10 minutes (don't marinate too long, or the fish will lose freshness).
While the fish marinates, add 5L of water to a steamer pot. Bring to a boil, then place a steamer basket on top.
After marinating, liquid will be released. Discard the excess liquid, along with the used cooking wine and ginger slices. Rinse the fish body and belly with water, then pat dry with paper towels.
Place the fish flat on a steaming plate. Insert ginger slices into the cuts and belly again.
Put the steaming plate into the steamer basket. Cover and steam over medium heat for 20 minutes.
During steaming, steam will condense on the fish and plate, forming fish broth. After taking it out, do not discard this broth; it is the essence of umami.
Use heat-resistant tongs to remove the plate. Drizzle 10g of steamed fish soy sauce over the fish and around it.
Evenly sprinkle the scallion pieces and bird's eye chilies over the fish and around it.
Pour 15g of vegetable oil into an iron pan. Heat over medium-low heat for 5 minutes. Do not use high heat, or the oil will evaporate quickly.
Slowly and evenly pour the hot oil over the fish. The umami-packed steamed mandarin fish with scallion oil is ready!

howtocook:aquatic/葱烧海参/葱烧海参.name: Braised Sea Cucumber with Scallion
howtocook:aquatic/葱烧海参/葱烧海参.description: This is a classic Shandong home-style braised seafood dish, featuring rich scallion aroma and soft, flavorful sea cucumber. The sauce is thick, savory, and slightly sweet. Sea cucumber is rich in collagen and trace elements, beneficial for nourishing the body. The overall cooking difficulty is low, but requires time to fully rehydrate dried sea cucumber beforehand. The actual braising process takes about 30 minutes. Beginners can succeed easily by following the steps.
steps: Cut the scallion whites into 1 cm sections. Set aside.
Cut the sea cucumber into 1 cm sections. Set aside.
Prepare a small bowl. Mix 20g oyster sauce, 10g light soy sauce, and 2g sugar. Sauce.
In another bowl, mix starch and water to make a slurry for thickening.
Heat the pan. Add 20-25 ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for oil to heat.
Add scallion whites. Reduce heat to low. Be careful not to burn them. Fry for about 3-5 minutes. Scallion whites.
Remove scallion whites with chopsticks and place on a plate. Set aside.
Pour in the prepared sauce. Stir-fry until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the cut sea cucumber. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add 100 ml water. Cook over medium-low heat for 5 minutes.
When the sauce is almost dry, add the starch slurry and the reserved scallion whites.
When the sauce becomes thick and sticky, turn off the heat. Plate and serve. Preview image.

howtocook:aquatic/蒜蓉虾/蒜蓉虾.name: Garlic Shrimp
howtocook:aquatic/蒜蓉虾/蒜蓉虾.description: This classic Cantonese dish is known for its fresh, fragrant, tender, and smooth texture. The shrimp are sweet and bouncy, with a strong aroma of garlic. Sea shrimp are rich in high-quality protein, calcium, selenium, and other trace elements. Paired with garlic sauce, it helps kill bacteria and stimulate appetite. The method is very simple: prepare the shrimp, spread the sauce, steam, and drizzle with hot oil. Even a beginner can easily master it. The whole process takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Cut from the middle of the shrimp head to 1 cm from the tail
Spread the garlic sauce in the middle of the shrimp body and place on a plate
Pour hot water into a pot, put the plate in the pot, and steam over high heat for 3 minutes
Heat oil until hot, pour over the shrimp on the plate, then add light soy sauce

howtocook:aquatic/蒜香黄油虾/蒜香黄油虾.name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
howtocook:aquatic/蒜香黄油虾/蒜香黄油虾.description: Garlic Butter Shrimp is a classic Western seafood dish. The shrimp is tender and bouncy, coated in rich garlic and butter with a hint of lemon. Shrimp is high in quality protein, and the butter provides energy, making it a quick nutritional boost. The recipe is simple, requiring only a few common ingredients. Even beginners can easily make it, from prep to plate in about twenty minutes.
steps: Remove the heads and shells from the shrimp, leaving the tails on. Use a toothpick to devein, rinse, and pat dry with paper towels.
Mince the garlic and set aside.
Heat a frying pan over medium heat and add 10 ml olive oil.
When the oil is hot, add the shrimp and sear for 1-1.5 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, add the butter. Once melted, add the minced garlic and sauté over low heat until fragrant (about 30 seconds).
If using white wine, add it now and cook until the alcohol evaporates (about 1 minute).
Return the shrimp to the pan and toss to coat evenly with the garlic butter sauce (about 1 minute).
Squeeze in lemon juice, toss to combine, and immediately turn off the heat.
Plate and drizzle with remaining sauce from the pan.

howtocook:aquatic/蛏抱蛋/蛏抱蛋.name: Razor Clams with Egg
howtocook:aquatic/蛏抱蛋/蛏抱蛋.description: Razor clams with egg is a traditional home-style dish from Fuzhou, Fujian. The clam meat is coated in egg and pan-fried until crispy outside and tender inside, with the savory aroma of onion and light soy sauce. Razor clams are rich in high-quality protein, zinc, iron, and other trace elements, making them a balanced match with eggs. The difficulty is moderate; you need to control the frying heat, but the steps are not complicated. It takes about 30 minutes to serve.
steps: Bring water to a boil, add clams, cook for 2 minutes, then remove and shell them. Place in a large bowl.
Add onion, light soy sauce, cooking wine, chicken bouillon, and cornstarch to the bowl. Stir well.
Crack 2 eggs into the bowl and continue stirring.
Heat oil in a pan, pour in the clam mixture. Fry until one side is golden, then flip and continue frying.

howtocook:aquatic/酱炖蟹.name: Braised Crab with Sauce
howtocook:aquatic/酱炖蟹.description: This dish is a limited autumn flavor from Pinghu area of Zhejiang, with a savory and salty taste. The crab roe is rich and smooth with sauce fragrance, the crab meat is sweet, and the sauce is perfect for mixing with rice. Rich in high-quality protein and trace elements. Moderate difficulty, suitable for beginners with some experience. Preparation to finish takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Scrub the crabs clean, then split them in half on a cutting board.
Add vegetable oil to the pot, stir-fry minced ginger and bean paste until fragrant, add rock sugar and stir until dissolved, until small bubbles appear, then remove from heat.
Spread a layer of sauce on a plate, then place the cut crabs face down neatly on the sauce.
Add some scallion sections and ginger slices. It is recommended to crack an egg or spread minced meat on the bottom of the plate.
Steam for 10-12 minutes.

ingredients.name: Crab (preferably river crab, alternatively swimming crab)
Vegetable oil (unrefined rapeseed oil, commonly called 'crude rapeseed oil' or 'local rapeseed oil', alternative: peanut oil)

howtocook:aquatic/阳朔啤酒鱼/阳朔啤酒鱼.name: Yangshuo Beer Fish
howtocook:aquatic/阳朔啤酒鱼/阳朔啤酒鱼.description: Yangshuo Beer Fish is a famous local dish from Yangshuo, Guangxi. It uses fresh fish from the Lijiang River, fried with scales on, then braised with beer instead of water. The scales are crispy, the flesh tender. The aroma of beer blends with the slight acidity of tomatoes, creating a rich and unique flavor. The fish is rich in high-quality protein and minerals. Paired with tomatoes and peppers, it is packed with vitamins and whets the appetite. Moderate difficulty; a bit challenging for beginners but doable by following the steps. It takes about 30 minutes to finish.
steps: Clean the fish, remove innards but keep scales. Rinse clean. Butterfly the fish from the belly (keeping the back connected). Marinate with 15ml cooking wine, ginger slices, and 3g salt for 15 minutes.
Cut tomato into chunks, green and red peppers into chunks. Smash garlic cloves, slice ginger, cut scallion into sections (separate white and green parts).

Rub the bottom of the pan with ginger slices. Heat the pan and add 30ml cooking oil. When oil is hot, place the fish in (skin side down). Fry until both sides are golden and scales are crispy. Remove and set aside.
Leave a little oil in the pan. Add scallion whites, ginger slices, and garlic cloves. Stir-fry until fragrant.
Add 15g Guilin chili sauce. Stir-fry until red oil releases.
Add tomato chunks. Stir-fry until tomatoes release juice and soften.
Pour in a whole bottle of beer. Add 15ml light soy sauce, 5ml dark soy sauce, 5g sugar, and 5g salt. Stir to combine.
Place the fried fish into the pan. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low heat. Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Uncover, add green and red pepper chunks. Continue cooking for 3-5 minutes.
Increase heat to reduce the sauce until thickened. Sprinkle with scallion greens. Serve on a plate.

howtocook:aquatic/香煎翘嘴鱼/香煎翘嘴鱼.name: Pan-Fried Topmouth Culter
howtocook:aquatic/香煎翘嘴鱼/香煎翘嘴鱼.description: Pan-fried topmouth culter is a flavorful home-style river fish dish. The fish is salted and dried for a couple of days, then pan-fried until the skin is golden and crispy. The meat is tender and savory, with a hint of spicy sweetness from broad bean paste and green peppers. Rich in protein and healthy fats, it's nutritious and easy to digest. Preparation requires 2-4 days for salting and drying, and cooking takes about half an hour. Frying needs patience with heat control, a bit challenging for beginners, but the result is aromatic and goes perfectly with rice.
steps: Have the fish killed and cleaned by making a cut along the back (ask the fishmonger to do it; never cut the belly open), then wash thoroughly.
Rub salt evenly over the fish surface. Add about 80ml cooking wine and 20g minced ginger. Place in the refrigerator for 1-2 days to marinate.
Remove the marinated fish, hang it up with string to air-dry until half-dry (about 1-2 days, depending on temperature and sunlight).
Before cooking, rinse the fish with clean water and drain well (to prevent oil splatter).
Heat the wok over high heat, then quickly reduce to low heat. Add oil, making sure to coat the entire surface. Slide the fish into the wok along the edge (fry the back side first).
After placing the fish in the wok (and after flipping), do not move the fish immediately (the skin might break). After about 30 seconds, try shaking the wok.
Fry the back side for about 1 minute, then flip and fry the other side for about 1-2 minutes, until both sides are golden.
When both sides are cooked, push the fish to the side of the wok, leaving space to stir-fry the broad bean paste until fragrant, then add ginger and garlic.
After the aromatics are fragrant, add cooking wine, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and hot water. The water level should be level with the fish or slightly less.
Turn to medium-high heat and cook for 5-10 minutes. Then add green pepper segments, sugar, chicken bouillon, thirteen-spice powder, and aged vinegar.
Turn to low heat for 2-5 minutes, add spring onions and cilantro, then serve.

howtocook:aquatic/鲤鱼炖白菜/鲤鱼炖白菜.name: Braised Carp with Napa Cabbage
howtocook:aquatic/鲤鱼炖白菜/鲤鱼炖白菜.description: Braised carp with napa cabbage is a fragrant and rich home-style stew. The fish is tender and flavorful, while the cabbage is sweet and absorbs the sauce. It's a typical northern Chinese dish. Carp is rich in high-quality protein and unsaturated fatty acids, paired with cabbage for dietary fiber and vitamins, offering balanced nutrition. The process requires frying and simmering; moderately difficult but not too complex, suitable for cooks with some experience. From prep to serving takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Clean the carp and make slashes on both sides (cut a few deep slits for better flavor absorption).
Wash the baby cabbage and set aside on a plate.
Heat oil in a pot. When hot, add a little salt, ginger, garlic, Pixian bean paste, cinnamon stick, and star anise. Stir-fry until fragrant.
Place the fish in the pot and fry for 3 minutes, flipping every 30 seconds.
Add water (enough to nearly cover the fish, slightly less is fine). Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and the baby cabbage.
Simmer over high heat for 15-20 minutes. When the sauce is almost dried up, add salt to taste. Remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:aquatic/鳊鱼炖豆腐/鳊鱼炖豆腐.name: Braised Bream with Tofu
howtocook:aquatic/鳊鱼炖豆腐/鳊鱼炖豆腐.description: Braised Bream with Tofu is a home-style red-cooked dish. The fish is tender and flavorful, the tofu soaks up the sauce, with a savory, slightly spicy taste. Bream is rich in high-quality protein and unsaturated fatty acids, while tofu provides plant protein and calcium, making a balanced meal. The cooking difficulty is moderate; frying the fish requires some patience to prevent sticking, but the steps are clear and beginners can try. It takes about 40 minutes from preparation to completion.
steps: Score the bream, top with ginger slices and cooking wine, and marinate for 5-10 minutes.
Cut the old tofu into pieces and set aside in water.
Add oil to the pan; you can add a little salt to prevent sticking. Pat the marinated fish dry with paper towels, place it in the pan, and fry both sides.
When both sides are browned, push the fish to the side, leaving space to add scallions, ginger, garlic, dried chili, bay leaves, and star anise; stir-fry until fragrant.
After the aromatics are fragrant, add cooking wine, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, rock sugar, and cinnamon. Pour in hot water until just level with the fish or slightly less.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then add the old tofu, placing it around the edges. Add salt, then reduce to low heat.
Simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes, then increase heat to reduce the sauce slightly. Serve.

howtocook:aquatic/黄油煎虾/黄油煎虾.name: Pan-fried Shrimp with Butter
howtocook:aquatic/黄油煎虾/黄油煎虾.description: Pan-fried Shrimp with Butter is a home-style seafood dish with rich flavor, tender and springy shrimp meat. Fresh shrimp are rich in high-quality protein and minerals like calcium and zinc, paired with butter for added creaminess and energy. It's relatively simple to make, just need patience in handling live shrimp; even beginners can master it, taking about an hour in total.
steps: Remove the heads from the fresh shrimp, pulling out the vein along the way (if not done well, you can remove it when butterflying the shrimp in the next step). Use scissors to cut or a knife to slit open the back of the shrimp. Drain and set aside.
Prepare the sauce: In a small bowl, combine all of the light soy sauce, rice wine, sugar, and salt as listed above. Stir well and set aside.
Heat the pan over medium-high heat. Add cooking oil into the hot pan and wait for 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add all the shrimp to the pan. Start grinding black pepper from the bottle, sprinkle evenly over the shrimp, and stir-fry.
Once the shrimp change color, add the butter. When the butter has completely melted, pour in the prepared sauce. Continue stir-frying.
Stir-fry over high heat for 15 seconds to reduce the sauce, then serve.

howtocook:breakfast/吐司果酱.name: Toast with Jam
howtocook:breakfast/吐司果酱.description: This is an extremely simple and quick breakfast. Toast lightly crispy, then sandwich with sweet jam. Crispy outside, soft inside, easy to carry. A typical convenient Western-style light meal. Mainly provides carbohydrates and sugar, quickly replenishing morning energy. Almost zero skill required, perfect for busy beginners who are not good at cooking. From start to finish takes about 2 minutes, no need to turn on the stove, and almost no dishes to wash.
steps: Put the toast into the toaster.
Set the browning level; it will pop up automatically when done.
After two minutes, the toast is done and pops up.
Take out one slice of toast, spread with jam, then cover with the other slice.
Wrap it in a napkin so you can eat it on the go or eat it before leaving.

ingredients.name: Jam
ingredients.note: Enough to spread on one side of toast

howtocook:breakfast/太阳蛋.name: Sunny Egg
howtocook:breakfast/太阳蛋.description: Sunny Egg is a quick homemade breakfast made in the microwave. The texture is tender and smooth, with the egg white set and the yolk slightly runny – a typical quick dish. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and various vitamins, easy to digest and absorb. The method is very simple and beginner-friendly. With a controllable microwave, it takes only 3 minutes; with a non-controllable one, just a few minutes of repeated heating.
steps: Prepare a small bowl, pour in the oil that was calculated in the previous step, sprinkle salt, and stir well. Tilt the bowl to coat the surface with oil.
Take out one egg and crack it into the small bowl.
Poke holes in the yolk surface. Poke 5 holes with a toothpick or 1 hole with a chopstick.
Place in the microwave and microwave on medium heat for 3 minutes.
Prepare a small bowl, pour in the oil that was calculated in the previous step, sprinkle salt, and stir well. Tilt the bowl to coat the surface with oil.
Take out one egg and crack it into the small bowl.
Poke holes in the yolk surface. Poke 5 holes with a toothpick or 1 hole with a chopstick.
Place in the microwave and microwave for 1 minute.
while (the sunny egg is not largely solidified) microwave for 30 seconds;

howtocook:breakfast/完美水煮蛋.name: Perfect Boiled Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/完美水煮蛋.description: This is an egg made using a cyclic water boiling method developed by scientists. The yolk is dense and delicate, the white is evenly solidified, and the texture is layered. It retains more nutrients, with polyphenol content 23% higher than traditional boiling methods, and rich in protein. The difficulty is high, requiring precise temperature control and repeated transfers. Estimated cooking time is about 35 minutes.
steps: Prepare two pots of water: Pot A maintains 100°C boiling water, Pot B maintains 30°C warm water.
Use a slotted spoon to put the eggs into Pot A, start the timer.
Precisely every 2 minutes, transfer the eggs to the other pot of water.
Repeat the transfer operation a total of 16 times (total time 32 minutes).
After the last transfer, let sit in Pot B for 30 seconds.
Immediately place in ice water (0°C) to stop heating (hold for 30 seconds).
When peeling, start from the blunt end (air cell) and peel the membrane along the longitudinal axis.

howtocook:breakfast/微波炉蒸蛋.name: Microwave Steamed Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/微波炉蒸蛋.description: A quick steamed egg dish made in the microwave, tender and smooth like pudding. It's a home-style fast dish. Rich in high-quality protein, easy to digest and absorb, suitable for breakfast or light meal. Very simple to make, even beginners can handle it. Takes about 10 minutes in total.
steps: Beat the eggs, add warm water or broth, salt, and light soy sauce. Stir gently to avoid bubbles.
Strain the egg mixture into a heatproof bowl. If there are bubbles on the surface, poke them with a toothpick.
Cover with plastic wrap and poke 8-10 small holes, or use a microwave-safe lid (leave a gap).
Place in the microwave and heat:
700W: 1 min 30 sec → then heat for 20-30 sec more if needed until the surface just sets.
600W: about 1 min 40 sec to 2 min 10 sec.
800W: about 1 min 10 sec to 1 min 40 sec.
After heating, let it sit for 1 minute to allow residual heat to fully cook the center.
Drizzle with sesame oil and sprinkle chopped green onions before serving.

howtocook:breakfast/微波炉荷包蛋.name: Microwave Poached Egg
howtocook:breakfast/微波炉荷包蛋.description: Microwave poached egg is a quick and easy home-cooked dish. The egg white is tender and smooth, the yolk is soft and sticky, with the fragrance of sesame oil. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and lecithin. The method is extremely simple, even beginners can master it easily, taking only about 2 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Crack the egg into a small bowl, use chopsticks to poke 2 holes in each egg yolk to avoid messing up the microwave when heating.
Then pour room temperature drinking water into the bowl.
Then add salt to the bowl.
Finally add sesame oil.
Place the bowl with ingredients into the microwave and heat on high for 80 seconds.
After the set time, use a cloth to pad your hands and take out the finished product.

howtocook:breakfast/微波炉蛋糕.name: Microwave Cake
howtocook:breakfast/微波炉蛋糕.description: A quick microwave cake, soft and sweet, a family dessert that even beginners can easily make. Based on eggs and flour, with butter for energy and rich aroma, sugar for quick energy boost, adding nuts or fruits for fiber and vitamins. Only a few ingredients needed, finished in one minute on high microwave power, from prep to taste in as little as 2 minutes, about 20 minutes for a beginner.
steps: Add the following ingredients, making sure not to exceed 3/4 of the container
Scoop butter the size of half an egg into the container, microwave for 15 seconds until melted
Mash/melt chocolate/banana into small pieces/paste
Crack an egg and beat it well
15g white (or brown) sugar (add more if you have a sweet tooth) (see note 4)
Add 1g salt (if later flavor ingredients contain salt, adjust accordingly)
Add 2.5g baking powder
Add 15g flour (gluten content doesn't matter much)
Add any flavor ingredients you like! (Do not add dry ingredients like nuts and cookies now)
Mix until no dry powder remains, resembling thick yogurt
Add optional dry ingredients (excluding instant powders) on top
Praise yourself 🥰
Microwave on high for 1 minute (until fluffy cake form)
Take out the cup (it's hot!!! 😱) and take a photo for social media, then eat

howtocook:breakfast/意式香肠北非蛋.name: Italian Sausage Shakshuka
howtocook:breakfast/意式香肠北非蛋.description: Italian Sausage Shakshuka is a rich and fragrant Mediterranean-style dish. Soft-boiled eggs sit in a sweet and sour tomato meat sauce, served with crusty bread for dipping. The main ingredients provide high-quality protein, while tomatoes, bell peppers, and onions bring vitamins and fiber. It's easy to make, just pay attention to the heat when steaming the eggs. It takes about 30 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Remove the casing from the Italian pork sausage and set aside; dice the onion; julienne the bell pepper.
Pour 10-15ml olive oil into a wide skillet and heat over medium heat.
Add the prepared sausage or luncheon meat and stir-fry until golden brown and crispy on the edges (if using raw sausage, break it up with a spoon as it cooks).
Add the diced onion and julienned bell pepper to the pan and cook with the meat, stirring for 4-5 minutes until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.
Pour in the Barilla Napoli pasta sauce and stir well. Reduce heat to medium-low and let it simmer gently for 2-3 minutes (if the sauce is too thick, add 15ml water).
Using the back of a spoon, make 3-4 small wells in the sauce.
Carefully crack an egg into each well.

Cover the skillet and let the egg whites steam until set. Wait 3-5 minutes. Note: Check frequently. When the egg whites are opaque and set but the yolks still jiggle when the pan is gently shaken (soft-boiled state), it's done.
Turn off the heat. Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese, fresh parsley, or dried chili flakes to taste.
Serve the skillet directly at the table with crusty bread for dipping into the yolks and sauce.

ingredients.name: Italian pork sausage / Chorizo / Luncheon meat

howtocook:breakfast/手抓饼.name: Hand-Grasp Pancake
howtocook:breakfast/手抓饼.description: Hand-Grasp Pancake is crispy outside and soft inside, with distinct layers. It is a beloved home-style pastry. The dough is made with the half-boiled water method, making it elastic and chewy. Paired with eggs and vegetables, it is nutritionally balanced. The recipe is beginner-friendly, taking only about 40 minutes from mixing to serving, perfect for busy mornings.
steps: Put flour in a bowl, add boiling water and stir into flocculent, then add cold water and knead into a smooth dough. Cover with a damp cloth and let rest for 20 minutes.
Divide the dough into portions of about 100 grams each, roll into balls, and roll out into thin sheets.
Evenly brush with cooking oil, sprinkle with salt, roll into a snail shape, and let rest for 10 minutes.
Roll the dough again into a thin pancake with even thickness.
Heat oil in a pan, fry over low heat until both sides are golden and bubbly.
On the cooked pancake, layer fried egg, lettuce, ham, cheese slice, and other toppings, then roll up.

howtocook:breakfast/桂圆红枣粥.name: Longan and Red Date Congee
howtocook:breakfast/桂圆红枣粥.description: A sweet, soft, and sticky traditional Chinese congee. Made by cooking glutinous rice with red dates and longan, it is naturally sweet and warms the body and stomach. It has nourishing effects such as enriching the blood, calming the mind, improving brain function, and tonifying the heart and spleen. The method is very friendly, even beginners can easily master it. It takes about 70 minutes from preparation to the aroma filling the house.
steps: Remove the longan pulp, rinse twice with water, and soak in a bowl for 10 minutes
Rinse the red dates twice with water and soak in a bowl for 10 minutes
Put the glutinous rice in a rice cooker, rinse twice, then add 2000 ml of water
Add the longan and red dates to the rice cooker
Turn on the rice cooker's cooking mode; the congee will be ready in 1 hour

howtocook:breakfast/水煮玉米.name: Boiled Corn
howtocook:breakfast/水煮玉米.description: Boiled corn is a quick and easy home-cooked dish, with a sweet and soft texture. Fresh corn is cooked with the husk on to retain its original aroma, and adding salt and sugar enhances the flavor. It is rich in dietary fiber, vitamins, and carbohydrates, providing a sense of fullness. The method is extremely simple, even beginners can easily master it. From preparation to serving, it takes only about 15 minutes.
steps: Peel off the outer husks of the fresh corn, leaving some husks on, and put into the pot.
Add water until it covers the corn by about half a finger's depth, then add salt and sugar.
After the water boils, reduce to low heat, cover, and continue cooking for 15-20 minutes. It's fine to cook the corn a bit longer.
After cooking, drain the water and let it cool before eating.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.note: Covers corn by half a finger

howtocook:breakfast/温泉蛋/温泉蛋.name: Onsen Tamago
howtocook:breakfast/温泉蛋/温泉蛋.description: Onsen Tamago originated in Japan. The egg white is semi-liquid and curd-like, while the yolk is warm and solid, giving a smooth and delicate texture—the exact opposite of a soft-boiled egg. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein, lecithin, and various vitamins, offering comprehensive nutrition. To make it, you need a thermometer to keep the water at 70°C. Although it requires attention to temperature, the steps are simple; after patiently waiting about half an hour, it's done. Serve with kombu soy sauce and chopped scallions for a fresh and savory flavor. It's an easy-to-make snack.
steps: Fill a pot with enough tap water so that the water level is about 3 cm above the eggs. Insert a thermometer into the water.
Turn on the heat (gas or induction) and gradually adjust the power or flame to keep the water temperature at 70°C.
Gently place the eggs into the pot. Do not stack them; they should all sit on the bottom with some space to move around.
Maintain the temperature for 25 minutes.
Prepare a bowl of ice water.
Remove the eggs and immediately transfer them to the ice water. Let them sit for 3 minutes.
Crack the egg into a small bowl. Done.

howtocook:breakfast/溏心蛋.name: Soft-Boiled Egg
howtocook:breakfast/溏心蛋.description: This is a home-style egg dish that can be easily made using a regular electric pot. The yolk is in a runny, soft-boiled state, tender and smooth. Each egg provides about 6 grams of high-quality protein, especially suitable for fitness enthusiasts to supplement nutrition. The process is simple and clear; as long as you master the heating and resting times, beginners can quickly get the hang of it. The entire process takes only about 15 minutes.
steps: Place the eggs in the electric pot. Do not stack the eggs; they should all be on the bottom with space to shake.
Pour in cold water until the eggs are submerged by about 2 cm.
Open the lid, heat at maximum power until the water comes to a rolling boil (about 85-95°C, slightly bubbling, not fully boiling).
Turn off the heat, cover the lid, and let the eggs rest.
For a runny yolk in the center, let rest for 6 minutes.
For a fully cooked, crumbly yolk, let rest for 10 minutes.
Drain the water, rinse the eggs with cold water for about 1 minute, then peel and serve.

howtocook:breakfast/煎饺.name: Pan-Fried Dumplings
howtocook:breakfast/煎饺.description: Pan-fried dumplings are a home-style pastry with a golden crispy bottom and tender, juicy filling, commonly found on northern Chinese tables. Made with frozen dumplings, they are rich in carbohydrates and protein, simple and quick. Estimated difficulty is two stars, very beginner-friendly, and takes about 15 minutes to serve.
steps: Take out a frying pan (non-stick is best)
Add 10ml - 15ml cooking oil
Turn on the heat, place dumplings (spread them out as evenly as possible, avoid stacking)
Immediately add water until it reaches halfway up the dumplings
Cover with a lid (the stove should be on high heat)
Wait for 8 - 10 minutes
When only about 2mm of water remains, reduce to medium heat and start pan-frying
When all water has evaporated, shake the pan to heat the dumplings evenly
Add black sesame seeds and chopped green onions, then cover and let steam for 10 seconds
After 1 - 2 minutes, take out one dumpling to check the bottom; if golden and crispy, remove immediately

howtocook:breakfast/蒸水蛋.name: Steamed Egg Custard
howtocook:breakfast/蒸水蛋.description: This is a home-cooked dish known for its smooth, pudding-like texture and elegant, delicious flavor. It is enjoyed across China, and in the north it is affectionately called 'egg cake'. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and lecithin, making them a convenient choice for daily nutrition. The key is to master the egg-to-water ratio and slow steaming over low heat. It's very beginner-friendly and takes about 15 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Crack eggs into a bowl and beat.
In another container, pour in warm water (20-30°C) 1.5 times the volume of the egg liquid (half an eggshell is 0.5 times water), and dissolve the salt in the water.
Pour the salted water into the egg mixture, stir evenly in one direction (clockwise or counterclockwise). Skim off any bubbles and discard, or strain for a smoother texture.
Cover the bowl with aluminum foil (or a plate), and place it into a pot with a lid that has about 3cm depth of water pre-added.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to the lowest heat and steam for another 4 minutes.

howtocook:breakfast/蒸花卷.name: Steamed Scallion Rolls
howtocook:breakfast/蒸花卷.description: This is a traditional northern Chinese flour-based food, soft and delicious, with a light wheat aroma. Using frozen scallion rolls to steam, it can quickly provide carbohydrates and dietary fiber. The method is extremely simple, even beginners can master it easily, and the whole process only takes half an hour, making it an ideal quick breakfast.
steps: Take out 5 scallion rolls from the packaging bag.
Arrange the rolls flat on a plate, try not to overlap.
Pour 400ml water into the pot, place the steaming rack inside, put the plate with rolls on the rack, and cover with lid.
Turn to high heat until water boils.
Reduce to medium heat and steam for 15 minutes.
Open the lid and feel the surface temperature of the rolls with your hand. If not hot enough, continue to cover and heat; otherwise, turn off the heat and remove.
Take out the plate and let it cool to 50°C before serving.

howtocook:breakfast/燕麦鸡蛋饼.name: Oat Egg Pancake
howtocook:breakfast/燕麦鸡蛋饼.description: A quick and nutritious breakfast. Soft, with the grain aroma of oats and the richness of eggs and milk. A simple home-style dish. Rich in protein, carbs, and fiber, it provides energy. Great for fitness enthusiasts and weekday mornings. Easy to make, beginner-friendly; from prep to plate takes about ten minutes.
steps: Mix milk and dry oats until thick and sticky.
Beat eggs until uniform in color.
Pour the egg mixture into the oat milk and stir until thick and even.
Add a layer of butter to a non-stick pan and spread evenly.
Pour in the batter and spread into a pancake shape.
Cook over low heat for 2-3 minutes. If adding vegetables, sprinkle chopped greens during cooking.
Flip and cook for another 2 minutes.
Serve with the remaining milk as breakfast.

howtocook:breakfast/牛奶燕麦.name: Milk Oatmeal
howtocook:breakfast/牛奶燕麦.description: A quick, nutritious breakfast high in protein and fiber, combining the smoothness of milk, the graininess of oatmeal, and the crispy tenderness of a fried egg. Warm, soft, and easy to digest, it keeps you full. The process is simple and beginner-friendly, taking about three minutes. Pair with fruits or vegetables for a fresher taste.
steps: Pour milk into a breakfast cup (cold is fine)
Prepare 200ml water. If it's drinking water, add oatmeal directly; otherwise, bring to a boil then add oatmeal
Cook for 2 minutes after water boils, oatmeal is done
Scoop out the cooked oatmeal and add to the milk (try not to pour in the oatmeal water, as it affects texture)
Replace oatmeal with quick-cook oatmeal
Pour milk into the container with quick-cook oatmeal and stir
Place the mixture in a microwave
Microwave on medium power for 4 minutes
Heat a pan, add a thin layer of oil. When oil is hot, fry the egg, 20 seconds per side. Consider seasoning (3g pepper salt, optional)
Turn off the heat and plate

howtocook:breakfast/空气炸锅面包片.name: Air Fryer Bread Slices
howtocook:breakfast/空气炸锅面包片.description: Air fryer bread slices are an extremely simple and quick staple food, crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, with pure wheat flavor. They are especially suitable for weight loss periods. Rich in dietary fiber and plant protein, they provide a longer feeling of fullness. The method requires no skill; just put the bread slices in the air fryer. Very beginner-friendly, from preparation to completion takes only about five minutes.
steps: Take out two slices of bread (preferably whole grain bread slices)
Place the bread slices vertically into the air fryer
Bake at 200°C for 5 minutes
Remove and serve

howtocook:breakfast/美式炒蛋.name: American-style Scrambled Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/美式炒蛋.description: This is a soft and fluffy American home-style egg dish. Adding milk makes the curds fine and the texture moist, rich in high-quality protein and calcium. The method is simple and beginner-friendly, taking about 20 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Crack eggs into a large bowl, add salt and whisk until foamy. Let rest for 15 minutes.
Cut butter into small pieces and add to pan. Pour in egg mixture and cook over low heat, stirring constantly.
As soon as the butter melts, quickly stir and break the eggs into fine curds. Turn off the heat just before the eggs are fully set.
Add milk and stir for 15 seconds until the scrambled eggs are moist and creamy. Serve on a plate.

howtocook:breakfast/苏格兰蛋/苏格兰蛋.name: Scottish Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/苏格兰蛋/苏格兰蛋.description: This simplified Scottish egg has a crispy outer layer and a soft, runny yolk inside, combining the milky aroma of cheese and the smoky flavor of bacon. It is a creative snack derived from British cuisine. Eggs provide high-quality protein, cheese supplies calcium, and paired with the hand-grabbed flatbread crust, it meets the energy needs of a meal. The cooking process is not complicated, and beginners can easily master it. From preparation to serving, it takes only about 20 to 30 minutes.
steps: Put eggs in cold water, bring to a boil, and cook for 3 minutes. Then remove.
Remove the eggs and place them in ice water for easier and more complete peeling.
Wrap each egg with a slice of cheese.
Wrap each egg with a slice of bacon.
Cut off both ends of the hand-grabbed flatbread and wrap the egg in a rectangular shape.
Heat oil to about 60% (when the surface shimmers, wisps of smoke appear, and bubbles form around a chopstick inserted). Fry until golden brown.
Air fry at 160°C for 15 minutes.
Cut open and serve.

howtocook:breakfast/茶叶蛋.name: Tea Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/茶叶蛋.description: This traditional snack is rich in tea aroma, savory and delicious, packed with high-quality protein, making it a quick and nutritious breakfast choice. The steps are clear, moderate difficulty. Beginners should pay attention to heat control and spice ratios. It takes about 30 minutes to complete, serving 2-3 people, perfect for a relaxed weekend preparation.
steps: Boil the eggs in cold water over high heat for about 8 minutes (adjust based on your stove).
Remove the eggs and rinse with cold water.
Tap the eggs against each other to crack the shells evenly.
Put the eggs back in the pot, add star anise, bay leaves, cinnamon, fennel seeds, rock sugar, black tea, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and salt.
Add water until the eggs are fully submerged.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low heat and simmer for 15 minutes.

howtocook:breakfast/蛋煎糍粑.name: Egg-fried Glutinous Rice Cake
howtocook:breakfast/蛋煎糍粑.description: A quick and easy snack, slightly crispy on the outside, soft and sticky inside, wrapped in egg aroma and sweetness. It's a Southern home-style flavor. Glutinous rice cakes provide rich carbs, eggs add protein, high in calories but very filling. The process is extremely simple, even beginners can handle it, and it takes only about ten minutes.
steps: Cut the glutinous rice cake into small rectangular pieces for easier frying.
Crack an egg into a bowl and beat it, add 2g of salt.
Dip each piece of glutinous rice cake into the beaten egg, coating both sides.
Pour 10ml of vegetable oil into the pan, place the coated pieces in, and fry over low heat until soft.
Slowly pour the remaining egg mixture over the surface of the rice cakes.
Flip the rice cakes with chopsticks or a spoon, fry until golden brown on both sides, then serve.

howtocook:breakfast/金枪鱼酱三明治.name: Tuna Salad Sandwich
howtocook:breakfast/金枪鱼酱三明治.description: This Western-style breakfast sandwich combines water-packed tuna with mayonnaise and Russian pickled cucumber juice, offering a savory, slightly tangy flavor and creamy texture. Paired with crispy toast, it's especially filling. Rich in high-quality protein and omega-3s, it provides a quick energy boost. The preparation is simple: just mix the sauce and heat it with a sandwich press. Even beginners can make it in 5 minutes.
steps: Pour the tuna, mayonnaise, and Russian pickled cucumber juice into a bowl. Stir with a spoon, ensuring the tuna chunks are broken up and the mixture becomes a paste. Set aside.
Place 1 slice of bread on the sandwich press.
Spread the prepared tuna mixture onto the bread. Suggested amount: 10-15 ml.
Cover with another square bread slice, press the sandwich press, and turn it on.
When the sandwich press automatically stops heating, remove and serve.

howtocook:breakfast/韩国麻药鸡蛋.name: Korean Marinated Eggs
howtocook:breakfast/韩国麻药鸡蛋.description: A sweet and spicy Korean soft-boiled egg side dish, with sauce coating the whites, smooth and tender, with a slight spicy aftertaste. Eggs provide high-quality protein and lecithin, nutritious and filling. The recipe is extremely simple, even beginners can easily master it. From boiling to marinating in the fridge, it takes about an hour and a half.
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Once boiling, add the eggs and cook for 7 minutes (for perfect soft-boiled eggs).
While the eggs are cooking, prepare the sauce: add 4 tablespoons soy sauce, 4 tablespoons water, 2 tablespoons mirin, and 2 tablespoons sugar to a small pot.
Heat the sauce until boiling, cook to evaporate the alcohol from the mirin, then turn off the heat and let it cool.
Add the sliced chili peppers into the cooled sauce.
Remove the boiled eggs, cool in ice water, then peel the shells.
Place the peeled eggs into the prepared sauce, drizzle 5ml sesame oil on top, and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
Refrigerate for 1-2 hours to allow the eggs to absorb the flavors.

ingredients.name: Korean soy sauce (or light soy sauce)
Chili pepper (bird's eye chili or green/red pepper)

howtocook:breakfast/鸡蛋三明治.name: Egg Sandwich
howtocook:breakfast/鸡蛋三明治.description: A simple and quick Western-style light meal. Soft bread slices hold creamy egg salad and savory bacon, tender yet crispy. Eggs provide high-quality protein, and bacon and butter quickly replenish energy. Very beginner-friendly, easy even for new cooks, taking only about 10 minutes.
steps: Trim the crusts off the bread slices and set aside.
Hard-boil the eggs, then mash them.
Mix the eggs, mayonnaise, salt, and black pepper.
Melt butter in a pan and fry the bacon until cooked.
Assemble the sandwich: place the egg mixture and bacon between two bread slices.
Cut the sandwich diagonally into triangles and serve.

howtocook:condiment/油泼辣子/油泼辣子.name: Oil-Splashed Chili Oil
howtocook:condiment/油泼辣子/油泼辣子.description: A fragrant and spicy Shaanxi-style condiment, with glossy red oil, intertwined aromas of sesame and garlic. It tastes spicy but not harsh, with a lingering aftertaste. Chili peppers are rich in vitamin C and capsaicin, which can stimulate appetite and warm the body. The process requires controlling oil temperature, a bit challenging for beginners, but step by step can succeed. Takes about 10 minutes in total, a finishing touch for home-style cold dishes and noodles.
steps: Take out the garlic head, break off 2 small garlic cloves, and peel them.
Take out the cutting board and chop the small garlic cloves and bird's eye chilies.
Take out a bowl and pour in peanut oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the other aromatics and scallion. When the aromatics turn brown, remove and discard.
Take out an iron wok, pour the oil from the bowl into the wok, and heat for 2 minutes (the rapeseed oil should heat until smoking).
Now the bowl is empty.
Add dried chili flakes, toasted white sesame seeds, chopped garlic, bird's eye chilies, salt, five-spice powder, and caoguo to the empty bowl as the 'seasoning'.
Turn off the heat and let the oil cool to 210°C.
Pour the hot oil from the wok into the bowl and stir with a spoon. (For extra heat, you can also add the same 'seasoning' to another bowl at 165°C and mix at the end.)
After pouring the oil and stirring briefly, add white vinegar; it will re-boil. Continue stirring. The vinegar enhances the aroma.
Let the chili oil cool until warm, then add sugar and MSG. The sugar softens the spiciness, making it less harsh.

howtocook:condiment/油酥.name: Oil Pastry
howtocook:condiment/油酥.description: This is a Chinese pastry ingredient, often used for making flatbreads. It gives the final product distinct layers, crispy outside and soft inside. It mainly provides carbohydrates and fats for energy. The method is very simple and beginner-friendly, expected to take only 3 minutes to prepare.
steps: Put flour in a small bowl, add salt.
Add hot oil at 200°C.
Stir with chopsticks until it becomes a smooth paste with no lumps.

howtocook:condiment/炸串酱料.name: Fried Skewer Sauce
howtocook:condiment/炸串酱料.description: This versatile fried skewer sauce is spicy, rich, savory, and slightly salty, with a street-food style. It mainly provides fat and spices that bring aromatic compounds, stimulating appetite. Very easy to make, no pressure for beginners, only about 10 minutes total.
steps: Mix all ingredients in a container.
Heat oil in a pan, enough to submerge all ingredients in the container.
Pour hot oil in three batches, 1/3 each time, while stirring.
Finally add 10ml sesame oil, 10ml light soy sauce, 10ml Sichuan pepper oil, 10ml oyster sauce.

ingredients.name: Dried chili powder (both fine and coarse)

howtocook:condiment/简易版炒糖色.name: Simple Caramel Color
howtocook:condiment/简易版炒糖色.description: Caramel color is a key technique for coloring red-braised dishes in Chinese cuisine. When the heat is right, it imparts a glossy red sheen and a unique caramelized aroma. The main ingredient is sugar, which provides energy, but more importantly, it harmonizes the color and flavor of the dish. This simple version of caramel color requires some heat control; beginners may need practice to avoid bitterness or excessive sweetness. The entire process takes about ten minutes.
steps: Turn on the heat and pour 100ml of boiling water into the pot.
Then pour 100ml of oil into the pot, with as short an interval as possible from the first step. At this point, the heat can be high or medium; if in a hurry, high heat is fine.
Add rock sugar (if the rock sugar is too clumped, break it up in advance, the less clumped the better).
Adjust the heat to medium.
Start stirring.
Keep stirring continuously until it turns brownish-black, then reduce to low heat.
It will thin out, turn tea-red, then sauce-red, and small bubbles will appear. Prepare to perform operation 1 or operation 2 below.
The small bubbles will gradually disappear, then large bubbles will appear. When the large bubbles appear, the caramel color is ready.

At this point, you must quickly proceed to the next step (regardless of which operation, be prepared in advance and act fast! Otherwise, the heat will be too high and the caramel will become bitter). Choose based on the dish style and personal taste.
Operation 1: Directly add 400ml of boiling water to cool it down.

Operation 2 (advanced seasoning version): When the sugar liquid turns jujube-red and dense small bubbles appear, quickly add pre-prepared aromatics such as scallion, ginger, garlic, and Sichuan peppercorns, and stir-fry. After the aroma releases (within about 5-10 seconds), immediately pour in 400ml of boiling water to stop the reaction. (Note: If the subsequent recipe requires stir-frying meat for coloring, add the meat and stir-fry first before adding water.)

ingredients.name: Rock sugar / White sugar / Caster sugar (choose any one)

howtocook:condiment/糖醋汁.name: Sweet and Sour Sauce
howtocook:condiment/糖醋汁.description: This sauce is sweet and sour, and is the classic flavor base for Chinese sweet and sour dishes. It's simple to mix with easy-to-remember proportions, even beginners can handle it. From prep to finish, it takes about 10 minutes. The sugar provides energy, and the vinegar aroma helps stimulate appetite and cut grease, instantly adding flavor to home-style dishes.
steps: Mix all seasonings in a small bowl according to the proportions.
After handling according to the specific dish, pour the prepared sweet and sour sauce into the pan.
Cook for 5-10 minutes depending on the dish.
Reduce the sauce over high heat to thicken, enhance aroma, and add gloss.

howtocook:condiment/草莓酱/草莓酱.name: Strawberry Jam
howtocook:condiment/草莓酱/草莓酱.description: This is a sweet and sour, fruity homemade jam, perfect for spreading on bread. Strawberries are rich in vitamin C, but the sugar content is high during cooking - adjust to taste. The method is very simple, even beginners can easily make it. It takes about two hours from preparation to completion.
