steps: Wash strawberries and remove stems
Chop the strawberries and place in a suitable bowl
Pour white sugar into the bowl and mix well with the strawberries
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let sit for 1 hour
Pour the strawberry-sugar mixture into a non-stick pan and bring to a boil over high heat
After boiling, reduce heat to low and stir continuously until the jam thickens, then turn off the heat
Let the strawberry jam cool, then transfer to a prepared sealed jar

howtocook:condiment/葱油.name: Scallion Oil
howtocook:condiment/葱油.description: Scallion oil is a home-style seasoning oil that adds fragrance and umami. It carries a rich scallion aroma and the complex savory flavor of dried shrimp. Suitable for mixing into fillings, cold dishes, or as a finishing oil for hot dishes. It mainly provides energy from oil, and the scallion, ginger, and other aromatics contain plant active compounds that help remove fishy smells and stimulate appetite. Simple to make, beginner-friendly, generally about 30 minutes including prep and cooking.
steps: Soak dried shrimp in 50°C warm water, add 10ml cooking wine to remove fishy smell, soak for 10 minutes then remove and drain.
Wash scallions and cilantro, cut into 5cm long sections, wipe dry surface moisture.
Cut onion into strips, boil in hot water for 5 minutes, remove and drain.
Peel ginger, slice.
Pour all oil into pot, add all pre-treated ingredients, fry over medium-low heat for 20 minutes.

howtocook:condiment/蒜香酱油.name: Garlic Soy Sauce
howtocook:condiment/蒜香酱油.description: Garlic Soy Sauce is a home-style dipping sauce with rich garlic flavor, savory and oily. It is commonly used to enhance the taste of dishes like white cut chicken and dumplings. Garlic and sesame seeds have antibacterial effects and provide unsaturated fatty acids respectively. The method is extremely simple, very beginner-friendly, and takes only about 5 minutes in total.
steps: Crush the garlic
Add soy sauce to the dipping dish
Heat the wok, add peanut oil. When the oil is hot, add the crushed garlic and fry for half a minute.
After half a minute, turn off the heat, pour the hot oil into the dipping dish, and stir with chopsticks.

howtocook:condiment/蔗糖糖浆/蔗糖糖浆.name: Sucrose Syrup
howtocook:condiment/蔗糖糖浆/蔗糖糖浆.description: Homemade beverage syrup, mix white sugar and water in a 1:1 ratio to dissolve, easy to control sweetness of cold drinks, a basic recipe for mixing drinks. Rich in carbohydrates, quickly replenishes energy. Extremely simple, very beginner-friendly, only takes a few minutes, can be refrigerated for a week.
steps: Mix the weighed white sugar and drinking water in a container
Cover the container and refrigerate

howtocook:dessert/反沙芋头/反沙芋头.name: Sugar-Coated Taro
howtocook:dessert/反沙芋头/反沙芋头.description: Sugar-coated taro is a traditional Chaoshan snack with a crispy exterior and soft, sweet interior, often enjoyed as an afternoon tea treat. Lipu taro is rich in dietary fiber, potassium, and various vitamins, providing long-lasting satiety. It's not difficult to make but requires mastering the sugar syrup temperature; suitable for beginners with some basic skills, taking about 20 minutes in total.
steps: Cut the taro into thick strips (slightly larger so they don't break during stir-frying).
Add enough oil to submerge the taro. Heat the oil until it's hot (insert chopsticks and bubbles form).
Add the taro strips to the oil and fry until they float, usually when they turn slightly golden and a chopstick can easily pierce through.
Save the oil used for frying; it can be reused for stir-frying other dishes.
Next is the crucial step: combine sugar (30g) and water (15g) in a 2:1 ratio. Heat until it bubbles without changing color.
Add the chopped scallions and taro, then turn off the heat and stir-fry. As the temperature drops, the sugar will crystallize into a frosted coating.
Plate and serve!

howtocook:dessert/咖啡椰奶冻/咖啡椰奶冻.name: Coffee Coconut Milk Pudding
howtocook:dessert/咖啡椰奶冻/咖啡椰奶冻.description: This dessert combines the richness of coffee with the sweetness of coconut milk, with a texture as smooth and tender as pudding, rich in layers. Whipping cream and coconut juice provide high-quality fat and plant nutrition, bringing satisfaction. The method is very friendly: just mix and heat, no complicated techniques needed, beginners can succeed at first try. Excluding refrigeration, actual operation takes about 1 hour, plus refrigeration to set, about 4 hours in total to enjoy.
steps: Mix the measured whipping cream, coconut juice, espresso, and coconut water together and set aside.
Heat the above liquid for 1 minute to reach a temperature of 50-60°C.
(Optional) Add extra sugar if you have a sweet tooth.
Add gelatin, stir until dissolved, and cook for 1 minute.
(Optional) Strain (this step makes the coconut milk pudding smoother in texture).
Pour into molds.
(Optional) Remove surface bubbles. This step improves the texture and appearance of the coconut milk pudding.
Place in the refrigerator and wait for 3 hours.

howtocook:dessert/奥利奥冰淇淋/奥利奥冰淇淋.name: Oreo Ice Cream
howtocook:dessert/奥利奥冰淇淋/奥利奥冰淇淋.description: The Oreo ice cream has a delicate texture, blending the milky flavor with the crunchy sweetness of Oreos. The pure animal cream makes it light and not greasy. It mainly provides energy, contains fat and carbohydrates, and is suitable as a refreshing small dessert. The preparation is relatively simple; you only need to handle the cookie filling and whip the cream. Even beginners with a bit of patience can do it. The entire preparation takes about half an hour, then freeze for at least 4 hours before enjoying.
steps: Twist open the Oreos and remove the cream filling, set aside.

Use chopsticks to crush the Oreo cookies. Half of the cookies should become powder, and the other half should be in pieces less than 0.5 cm. Set aside. (You can search for 'Oreo cookie crumbs' on Taobao to save time and effort ^-^)
Pour all the cream into a deep container and add the prepared sugar.
Start whipping with an electric hand mixer on high speed until when you lift the mixer, the cream holds a peak (0.5 cm to 1 cm) and doesn't drip completely like liquid (some dripping is normal).
After whipping, add the crushed Oreos into the cream and mix well until you see Oreos at the bottom.
Optional: Pour the mixture into ice cream molds.
Place in the freezer (-18°C) for at least 4 hours before removing.

ingredients.name: Heavy cream (recommended brand: Anchor animal cream)

howtocook:dessert/戚风蛋糕/戚风蛋糕.name: Chiffon Cake
howtocook:dessert/戚风蛋糕/戚风蛋糕.description: This beginner-friendly dessert has a delicate, soft texture and a cloud-like lightness, making it a classic in Western baking. The main ingredients, eggs and milk, provide high-quality protein and calcium. The process requires careful whipping, folding, and baking, which can be challenging, but with patience and following the steps, even a novice can succeed. From prep to finish, it typically takes 1.5 to 2 hours.
steps: Take fresh eggs out of the refrigerator.
Prepare two bowls and dry them; one for egg whites, one for yolks.
The bowl for egg whites can have a few drops of water, but no oil; the bowl for yolks must be dry.
Crack the eggs, using your hands or an egg separator, to separate whites and yolks into the two bowls.
During separation, the yolks must not break, and no yolk should get into the whites, otherwise it will severely affect whipping. (The white chalaza can go into the whites; it won't affect.)
(Note: If not using a stand mixer, the bowl for egg whites is also the whipping bowl. To avoid overflow, after adding all the whites, the volume should not exceed 1/8 of the bowl.)
Prepare a new empty bowl. Add all the oil and all the milk from the recipe.
Whisk quickly by hand until the oil and milk are fully combined, forming a milky white emulsion (emulsification complete).
Sift all the cake flour from the recipe into the emulsion. Use a spatula to stir in a zigzag pattern until no visible dry flour remains (the batter may be a bit lumpy; that's normal).
Add all the separated egg yolks to the bowl. Continue stirring in a zigzag pattern with the spatula.
The batter will become very smooth and silky, and gluten development is minimized. Set aside.
Prepare the remaining 3/4 of the sugar. Divide into three portions, each 1/4 of the total.
Add lemon juice or white vinegar to the egg whites (optional).
With the mixer on medium speed, whip the egg whites until large bubbles form. Add the first portion of sugar.
Increase to high speed, whip until the bubbles are finer. Add the second portion of sugar.
Continue on high speed until soft peaks form. Add the third portion of sugar.
Reduce to medium-low speed and whip until stiff peaks form.
The egg whites are now ready.
(For reference on the egg white state, see the picture in the attached link.)
(Keep the beaters close to the bottom of the bowl to prevent the top from being whipped while the bottom is still liquid.)
Give the yolk batter a few quick stirs.
Using a spatula, take 1/3 of the meringue and add it to the yolk batter.
Use the folding technique to avoid deflating the meringue.
The folding technique is:
With your right hand, insert the spatula from the center of the bowl to the bottom.
Scrape towards the 8 o'clock position until you hit the side of the bowl, then lift the spatula up and bring it back to the center, dropping the batter back.
With your left hand, turn the bowl from the 9 o'clock position to the 7 o'clock position (a 60-degree rotation) to complete one cycle.
The speed is about two strokes per second.
This method is from 'Chef Koyama's Cake Classroom'. In simple terms, it's like stir-frying.
Pour the mixture of 1/3 meringue and yolk batter into the remaining 2/3 meringue. Continue folding until evenly combined.
Pour the batter into the pan. Tap the pan a few times to release large air bubbles.
Total baking time: 30–35 minutes for a 6-inch cake, 50 minutes for an 8-inch cake. Adjust based on your oven, generally within ±5 minutes. (Watch the cake in the last few minutes.)
Preheat the oven with top heating at 150°C and bottom heating at 160°C; it takes about 10 minutes to reach the temperature.
Once preheated, place the pan on the lower rack.
Choose variable temperature baking, divided into two stages.
First stage: set top heating to 150°C, bottom heating to 160°C.
Bake for the first 3/5 of the total time.
Second stage: set top heating to 160°C, bottom heating to 170°C.
Bake for the remaining 2/5 of the total time. Simply adjust the oven temperature to switch.
When done, remove from the oven.
Be careful, it may be hot. Use a towel for protection.
Preheat the air fryer to 130°C for 5 minutes.
Place the pan with batter into the air fryer. Immediately cover the pan with aluminum foil (shiny side up, matte side touching food), and seal the edges to prevent it from blowing off.
First stage: set to 130°C and bake for 35 minutes.
Second stage for browning: open the air fryer, remove the foil. Increase temperature to 150°C and bake for another 10–15 minutes.
Check for doneness: the cake should be puffed and golden brown on top. Insert a toothpick into the center; if it comes out clean, it's done.
After removing, the process of tapping out hot air and inverting to cool is exactly the same as for the oven version.
(Optional) Drop the pan from a height to release hot air.
Invert the pan and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Do not remove the cake from the pan while it's still warm, as it may be damaged.
Be careful, it may be hot. Use a towel for protection.
Unmold and serve.

howtocook:dessert/提拉米苏/提拉米苏.name: Tiramisu
howtocook:dessert/提拉米苏/提拉米苏.description: This classic Italian dessert requires no oven. The mascarpone cream is smooth and light, and the ladyfingers soaked in coffee are rich and soft. It is an entry-level dessert. Rich in protein and calcium, but high in sugar and calories, best enjoyed occasionally. Simple to make, very beginner-friendly. It takes about half an hour of hands-on time, then refrigerate for four hours to set.
steps: Separate egg yolks from whites
Add 10g sugar to the egg whites and whip until soft peaks form
Gradually add 40g sugar to the egg yolks in three additions, stirring until smooth
Gradually add mascarpone cheese to the egg yolks in three additions, stirring until smooth
Finally add rum to the egg yolk mixture and stir to combine
Fold the whipped egg whites into the egg yolk-mascarpone mixture in three additions
Quickly dip both sides of each ladyfinger in coffee and arrange in a single layer in a dish
Alternate layers of mascarpone cream and ladyfingers (adjust according to your dish and preference)
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or less if you're impatient)
Remove from fridge, dust with cocoa powder, and serve

howtocook:dessert/无厨师机蜂蜜面包/无厨师机蜂蜜面包.name: No-Mixer Honey Bread
howtocook:dessert/无厨师机蜂蜜面包/无厨师机蜂蜜面包.description: This is a home-style small honey bread that requires no stand mixer. It's soft and sweet, with a slightly crispy bottom. The surface is brushed with honey water and sprinkled with sesame seeds, giving off a rustic sweet fragrance. Main ingredients are bread flour, milk, eggs, and butter, providing plenty of carbs, protein, and calcium. It's reassuring as breakfast or a snack. The method is simple, all hand-kneaded, with flexible fermentation time. You can even refrigerate the dough overnight and bake the next day, very beginner-friendly. Total time about 3 hours, most of which is waiting for the dough to rise; actual hands-on work is not laborious.
steps: Make the dough: Mix together the flour, milk (recommend heating to 40°C, I use microwave 15-20s), yeast, egg, sugar, and salt.
Mix the dough until the ingredients are well combined into a ball.
Add the butter and mix.
Continue mixing and hand-knead until evenly combined.
Start proofing: cover the bowl with plastic wrap, let it sit at room temperature (10-20°C) for 1-2 hours. A little longer won't hurt the result.
When the dough has visibly doubled in size, it's time to divide the dough. At this point, the dough should no longer be very sticky.
Divide the dough: ideally each piece is 60g (for looks), but you can adjust the size to your liking.
Roll each small piece with a rolling pin into a tongue shape, then roll it up. Rest again for 10 minutes.
Again roll out into a tongue shape with rolling pin and roll up. Cut in half from the middle (one becomes two).

Again roll out into a tongue shape and roll up, then cut in half (two become four). (You can repeat the rolling and cutting a few times depending on your time, just aim to control the size of each piece.)
Line a baking pan with parchment paper and pour in peanut oil. Dip the bottom of each rolled piece in water and then in flour, then place in the pan.
Proof again (cover with plastic wrap). At this step, you can put it in the fridge and bake the next day.
Brush with egg wash.
Bake at 180°C (355°F) for 18-20 minutes.
After taking out of the oven, brush with honey water and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

howtocook:dessert/炸鲜奶/炸鲜奶.name: Fried Milk Custard
howtocook:dessert/炸鲜奶/炸鲜奶.description: Fried Milk Custard is a classic dessert with a crispy exterior and creamy interior, sweet and delicious. It's a home-style snack. Rich in protein and calcium, with balanced nutrition. Medium difficulty, beginners can try. Preparation time about 20 minutes, but requires 1 hour refrigeration to set.
steps: Pour the milk into a bowl.
Add cornstarch and sugar, stir until well combined.
Brush the mold with cooking oil.
Pour the milk mixture into the pot, bring to a boil over medium heat.
After boiling, reduce to low heat, stirring constantly.
When the milk thickens, remove from heat and pour into the mold.
Place the mold in the refrigerator to cool for 1 hour.
Take it out, cut into evenly sized strips, then place in a bowl.
Pour half of the breadcrumbs into the bowl, coat the milk strips, then remove and set aside.
In a new bowl, beat the eggs and mix well, set aside.
Coat the milk strips with egg wash and the remaining breadcrumbs.
Pour enough oil into the pot to cover the strips, then fry them.
When the strips turn golden, turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:dessert/烤箱版巴斯克芝士蛋糕/烤箱版巴斯克芝士蛋糕.name: Oven-Baked Basque Cheesecake
howtocook:dessert/烤箱版巴斯克芝士蛋糕/烤箱版巴斯克芝士蛋糕.description: Basque cheesecake is a dessert with a rich, dense texture, caramelized on the outside and moist inside. It's a classic from the Basque region of Spain. Main ingredients are cream cheese and eggs, rich in high-quality protein and calcium. The process is very simple, even beginners can easily handle it. From prep to out of the oven takes about 30 minutes, but it's recommended to chill overnight before serving, total time about 8+ hours.
steps: Soften cream cheese in microwave: 10s + 10s.
Add sugar to cream cheese and beat with mixer until smooth.
Add 2 whole eggs and 1 egg yolk, mix well.
Add heavy cream, mix well.
Add cake flour, mix well.
Bake at 220°C (425°F) for 20-25 minutes (I usually do 20 or 22 minutes).
Let cool, then refrigerate, preferably overnight.

howtocook:dessert/烤蛋挞/烤蛋挞.name: Baked Egg Tarts
howtocook:dessert/烤蛋挞/烤蛋挞.description: Baked egg tarts are a classic Chinese dessert with a crispy crust and silky smooth filling, rich in milky flavor. They are rich in protein, calcium, and various vitamins, providing ample energy. The preparation is very simple, even beginners can easily master it, and the semi-finished product can be frozen for later use. From preparation to serving, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Place a bowl on a kitchen scale and weigh 450g of whipping cream (the density of whipping cream is taken as 1 here).
Add 80g of white sugar (medium sweetness, adjust to taste; recommended range 60-100g).
Add 200g of milk (the density of milk is taken as 1 here).
Take 8 egg yolks and add them; the egg whites can be saved for other uses.
Stir all ingredients evenly until the white sugar is completely dissolved.
Strain the mixed ingredients through a sieve to remove egg membranes, eggshell bits, undissolved sugar, and lumps of whipping cream.
Now preheat the oven to 220°C (about 10 minutes) and remember to take out the baking tray.
Place the tart shells evenly on the baking tray with a 0.5 cm gap between them.
Pour the strained mixture into the tart shells, filling until the liquid is 0.5 cm below the rim; do not overfill.
At this point, the semi-finished egg tarts are complete. They can be directly frozen for at least 12 hours for storage.
Place the semi-finished egg tarts in the oven and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Once baked, they are ready to serve.

howtocook:dessert/玛格丽特饼干/玛格丽特饼干.name: Margaret Cookies
howtocook:dessert/玛格丽特饼干/玛格丽特饼干.description: Margaret Cookies are crumbly and rich in butter aroma, a classic Western-style small dessert. They are mainly made from egg yolks, butter, and flour, rich in carbohydrates and fats, providing quick energy. The difficulty is moderate, taking about 45 minutes from preparation to finish, and even beginners can easily make them.
steps: Melt butter over hot water; mash the egg yolks and set aside.
Add sugar, salt, and mashed egg yolks to the melted butter; mix well.
Add cake flour and cornstarch; knead into a dough.
Divide the dough evenly into small pieces about 8 grams each, and roll into balls.
Use your thumb to lightly press each ball to create cracks.
Preheat oven to 150°C, place the balls in the oven, and bake for 20 minutes.
Let cool slightly before serving.

howtocook:dessert/红柚蛋糕/红柚蛋糕.name: Red Pomelo Cake
howtocook:dessert/红柚蛋糕/红柚蛋糕.description: Red Pomelo Cake is a refreshing dessert that can be easily made with an air fryer, combining the tart fruity flavor of red pomelo pulp with the soft texture of cake, perfect for a single serving afternoon tea. Eggs and flour provide protein and carbs, while red pomelo is rich in vitamin C, offering simple basic nutrition. The difficulty is medium, and the key is to stir gradually into a smooth light yellow batter. Beginners can succeed if they patiently follow the steps. From preparation to serving takes about 35 minutes, not too much time when you crave a freshly baked dessert.
steps: Crack 2 eggs into a foil pan, add 20g red pomelo pulp
Pour 15ml oil into the foil pan and shake to evenly cover the bottom
Add 10g sugar, 40g flour, and 40ml water to the foil pan
Stir clockwise with chopsticks until a light yellow batter forms
Add 5g sugar, 40g flour, and 40ml water to the foil pan
Continue stirring with chopsticks until a light yellow batter forms
Place the foil pan in the air fryer basket and bake at 180°C for 15 minutes
Open the air fryer, carefully remove the foil pan, and flip the cake with chopsticks or a spoon
Continue baking at 180°C for 8 minutes
Remove and enjoy

howtocook:dessert/胡萝卜甜糕.name: Carrot Halwa
howtocook:dessert/胡萝卜甜糕.description: Carrot Halwa is a well-known winter dessert in India. Shredded carrots are slow-cooked with milk until thickened, then enriched with ghee and nuts. The texture is dense and sweet, with a golden-red color. Rich in beta-carotene, calcium, and healthy fats, it is warming and nourishing. The process is simple but requires patience for beginners; it takes about 45 minutes to complete.
steps: Peel the carrots and grate them into fine shreds using a grater.
In a heavy-bottomed pan, add 20g of ghee (half the amount) and heat over medium heat until melted.
Add the shredded carrots and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Pour in 500ml of whole milk and stir well.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low heat and maintain a gentle simmer.

Continue stirring for 25-30 minutes until the milk is almost completely absorbed and evaporated by the carrots (this is the most critical step; patience and constant stirring are needed to prevent burning).
When there is almost no liquid left in the pan, add 80g of sugar and stir well.
The mixture will release water after adding sugar; continue to stir over medium-low heat for 5-8 minutes until the liquid evaporates again.
Add the remaining 20g of ghee and 3g of ground cardamom, and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
In another small pan, fry the cashews, almond slices, and raisins separately in a little ghee until golden (about 1 minute each); note that the raisins will puff up.
Mix half of the fried nuts and raisins into the carrot halwa.
Transfer to a serving dish and sprinkle the remaining nuts and raisins on top as decoration.

howtocook:dessert/芋泥雪媚娘/芋泥雪媚娘.name: Taro Mochi
howtocook:dessert/芋泥雪媚娘/芋泥雪媚娘.description: Taro Mochi has a soft and chewy outer skin, with a delicate sweet taro paste and light cream filling, creating rich layers of texture. This is a Chinese dessert inspired by home cooking. Taro and purple sweet potato provide dietary fiber and anthocyanins, while milk and cream add calcium. No oven required, simple steps, beginner-friendly. Takes about two hours from start to finish.
steps: Cut taro into chunks, boil on high heat until soft (about 40 minutes), then put all into a blender.
Add 30g milk, 25g heavy cream, and blend into a puree.
Then add 3g purple sweet potato powder, 18g white sugar, and continue blending until smooth taro paste.

Get another bowl, add all glutinous rice flour b, 22g corn starch, 135g milk, 50g white sugar, mix well and sift once. Cover with plastic wrap, poke small holes, and steam over medium heat for 30 minutes.
While steaming, put glutinous rice flour a into a pan and stir-fry over low heat until slightly yellow (cooked), set aside as hand flour.
Take the steamed glutinous rice and milk mixture (jelly-like) out, immediately add 30g butter, knead until butter is fully absorbed, then refrigerate for one hour.
Take another bowl, add 120g heavy cream, 8g white sugar, whip until peaks form, put into a piping bag and set aside.

Take out the chilled dough, knead for 5 minutes, divide into 30g portions. Evenly dust with 2g hand flour to prevent sticking. Roll into circles. First pipe 5g whipped cream, then place 30g taro paste, then wrap the dough like a bun (trim excess dough if needed).
After wrapping, evenly dust with 2g hand flour to prevent sticking.
Repeat the last two steps until all ingredients are used.

howtocook:dessert/英式司康/英式司康.name: English Scones
howtocook:dessert/英式司康/英式司康.description: English scones are a classic afternoon tea treat, crispy on the outside and soft inside, with rich egg and milk aroma. They taste even better with jam or cream. Made mainly from flour, butter, eggs, and cream, they are rich in carbohydrates and protein, providing quick energy. The process is simple; with basic kneading techniques, even beginners can succeed. It takes about an hour from preparation to baking.
steps: Beat the eggs and measure out 30g of egg liquid into a clean bowl. Add the full amount of heavy cream and cream cheese; mix well. If the cheese is too hard, heat it in a water bath to about 40°C before mixing.
Place the cake flour, salt, sugar, and baking powder in a clean bowl; mix well.
Cut the butter into small pieces and add to the previous mixture. Rub the butter into the mixture with your hands until it resembles coarse cornmeal.
Pour the egg-milk mixture from step 1 into the flour-butter mixture. Stir until combined. Press and fold to form a uniform dough.
Place the dough on a work surface and roll out to 1.5cm thickness. Cut into desired shapes with a knife or cutter.
Dip a brush into the remaining 20g of egg liquid and brush onto the surface of the scones.
Preheat the oven to 180°C and bake for 27 minutes.

howtocook:dessert/草莓冰淇淋/草莓冰淇淋.name: Strawberry Ice Cream
howtocook:dessert/草莓冰淇淋/草莓冰淇淋.description: This is an easy homemade strawberry ice cream that requires no ice cream maker. It's fruity, rich, and creamy. Strawberries are packed with vitamin C, and the sweetened condensed milk and cream provide energy and calcium. The recipe is simple and foolproof, perfect for beginners. Total time is about 8 hours, mostly freezing, with only a few tens of minutes of hands-on work.
steps: First make the strawberry syrup. Wash the strawberries and remove the green tops. Cut the strawberries into 5mm small pieces. Reserve half of the chopped strawberries to fold into the ice cream later.
Place the other half of the chopped strawberries and sugar into a saucepan. Stir and cook over medium heat until the strawberries release liquid and form a syrup in the pan.
Let the strawberries heat in the syrup, stirring occasionally, until they break down and deform, and the syrup thickens slightly.
When the syrup holds its shape for 3 seconds, it's ready. Remove the syrup from the heat, stir in vanilla and salt. Set the strawberry syrup aside to cool.

While the syrup cools, prepare the ice cream base. In a bowl, combine sweetened condensed milk, heavy cream, vanilla extract, and salt. Use a hand mixer to whip the mixture until it becomes light and fluffy, and forms soft peaks.

Fold the reserved chopped fresh strawberries into the ice cream base. Transfer the whipped ice cream base to a freezer-safe container. Drizzle the cooled strawberry syrup over the ice cream, then gently swirl it into the mixture.
Cover the ice cream and freeze for eight hours, then scoop and serve.

howtocook:dessert/酸奶意式奶冻/酸奶意式奶冻.name: Yogurt Panna Cotta
howtocook:dessert/酸奶意式奶冻/酸奶意式奶冻.description: A twist on the classic Italian dessert, adding plain yogurt for a lighter, smoother texture—sweet but not cloying. Perfect as a post-meal treat with fruit or jam. Dairy provides calcium and protein, making it a gentle yet nutritious choice. Simple steps: just heat, stir, and chill. Even beginners can master it. From prep to serving, about 5 hours.
steps: Cut gelatin sheets into small pieces, soak in cold water
Place heavy cream and sugar in a pot, heat to 60°C
Turn off heat. Remove gelatin from water, squeeze dry, add to hot cream, stir until dissolved
Cool cream to 40°C, add plain yogurt, mix well
Strain the mixture through a sieve twice
Pour into serving containers, refrigerate for at least 4 hours

howtocook:dessert/雪花酥/雪花酥.name: Snowflake Crisp
howtocook:dessert/雪花酥/雪花酥.description: Snowflake Crisp is a small dessert with a crispy, sweet, and rich milky flavor. It is a simple and quick home-baked treat, also suitable for gift boxes. Since it contains butter, milk powder, and mixed nuts, it provides ample energy and protein, though it is high in calories, so enjoy in moderation. The difficulty is moderate, somewhat challenging for beginners, but with attention to heat and stirring technique, it can be completed in about 30 minutes.
steps: If cookies are larger than a one-yuan coin, cut them into small pieces first.
Add unsalted butter to the pan, heat over low heat until fully melted.
Add marshmallows to the pan, stir with a spatula until melted and evenly combined with the butter.
Add 20g milk powder to the pan, stir with a spatula until combined, then immediately turn off the heat.
Add all prepared mixed nuts and cookies while hot, stir with a spatula.

After stirring until cool enough to touch, put on disposable plastic gloves, knead or pull the mixture in the pan to evenly distribute cookies and nuts with the marshmallow-butter-milk powder mixture.
Press the mixture into a mold, compact the corners, roll flat with a rolling pin, and press the unfilled side into a straight edge by hand.

Let cool at room temperature. Once completely cooled, unmold, cut into pieces along the mold lines or to desired size, sprinkle with remaining milk powder, making sure to coat all sides of the snowflake crisp.

howtocook:dessert/魔芋蛋糕/魔芋蛋糕.name: Konjac Cake
howtocook:dessert/魔芋蛋糕/魔芋蛋糕.description: Konjac cake is a low-calorie, gluten-free dessert. It uses erythritol instead of white sugar, with a light and moist texture, cocoa aroma, especially suitable for weight loss. It is rich in dietary fiber and protein, giving a strong feeling of fullness. It is somewhat difficult to make, requiring whipping egg whites and careful folding, but it takes only about half an hour, making it a quick and healthy baking option.
steps: Before preparing the batter, preheat the oven to 350°F (150°C) to save time
Take fresh eggs out of the refrigerator
Prepare two dry containers, one for egg whites and one for yolks
The container for egg whites can have a little water but no oil; the container for yolks must be dry
Crack the eggs, manually or using an egg separator, to separate whites and yolks into the two containers.
During separation, yolks must not break, and no yolk should get into the whites, otherwise it will affect whipping. (White chalaza can go into whites, it's fine)
(Note: if not using a stand mixer, the container for egg whites is also the mixing bowl; to avoid overflow, the egg whites should not exceed 1/8 of the container's volume)
Add 1g cream of tartar to the egg whites
Beat on high speed until frothy with large bubbles, then add 50g erythritol
Beat on medium-low speed until stiff peaks form (when lifting the whisk, short upright peaks form; bowl can be inverted without whites falling)
At this point the egg whites are properly whipped
Keep the beaters close to the bottom of the bowl to avoid having the top whipped but bottom still liquid)
Add the separated yolks to the whipped egg whites, mix on medium-low speed until evenly combined
Add 10g cocoa powder and 10g konjac flour to the egg white mixture. First fold with a knife because using a mixer would cause powder to fly
The folding technique is:
First, insert a spatula into the center of the bowl all the way to the bottom
Scrape towards the 8 o'clock direction until hitting the bowl wall, then lift the spatula up, bring it back to the center, and drop the batter back
With your left hand, turn the bowl from 9 o'clock to 7 o'clock, a 60-degree rotation, completing one cycle
Speed about 2 strokes per second
This method is from 'Small Island Teacher's Cake Classroom'. In simple terms, stir like you're stir-frying.
After folding, use a mixer on low speed to mix evenly
Line the mold with parchment paper, fitting the bottom and sides
Pour the batter into the mold, smooth the top with a knife, then tap the mold a few times to remove large air bubbles
Bake for 25 minutes
Once baked, use oven mitts to remove from the oven
(Optional) Drop the mold from a height to release steam
Invert the mold for 10 minutes to cool the cake
Removing the cake before cooling will damage it
This step may be hot, be sure to wear oven mitts
Unmold and slice with a knife to serve

howtocook:dessert/龟苓膏/龟苓膏.name: Guilinggao
howtocook:dessert/龟苓膏/龟苓膏.description: Guilinggao is a traditional Cantonese dessert with a smooth, jelly-like texture, slightly bitter with a sweet aftertaste. Often enjoyed as a summer treat, it helps clear heat, remove dampness, nourish yin, and beautify the skin. This healthy snack is easy to make—even beginners can master it. Just prepare ingredients, mix, heat, and chill for about two hours before serving.
steps: In a pot, mix 25 g of guilinggao powder with 120 ml of cold water until no lumps remain.
In another container, add 100 g of granulated sugar and pour in 500 ml of boiling water, stirring until the sugar is completely dissolved.
Slowly pour the sugar water into the guilinggao powder mixture, stirring immediately to prevent clumps.
Place the combined liquid on the stove over medium-low heat, stirring continuously to avoid sticking.
Heat until the liquid thickens and small bubbles start to appear, then turn off the heat.
Quickly pour the liquid into molds and let it cool naturally to set. For best texture, refrigerate for 1–2 hours before serving.

howtocook:drink/B52轰炸机.name: B52 Bomber
howtocook:drink/B52轰炸机.description: This cocktail has distinct layers. First, the kick of vodka, then the sweet aftertaste of coffee cream. When lit, it offers a unique ice-and-fire experience. A classic creative cocktail. Kahlúa and Baileys provide a little caffeine and cream, but the nutrition is mainly alcohol. Making it requires practice in layering and lighting. A bit challenging for beginners, but the whole process takes only about 5 minutes.
steps: Pour Kahlúa into the bottom of a liqueur glass to 1/3 full. (10ml)
Slowly pour Baileys Irish Cream along the back of a spoon to also 1/3 full (10ml). Go slow to keep the layers distinct. (Too fast and the Baileys will mix with the coffee liqueur.)
Finally, pour Skyy Original Vodka on top (10ml).
Warm the rim of the glass with a lighter.
Last step: light it. See the small blue flame?

howtocook:drink/Mojito莫吉托.name: Mojito
howtocook:drink/Mojito莫吉托.description: This cocktail has a refreshing sweet and sour taste, with the coolness of mint and the slight buzz of rum. It is a classic summer drink. Low in alcohol, paired with vitamin C-rich lime, it can quench thirst and refresh the mind. Very easy to make, beginner-friendly, from prep to finish takes about ten minutes.
steps: Cut one of the halved limes into small pieces, place into a highball glass, then muddle with a muddler to extract juice;
Rub 3-4 mint sprigs along the rim of the glass, then place them into the glass;
Add 20ml simple syrup;
Add 45ml golden rum;
Squeeze the remaining half lime into the glass;
Stir gently to partially dissolve the sugar/syrup;
Fill the glass with crushed ice until it reaches 3/4 full;
Add chilled soda water until it just covers the crushed ice;
Stir with a swirling motion for half a minute;
Top up the highball glass with crushed ice;
Slap the remaining mint sprig to release aroma, insert into the crushed ice as garnish.

howtocook:drink/冬瓜茶.name: Winter Melon Tea
howtocook:drink/冬瓜茶.description: Winter melon tea is a traditional refreshing and heat-relieving drink. It tastes sweet and mild, with a unique fragrance of winter melon, and tastes even better when chilled. Winter melon itself is low in calories and rich in water. When boiled with rock sugar, it replenishes energy and promotes hydration. The process is not complicated but requires some patience for beginners. It takes about 4 to 5 hours in total.
steps: Prepare the winter melon: Peel the winter melon, remove the seeds, and cut into small pieces (each piece no larger than 4 cm).
Add rock sugar: Add rock sugar to the winter melon, mix well, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.

Cook the winter melon: At this point the winter melon has released a lot of water. Pour it into a pot, bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low heat and simmer slowly for 1 to 2 hours, stirring frequently to prevent sticking.
Strain the winter melon tea: Use a strainer to filter the cooked winter melon liquid, remove the winter melon pieces, and keep only the tea liquid.
Cool: Let the winter melon tea liquid cool down, then pour into a clean container and refrigerate.
Enjoy: The cooked winter melon tea liquid is a concentrate. Dilute with water or other beverages according to personal preference. It can be served cold or hot.

howtocook:drink/冰粉/冰粉.name: Ice Jelly
howtocook:drink/冰粉/冰粉.description: Ice Jelly is a traditional snack from Xinyang, Henan. It has a smooth, jelly-like texture and is served with mint juice or lemon juice to cool down and relieve summer heat. It belongs to Henan cuisine. Made by hand-rubbing ice jelly seeds, it is natural and additive-free, rich in plant colloids. The preparation is very simple and beginner-friendly. The whole process takes about 3 hours, of which 2.5 hours is setting time, requiring almost no hands-on work.
steps: Pour the cooled boiled water into a basin.
Wrap all the ice jelly seeds in a cheesecloth and tie the opening.
Put the wrapped ice jelly seeds into the cooled boiled water and vigorously knead for 6 minutes.
Then let the cooled boiled water sit for 2.5 hours to set.

Then scoop the ice jelly into prepared disposable clear plastic cups, add 10ml mint juice or 10g mint powder (lemon juice, hawthorn juice, mulberry juice also work), add water-glow ice cubes, and slowly stir with a spoon.

howtocook:drink/印度奶茶.name: Indian Milk Tea
howtocook:drink/印度奶茶.description: Indian milk tea is a home-style drink full of exotic flavor, freshly brewed with black tea, milk, and a variety of spices. It has a rich and smooth taste, with a warm spiciness from ginger, cardamom, and cinnamon. The milk provides high-quality protein and calcium, while the spices help warm the body, ward off cold, and promote digestion. The process is simple and intuitive, friendly for beginners, taking about 10 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Smash the ginger (no need to chop too finely, just smash to release the juice)
Crack the green cardamom pods with the back of a knife to expose the seeds
Crush the black peppercorns
Pour 120ml water into a small saucepan
Add the smashed ginger, cracked cardamom, cinnamon stick, cloves, and crushed black peppercorns
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat and cook for 2 minutes to let the spices release their flavors
Add 8g black tea and continue to cook over low heat for 2 minutes until the tea darkens
Pour in 120ml whole milk
Heat over medium heat, watching carefully. When the milk tea starts to foam and rise, immediately reduce to low heat (milk can easily boil over)
Simmer for 2-3 minutes, occasionally lifting the saucepan and pouring the tea back (repeat 3-4 times) to fully blend the tea and milk
Add sugar and stir to dissolve
Turn off the heat
Strain the tea into a cup through a fine mesh sieve to remove the tea leaves and spice residues
Serve hot

ingredients.name: Black tea (CTC tea is best, or Assam black tea)

howtocook:drink/可乐桶.name: Cola Bucket
howtocook:drink/可乐桶.description: Cola Bucket is a simple and easy-to-mix casual cocktail with a refreshing and sweet taste. The bubbles of cola and lemon juice nicely balance the harshness of whiskey, making it suitable for parties. It mainly provides energy, and lemon adds vitamin C. The whole mixing process takes only 5 minutes, and beginners can easily master it.
steps: Cut the lemon in half (knife perpendicular to the line connecting the stem and tip), then cut a slice from one half and set aside.
Cut the lemon in half again, then use a lemon squeezer to juice the four lemon wedges into a container and set aside.
Place the squeezed lemon wedges into a container and set aside.
Choose a cup, preferably a large glass or tin bucket with a capacity of about 1 liter.
Add ice cubes and the squeezed lemon wedges into the cup (you can arrange the lemon and ice cubes according to personal preference).
Pour in 15 ml of lemon juice (if you like it sour, you can add more or all of it).
Slowly pour Coca-Cola along the cup wall until it reaches 3/4 from the rim (control the ratio of cola to whiskey at about 5:1).
Pour whiskey until the cup is full.

howtocook:drink/奇异果菠菜特调/奇异果菠菜特调.name: Kiwifruit Spinach Special Drink
howtocook:drink/奇异果菠菜特调/奇异果菠菜特调.description: This is a refreshing and healthy fruit and vegetable drink, with a sweet and sour smooth texture, natural fruity aroma of kiwifruit and apple, and a light spinach flavor that doesn't overpower. Rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, it helps promote digestion. It's very simple to make, even beginners can succeed on the first try, and it takes only about 10 minutes.
steps: Cut the kiwifruit in half, then each half into four small pieces
Dice the apple
Remove the stems from the spinach leaves, keeping only the leaves
Chop the spinach
Pour them all into the blender cup
Add water
Add white sugar
Start the blender and blend for about four 15-second intervals (stop every 15 seconds to check the consistency)

howtocook:drink/奶茶.name: Milk Tea
howtocook:drink/奶茶.description: This is a home-style drink easily made with black tea and milk powder, blending tea aroma and milky flavor, smooth and warm. The polyphenols in black tea combined with the protein and calcium in milk can both refresh and supplement daily nutrition. The method is very simple, beginners can succeed on the first try, and it takes only about half an hour to enjoy homemade milk tea.
steps: Place 2 tea bags in a cup and add 180-200mL boiling water.
Wait for 20-30 minutes.
Weigh 11-12g milk powder and 5-7g granulated sugar, and add them separately to the liquid from the previous step.
Stir well and serve.

ingredients.name: Black tea bag (recommend Lipton Yellow Label Black Tea)

howtocook:drink/杨枝甘露.name: Mango Pomelo Chia Seed Dessert
howtocook:drink/杨枝甘露.description: This is a modified version of the classic Hong Kong dessert, using chia seeds instead of sago. The texture is smooth and tender with a subtle crunch from the seeds. The sweetness of mango and the slight tartness of pomelo intertwine, while coconut milk adds richness. Rich in dietary fiber, vitamin C, and healthy fats, it's refreshing and perfect for cooling down. Very easy to make, even beginners can do it. From prep to serving, it takes about 20 minutes.
steps: Soak chia seeds in milk for 10 minutes.
While the seeds are soaking, peel and dice half a mango and the pomelo, place in a cup.
Cut the other half mango into small pieces, put in a blender with ice cubes and coconut milk, blend into a puree.
Pour into the cup, add garnishes (if any).
Enjoy while writing code!!

howtocook:drink/柠檬水/柠檬水.name: Lemonade
howtocook:drink/柠檬水/柠檬水.description: This drink features fresh lemon as the main ingredient, paired with fruit syrup to balance sweet and sour. It's refreshing and cool when iced. Lemons are rich in vitamin C, and regular consumption helps replenish nutrients, refresh, and whiten. The steps are minimal: just pound, add ingredients, and shake. Even beginners can finish in 3-5 minutes.
steps: Weigh 40~45 grams of lemon and put into a shaker
Cover the shaker and pound about 10 times
Add 40~45 grams of fruit syrup
Top up with water
Shake well
Finally, add ice to taste

howtocook:drink/泰国手标红茶/泰国手标红茶.name: Thai Hand-Pick Tea
howtocook:drink/泰国手标红茶/泰国手标红茶.description: This is a classic milk tea from the streets of Thailand. Rich tea aroma, smooth and silky texture, sweet milky flavor from condensed milk and fresh milk. The combination provides a small amount of protein and calcium, but is high in sugar. Suitable as a refreshing drink to beat the heat. The steps are simple, but require repeated filtering and a long refrigeration time. Total time about 4 hours 20 minutes. Patience required for beginners.
steps: Bring 600cc of water to a rolling boil over high heat.
Put 20g of tea powder in a filter bag, pour the boiling water into the filter bag, and filter 20 times.
Using a scale, measure 24g of condensed milk, 24g of white sugar, and 18ml of milk, and put them into a kettle or pitcher of at least 1000ml capacity.
Pour the filtered tea into the pitcher and stir until the sugar dissolves.
Place the pitcher in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours.
Add 6-8 ice cubes before serving.

howtocook:drink/海边落日/海边落日.name: Seaside Sunset
howtocook:drink/海边落日/海边落日.description: This cocktail has distinct layers of color: the grenadine at the bottom resembles sunset clouds, the orange juice in the middle is translucent, and the blue curaçao on top looks like seawater, creating the imagery of a seaside sunset. It tastes sweet and sour, refreshing, with a hint of rum. Orange juice and lemon juice contain a small amount of vitamin C, and the alcohol helps you relax. The mixing steps are not complicated, but pouring the liquid along the stirring rod to create layers requires a bit of patience. Even beginners can succeed by following along. Preparation and mixing take about 15 minutes.
steps: Wash the lemon and cut a slice
Choose a glass, preferably a clear glass with a capacity of 350–400 ml
Add large ice cubes and stir the glass with a stirring rod to chill
Add grenadine syrup
Pour orange juice along the stirring rod into the glass until it is half full
Gently stir half a turn
Pour in soda water
Take a mixing glass, add white rum + blue curaçao + lemon juice + ice. Then shake, shake
Gently pour into the glass
Garnish with lemon slice and straw

howtocook:drink/牛油果拉西.name: Avocado Lassi
howtocook:drink/牛油果拉西.description: An innovative twist on the traditional Indian yogurt drink Lassi, this beverage is creamy, smooth, refreshingly sweet without being cloying, making it perfect as a breakfast or afternoon pick-me-up. Avocados are rich in monounsaturated fatty acids and dietary fiber, while the yogurt provides high-quality protein and probiotics, offering long-lasting satiety in one glass. Extremely easy to make, even beginners can succeed with zero failures—only about 5 minutes from preparation to enjoyment.
steps: Cut the avocado in half, remove the pit, peel off the skin, and cut the flesh into small pieces. Set aside.
Add 120ml plain yogurt, the avocado pieces, 1 tablespoon honey, and 60ml cold milk to a blender.
(Optional) Add a pinch of cardamom powder for a unique flavor.
Blend for about 1 minute until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy.
Pour the drink into a glass.
(Optional) Garnish with a few mint leaves or sprinkle 5g of chopped nuts on top.

howtocook:drink/百香果橙子特调/百香果橙子特调.name: Passion Fruit and Orange Special Drink
howtocook:drink/百香果橙子特调/百香果橙子特调.description: This is a sweet and sour refreshing summer drink that combines the tropical aroma of passion fruit with the sweetness of oranges. Paired with jasmine green tea or soda water, the layers of flavor are distinct, icy and refreshing. Rich in vitamin C, it helps boost immunity and moisturize the skin. Steps include marinating, brewing tea, and juicing. There are a few steps but moderate difficulty, beginners can try. Need to marinate passion fruit one day in advance, total time about 24 hours, actual hands-on time about 30 minutes.
steps: Marinate the passion fruit (this makes two servings because small amounts are hard to handle)
Scoop out the pulp of three passion fruits (including the juice) and place in a container
Weigh out 30 grams of white sugar and add
Weigh out 10 grams of honey and add (if none, substitute with 5 grams of white sugar)
Stir well
Cover the container with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 12–24 hours to marinate
Prepare jasmine green tea (recommended ratio => tea : water : ice = 1–2 : 50 : 30)
Weigh 3–6 grams of tea leaves into a container, add 150 ml boiling water, steep for 6 minutes
Add 90 grams of ice cubes to the brewed tea to cool, stir to speed up cooling
Once the ice has melted, strain out the tea leaves to obtain about 240 ml of jasmine green tea
Handle the orange (can be done while the tea is steeping)
Cut the orange in half (cut perpendicular to the line between stem and blossom end), then slice one thin round from one half and set aside
Cut the orange halves again to get four wedges, use a juicer to squeeze the juice into a container and set aside
Final assembly
Choose a cup, preferably a transparent glass with a capacity of 350–400 ml
Place 70 grams of ice cubes at the bottom, then pour all the orange juice over them
Place the previously prepared orange slice against the inner wall of the cup
Pour one serving of the marinated passion fruit over the ice
Slowly pour the jasmine green tea until the cup is full, to create layers
Place a decorative green leaf on the surface (optional)

ingredients.name: Soda water
ingredients.note: to taste, alternative to jasmine green tea

howtocook:drink/砂糖椰子冰沙/砂糖椰子冰沙.name: Sugar Coconut Ice Slush
howtocook:drink/砂糖椰子冰沙/砂糖椰子冰沙.description: Sugar Coconut Ice Slush is a quick tropical cold drink, with the sweet coconut aroma blended with the caramelized fragrance of coarse brown sugar, offering a smooth and icy texture. Coconut water is rich in natural electrolytes and minerals, quickly replenishing hydration, while coffee sugar adds carbohydrate energy. It's extremely easy to make: just freeze the coconut water solid and smash it into slush—no stress for beginners. It requires more than 10 hours of freezing in advance, but the actual prep takes only a few minutes, making it a perfect afternoon ice treat.
steps: Pour out 200ml from a 500ml bottle of coconut water, then tighten the cap immediately.
Place the bottle in the freezer and freeze for at least 10 hours.

Take out the bottle. Once confirmed that the coconut water is completely frozen, forcefully hit it against a hard surface like a wall corner, chair back, or table edge. (Make sure the surface won't be damaged.)
When the frozen coconut water turns into slush, open the cap and pour out the slush.
Evenly sprinkle coffee sugar or crushed nuts on top of the slush.
Done.

howtocook:drink/耙耙柑茶/耙耙柑茶.name: Pomelo Tea
howtocook:drink/耙耙柑茶/耙耙柑茶.description: Pomelo Tea is a fruit tea drink that combines the sweetness of pomelo with the fragrance of jasmine. It tastes refreshing and sweet, a creative beverage not limited to any specific cuisine. Pomelo is rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, and when paired with green tea, it offers a clean and invigorating experience. The recipe is very simple, beginner-friendly, and takes only about 15 minutes from steeping to blending.
steps: Prepare jasmine green tea (recommended ratio: tea : water : ice = 1~2 : 50 : 30)
Weigh 2~4 g of tea leaves into a container, add 100 ml of boiling water, steep for 6 minutes
Add 60 g of ice cubes to the brewed tea to cool, stir to speed cooling
Once ice has melted, strain out the tea leaves to obtain about 160 ml of jasmine green tea
Formal preparation
Choose a cup, preferably a transparent glass with a capacity of about 300 ml
Remove the flesh segments from the pomelo and peel off the outer skin to get the pulp
Place 130 g of pulp and 130 ml of jasmine green tea into a blender
Add 10 g of cane syrup (optional, if you prefer it sweeter)
Cover and blend for 20 seconds, then pour the mixture into the cup
Take a little pulp, tear it into small pieces, and scatter on the surface

howtocook:drink/菠萝咖啡特调/菠萝咖啡特调.name: Pineapple Coffee Special
howtocook:drink/菠萝咖啡特调/菠萝咖啡特调.description: This special drink combines the rich aroma of coffee with the sweet and sour taste of pineapple. The bubbles are refreshing, the cream topping is smooth, and the layers of flavor are rich. Pineapple is rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, and when paired with coffee, it can help refresh and energize. The difficulty level is moderate; just whip the cream and mix the liquids. It takes about 10 minutes in total to complete.
steps: Add ice, coffee, pineapple juice, and soda water to a glass in that order.
Whip the cream with sugar until soft peaks form. Add rum and milk, mixing until smooth and pourable.
Pour the cream over the mixture from step 1.
Sprinkle sea salt on top of the cream.

howtocook:drink/酒酿醪糟/酒酿醪糟.name: Fermented Sweet Rice (Jiuniang)
howtocook:drink/酒酿醪糟/酒酿醪糟.description: This is a traditional Chinese fermented dessert with a sweet, slightly intoxicating flavor and a light wine aroma. It helps strengthen the spleen and promote digestion. The process is simple and fun, though it requires fermentation time; actual hands-on work is only about 1 hour. Total preparation time is about 2 days.
steps: Wash 800g glutinous rice clean and put it in a rice cooker. Add 720g water and select the steam/cook mode.
After cooking, turn out the rice and spread it to cool. Use clean tools to cool it to 30°C (use a thermometer if possible; it should feel warm but not hot to the touch).
Dissolve 8g Angel sweet rice yeast in 20ml warm water (about 30°C). Sprinkle evenly over the glutinous rice and mix well.

Add 600g water to the rice to help mix the yeast evenly. Let sit for 2-3 minutes until the rice absorbs the water. This addition ensures the fermented rice will have plenty of liquid during the first fermentation.
Use a rolling pin to make a small well in the center of the rice (to allow the wine to collect).
Transfer the mixed rice into a clean container, gently press the surface flat, and seal tightly with a lid or plastic wrap.
Place in an environment at 28-32°C for 24-48 hours. Do not shake or move it during fermentation.
Signs of successful fermentation: clear wine seeps into the center well, the whole batch has a light wine aroma, no off-odors, no sourness.
After fermentation, refrigerate immediately (you can add osmanthus flowers during this step). Use clean utensils each time when serving. It can be refrigerated for 7-10 days.
For a second fermentation, add 500ml water to increase the yield (up to 800g water total).
The fermentation will continue. To stop it, steam for 10 minutes to sterilize, or refrigerate.
Wash 800g glutinous rice clean, soak in water for 6-8 hours (8-10 hours in winter) until grains are plump.
Drain the rice, place in a steaming cloth, and steam over water for 40-60 minutes. Every 20 minutes, open the lid and stir to ensure even cooking.
After steaming, turn out the rice and cool it. Add the yeast, then follow the same steps as above.
Of course, experienced makers can experiment with different grains. You can replace 20% of the glutinous rice with millet, oats, yellow rice, black glutinous rice, etc.

howtocook:drink/酸梅汤（半成品加工）.name: Sour Plum Drink (Using Premix)
howtocook:drink/酸梅汤（半成品加工）.description: Sour Plum Drink (Using Premix) is a sweet and sour, refreshing summer drink with traditional Beijing flavor. It helps quench thirst and aid digestion. The method is extremely simple—just stir in order. It's easy for beginners and takes about five minutes.
steps: Take 1177 grams of drinking water.
Add 60 grams of sour plum crystal solid drink, and stir clockwise with a spoon for 50 circles.
Add the remaining 60 grams of sour plum crystal solid drink, and stir clockwise with a spoon for another 50 circles.
Add 9 grams of sugar cubes, and stir clockwise with a spoon for 100 circles.
Add 48 grams of Beijing Erguotou liquor, and stir clockwise with a spoon for 30 circles.

howtocook:drink/酸梅汤/酸梅汤.name: Sour Plum Drink
howtocook:drink/酸梅汤/酸梅汤.description: Sour Plum Drink is a traditional beverage that is sweet and sour, refreshing and cooling. It is especially invigorating when served chilled. The ingredients like dark plum, hawthorn, and licorice help stimulate salivation, quench thirst, aid digestion, and whet the appetite. The method is simple and beginner-friendly: just soak, boil, and chill. The whole process includes soaking, two rounds of boiling, and chilling, taking about 6 hours or more. It is recommended to prepare in advance.
steps: Rinse the ingredients (except dried osmanthus and rock sugar). Soak in 1.5 liters of water at room temperature for over 2 hours (except dried osmanthus and rock sugar).
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Cover, reduce to low heat, and simmer for 40 minutes. This is the first decoction.
Place rock sugar in a bowl. Strain the first decoction and pour the hot liquid over the sugar. Stir until the sugar dissolves.
Put the herbs back into the pot and add 600 ml of water. Bring to a boil over high heat. Cover, reduce to medium heat, and simmer for 20 minutes. This is the second decoction.

Finally, combine the second decoction with the rock sugar water while hot. When the mixture cools to 60-70°C, add dried osmanthus (do not exceed 80°C). Cover and let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 3 hours.
Strain out the dried osmanthus before serving. Sip slowly like tea. The drink is icy cold, with a pleasant sourness that awakens the palate, balanced sweetness, and a rich, lingering aftertaste.

howtocook:drink/金汤力/金汤力.name: Gin and Tonic
howtocook:drink/金汤力/金汤力.description: This is a classic gin cocktail. It tastes refreshing and slightly bitter, with the bright sourness of lemon and the fine bubbles of tonic water, making it light and invigorating. Although it mainly provides hydration and a bit of calories from alcohol, fresh lemon juice supplements vitamin C and helps refresh. The method is very simple, even a beginner can master it easily, and it takes only about 5 minutes to complete.
steps: Cut the lemon in half (with the knife perpendicular to the line connecting the lemon's ends), then cut one slice from one half and set aside.
Cut the lemon halves in half again, then juice the lemon quarters using a citrus press into a container and set aside.
Choose a glass, preferably a clear glass with a capacity of 350-400 ml.
Place 100 g of ice at the bottom of the glass.
Pour in 30-40 ml of gin.
Pour in 15 ml of lemon juice (add more or all if you like it sour).
Stir well with a spoon.
Place the prepared lemon slice in the glass.
Slowly pour tonic water along the side of the glass until full (do not pour over the ice to avoid losing carbonation).
Gently lift and lower the spoon to mix the liquid (do not stir in circles to avoid losing carbonation).
Place a decorative green leaf on the surface (optional).

howtocook:drink/金菲士/金菲士.name: Gin Fizz
howtocook:drink/金菲士/金菲士.description: A classic gin cocktail, sweet and sour with a refreshing fizz. Lemon provides rich vitamin C, balanced by cane sugar syrup. Very easy to make; even a beginner can whip it up in 5 minutes at home. Note: alcohol is harmful to health, drink in moderation.
steps: Cut the lemon in half (knife perpendicular to the line between the lemon's ends), and cut one slice from one half for later use
Cut the remaining half in half again, then use a juicer to squeeze the juice from the 4 lemon wedges and set aside
Choose a glass, preferably a clear glass with a capacity of 350–400 ml
Place 100 g of ice cubes at the bottom of the glass
Pour in 30–40 ml of gin
Pour in 20 ml of lemon juice (add more if you like it sour, or use all of it)
Pour in 30–40 g of cane sugar syrup
Stir well with a spoon
Place the reserved lemon slice in the glass
Slowly pour soda water along the inner wall of the glass until full (do not pour over the ice to avoid losing fizz)
Use a spoon to gently lift and lower the liquid to stir evenly (do not stir in a circular motion to avoid losing fizz)
Place decorative green leaves on the surface (optional)
Pour the ice, gin, lemon juice, and cane sugar syrup into a shaker and shake well
Pour the shaken mixture into the glass (if the ice is too crushed, you may strain it out and add fresh ice)

howtocook:drink/长岛冰茶.name: Long Island Iced Tea
howtocook:drink/长岛冰茶.description: Long Island Iced Tea is a classic cocktail that tastes like iced tea, with a smooth entry but high alcohol content. It's not particularly nutritious, but an occasional drink can help you relax—just don't overdo it. The recipe is very simple: just mix four base spirits, a liqueur, and juice in the right proportions. Even a beginner can make it easily. It takes about 5 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Cut the lemon in half and squeeze 30ml lemon juice into the glass.
Add to the glass in order:
15ml gin,
15ml tequila,
15ml vodka,
15ml white rum,
15ml orange liqueur;
Slowly pour 20ml maple syrup into the glass, stirring as you pour;
Add 75ml cola to the glass;
Add ice until the glass is full;
Gently stir for 20 seconds;
Enjoy.

howtocook:meat_dish/农家一碗香/农家一碗香.name: Farmhouse One-Pot Stir-Fry
howtocook:meat_dish/农家一碗香/农家一碗香.description: This is a savory and rice-friendly Hunan home-style stir-fry. The richness of fatty pork, the tenderness of egg, and the fragrance of green peppers blend together, creating layers of texture. Pork and egg provide high-quality protein, paired with green peppers for vitamin C, making it a balanced dish. Medium difficulty; pay attention to the heat when rendering fat and the order of seasoning. Beginners can master it with a little practice. Prep time: about 7 minutes, cooking time: about 10 minutes, total about 17 minutes.
steps: Preparation: Slice the pork, preferably separating the fatty and lean parts. Cut the green pepper and bird's eye chili into sections. Smash the garlic cloves with the flat of the knife to make minced garlic. Shred the ginger. Crack the egg into a small bowl and beat with chopsticks.
Once prepared, start cooking. Add oil to a pan over low heat. When hot, pour in the beaten egg and stir-fry until just set. Transfer back to the small bowl and set aside.
Add a little more oil to the pan over low heat. Once hot, add the fatty pork slices and render the lard.
When the fat turns golden, increase the heat to medium, add the lean pork, and stir-fry.
When the lean meat changes color, add the shredded ginger, minced garlic, and doubanjiang (chili bean paste). Stir-fry evenly to coat the pork.
Add the green pepper and bird's eye chili, and the cooked egg. Add light soy sauce and sugar. Continue stir-frying until the peppers are just tender but still crisp.
Serve!

howtocook:meat_dish/冬瓜酿肉/冬瓜酿肉.name: Steamed Winter Melon Stuffed with Meat
howtocook:meat_dish/冬瓜酿肉/冬瓜酿肉.description: Steamed winter melon stuffed with meat is a home-style dish that combines meat and vegetables. It is tender and smooth, with the savory meat filling complementing the sweet winter melon, refreshing and not greasy. Pork mince provides high-quality protein and essential amino acids, while winter melon helps clear heat, promote urination, and replenish water. The recipe is simple, even beginners can easily master it with a little practice on rolling the meat. From preparation to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Peel the winter melon and cut into slices 25 cm long and 3 cm thick.
Place the sliced winter melon in a bowl, add 15g salt, rub it evenly, and let sit for 10 minutes.
While the winter melon sits, in another bowl combine the minced pork, minced scallion and ginger, 5g salt, 5g starch, pepper, light soy sauce, and pepper.
Using chopsticks, stir the meat mixture clockwise until the ingredients are evenly mixed (about 2 minutes).
Rinse the marinated winter melon (which will have softened) with clean water three times.
Take one slice of winter melon, roll it up, and stuff the meat filling inside.
Arrange the rolls on a plate, placing them around the edge.
Crack 1 egg into the center circle of the plate.
Place the plate in a regular iron steamer over boiling water, cover, and steam for 15 minutes.
Remove the lid and take out the steamed winter melon stuffed with meat.
Pour the liquid from the plate into a saucepan, add water starch and 50ml of water, bring to a boil.
Pour the sauce over the stuffed winter melon.

howtocook:meat_dish/冷吃兔.name: Cold Rabbit (Leng Chi Tu)
howtocook:meat_dish/冷吃兔.description: This is a classic Sichuan Zigong salt-gang dish. It's spicy, numbing, and dry-fried, with firm and chewy rabbit meat that delivers an initial kick of heat followed by a savory finish. Rabbit is high in protein, low in fat, rich in lecithin and various amino acids, nutritious and easy to digest. The process involves handling multiple spices and strict heat control, which can be challenging for beginners. From prep to completion takes about 1.5 hours, but the flavor improves after resting overnight, making it worth preparing in advance.
steps: Peel and mince garlic and ginger; set aside. Wash star anise, cinnamon stick, bay leaves, sand ginger, white cardamom, and cumin seeds; set aside.
Cut dried chilies into 2 cm sections, wash, and set aside.
Wash scallions/spring onions/onion; cut onion into small pieces.
Chop rabbit meat into 2 cm pieces. Add salt, cooking wine, and MSG (or chicken powder). Marinate for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok. When oil reaches 40% hot (around 140°C), add scallions/spring onions/onion. Stir-fry over medium-low heat until fragrant and slightly charred, then remove them.
Turn up the heat to high. When oil reaches 80% hot (around 240°C), add rabbit meat. Reduce heat to medium-low and fry until the rabbit turns slightly golden brown, then remove.

Increase oil temperature again. Add dried chilies, green Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, cinnamon, bay leaves, sand ginger, white cardamom, and cumin seeds. Turn to low heat and fry until the chilies become crispy.
Return the rabbit meat to the wok. Add oyster sauce and stir-fry for a few minutes.
Turn off the heat. Add minced garlic, ginger, and white sesame seeds. Stir well to combine.
Let it rest overnight for better flavor.

ingredients.name: Green Sichuan peppercorns
ingredients.note: 1 full bowl for 3 jin rabbit meat

ingredients.name: Cinnamon stick
ingredients.note: a piece the size of a thumb

ingredients.name: Sand ginger
ingredients.note: a piece the size of a soybean

howtocook:meat_dish/凉拌鸡丝/凉拌鸡丝.name: Cold Shredded Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/凉拌鸡丝/凉拌鸡丝.description: A home-style cold dish with low oil and calories. The shredded chicken is tender and flavorful. The combination of Sichuan peppercorn oil and black vinegar creates a slightly numbing and sour refreshing taste, perfect for summer to relieve greasiness. Chicken breast is rich in high-quality protein and low in fat. The recipe is simple, even beginners can easily master it. It takes about 30 minutes from preparation to completion.
steps: Slice ginger, set aside.
Pour 4 liters of water into a pot.
Add chicken breast and ginger slices.
Pour in 20 ml of cooking wine.
Turn to high heat and bring to a boil without covering.
After boiling, reduce to medium heat and skim off the foam with a spoon.
Continue cooking for 5-7 minutes; cook for 5 minutes if fresh, 7 minutes if frozen.
The size of the chicken breast affects cooking time. Insert a chopstick into the chicken; if it goes in easily, the chicken is done. If not, extend cooking time.
Rinse the chicken breast with cooled boiled water to bring it to room temperature.
Shred the chicken breast into thin strips along the grain.
Prepare a bowl.
Add the prepared Sichuan peppercorn oil, light soy sauce, black vinegar, sugar, and salt to the bowl.
Stir the sauce until the sugar and salt are mostly dissolved.
Pour the sauce over the shredded chicken and mix well.

howtocook:meat_dish/卤菜/卤菜.name: Braised Meat
howtocook:meat_dish/卤菜/卤菜.description: This is a classic Chinese braised dish. The meat is tender and flavorful with a rich aroma. The marinade can be reused and becomes more fragrant over time. It is suitable as a cold dish, appetizer with alcohol, or paired with staples. The dish is rich in high-quality protein and various vitamins, with balanced nutrition. It requires a bit of patience, but the steps are clear and beginner-friendly. Pre-soaking to remove blood, preparing caramel color, and pressure cooking take about 1 hour, followed by overnight soaking for full flavor. Take out and slice the next day to enjoy.
steps: Soak the beef shank in cold water for more than 3 hours to remove blood.
Prepare caramel color: Add 30g sugar to a pan, heat on low until sugar melts and turns dark brown, add 150ml hot water, stir well, and set aside.
Cut the onion into chunks, crush the ginger and garlic, break the dried chili peppers into pieces, and set aside.
Add enough water to a pot, add the spice pack, onion, ginger, garlic, and dried chili peppers, bring to a boil over high heat.
Add 15ml each of soybean paste, bean paste, oyster sauce, and fermented bean curd, stir well.
Pour in the prepared caramel color and mix well.
Add 120ml light soy sauce and 60ml dark soy sauce, stir well.
Add 10-15g salt to season.
Pour in 1 can of beer (330ml) and bring to a boil again.
Blanch the beef shank in boiling water for 2-3 minutes, remove and rinse off any scum with hot water.
Place the blanched beef shank into the boiling marinade, ensuring the marinade covers all meat.
Cover with the pressure cooker lid, cook for 25-30 minutes after it starts steaming.
After cooking, do not open the lid; let it cool naturally and soak overnight (this will enhance the flavor).
Remove the braised meat and refrigerate to set.
Before serving, take out and slice; can be eaten directly or served cold with dressing.

How to reuse the marinade: After braising, strain the marinade, discard all solids, bring to a boil again to sterilize, cool, and store in refrigerator or freezer. When using, add seasonings according to the original ratio. (Add water based on the marinade's condition.)
If stored properly, the marinade can be used for a long time and becomes more aromatic with age.
Important note: When braising vegetables, use a separate portion of the marinade and do not reuse it after braising vegetables.

Mix minced garlic, chopped green onion, white sesame seeds, and chili powder in a 1:1:1:1 ratio. Add bird's eye chili according to preference. Heat vegetable oil with a little sesame oil or Sichuan pepper oil, pour it over the seasonings in batches. Then add 10ml each of light soy sauce, vinegar, and oyster sauce, 5ml sugar, MSG/chicken essence, and finally 15ml braising liquid. Mix well.
When serving cold, pair with smashed cucumber, wood ear mushrooms, fried peanuts, cilantro, etc.

ingredients.name: sesame oil or Sichuan pepper oil

howtocook:meat_dish/口水鸡/口水鸡.name: Mouthwatering Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/口水鸡/口水鸡.description: Mouthwatering Chicken is a classic Sichuan cold dish. The chicken, coated in chili oil, is icy, bouncy, numbing, spicy, and aromatic. It's appetizing and refreshing in summer. Chicken is rich in high-quality protein, paired with crushed peanuts and spices for energy and flavor. Medium difficulty; pay attention to the cooking time of the chicken and the seasoning of the chili oil. Suitable for cooks with some experience. From preparation, boiling, cooling to pouring the sauce, it takes about 1.5 hours.
steps: Slice ginger, set aside 1 scallion, and 15 Sichuan peppercorns.
Wash the chicken, place it in a pot, add enough water to cover the chicken, add ginger slices, scallion, and Sichuan peppercorns. Bring to a boil over high heat.
After boiling, reduce to medium-low heat and cook for 20 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Remove the chicken and place it in ice water until completely chilled.
Take out the chicken, cut into pieces, and arrange on a plate. Set aside.
Heat the pan over low heat, add peanuts, and roast until the skins crack. (Keep stirring to avoid burning.)
Cut one scallion into sections, mince the garlic, take 15 Sichuan peppercorns, peel and chop the peanuts.
Heat oil in the pan, add scallion sections, Sichuan peppercorns, and half of the minced garlic. Stir-fry until fragrant.
When the oil is about 80% hot, turn off the heat and strain out the hot oil.
Pour the hot oil into a bowl with chili powder, stir, then strain to obtain the chili oil.
Add the remaining minced garlic, light soy sauce, vinegar, salt, MSG, sugar, sesame oil, and Sichuan pepper powder to the chili oil. Mix well and let cool.
Sprinkle chopped peanuts over the chicken, pour the chili oil over the chicken, and garnish with cilantro. Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/可乐鸡翅.name: Cola Chicken Wings
howtocook:meat_dish/可乐鸡翅.description: These cola chicken wings are shiny red, tender, and have a savory-sweet taste. They're a creative dish loved by families. Chicken wings are packed with protein and fat, and simmering them with cola makes the meat super tender. Cooking them requires a good handle on frying and reducing the sauce—a bit challenging for newbies, but just follow the steps and you'll nail it. It takes about 40 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Put chicken wings in a pot, add cold water to cover. Add 1 slice of ginger and 10-20 ml of cooking wine. Bring to a boil over high heat (about 2 minutes), skim off the foam, then drain.
This step is for fresh chicken wings. For freshly bought wings, simply score them and marinate with light soy sauce.
Remove the chicken wings, score each side twice with a knife. Marinate with about 10 g light soy sauce for 10 minutes (enough to coat the surface).

Heat oil in the pan over low heat again, first sauté the remaining ginger slices until fragrant, then add the marinated chicken wings. Pan-fry until golden, then flip (until both sides are golden). Stir the wings and ginger with a spatula 4-5 times (to prevent sticking).
When wings are golden, pour in cola to cover them. Bring to a boil over high heat, then skim off the black foam (including blood). Add the scallion knot.
Seasoning: Add 2 g salt, 10 g sugar, 3 g light soy sauce (you can add 3 g dark soy sauce for color).
When the scallion knot turns yellow, remove it along with the ginger slices. Turn to medium heat and continue simmering the cola chicken wings.
When the cola reduces to a syrupy consistency, turn to low heat to let the sauce cling to the wings. Remove from heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/台式卤肉饭/台式卤肉饭.name: Taiwanese Braised Pork Rice
howtocook:meat_dish/台式卤肉饭/台式卤肉饭.description: Taiwanese braised pork rice is a fragrant Taiwanese home-cooked dish. The meat strips are tender and flavorful, the sauce is amber and glossy, poured over hot rice for a mouthful of savory fat. Rich in high-quality protein, collagen, and carbs, it quickly replenishes energy. The recipe is very beginner-friendly. Just prepare the ingredients and master the heat. It takes about 1.5 hours total, with only half an hour of hands-on work; the rest is slow simmering.
steps: Cut the pork belly with skin into thin strips of $0.7cm (length) \times 0.7cm (width) \times 2.5cm (height)$.
Mince shallots and garlic; set aside.
Boil eggs until hard, peel, and score the whites with a knife (for better flavor absorption); set aside.
Heat the wok over high heat, add 15 ml cooking oil, and swirl to coat the bottom.
The oil prevents the pork belly from sticking when first added. Wait 5 seconds for the oil to heat slightly.
Add the pork belly strips and stir-fry until the meat turns slightly white. Drizzle 10 ml rice wine along the wok edge. Continue stir-frying until the pork stops releasing fat.
Add the minced shallots to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the minced shallots to the wok and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Push the pork to the side, add rock sugar, and heat until melted and bubbly into caramel. Then mix with the pork to coat evenly.
Add light soy sauce and stir-fry until fragrant.

Splash in 25 ml rice wine, then add water until it covers the pork. Add white pepper powder, five-spice powder, star anise, bay leaves, and boiled eggs. Bring to a boil, then reduce to low heat and braise for 1 hour.
Note: Different soy sauces vary in saltiness. Taste the sauce while braising; add more soy sauce if too sweet, or a bit of sugar or water if too salty.
During simmering, stir and check every 15 minutes to prevent sticking.
If it becomes too dry, add 100 ml water and continue simmering.
After 1 hour, increase heat to high to reduce the sauce until thick and glossy amber, then done.
After braising, serve a bowl of rice, ladle the tender braised pork over the rice, add the braised egg, and enjoy.

howtocook:meat_dish/咕噜肉.name: Sweet and Sour Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/咕噜肉.description: Sweet and Sour Pork is a classic Cantonese home-style dish. It's sweet and sour, appetizing, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside. The fruity aroma of pineapple makes the pork refreshing and cuts through the richness. Pork provides high-quality protein, while green pepper and pineapple add vitamins and dietary fiber. The tricky part is controlling the frying temperature, which is a bit challenging for beginners, but following the steps will ensure success. From marinating to serving, it takes about 40 minutes, making it a great quick dish to serve with rice for the family.
steps: Wash the pork shoulder (100g), then pat dry with paper towels. Cut the pork into pieces slightly smaller than the desired final size.
Marinate the pork shoulder with salt (1/2 tsp) for 20 minutes.
Chop the green pepper (25g).
Cut the canned pineapple slices (75g) into pieces.

In a bowl, combine ketchup (4 tbsp), white vinegar (2 tsp), minced garlic (1 tbsp), light soy sauce (½ tsp), cornstarch (2½ tsp), white sugar (2 tbsp), salt (¼ tsp), and water (200 ml). Mix well to make the sauce.
Coat the pork pieces with cornstarch (6 tbsp).
Add oil (500 ml) and heat over medium heat.
Fry the pork pieces in the oil over medium heat for 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the pork pieces back, then fry over high heat for 1 minute, turning as needed.
Add oil (1 tsp) and the sauce, heat over medium heat for 3 minutes.
Add the green pepper and pineapple, heat over high heat for 2 minutes.
Toss the fried pork pieces with the sauce until well coated. Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/咖喱肥牛/咖喱肥牛.name: Curry Beef Rolls
howtocook:meat_dish/咖喱肥牛/咖喱肥牛.description: Curry Beef Rolls is a rich and savory home-cooked dish that goes well with rice. The spicy aroma of curry combines with the fatty fragrance of beef rolls, paired with potatoes, carrots, and other vegetables, resulting in a soft and hearty texture. Rich in protein, vitamins, and calcium, it offers a balanced mix of meat and vegetables. The recipe is simple and beginner-friendly, typically taking only 40 minutes to serve, making it great for lunchboxes.
steps: Cut the onion into strips, and the carrots and potatoes into chunks. Set aside.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Add the beef rolls, skim off the foam, then drain and set aside.
Heat the pan, add 10-15ml of cooking oil, and wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add the onion and stir-fry until soft and translucent.
Add the potatoes and carrots, and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add cold water until all ingredients are just submerged.
Add the bay leaves and curry blocks to the pot, cover with a lid. Once the water boils, reduce the heat and simmer until the potatoes and carrots are tender (check with chopsticks).
Add the beef rolls and milk, cover, and simmer on low heat for 2-3 minutes. Serve. Stir gently with a spoon to avoid breaking the beef rolls.

howtocook:meat_dish/商芝肉.name: Shangzhi Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/商芝肉.description: Shangzhi Pork is a traditional dish from Shangxian County, Shaanxi. It features a reddish color, tender texture, rich but not greasy, with the unique fragrance of shangzhi (a type of fern). Pork provides high-quality protein and fat, while the fern vegetable shangzhi adds dietary fiber and various trace elements.
