This dish has elaborate steps and requires knife skills and heat control, suitable for those with some cooking experience. From preparation to serving, it takes about 2.5 hours.
steps: Scrape and wash the pork clean, put it in a pot and boil until six-tenths done (turns white). Remove while hot and brush the skin with honey and vinegar.

Put lard in a wok and heat over high heat to 80% hot (about 200°C, oil surface is calm with much blue smoke). Add the pork skin-side down and fry until golden red. Remove and soak in the pot with cold meat broth (the previous boiling pot) until soft. Place on a cutting board and slice into 10 cm long, 0.6 cm thick pieces. Still skin-side down, arrange neatly in a steaming bowl.
Cut 5g scallion into 2.4 cm segments, and another 5g into 2.4 cm diagonal slices. Peel and wash ginger: cut 1.5g into slices, mince 5g. Cut the egg crepe into isosceles triangles with 2.4 cm sides.

Put shangzhi into boiling water and cook until soft, remove, discard tough stems and impurities, wash, cut into 3 cm segments. Place in a bowl, add 5g soy sauce, 1g salt, 10g lard, mix well, and spread over the pork slices. In a small bowl, combine 100g chicken broth, 5g soy sauce, 0.5g salt, 15g cooking wine, stir, and pour into the steaming bowl. Add ginger slices, scallion segments, and star anise. Steam over high heat for about half an hour, then turn to low heat and continue steaming for about 1.5 hours until tender. Remove, discard ginger, scallion, and star anise. Pour out and strain the original juice. Invert the pork onto a soup plate.

In a wok, add 100g chicken broth and the strained juice. Bring to a boil over high heat. Add ginger mince, scallion slices, and MSG, stir well. Add the egg crepe pieces, drizzle with sesame oil, and pour over the pork in the soup plate. Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/啤酒鸭/啤酒鸭.name: Beer-Braised Duck
howtocook:meat_dish/啤酒鸭/啤酒鸭.description: This is a unique home-style dish. The duck meat is tender and savory, with a subtle aroma of beer—warming but not greasy. Duck is rich in protein, and beer helps remove any gamey smell while enhancing flavor. It's beginner-friendly, taking about 1 hour from prep to serving.
steps: Cut the duck into 3 cm pieces. Blanch the duck in cold water with ginger slices and cooking wine. Drain and set aside.
Heat the wok with about 100ml cooking oil over high heat. Add the duck and stir-fry until browned.
When the duck is fully changed color (pale), push it to one side. Add doubanjiang and sugar, stir-fry on low heat until fragrant and caramelized.
Add cloves, star anise, bay leaves, dried chili peppers, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, garlic. Stir-fry until fragrant.
Pour in the beer to cover the duck. Add salt and chicken bouillon. Cook over medium heat for 30 minutes (if you prefer softer meat, cook for an additional 5 minutes).
Serve on a plate and enjoy.

howtocook:meat_dish/回锅肉/回锅肉.name: Twice-Cooked Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/回锅肉/回锅肉.description: Twice-Cooked Pork is a classic Sichuan dish, using pork belly or rear leg meat. The finished dish is red and bright, with translucent and slightly curled fat, dry and fragrant lean meat, rich but not greasy, savory with a hint of spiciness, carrying the complex aroma of garlic sprouts and broad bean paste. Pork is rich in high-quality protein and essential fatty acids, and with green and red peppers and garlic sprouts, it provides vitamins. This dish has many steps—boiling, slicing, stir-frying—requiring some control over heat and knife skills, but beginners can succeed if they follow the process. The total time is about 40 minutes.
steps: Heat the pan, press the pork belly firmly against the pan with your hand to sear the skin.
This step is to treat the sweat glands on the skin (or ask the butcher to burn the skin; ignore if you like the glands).
Scrub the skin clean with a steel wool, removing all carbonized black parts; otherwise, it will be bitter.
Put the pork belly in a pot, add enough cold water to cover it, then add ginger slices, cooking wine, and scallions (tie 2 scallions into a knot).
Bring to a boil over high heat, skim off the foam, and continue to boil for 15 minutes, until the lean part can be easily pierced with chopsticks.
Cut green and red peppers into rings.
Cut garlic sprouts into sections.
Slice ginger into small thin pieces.
Mix 5ml broad bean paste and 5ml light soy sauce in advance.
Remove the cooked pork belly and cool it in cold water.
Dry the pork belly, then slice it into 2mm thin slices with fat on top and lean on bottom (thicker slices will have poor texture and be greasy).
Heat the pan, add a thin layer of oil to coat the pan.
Stir-fry the pork belly slices until the fat turns translucent and the slices are slightly curled (like lamp holders; rear leg meat works best).
Add the broad bean paste-soy sauce mixture and 5g MSG, stir-fry for 15 seconds.
Add green and red pepper rings and small ginger slices, then add another 5ml broad bean paste, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add garlic sprouts and stir-fry for 60 seconds.
Plate and serve.
If using frozen pork belly, thaw for 0.5 hour; if fresh, refrigerate for 1 hour, then slice into 2-5mm thin slices.
Over medium heat, dry-fry the peppers in the pan for 30-45 seconds, then remove them.
Heat the pan, add a thin layer of oil to coat, then add ginger slices and stir-fry for 15 seconds.
Add the pork belly slices, stir-fry every 10 seconds, until they turn golden brown (the longer you stir-fry, the firmer the texture).
Add the previously dry-fried peppers, 10ml broad bean paste, and light soy sauce to season, continue stir-frying for 60 seconds.
Add the cut garlic sprouts and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Plate and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/土豆炖排骨/土豆炖排骨.name: Braised Pork Ribs with Potatoes
howtocook:meat_dish/土豆炖排骨/土豆炖排骨.description: The ribs are tender and fall off the bone, the potatoes are soft and flavorful, with a rich savory sauce that is slightly sweet and spicy. It is a typical northern home-style braised dish. Rich in protein, calcium, and carbohydrates, nutritionally balanced. Preparation is somewhat challenging, requiring mastery of caramelizing sugar and heat control, but the steps are clear and beginners can try it. The whole process takes about 1.5 hours.
steps: Prepare potatoes: Peel 300g potatoes and cut into irregular rolling cuts. Immediately soak in a bowl of water to prevent oxidation and blackening. Set aside.
Prepare ginger and scallions: Slice 30g ginger into thin slices (divide into two portions of 15g each). Prepare 25g scallions: tie 15g into a knot, finely chop the remaining 10g. Set aside.
Prepare sauce bowl (one-click use): In a small bowl, add 10ml light soy sauce, 5ml dark soy sauce, 5ml oyster sauce, 5ml soybean paste. Stir thoroughly. Place within easy reach.

Prepare spice dish (one-click use): In a small dish, place 5g dried chilies, 1g star anise (about 1-2 pieces), 1g Sichuan peppercorns (about 10), 1g cinnamon stick (a small piece), and the 15g ginger slices set aside earlier. Set aside.
Start water boiling: Press the electric kettle to boil 700ml water. (Ensure the water is ready when needed.)

Cold water blanching: Place 750g pork ribs in cold water. Add 15g scallion knot, 15g ginger slices, 15ml cooking wine. Bring to a boil over high heat, then continue boiling for 2 minutes to draw out blood foam.
Hot rinse, cold prevent: Drain the ribs. Rinse off any remaining foam with hot water (cold water will cause the meat to shrink and harden).
Thoroughly dry (prevent splatter): Pat the ribs completely dry with paper towels. Place on a plate near the wok (to prevent oil splatter when adding to hot caramel).

Caramelize sugar: Heat oil in wok over medium-low heat, add 20ml cooking oil. Add 10g white sugar and stir constantly. Cook until sugar completely melts, bubbles, and turns a reddish-brown caramel color.
Color and sear: Quickly add the dried 750g ribs and turn to medium-high heat. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until ribs are lightly golden and evenly coated with caramel.
One-click aromatics: Clear a small space in the wok, add the spice dish directly. Stir-fry in the hot oil for about 10 seconds to release fragrance.

One-click seasoning: Drizzle the remaining 10ml cooking wine along the edge of the wok to create wok hei. Then pour in the sauce bowl. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds until ribs are fully coated and fragrant.
Add water and braise: Add 700ml boiling water (enough to submerge ribs). Bring to a boil over high heat. Cover, reduce to low heat, and simmer for 40 minutes.
Add potatoes: After 40 minutes, uncover. Drain the 300g potatoes from water and add to the pot. Re-cover and simmer for another 15-20 minutes until potatoes are easily pierced with a chopstick.

Season and reduce sauce: Uncover, add 3g salt (taste and adjust if needed). Turn to high heat and stir-fry to reduce sauce for 3-5 minutes until thickened and coats the ribs and potatoes. Turn off heat.
Garnish and serve: Plate the dish and sprinkle the reserved 10g chopped scallions on top as garnish.

howtocook:meat_dish/奶酪培根通心粉/奶酪培根通心粉.name: Cheese Bacon Macaroni
howtocook:meat_dish/奶酪培根通心粉/奶酪培根通心粉.description: Cheese Bacon Macaroni is a classic American home-style dish. Rich and creamy cheese coats the chewy macaroni, paired with crispy bacon, savory and satisfying. Rich in protein and calcium, but high in calories and fat, so it's recommended to enjoy occasionally. Making it requires some patience; mastering the basic white sauce and heat control can be a bit challenging for beginners, overall difficulty is medium. Without baking, it can be served in about 30 minutes. If baking, additional preheating and baking time is needed, total about 1 hour.
steps: Grate the cheese into fine crumbs.
Cut the onion into strips.
Cook the macaroni in slightly salted water for 6 minutes.
Medium heat.
Put butter in the pan and wait until melted.
Add the onion.
When the onion softens, add the garlic.
After the garlic aroma comes out, add the meat and wait 5 seconds.
Low heat.
Add milk in four batches, stirring for 5 seconds after each addition.
Add flour and stir thoroughly.
Add cheese and stir until well combined.
Mix the macaroni with the cheese sauce.
If not baking, you can eat it directly.
Baking:
Preheat oven to 180°C.
Spread the additional 50g cheese on top of the macaroni.
After the oven preheats to 180°C, put in the macaroni.
Bake until golden brown on top, about 24 minutes.

howtocook:meat_dish/姜炒鸡/姜炒鸡.name: Stir-fried Chicken with Ginger
howtocook:meat_dish/姜炒鸡/姜炒鸡.description: Stir-fried Chicken with Ginger is a Hunan-style rice-wrecking dish. The chicken is dry-fried and flavorful, with rich ginger aroma, spicy but not greasy. Chicken is high in quality protein, and ginger helps dispel cold, warm the stomach, and stimulate appetite. The recipe is simple, even beginners can master it easily. From marinating to serving, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Cut the chicken into 1cm pieces as much as possible, wash and drain, then marinate with light soy sauce and cooking wine for 30 minutes.
First heat the wok over high heat until it just starts to smoke, add cooking oil, wait 5 seconds.
Add ginger slices, turn to medium heat and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the chicken pieces and stir-fry for 3 minutes, until the moisture is dry and the chicken oil is released.
Add the chopped chilies and garlic cloves, add salt and dark soy sauce, continue stir-frying for 30 seconds.
Pour in beer, simmer over medium-low heat for 2 minutes.
Increase heat to high to reduce the sauce, then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/姜葱捞鸡/姜葱捞鸡.name: Ginger and Scallion Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/姜葱捞鸡/姜葱捞鸡.description: Ginger and Scallion Chicken is a classic Cantonese home-cooked dish. The chicken is tender and juicy, with a rich aroma of scallion and ginger. It is savory and goes well with rice. Chicken thighs provide high-quality protein, and the combination with ginger and scallion warms the stomach and stimulates appetite. The cooking methods are mainly steaming and hand-shredding. The steps are clear and easy for beginners. It takes about 40 minutes from preparation to completion.
steps: Wash four chicken thighs clean and place them in a bowl.
Add salt-baked chicken powder and 5ml oil to the bowl, mix well.
Let the chicken thighs marinate for 15 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare a steamer and bring water to a boil.
After marinating, place the chicken thighs in the steamer and steam for 20 minutes.
Cut the ginger according to personal preference into 1) ginger paste, 2) ginger shreds, or 3) ginger pieces.
Cut the scallions into 0.5 cm pieces.
Put the scallions and ginger into a dipping bowl, and add salt and sugar.
Pour the remaining oil into another pot and heat until 60-70% hot.
Pour the hot oil over the scallions and ginger in the bowl.
After steaming, shred the chicken thighs into strips, not too thin, about 1 cm thick.
Pour the ginger-scallion oil over the shredded chicken and mix well.

howtocook:meat_dish/孜然牛肉.name: Cumin Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/孜然牛肉.description: Cumin Beef is a spicy and tender home-style stir-fry. The rich cumin flavor perfectly combines with the softness of beef and the crispness of green peppers. Beef is rich in high-quality protein and iron, paired with green peppers to supplement vitamins, making it nutritionally balanced. Preparation requires some knife skills and heat control, suitable for enthusiasts with basic cooking experience. Including marinating and prepping, it takes about 45 minutes from start to table.
steps: First, chop the bird's eye chilies and put them together with cumin seeds into a mortar, pound into granules for better flavor. If short on time, you can skip the pounding step.
Cut off the head and remove seeds of the green pepper (keep seeds if you like it spicy), then shred. Cut the scallion into sections.
Thaw the beef in advance, rinse with water, and pat dry with kitchen paper. Cut the beef into slices along the grain.
Then marinate the beef: add light soy sauce, starch, and oil, mix well, and let it sit for 30 minutes. You can also refer to learn marinating methods.
Heat oil in a wok, add scallion, stir-fry until fragrant, then add the marinated beef and stir-fry.
When the beef changes color, evenly add the cumin and chili granules and stir-fry until cooked through.
Then add the shredded green pepper, stir-fry until just cooked, then add salt.
Stir-fry over high heat for 1 minute, then turn off the heat and continue stirring for 30 seconds to ensure even heat, then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/宫保鸡丁/宫保鸡丁.name: Kung Pao Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/宫保鸡丁/宫保鸡丁.description: This iconic Sichuan dish features a numbing-spicy and tangy flavor profile, with a hint of sweetness and sourness. The aroma of dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns permeates the tender chicken cubes and crispy peanuts, creating a rich texture. It is rich in high-quality protein and unsaturated fats, making it an appetizing dish that pairs well with rice. The recipe includes both a simple and an advanced version, friendly for beginners. Just plan ahead for the marinating time. From preparation to serving, it takes about 1.5 hours.
steps: Debone the chicken leg using scissors, pound the meat side with the back of a knife, then cut into strips and dice into 1.5 cm cubes. Soak in clean water for 10 minutes, then drain and set aside (if using chicken breast, dice directly and proceed).

Put the green part of the scallion and 5g ginger slices in a bowl, pour in 50g boiling water and set aside. Cut the white part of the scallion into 1.5 cm rounds. Roast the peanuts in a microwave on high for 5 minutes until dry, set aside.

Add 2g salt, 5g dark soy sauce, 15g cooking wine, and 15g starch to the chicken cubes. Mix until slightly dry. Slowly add some of the scallion-ginger water, stirring until the chicken becomes sticky. Cover with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 1 hour.

Cut dried chilies into segments. Heat the wok over high heat, then reduce to low. Dry-roast the dried chilies until slightly charred, then remove and set aside. Roast the Sichuan peppercorns until fragrant, then remove and set aside.

Turn to high heat, add 20g vegetable oil. When the oil is 70% hot (bubbles form around chopsticks), add the chicken cubes. Fry until the tops turn white, then flip with a spatula. Fry for 30 seconds, then stir-fry evenly.

Add the scallion rounds and stir-fry. Add the remaining scallion-ginger water, and if it's less than 100g, add a bit more hot water (must be hot). Cover the wok, reduce to medium-low heat, and braise for 2 minutes.
Turn to high heat. Add the cooked peanuts, dried chilies, and Sichuan peppercorns. Add 2g chicken bouillon, 5g Zhenjiang vinegar, and 2g sugar. Stir-fry evenly.
Mix 10g starch with 50g water to make a slurry. Add to the wok and stir-fry evenly. Thicken to desired consistency.
Turn off the heat, drizzle with 10g sesame oil, and serve immediately.
Peel the asparagus lettuce and dice into 1 cm cubes. Set aside.
Cut the Erjingtiao chilies into 1 cm long segments.

Debone the chicken leg using scissors, pound the meat side with the back of a knife, then cut into strips and dice into 1.5 cm cubes. Soak in clean water for 10 minutes, then drain and set aside (if using chicken breast, dice directly and proceed).
Put the green part of the scallion and 5g ginger slices in a bowl, pour in 50g boiling water and set aside. Cut the white part of the scallion into 1.5 cm rounds.

Add 2g salt, 5g dark soy sauce, 15g cooking wine, and 15g starch to the chicken cubes. Mix until slightly dry. Slowly add some of the scallion-ginger water, stirring until the chicken becomes sticky. Cover with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
Turn to medium heat, add 20g vegetable oil. Add raw peanuts and stir-fry until the surface is slightly scorched. Remove the peanuts but leave the oil in the wok.
Continue heating until the oil is 70% hot (bubbles form around chopsticks). Add the chicken cubes and doubanjiang (fermented broad bean paste). Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
Add the prepared asparagus lettuce cubes and continue stir-frying for 1 minute.

Add the scallion rounds and stir-fry. Add the remaining scallion-ginger water, and if it's less than 100g, add a bit more hot water (must be hot). Add the Erjingtiao chili segments. Cover the wok, reduce to medium-low heat, and braise for 2 minutes.
Turn to high heat. Add the reserved peanuts and Sichuan peppercorns. Add 2g chicken bouillon, 5g Zhenjiang vinegar, and 2g sugar. Stir-fry evenly.
Mix 10g starch with 50g water to make a slurry. Add to the wok and stir-fry evenly. Thicken to desired consistency.
Turn off the heat, drizzle with 10g sesame oil and 5g chili oil, then stir-fry for another 10 seconds. Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/小炒肉.name: Stir-fried Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/小炒肉.description: Stir-fried Pork Belly is a classic spicy and appetizing Hunan home-style dish. The pork belly slices are crispy and not greasy, stir-fried with fresh chili peppers, fermented black beans, and bean paste to create a rich sauce. Rich in high-quality protein and fat, chili peppers provide plenty of vitamin C, which helps stimulate appetite and warm the body. The difficulty level is moderate, suitable for beginners with some basic skills. Pay attention to marinating the meat and controlling the heat. From preparation to serving takes about 1 hour, including 30 minutes of marinating and about 15 minutes of cooking.
steps: Slice the pork belly.
Place the meat in a bowl, add starch, dark soy sauce, and salt. Mix and marinate for 30 minutes.
Cut the scallions into sections.
Slice the red chili peppers and bird's eye chili peppers diagonally.
Heat the wok and add oil.
Once the oil is hot, add the pork belly and stir-fry. Stir-fry until it changes color, then remove from the wok.
Add garlic to the wok, stir-fry until fragrant, then add fermented black beans and stir-fry evenly.
Add bean paste and stir-fry evenly.
Add the previously stir-fried pork belly and continue to stir-fry evenly.
Add the red chili peppers, bird's eye chili peppers, and scallion sections. Stir-fry for 40 seconds.
Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/小炒鸡肝/小炒鸡肝.name: Stir-Fried Chicken Liver
howtocook:meat_dish/小炒鸡肝/小炒鸡肝.description: This stir-fried chicken liver dish has a rich garlic aroma, tender and smooth texture with a hint of cumin's smoky flavor. It's a home-style stir-fry. Chicken liver is rich in iron, vitamin A, and high-quality protein, which helps nourish blood and improve eyesight. The technique of blanching to remove the gamey smell is essential, which may be slightly challenging for beginners. From preparation to serving, it takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Wash the chicken livers and set aside.
Wash the garlic sprouts, cut into sections, and set aside.
Wash the scallions, take 100g and cut into sections, take 50g and slice, set aside.
Wash the ginger, take 70g and slice, take 50g and dice, set aside.
Step 1: Blanch
Put the cleaned chicken livers in a pot, add cold water to cover, and add 100g of scallion sections, 70g of sliced ginger, and 30ml of cooking wine.
Turn to high heat, when the water boils, reduce to medium heat and skim off any foam, then wait 5-10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, take out the livers, rinse under cold water and clean thoroughly.
Slice the cleaned livers and set aside.
Step 2: Stir-fry
Turn to medium heat. Add 30ml of cooking oil to the pot, wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add the 50g of sliced scallions and 50g of diced ginger, stir-fry until fragrant.
After releasing fragrance, add the sliced chicken livers, stir-fry until slightly golden.
Add the garlic sprouts, stir-fry for 30 seconds, then reduce to low heat once they are slightly cooked.
Add seasonings.
5g salt
5g chicken bouillon (MSG)
5g five-spice powder (or thirteen-spice)
5g ground pepper
10g barbecue seasoning or cumin powder (optional)
Turn to high heat, quickly stir-fry for 30 seconds to prevent sticking, then turn off the heat.
Transfer to a plate. Optionally sprinkle 5g of sesame seeds as garnish. If you don't like or don't have, you can skip.

howtocook:meat_dish/小炒黄牛肉/小炒黄牛肉.name: Stir-Fried Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/小炒黄牛肉/小炒黄牛肉.description: This Hunan-style stir-fry uses high heat to bring out the tenderness of the beef and the aroma of chili peppers, resulting in a spicy and refreshing taste with crisp celery, embodying the essence of Hunan cuisine. Beef tenderloin provides high-quality protein and easily absorbable iron, while celery adds dietary fiber for a balanced meal. The preparation and cooking steps are straightforward, with quick stir-frying over high heat throughout, making it beginner-friendly. From cutting to serving, it takes about one hour.
steps: Slice the beef tenderloin into pieces no wider than 3 cm and no thicker than 3 mm. Pour in 6 ml of soy sauce and mix well with your hands. Set aside.
Cut the celery into segments no longer than 5 cm. Set aside.
Slice the red chili peppers into strips. Set aside.
Cut the pickled chili peppers into small pieces. Set aside.
Cut the cilantro into segments no longer than 3 cm. Set aside.
Heat the wok, add 15 ml of cooking oil, and wait for 30 seconds over high heat until the oil is hot.
Add the red chili peppers and pickled chili peppers and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the beef and celery, then stir-fry over high heat for 1 minute.
Turn off the heat, sprinkle with cilantro, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/小米辣炒肉.name: Stir-fried Pork with Thai Chili Peppers
howtocook:meat_dish/小米辣炒肉.description: Stir-fried pork with Thai chili peppers is spicy and satisfying, a classic Southwestern Chinese dish. The tender pork paired with the fiery kick of Thai peppers whets the appetite. Pork is rich in protein and fat, while chili peppers provide plenty of vitamin C, helping to stimulate appetite. Although you need to master the heat for stir-frying, the steps are clear and even beginners can easily handle it. The total cooking time is about 25 minutes. Note: This dish is intensely spicy; it's not recommended for those who prefer light flavors.
steps: Wash the Thai chili peppers and cut them diagonally into larger pieces.
For the meat, cut into shreds or slices as you like. Add the seasonings (light soy sauce, oyster sauce, salt) and marinate for 5 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry the meat first, then remove and set aside.

Sauté ginger and garlic until fragrant, add doubanjiang (chili bean paste) and stir-fry, then add the sliced Thai peppers. Return the lean meat, stir-fry briefly, then add a bit of light soy sauce, chicken bouillon, salt, and sugar, and stir-fry.

howtocook:meat_dish/小酥肉.name: Crispy Fried Pork Strips (Xiao Su Rou)
howtocook:meat_dish/小酥肉.description: Crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with a savory flavor and a slight numbing sensation from Sichuan pepper. It is a home-style fried dish commonly made in the Sichuan and Chongqing region. Pork is rich in protein and fat, and after deep-frying, it is relatively high in calories, suitable for occasional indulgence. This dish has quite a few steps; mastering the heat for the initial frying to set the shape and the second frying for color can be a bit challenging for beginners. It takes about 1 hour and 30 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Slice old ginger into thin strips; do not cut the scallions.
According to the calculation formula, pour in cooking wine and water.
Knead with hands for 5 minutes to fully dissolve the flavor of ginger and scallion into the water.
Remove skin from pork and wash clean.
Cut into strips about 8–10 cm long and 1.5 cm thick.
According to the calculation formula above, add salt, thirteen-spice powder, pepper powder, MSG, chicken powder, crushed Sichuan pepper, whole Sichuan pepper, and light soy sauce.
Pour in the previously prepared ginger-scallion water.
Mix thoroughly and knead for 10 minutes until the meat absorbs all the liquid and becomes sticky.
Cover with plastic wrap and rest in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Add flour and sweet potato starch to the marinated meat, then add egg whites.
Knead thoroughly for 15 minutes.
Pour vegetable oil into the pot; control the amount based on pot size; oil depth should be at least 3 cm.
Heat over high heat to 150°C, then turn to low heat to maintain temperature.

Use chopsticks to place the coated meat strips into the oil, shaping them as desired; fry for 3–5 minutes to set. Look for a light golden color and a slightly firm feel when turning with a spatula. Exact time depends on meat size, oil temperature, and coating thickness.
Remove and drain oil.
Raise oil temperature to 180°C, add the first-fried strips, and fry until golden brown, then remove.

howtocook:meat_dish/尖叫牛蛙/尖叫牛蛙.name: Screaming Bullfrog
howtocook:meat_dish/尖叫牛蛙/尖叫牛蛙.description: This dish is spicy and appetizing, with tender and springy frog meat. It's a classic Sichuan home-style dish. Bullfrog is high in protein and low in fat, paired with pickled ginger, pickled peppers and other fermented ingredients to boost appetite. The method is straightforward, but oil temperature and seasoning ratios need some attention. Beginners can usually finish it in 1 to 2 hours.
steps: Clean the bullfrog meat and drain well. Add 10g or more of salt and 50ml or more of beer. Knead with hands for 5 minutes to remove the gamey smell.
Then rinse under running water until no blood or impurities remain. Drain well and place in a suitable container for marinating.
Add 5g salt, 30g cornstarch, 10ml cooking wine, and 5g pepper. Mix evenly with hands. Marinate for 5-10 minutes.
Shred or slice pickled ginger, pickled peppers, and wild mountain peppers (depending on knife skills). Cut green and red chilies into rings. Peel garlic cloves.

Heat the wok, add 200ml cooking oil (add extra 100ml if using a flat-bottom pan). Heat to 60% (small bubbles appear). Add the marinated bullfrog and quickly deep-fry for 10 seconds. Remove immediately (do not over-fry or the meat will become tough).
Remove the frog meat, drain oil. Pour out the hot oil from the wok into a bowl, leaving 30ml. Add 20ml lard (if not available, keep a total of 50ml cooking oil in the wok).

When oil is 60% hot, add pickled ginger, pickled peppers, wild mountain peppers, and garlic. Stir-fry until fragrant. Add 20g doubanjiang. Stir-fry over medium heat until red oil appears (about 30 seconds). Pour in 400ml beer.
Then add the deep-fried bullfrog meat. Gently push with a spoon to mix, do not stir vigorously. Add 5g pepper and 5ml Sichuan pepper oil. Simmer over medium heat for 3 minutes.
Increase heat to high and reduce sauce for 30 seconds. Add green and red chili rings. Cook for 10 more seconds, then prepare to plate.
Transfer to a bowl, sprinkle with chopped scallions, and serve!

howtocook:meat_dish/尖椒炒牛肉.name: Stir-fried Beef with Hot Peppers
howtocook:meat_dish/尖椒炒牛肉.description: A home-style stir-fry, savory and slightly spicy. The beef is tender and flavorful, while the hot peppers are crisp and refreshing. Beef provides high-quality protein and iron, and hot peppers are rich in vitamin C, making a balanced dish. Although marinating requires patience, the steps are clear, suitable for beginners, with moderate difficulty. From prep to finish, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Mince garlic into paste
Cut scallions into sections
Slice ginger into slices
Cut hot peppers into sections
Place beef in a bowl
Add ginger, salt, soy sauce, and sugar; marinate for 30-40 minutes
After marinating, remove the ginger
Add cold oil to the pan, heat until bubbles appear occasionally
Add minced garlic
When garlic turns golden, add hot peppers
When the pepper skin wrinkles slightly, add marinated beef and stir-fry
Before the beef is fully cooked, add scallions and continue stir-frying
Stir-fry until beef is cooked through, then turn off heat and serve

howtocook:meat_dish/山西过油肉.name: Shanxi Fried Sliced Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/山西过油肉.description: Shanxi Fried Sliced Pork is a traditional famous dish of Shanxi. The pork slices are tender, garlic sprouts are crisp, savory with a hint of sourness, carrying the rich aroma of aged vinegar. It is a typical local home-style flavor. Pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein, paired with wood ear mushrooms, garlic sprouts, and onions for balanced nutrition. This dish requires some skill in heat control and battering, making it more suitable for enthusiasts with some cooking experience. From preparation to serving, it takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Soak the wood ear mushrooms in advance; if in a hurry, use hot water to speed up.

Slice the pork tenderloin and put into a bowl. Add 20ml light soy sauce, cooking wine, ground Sichuan pepper, crack in one egg, mix well with your hand. Add starch (sweet potato starch recommended) and mix well. Pour in 300ml cooking oil to seal the batter, and marinate for 15 minutes.
Cut garlic sprouts into sections about 3cm long. Cut onion into diamond-shaped pieces and set aside.
Heat a wok with more oil. When oil reaches 50% heat (about 150°C), add the marinated pork slices. Quickly separate them, then remove and drain oil.
Pour out excess oil from the wok, leaving 10ml for stir-frying. Heat oil to 70% heat (about 210°C).

Add chopped scallion, ginger, and garlic to sizzle. First add garlic sprouts and stir-fry until just cooked, then add wood ear mushrooms and onion. Add light soy sauce and ground Sichuan pepper. Stir a few times, then add the previously fried pork slices and stir-fry.
Add 10g salt. Just before removing from heat, add 10ml vinegar and chicken bouillon. Remove and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/巴基斯坦牛肉咖喱/巴基斯坦牛肉咖喱.name: Pakistani Beef Curry
howtocook:meat_dish/巴基斯坦牛肉咖喱/巴基斯坦牛肉咖喱.description: This Pakistani-style beef curry is rich and tender, with a sour and spicy kick and a strong aroma of spices. Beef provides high-quality protein, paired with tomatoes and yogurt for balanced nutrition. The method is not too complicated, but slow cooking requires some patience, making it perfect for a leisurely weekend. From start to finish, it takes about two and a half hours to serve up a warm pot of exotic deliciousness.
steps: Cut all screw chilies into sections and set aside.
Blend all tomatoes into a puree and set aside.
Cut the beef into 2cm cubes and wash clean.
Pour a layer of oil into the wok (to prevent the tomato puree from boiling and evaporating).
Pour in the tomato puree and stir continuously for 2-3 minutes, waiting for it to turn a deeper red.
Add 5g garlic powder, 5g ginger powder, and 1 packet (50g) of Masala powder, and mix well.
Add the beef and screw chili sections, and stir to combine.
Add 1 box of yogurt (to make the sauce thick), and stir well.
Transfer everything to a rice cooker or slow cooker, add 250 ml of water, and set to stew/slow cook mode for 2-3 hours.
Wait until done, then open the lid and check that the beef is tender. It's ready to eat.

howtocook:meat_dish/带把肘子.name: Braised Pork Hock with Handle
howtocook:meat_dish/带把肘子.description: Braised Pork Hock with Handle is a traditional famous dish from Dali, Shaanxi. It is named because the claw resembles a handle. The finished dish has sticky and soft skin, tender and non-greasy meat. It is eaten with sweet bean sauce and scallion segments, savory and mellow. Rich in collagen and high-quality protein, nourishing and beautifying. It requires deboning, shaping, and long steaming, which is somewhat challenging for beginners. The entire process takes about 3.5 hours.
steps: Scrape and clean the pork hock. Place it on the cutting board with the top end outward, the handle (claw) inward, and the skin side down.

Using a knife, cut the skin open along the leg bone from the top end to the handle. Remove the meat on both sides of the leg bone (meat separated on three sides), leaving the bone attached at the bottom to expose the bone. Then smash the two sections of the leg bone in the middle with the back of the knife (or use a cleaver).
Put the hock in a pot and boil until 70% cooked (normal appearance, interior light red). Remove, wipe dry with a clean cloth, and while hot, brush the skin with dark soy sauce.

Prepare a steamer. Place star anise and cinnamon on the bottom. First, break the joint of the handle by hand without damaging the skin. Then place the hock skin side down into the steamer. Depending on the size, nestle the handle against the edge of the steamer to form a round shape.

Sprinkle with salt. Cover the meat with sterilized clean gauze. Then spread sweet bean sauce (50g), scallion (75g), fermented red bean curd, dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, ginger, and garlic onto the gauze. Steam over high heat for about three hours (until thoroughly tender).

After steaming, remove and uncover the gauze. Invert onto a plate. Discard the star anise. Serve with scallion segments and a small dish of sweet bean sauce on the side (or spread the sauce on the hock and serve scallion segments separately).

ingredients.name: Pork front hock with foot and claw

howtocook:meat_dish/干煸仔鸡/干煸仔鸡.name: Dry-Fried Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/干煸仔鸡/干煸仔鸡.description: An innovative Sichuan dish with a sweet-spicy flavor, originating from Peking University's cafeteria. The chicken is marinated then fried, crispy outside and tender inside. Potatoes are soft and sticky, green peppers are crisp, all coated in a bright red sweet-spicy sauce. Salty-sweet with a hint of spice, it's very appetizing. Rich in protein, carbohydrates, and vitamins. Involves deep-frying and sauce reduction, slightly challenging for beginners, but following steps will ensure success. Takes about 1 hour in total.
steps: Debone chicken thighs (skip if using boneless thigh fillets). Tenderize the meat with the back of a knife, then cut into 2cm cubes.

Add salt, chicken bouillon (optional), white pepper, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and cooking wine to the chicken. Mix until sticky, then add cornstarch and mix. Add cooking oil to prevent sticking. Marinate for 30 minutes.
Peel potatoes, cut into 2cm cubes. Boil in water for 5 minutes, then drain well to avoid splattering when frying.
Remove seeds from green peppers, cut into 2cm pieces, set aside in a colander.
Add enough oil to a wok (enough to submerge ingredients). Heat oil to 180°C, then fry potato cubes for 3 minutes. Remove.
Reheat oil to 180°C, then fry chicken cubes for 2 minutes. Remove.
Reheat oil to 180°C, then fry chicken cubes again for 1 minute. Remove.
Reheat oil to 180°C, then fry potato cubes again for 1 minute. Pour the oil and potatoes through a colander to drain, letting the green peppers on the colander wilt slightly from the hot oil.

Add 5ml cooking oil to the wok. Stir-fry garlic cloves over low heat until golden. Add Sichuan chili bean paste (red oil) and stir-fry until fragrant. Add sugar and stir until melted. Add ground Sichuan peppercorns. Add 40ml water, stirring constantly until the sauce thickens.
Add the fried chicken, potatoes, and green peppers. Toss to coat evenly with the sauce. Remove from heat and serve.

ingredients.name: Pixian red oil chili bean paste

howtocook:meat_dish/乡村啤酒鸭.name: Country Beer Duck
howtocook:meat_dish/乡村啤酒鸭.description: Duck meat is braised with beer, resulting in a rich broth and tender, flavorful meat with a hint of beer aroma. It's a unique home-style dish. Duck is nutritious, rich in protein, B vitamins, and various minerals. Braising with beer enhances its flavor. Although the steps are many, it's not complicated, and beginners can finish in about 1 hour.
steps: Wash duck meat and put into pot
Add water to cover duck
Add 20 ml cooking wine
Add 1 reserved scallion
Add 2 cm ginger, smashed
Bring to a boil over high heat
Skim off foam
Remove duck, rinse clean with water and set aside
Clean pot and heat over high heat, add 60 ml peanut oil
When oil reaches 60°C, add a handful of Sichuan peppercorns (30 pieces)
Add duck and stir-fry for 4 minutes
After 2 minutes, add all spices
At 3 minutes, add all aromatics (ginger, garlic, bird's eye chili)
Add 1000 ml beer
Braise duck for 30 minutes
At 10 minutes, add salt (3 g), light soy sauce (10 ml), dark soy sauce (5 ml)
At 20 minutes, add all side ingredients (green and red pepper segments)
At 29 minutes, add garlic sprout segments and scallion segments
Stir-fry for 1 minute
Serve

howtocook:meat_dish/广式萝卜牛腩/广式萝卜牛腩.name: Cantonese Radish Beef Brisket
howtocook:meat_dish/广式萝卜牛腩/广式萝卜牛腩.description: This is a Cantonese home-style stew. The beef brisket is tender and flavorful, and the radish absorbs the broth, becoming sweet and rich. The overall taste is savory with a hint of sweetness. Rich in high-quality protein and dietary fiber, it appetizing and aids digestion. The steps are a bit many but moderate difficulty; even beginners can succeed by following the order. It takes about 2.5 hours from start to finish.
steps: Roll-cut the radish into chunks and set aside.
Blanch the whole brisket with 2 slices of ginger and a bunch of scallion knots. After the water boils, cook for 5-10 minutes, then remove and cut into pieces.
Cut the brisket into pieces of your preferred size (the brisket has been blanched, so it won't shrink much during stewing; larger pieces will take a bit longer to stew).

Prepare the sauce for stewing: mix the fermented red bean curd, hoisin sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, and salt according to the amounts above (I ran out of rock sugar so I used granulated sugar instead).

Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry ginger and garlic until fragrant. Add the brisket and stir-fry until dry. Add the prepared sauce and stir-fry until aromatic. If you prefer a darker color, you can add a bit of dark soy sauce to enhance the color.
After the seasonings are well mixed, pour in hot water.
Transfer the brisket to a soup pot, add cinnamon stick, star anise, bay leaves, and dried chili peppers. Stew for about 2 hours.
When the brisket is half-tender, add the white radish and continue stewing for 30 minutes.
Once the radish is tender, it's done. A pot of fragrant radish beef brisket is ready.

howtocook:meat_dish/徽派红烧肉/徽派红烧肉.name: Anhui-Style Braised Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/徽派红烧肉/徽派红烧肉.description: Anhui-Style Braised Pork Belly is a classic home-style dish with a bright red color, fatty but not greasy, with a soft, sticky, and chewy texture, blending salty and sweet flavors. Made with pork belly, it is rich in high-quality protein and fat, providing ample energy. Cooking requires mastering caramel coloring and heat control, which is somewhat challenging. Estimated cooking time: about 45 minutes.
steps: Cut pork belly into 2-3 cm cubes.
Add 150 ml cooking oil to the pot, add pork belly, fry for 2 minutes, then add salt, stir-fry for 2 minutes, then remove from pot.
Add 50 ml cooking oil to the pot, add white sugar, stir-fry until caramel colored.
Add pork belly, stir-fry for 30 seconds, add ginger slices and garlic cloves, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add cooking wine, five-spice powder, scallion, add water to cover pork belly, cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, cook over medium heat for 20 minutes.
Uncover, reduce sauce over high heat, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/意式烤鸡.name: Italian Roasted Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/意式烤鸡.description: Italian Roasted Chicken is a home-style oven dish with Mediterranean flavors. Chicken thighs are marinated with garlic and olive oil, then roasted until the skin is slightly charred and the inside is tender and juicy. Served with lemon juice and parsley, it tastes light and not greasy. Chicken is rich in high-quality protein, and olive oil provides unsaturated fatty acids, making it nutritionally balanced. The difficulty is moderate; you need to preheat the oven in advance. Beginners can follow the steps successfully. The total time from marinating to serving is about 50 minutes.
steps: Coat chicken thighs with salt, black pepper, olive oil and minced garlic.
Place in a preheated oven at 180°C and bake for 30-40 minutes or until cooked through.
Chop parsley into small pieces and set aside.
Squeeze lemon juice and set aside.
Remove the cooked chicken and drizzle with lemon juice.
Sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/新疆大盘鸡/新疆大盘鸡.name: Xinjiang Big Plate Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/新疆大盘鸡/新疆大盘鸡.description: Xinjiang Big Plate Chicken is a flavorful and colorful Xinjiang home-style dish. The chicken is tender and not dry, the potatoes are soft and absorb the sauce, with a spicy and numbing taste that carries a hint of sweetness and a pleasant aftertaste. It is rich in high-quality protein and carbohydrates, and with bell peppers it also provides vitamin C. The cooking process requires caramelizing sugar and controlling the sauce reduction, which may be a bit challenging for beginners, but it's not difficult if you follow the steps carefully. It takes about 1 hour in total.
steps: Chop the meat into chunks. Soak in water with salt for 5 minutes to remove blood and odor, then drain.
Wash the green onions, garlic, peppers, and potatoes. Peel the potatoes.
Cut the white part of the green onions into 4cm long sections. Cut the bell peppers and long peppers into chunks.
Cut the potatoes into rolling cuts (roll-cut), meaning cut and rotate the potato. Each piece should be about 4cm by 4cm.

Caramelize sugar: Add oil to the pot, then add white sugar. Stir constantly with a spatula until the sugar melts and turns a caramel color. Then add the drained chicken and stir-fry to coat with color.
Add Sichuan peppercorns, bay leaves, cardamom, and dried chili peppers. Stir-fry.
Add 5g salt, 7ml light soy sauce, 10g oyster sauce, 100g cooking wine (or beer), and 1 liter of water.
Turn to medium heat and bring to a boil. Then reduce to medium-low heat and simmer to infuse flavors.

When the liquid reduces until the chicken is almost exposed, place the potatoes on top of the chicken. Do not stir, just cover them on the surface to prevent sticking. Continue to simmer covered. After a while, add the green onions, bell peppers, and sweet peppers, and continue simmering.
When the sauce is nearly reduced, you can stir to let the potatoes absorb the sauce. Finally, turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/无骨鸡爪/无骨鸡爪.name: Boneless Chicken Feet
howtocook:meat_dish/无骨鸡爪/无骨鸡爪.description: Sour and spicy, appetizing, with a Q-elastic texture. It is a home-style cold dish. Chicken feet are rich in collagen, beneficial for the skin. The deboning process is tedious and labor-intensive, making it relatively difficult and not suitable for beginners. Total time about 8 hours 15 minutes.
steps: Use scissors/knife to cut off all the nails from the chicken feet, including the flesh and bone under the nail, leaving no nails at all
Wash them clean and set aside
Put chicken feet into a large pot, prepare to remove fishy smell
Add scallion, cooking wine, and ginger all in
Add water to cover the chicken feet
Boil over high heat; skim off any foam that rises
After water reaches 100°C and boils, wait 10 minutes
Turn off heat, take out, drain water, rinse clean, put into a bowl
Put in the freezer compartment of the fridge for 20 minutes
Put all into the refrigerator (not freezer), then take out 10 pieces at a time to debone
Starting from the tips of the chicken toes, use a knife to slit each toe all the way to the back of the foot
Then from the back of the foot, slit all the way down to the whole arm
Bend each toe joint until you hear a popping sound
Press the tip of each toe and push inward; same for every toe, first push to the middle palm and back part
After each toe's skin comes off, push from the palm toward the arm part until the whole bone comes off
Place in a bowl and set aside
Cut bird's eye chilies into uniform small pieces
Mince garlic, onion, and cilantro
Cut lemon in half and squeeze lemon juice into the container with chicken feet
Pour all seasonings into the container with chicken feet; also add bird's eye chilies, garlic, onion, and cilantro
Mix evenly by hand
Refrigerate overnight (6 hours)
After mixing, pour all over the prepared chicken feet

howtocook:meat_dish/杀猪菜.name: Pork and Sauerkraut Stew
howtocook:meat_dish/杀猪菜.description: This is a classic stew from Northeast China. It is sour, refreshing, savory, and warms the heart and stomach. The sauerkraut is rich in lactic acid bacteria and dietary fiber, the ribs provide high-quality protein and calcium, and the blood sausage supplies iron. Care is needed when handling the blood sausage to prevent it from bursting during cooking, which can be a challenge for beginners. From prep to serving, it takes about an hour.
steps: Use a toothpick to poke many small holes in the blood sausage. Then gently boil it in water over low heat for ten minutes. Do not let the water boil; keep it at 80°C, otherwise the blood sausage will easily burst.
Cut the cooked blood sausage into pieces and set aside.
Blanch the ribs with cooking wine, then drain and set aside.
Add rapeseed oil to the pot, then add garlic cloves, dried chili peppers, and ginger powder. Stir until fragrant.
Add the ribs and stir-fry until the surface turns golden brown.

Wash the sauerkraut and squeeze out excess water. Add it to the pot along with sesame oil and stir-fry over high heat for two minutes. The sesame oil helps remove the sourness and enhances the fragrance.
Add 600 ml of hot water.
Transfer to an electric pressure cooker. Add bay leaves, star anise, a scallion knot, and salt.
Pressure cook on the 'rich flavor' mode for 40 minutes.

After the time is up, release the pressure and open the lid. Add the blood sausage and goji berries, cover, and let it sit for two minutes. The blood sausage is already cooked, so no further heating is needed.
Pour into a bowl. Prepare the dipping sauce according to the table above, and enjoy.

howtocook:meat_dish/枝竹羊腩煲/枝竹羊腩煲.name: Braised Lamb Brisket with Bean Curd Sticks
howtocook:meat_dish/枝竹羊腩煲/枝竹羊腩煲.description: This is a traditional claypot dish from Guangdong. The lamb and bean curd sticks are harmonized with zhuhou sauce and fermented bean curd, resulting in a rich sauce flavor, luscious yet not greasy, warming both body and soul, especially suitable for cold winter. Lamb is rich in protein and iron, can warm and nourish qi and blood, while bean curd sticks provide high-quality plant protein, making a balanced combination. Although there are many preparation and stewing steps, the heat control throughout is not complicated; beginners can succeed by following the steps. It takes about two and a half hours from prep to serving.
steps: Prep: Soak shiitake mushrooms for 2-3 hours until soft. Soak bean curd sticks for 30 minutes until soft.
Prepare sauce 1: Mix fermented red bean curd, zhuhou sauce, 20g fermented bean curd, and dark soy sauce in a small bowl.
Prepare sauce 2: Mix 20g fermented bean curd, sugar, and chili oil in a small bowl.
Remove the stems from the soaked mushrooms.
Cut the soaked bean curd sticks into 5cm sections, squeeze out excess water.
Peel and slice the onion. You can also use peeled shallots for better flavor.
Cut scallions into about 5cm sections.
Put lamb brisket in a pot with cold water, add 2-3 slices of ginger, bring to a boil over high heat, then turn off.
Remove the lamb and place it in a basin of cold water to cool, making it more chewy.
Heat the pot, add cold oil, then stir-fry 4-5 slices of ginger, onion/shallots, white parts of scallions, and 7-8 garlic cloves until fragrant.
Add the cooled lamb, stir with chopsticks for about 2-5 minutes until golden brown.
Add prepared sauce 1, stir-fry for about 2 minutes until evenly colored.
Pour in enough water to just cover the ingredients.
Add mushrooms, rock sugar, bay leaves, star anise, and cinnamon.
Cover, reduce to low heat, and simmer for 90 minutes.
Uncover, add bean curd sticks, cover, and cook over medium heat for 20 minutes.
Uncover, add sauce 2, and stir well.
Turn off heat, add green parts of scallions or cilantro before serving.

howtocook:meat_dish/柱候牛腩/柱候牛腩.name: Chu Hou Beef Brisket
howtocook:meat_dish/柱候牛腩/柱候牛腩.description: This is a classic Cantonese-style dish. The meat is richly aromatic, bright red in color, and the tendon-in brisket is tender and flavorful with a rich texture. It is high in quality protein and collagen, nourishing and warming. Although it requires some patience, using a pressure cooker significantly shortens the time, making it beginner-friendly. Regular simmering takes about 3 hours, while a pressure cooker takes about 1 hour.
steps: First, prepare the aromatics and sauces:
Bowl 1: Dice ginger into 1.5cm x 1.5cm cubes, peel half a head of garlic, if using bird's eye chili, cut into 1cm sections
Bowl 2: Add 2 bay leaves, 0.5g Sichuan peppercorns, 2 star anise, 3 dried chilies, 3 cloves, 2 slices licorice
Bowl 3: Add 2 pieces fermented red bean curd
Bowl 4: Add 15g Pixian doubanjiang
Bowl 5: Add 10g rock sugar
Bowl 6: Add 10g Huadiao wine and 10g baijiu
Bowl 7: Add 50g Chu Hou paste, 20g oyster sauce, 20g char siu sauce, 5g dark soy sauce, 60g light soy sauce
Do not cut the beef. Place it directly into a pot of cold water, bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, remove the beef.
Rinse off any impurities from the beef, then cut into 4cm cubes. Drain and set aside in a bowl.
Over high heat, heat oil in a wok, add Bowl 1 (ginger, garlic, bird's eye chili) and stir-fry until fragrant.
Reduce to medium-low heat, add Bowl 2 (spices), stir-fry for about 30 seconds.
Over medium-low heat, add Bowl 3 (fermented red bean curd) and mash it with a spatula.
Over medium-low heat, add Bowl 4 (doubanjiang), stir-fry for about 30 seconds.
Over medium-low heat, add Bowl 5 (rock sugar), stir until melted.
Over medium-low heat, add the beef brisket, stir-fry until the beef is colored.
Increase to high heat, pour Bowl 6 (wine) along the edge of the wok, stir-fry quickly until the beef surface is slightly browned.
Add Bowl 7 (sauce mixture), stir-fry quickly, watch the bottom, cook until small bubbles appear and the sauce thickens slightly.
Transfer all contents to another pot or a pressure cooker. Add water to cover the ingredients.
Choose the cooking time based on the pot used:
a. Regular pot: Simmer on low heat for 3 hours.
b. Pressure cooker: Cook under pressure for 40 minutes.
After the time is up, open the lid and season. If not salty enough, add salt or light soy sauce (add a little at a time, taste until right). If not sweet enough, add sugar similarly.
Once seasoned, remove the beef brisket from the pot.

If adding radish and potato, peel and cut into 2cm thick slices, add to the pot and cook for 10-15 minutes (or until desired tenderness). If using a pressure cooker, cook under pressure for 5 minutes.
Once cooked, remove the radish and potato along with the beef brisket, place together.
Strain the sauce and pour over the meat and vegetables.

howtocook:meat_dish/桂林十八酿/桂林十八酿.name: Guilin Eighteen Stuffed Delicacies
howtocook:meat_dish/桂林十八酿/桂林十八酿.description: A traditional Guilin series of stuffed dishes. Seasoned pork, shrimp paste, and other fillings are stuffed into more than a dozen hollow ingredients such as green peppers, bitter melon, snails, and eggplants, then pan-fried and braised. Each dish has its unique flavor, savory and juicy, with a soft, tender, or chewy texture. It's a rich local home-style flavor. The fillings provide high-quality protein, paired with various vegetables, rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, making for a balanced nutrition. The steps require stuffing each ingredient one by one and controlling the heat, requiring a bit of patience, but the logic is simple, so even beginners can try. It takes about 1.5 hours total.
steps: Stuffed green peppers: Remove seeds and hollow out green peppers → stuff with pork filling or shrimp paste → pan-fry until tiger-skin color → braise in sauce
Stuffed snails: Blanch snails and remove meat → mince snail meat with pork → stuff back into shells → braise with perilla and mint
Pork filling: The most basic and common choice, with 30% fat and 70% lean for the best texture
Shrimp paste filling: Sweet and bouncy, often used in stuffed green peppers
Snail and pork mixed filling: The soul of stuffed snails, snail meat to pork ratio 1:1, add perilla and mint
Mixed filling: Pork and shrimp paste combined, offering both sweetness and richness

howtocook:meat_dish/梅菜扣肉/梅菜扣肉.name: Steamed Pork Belly with Preserved Mustard Greens
howtocook:meat_dish/梅菜扣肉/梅菜扣肉.description: This is a traditional Hakka dish. The finished dish has a glossy red-brown sauce, thick gravy, and the pork belly is fatty but not greasy, tender and fragrant. Pork belly is rich in high-quality protein and fatty acids, and paired with preserved mustard greens provides dietary fiber. The process is somewhat lengthy, requiring mastery of boiling, frying, and steaming times, which can be challenging for beginners. From preparation to serving, including the time to soak the preserved mustard greens, it takes about 2.5 hours.
steps: Soak the preserved mustard greens in clean water for 1 hour.
Pour 50 ml cooking oil into a wok. Place the whole pork belly skin-side down and cook for 1 minute. Remove and scrape off the skin [optional].
Add boiling water to a pot, put in the pork belly, and boil over high heat for 20 minutes (until a chopstick can pierce through). Remove the pork belly.
Evenly rub the pork belly with dark soy sauce, five-spice powder, and white sugar. Let it sit for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok, add the pork belly, and deep-fry over medium heat until both sides are golden brown (3-5 minutes).
Heat oil in a wok, add the preserved mustard greens, along with chopped bird's eye chilies, minced garlic, chicken bouillon, and table salt. Stir-fry until the greens are dry.
Slice the pork belly (0.5-1 cm thick), place in a large bowl, and scatter the preserved mustard greens on top.
Steam over medium heat for 45 minutes.
Invert a plate over the large bowl, then flip the bowl to transfer the pork belly onto the plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/椒盐排条.name: Salt and Pepper Pork Strips
howtocook:meat_dish/椒盐排条.description: Salt and Pepper Pork Strips is a classic home-style dish in Shanghai cuisine, crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with a savory and aromatic flavor. Pork chops provide high-quality protein and essential amino acids to replenish energy. Although this dish has several steps, it's not difficult to follow the process, and beginners can confidently give it a try. From prep to serving, it takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Wash the pork chops, debone, pound with the flat side of a knife to loosen, cut into thick slices, then into thick strips.
Add salt and pepper powder, mix well. When it becomes sticky, gradually add scallion-ginger water. Refrigerate for 20 minutes to marinate.
Prepare the batter. Mix 80 g flour, 20 g starch (note: 20 g starch, reserve the remaining 60 g), 2-3 g custard powder, and 1 g salt.
Beat in one egg, stir, then gradually add 100 g water, then add 10 g oil. Mix repeatedly until the batter is smooth and slightly thick.
Take the remaining 60 g starch, coat the pork strips with starch, then coat with batter.

Add oil to the pot to submerge the ingredients, heat to about 150°C - 160°C. Fry the pork strips until light golden, then remove. They may stick together when first added; do not disturb. Once set, use chopsticks to turn and separate.
When the oil temperature rises again to 150°C - 160°C, refry the strips until golden brown, then remove.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper powder, stir well, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/水煮牛肉/水煮牛肉.name: Sichuan Boiled Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/水煮牛肉/水煮牛肉.description: Sichuan Boiled Beef is a classic spicy and numbing Sichuan dish, with tender and smooth beef, bright red soup, and a hot, spicy, and fresh flavor. Beef is rich in high-quality protein and iron, and pairing it with bean sprouts adds dietary fiber. This dish is not too difficult for beginners, but pay attention to heat control and safety when pouring hot oil. The total preparation time is about 40 minutes.
steps: Wash and slice the beef.
Add 15g shredded ginger, 1 egg, 15g starch, 8g oyster sauce, and 10ml cooking wine. Mix well and marinate for 15 minutes.
Wash and chop the cilantro.
Pour oil into a pot, add broad bean paste, 5g shredded ginger, and sliced garlic.
Pour in boiling water and cook into a red broth.
Wash the bean sprouts and remove the tails. Blanch them in boiling water until cooked.
Spread the bean sprouts at the bottom of a bowl.
Add the beef slices one by one into the red broth. Once cooked, remove them.
Place the beef on top of the bean sprouts, and sprinkle with cilantro stems.
Sprinkle with cilantro leaves, chili powder, and chili rings.
Heat oil in another pan, then pour the hot oil over the dish. It's done.

howtocook:meat_dish/水煮肉片.name: 水煮肉片 (Boiled Sliced Pork in Chili Sauce)
howtocook:meat_dish/水煮肉片.description: 水煮肉片 is a classic Sichuan dish with spicy and numbing flavor, tender sliced meat, and crunchy vegetables. It's excellent with rice. Pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein, combined with bean sprouts and other vegetables for balanced nutrition. The preparation process is a bit elaborate, especially the marination of the meat and making the knife-cut chilies. Beginners may need about 1 to 2 hours.
steps: Cut the tenderloin into small pieces, then slice into 2mm thin slices (adjust thickness to preference). Place in a bowl, wash with water twice to remove blood and impurities, drain and squeeze dry.
Add 1.5g salt, 1g white pepper, 5g light soy sauce, 3g cooking wine to the bowl. Stir in one direction for 2 minutes to season.
In another bowl, mix one egg white with 7g potato starch, stir evenly in one direction, then pour into the meat.
Stir again in one direction until the meat surface forms a smooth coating, making the meat more tender.
Finally, add 30g vegetable oil and gently mix (to prevent the slices from sticking).
100g mung bean sprouts, 1 lettuce (cut into strips), 3 celery stalks cut into segments, 2 garlic sprouts crushed and cut into segments.
20g garlic minced, small piece of ginger minced, 20g pickled red chilies minced.

15g dried small chili peppers, 3g green Sichuan peppercorns. Heat oil in a wok, swirl to coat, pour out excess leaving 50g oil. Add dried chilies and peppercorns, stir-fry on low heat until fragrant and the color turns dark (be careful not to burn). Transfer to a cutting board and chop finely.
Heat the wok, add 100g vegetable oil to 60% heat. Add 2g green Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies for aroma. Add the vegetables, 1g salt, stir-fry until just cooked. Transfer to a bowl as a base.

Clean the wok, add 150g vegetable oil to 60% heat. Add the prepared ginger, garlic, and pickled chilies, stir until fragrant. Add 10g doubanjiang (red oil chili bean paste), stir-fry on low heat until the oil turns red.

Add 800ml water (adjust as needed). Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat. Season with 2.5g salt, 1.5g chicken powder, 1g sugar for freshness, 1g white pepper, 5g water starch slurry (adjust as needed) to thicken the soup slightly.

When the soup is boiling, turn to low heat and slide the marinated meat slices one by one into the soup. Then turn to medium heat to cook the meat slices. Gently push with a spatula to prevent sticking. When the soup boils again and the meat is cooked, remove the meat and place on top of the vegetables in the bowl. Pour the soup over (not exceeding the ingredients).
Evenly sprinkle the chopped dried chili mixture, minced garlic, and chopped scallions on top.
Clean the wok, add 200g rapeseed oil, heat to 70% heat (smoking). Then pour the hot oil evenly over the meat in the bowl (be careful of safety). Ready to serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/洋葱炒猪肉.name: Stir-fried Pork with Onion
howtocook:meat_dish/洋葱炒猪肉.description: This is a home-style stir-fry with a savory-sweet flavor and rich soy sauce aroma. Once the onion is stir-fried until soft, its taste becomes mild, pairing perfectly with tender pork slices to make a great rice dish. Pork provides high-quality protein and B vitamins, while onion offers dietary fiber and various antioxidants, stimulating appetite and aiding digestion. Simple to make, even beginners can master it, and it takes only about 15 minutes.
steps: Slice the onion, slice the pork, crush the garlic, and mix the above seasonings; set aside.
Pour 1 tablespoon of cooking oil into the wok (wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up), then add the pork.
Stir-fry until the pork changes color, then add the garlic and stir until fragrant, then remove and set aside.
In the same wok, add the onion and stir-fry for 3–4 minutes, then add the seasonings and stir well.
Add the reserved pork and stir-fry until the pork is cooked through.
Once the pork is cooked, stir-fry for another 1–2 minutes, then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/淄博烧烤/淄博烧烤.name: Zibo BBQ
howtocook:meat_dish/淄博烧烤/淄博烧烤.description: This is a specialty barbecue from Zibo, Shandong. The unique way of wrapping meat in small pancakes with scallions offers a rich blend of meaty, savory, and onion flavors with layered textures. Pork belly is rich in high-quality protein and fat, scallions provide dietary fiber, and the small pancakes quickly replenish energy. Cooking requires control of charcoal heat and flipping skills, which may be slightly challenging for beginners. The whole process takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Cut the pork belly into chunks no thicker than 2cm, and thread them onto skewers. (Pre-marinate the pork belly for better flavor if desired.)
Cut the scallions into 5cm sections and set aside.
Thin the sesame paste with 10ml warm water, then mix evenly with sweet bean sauce and set aside. (Can substitute with 'sesame salt'.)
Light the charcoal and wait until it is red-hot with no visible flame (about 15 minutes).
Place the pork skewers evenly on the grill. Grill until the bottom side is golden and starting to render fat, then flip.
Continue grilling for about 2-3 minutes until both sides are golden and the edges are slightly charred, then remove.
Take a small thin pancake, lay it flat, and spread about 3-4g of the mixed sauce on it.
Place the grilled meat pieces and 1-2 sections of scallion on top, then roll up and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/清蒸鳜鱼/清蒸鳜鱼.name: Steamed Mandarin Fish
howtocook:meat_dish/清蒸鳜鱼/清蒸鳜鱼.description: Steamed mandarin fish has tender flesh and a prominent natural sweetness. It is a homestyle freshwater fish dish that emphasizes original flavors. Mandarin fish is rich in high-quality protein and various trace elements, easy to digest and absorb. Be careful with the fins when handling, but the overall steps are not complicated; even beginners can manage with a little attention. From prep to serving, it takes about half an hour.
steps: Ingredient preparation:
Cut open the mandarin fish from the belly, remove gills and innards, scale it, and scrape off the slime from the skin with a knife (you can also ask the fishmonger to do this).
Score the fish on both sides, place ginger slices on top (optional: add a little lard). Put the fish on a plate with chopsticks underneath to allow even heat distribution.
Split the large scallion and remove the core, keeping only the outer two layers.
Split the spring onion and set aside.
Remove the seeds and inner flesh of the red chili pepper, set aside.
Stack the prepared large scallion, spring onion, and chili together, cut into thin strips, and soak in water for later use.
Cooking process:

Add water to the pot (enough volume). When the water is hot, place the fish in, cover, and steam over high heat for 8-10 minutes. The four-character tip for steaming fish: 'Steam high, heat sufficient'.
While steaming, heat oil in another wok until it smokes.
After steaming, discard the water from the plate, remove the ginger slices, top with the shredded scallions, and pour the hot oil over.
Then drizzle with light soy sauce or steamed fish soy sauce, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/湖南家常红烧肉/湖南家常红烧肉.name: Hunan Home-style Braised Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/湖南家常红烧肉/湖南家常红烧肉.description: Hunan home-style braised pork belly is a richly flavored home-style dish. After slow simmering, the pork belly becomes tender but not greasy, soft and glutinous, with a savory and slightly spicy taste that lingers with a hint of sweetness. Rich in high-quality protein and fat, it provides ample energy. The cooking requires some patience and is moderately challenging for beginners, but following the steps makes it easy to master. The entire process takes about 1 hour.
steps: Rinse the pork belly with skin on, place in a pot with cold water, add 2-3 slices of ginger to remove the gamey smell, bring to a boil, then remove and rinse under cold water to clean off the white foam.
Cut the pork belly into 1.5cm x 1.5cm cubes.
Heat oil in a wok, add rock sugar and stir over low heat until caramelized, then add the cubed pork belly and stir-fry over medium heat until color changes.

Add prepared ginger slices, star anise, cinnamon, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, cooking wine, dried millet peppers, and salt. Stir-fry over low heat for 1 minute, then add boiling water to just cover the meat.
Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to low heat and simmer for 30 minutes. During simmering, stir every 10 minutes to prevent sticking.
When the liquid has reduced to one-third, turn to medium heat to reduce the sauce until thickened. Then remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/湘祁米夫鸭/湘祁米夫鸭.name: Xiangqi Rice Flour Duck
howtocook:meat_dish/湘祁米夫鸭/湘祁米夫鸭.description: A traditional specialty from the Two Qi areas of Hunan. The duck is stir-fried and then coated with mixed rice flour and steamed. The texture is soft and sticky, savory and fragrant, with a long-lasting aftertaste. Duck meat is rich in high-quality protein and B vitamins, combined with rice flour to provide energy, balanced nutrition. The process requires stir-frying first then pressure steaming, which is slightly challenging, suitable for friends with some cooking experience. From preparation to serving takes about 1 hour; if using an old duck, extend the steaming time.
steps: Mix glutinous rice flour, rice flour, steamed meat powder, fine chili powder, 5 grams salt, and white pepper powder together evenly.
Have the friendly vendor chop the duck into steaming pieces, slice the ginger, peel the garlic cloves, and slice the pork belly.
Heat the wok with cold oil, stir-fry the pork belly to render fat, then add more cooking oil and heat, add the duck and stir-fry.
Stir-fry the duck until the skin turns brown and crispy, then add ginger, garlic, and salt, continue stir-frying until fragrant.
Turn to low heat, pour in the rice flour mixture and stir-fry, coating the duck evenly. Add boiling water little by little while stirring.
Stir-fry until the duck and rice flour feel moist, then transfer to a bowl. Place in a pressure cooker with water and steam for 20-25 minutes.
Before serving, sprinkle some chopped green onions on top. Enjoy!

howtocook:meat_dish/澳门湿版免治牛肉饭/澳门湿版免治牛肉饭.name: Macau Wet-Style Minced Beef Rice
howtocook:meat_dish/澳门湿版免治牛肉饭/澳门湿版免治牛肉饭.description: This is a classic Macau Portuguese-style fast food. The minced beef is tender and savory, wrapped in a rich sauce and runny egg yolk, then mixed with rice for an oily and enticing taste. The minced beef provides high-quality protein, paired with eggs and onions for balanced nutrition. The steps are simple and easy to follow, perfect for beginners, taking about 30 minutes from prep to plate.
steps: Put 200g minced beef into a bowl, add 1 tablespoon light soy sauce, half tablespoon sugar, and 2g black pepper, mix well, and let marinate for 10 minutes.
Dice half an onion into small pieces and set aside.

Prepare the sauce: In a bowl, combine 2 tablespoons light soy sauce, 2 tablespoons ketchup, 1 tablespoon cooking wine, 1 tablespoon starch, half tablespoon dark soy sauce, and 50ml water, stir well and set aside.
In another pan, make a poached egg or a sunny-side-up egg, set aside.
Heat the pan, add 10g butter. Once melted, add the diced onion and cook over low heat until fragrant and translucent.
Add the marinated minced beef and stir-fry with the onion until the beef changes color.
Pour in the prepared sauce, add 2g black pepper, and continue to stir-fry until the sauce thickens, then turn off the heat.
Serve the cooked minced beef over hot steamed rice, and top with the prepared poached or sunny-side-up egg.
(Optional) Garnish with 5g chopped green onion.

howtocook:meat_dish/烤鸡翅.name: Baked Chicken Wings
howtocook:meat_dish/烤鸡翅.description: Marinated chicken wings are baked at high heat, resulting in slightly charred skin and tender, juicy meat with the savory flavor of black pepper and soy sauce. Rich in high-quality protein and collagen. Moderate difficulty, even beginners can easily handle it. It takes about 1 hour from preparation to serving. You can also add honey or five-spice powder to enhance the flavor according to your preference.
steps: Place chicken wings in a bowl
Add salt, black pepper, soy sauce, and cooking wine. Marinate for 30-40 minutes.
Preheat oven to 200°C.
Arrange the marinated chicken wings evenly on a baking sheet.
Place the baking sheet on the middle rack and bake for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the baking sheet, flip the chicken wings, and bake for another 15-20 minutes until fully cooked.

howtocook:meat_dish/煎烤羊排/煎烤羊排.name: Pan-Fried Lamb Chops
howtocook:meat_dish/煎烤羊排/煎烤羊排.description: A dish with a crispy exterior and tender interior, rich in aroma. First braised with spices, then pan-fried to a golden crust. Rich but not greasy, perfect for family meals or entertaining guests. Lamb is rich in high-quality protein, iron, and zinc, helping to warm and nourish the body. The steps are a bit many, but follow the process and even beginners can succeed; using a pressure cooker takes about 1 hour total, a regular pot about 1.5 hours.
steps: Cut the lamb chops into pieces along the bone.
Add cold water to a wok, put all lamb chops in. After the water boils, continue blanching for 2-3 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Put the blanched lamb chops, cinnamon, Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, Angelica dahurica, bay leaves, and cooking wine into a pot (pressure cooker or regular pot).
Add drinking water to cover all ingredients. If using a pressure cooker, cook for 30 minutes after pressure is reached. If using a regular pot, simmer for 80 minutes.
Remove the cooked lamb chops, drain well, and place them into an oil-free and water-free wok or frying pan.
Pan-fry over medium-high heat for 6-7 minutes until the fat renders out and the surface turns golden and crispy.
Keep the rendered lamb fat in the pan, add chili powder, cumin seeds, BBQ seasoning, ground cumin, and white sesame seeds, and stir-fry until well combined.
Add sugar and salt (if not added during braising), and stir-fry evenly.
After stir-frying evenly, turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/牛排/牛排.name: Steak
howtocook:meat_dish/牛排/牛排.description: This is a classic Western-style pan-seared steak, with a crispy exterior enclosing a tender and juicy interior, paired with butter, garlic, and herbs for rich flavor. Rich in high-quality protein, fats, and minerals like iron and zinc. The cooking process is beginner-friendly, but mastering the heat still requires some practice. It takes 15-30 minutes in total.
steps: Take the steak out of the refrigerator to thaw.
If the steak is frozen, transfer it to the refrigerator the night before and bring it to room temperature half an hour before cooking.
Do not thaw the steak in water or by heating.
Wash the cherry tomatoes, baby potatoes, and mushrooms, then cut them in half and set aside.
Peel the garlic, keep 5-8 larger cloves, and flatten them with the side of a knife. Set aside.
Preparing the steak.

For ribeye steak (see How to Choose Different Cuts of Steak), use kitchen scissors to vertically cut into the connective tissue at the center of the ribeye, making 1-2 cuts. This is because the 'eye' of the ribeye (the connective tissue) contracts sharply at high heat, causing the steak to deform and heat unevenly.
For sirloin steak, if you dislike the fat along the edge, you can trim it off slowly with a knife.
Then, wrap the steak in paper towels and pat it dry until no obvious moisture remains when you lift the steak from the cutting board.
Turn the stove to high heat (see appendix for heat level descriptions), and add olive oil to the pan. Heat the pan for 15-30 seconds.

Season the steak. Sprinkle sea salt and black pepper evenly over the entire surface of the steak, rubbing and massaging to embed the salt and pepper into the meat. Note: The steak must be cooked immediately after seasoning; it is not recommended to season in advance, as the salt will draw out moisture, affecting cooking and losing flavor.
Heat the oil to 60-80% hot (see oil temperature tips), then slowly place the steak into the pan, starting from the near side and moving away.
If you are using potatoes as a side dish, place them in a bowl and microwave for 10 minutes, or boil in water for 3-5 minutes.
Sear one side for 1.5 minutes (this timing is for a steak about 2-3 cm thick. If your steak is thinner, reduce to 1 minute. If thicker, increase to 2-2.5 minutes.)
Flip and sear the other side for 1.5 minutes.
For sirloin and filet mignon, use chopsticks or a spatula to stand the steak on its side and sear the edges for about 30 seconds.
Reduce the heat to medium, then quickly add butter, garlic, and thyme in order. Use a spatula to push the butter around the pan until fully melted. Place the garlic under the steak to enhance flavor.
Tilt the pan so the oil pools to one side.

Place the herbs and garlic on top of the steak, and use a spoon to continuously baste the steak with the oil from the pan. Do this for 30 seconds, then flip the steak and repeat for another 30 seconds.
You can gauge the heat by observing the oil when basting. If the oil bubbles on the steak and doesn't turn dark brown, the temperature is right.
If your steak wasn't properly dried or was thawed incorrectly, or if you seasoned it in advance, a lot of water will come out in the pan, resulting in the steak being 'boiled' and losing flavor.
If large oil bubbles appear on the steak surface and the oil turns dark brown, the oil is too hot. Remove the pan from the stove for a moment before continuing to baste.
Remove the steak to a cutting board, wrap it in aluminum foil, and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This process (called resting) allows the steak's own heat to continue cooking and lock in moisture.
Add the side dishes (potatoes, tomatoes, mushrooms) to the pan and sauté over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Take out the steak and cut it into strips about 1.5 cm wide. You can now observe the doneness of the beef.
Remove the side dishes, plate them, and drizzle with the remaining oil from the pan or steak sauce (optional).

howtocook:meat_dish/猪皮冻/猪皮冻.name: Pork Skin Jelly
howtocook:meat_dish/猪皮冻/猪皮冻.description: Pork skin jelly is a traditional cold dish commonly seen in northern Chinese New Year's Eve dinner. It is crystal clear, smooth and chewy, perfect for drinking. Pork skin is rich in collagen and has beautifying effects. This dish is a bit difficult; it requires patience to remove the gamey smell and long simmering. Estimated preparation time is about 24 hours.
steps: Cut the pork skin into pieces no larger than 10 cm. Soak in clean water for 12 hours. Then put in cold water, add 10g ginger and 50ml cooking wine, blanch for 5-10 minutes. Remove and put into cold water.
Place the blanched pork skin on a cutting board. Remove all the white fat layer, then cut into strips no thicker than 3 mm. Put in a bowl.
Add 20g white vinegar and 5g salt. Rub vigorously for 3 minutes, then rinse with clean water. The pork skin should now have almost no gamey smell.

Add 4kg water to the pot. Put in the pork skin, 10g scallion, 10g ginger slices, 10g star anise, and 5g Sichuan pepper. Bring to a boil over high heat, then simmer over low heat for 90 minutes until the pork skin is soft and tender.

Add 8g salt, 10g MSG, 15g chicken bouillon, 50ml light soy sauce, and 20ml dark soy sauce to season. Pour into a plate. Remove the scallion, ginger, and star anise. Let cool until it becomes jelly-like.
Refrigerate. When serving, cut into small pieces or thick slices.

howtocook:meat_dish/猪肉烩酸菜.name: Pork Braised with Sauerkraut
howtocook:meat_dish/猪肉烩酸菜.description: Pork braised with sauerkraut is a Northern Chinese home-style stew, with rich sour aroma, tender pork belly, and flavorful ribs. It mainly provides protein, and the sauerkraut also brings beneficial components like lactic acid bacteria. The steps are clear and easy for beginners to follow, but requires patient slow cooking, taking about 3 hours from start to finish.
steps: Cut the scallions into sections; cut 50g of ginger into sections and 50g into minced ginger; mince the garlic; set aside.
Shred all the sauerkraut, rinse with water 2 to 3 times, and set aside.

Put the ribs and pork belly into a pot, pour in cold water to submerge. Add all the scallion sections, 50g ginger sections, and 20ml cooking wine. Bring to a boil over high heat, then wait 5 minutes. Turn off the heat, remove the ribs and pork belly, rinse with cold water to remove scum, and set aside.
Slice or chunk the cooked pork belly, and set aside.
Wash the previous pot clean and dry it (otherwise the oil will splatter when added).

Add oil to the pot over medium heat, sauté the minced ginger and garlic until fragrant, then add the pork belly and ribs. Fry until golden, then add 10g five-spice powder and 15g light soy sauce, stirring with a spatula for 1-2 minutes.
Add the rinsed sauerkraut shreds to the pot and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
