Pour in purified water until the ingredients are just submerged, add 2 star anise, turn to high heat until the water boils. Then reduce to medium heat, cover, and simmer.
Wait 1.5 to 2 hours until the pork belly is tender (can be easily pierced with chopsticks).
Remove the lid, turn to high heat to reduce the sauce, stirring the ingredients until only a thin layer of liquid covers the bottom, then turn to low heat, ready to season.
Seasoning: add 10g of salt, stir well.
Turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/甜辣烤全翅.name: Sweet and Spicy Roasted Whole Chicken Wings
howtocook:meat_dish/甜辣烤全翅.description: These sweet and spicy roasted whole chicken wings are cooked in an air fryer, with a slightly crispy exterior and juicy interior. The sweet and spicy flavor is very popular. Chicken is rich in high-quality protein, and air frying instead of deep frying greatly reduces oil, making it lighter to eat. The process is clear and straightforward—just marinate in advance and flip once halfway. It's friendly for beginners. From preparation to serving takes about 3 hours, mostly waiting time for marinating, so it's not complicated.
steps: Take 4 fresh whole chicken wings and cut the skin between the two bones of the wingette with a knife, one cut on each side.

Place the 4 whole chicken wings in a bowl. Add 45 ml light soy sauce, 15 ml dark soy sauce, 10 g garlic powder, 5 g white pepper powder, 10 g sugar, 10 g sweet paprika, 5 g chili powder, 15 ml oyster sauce, 20 ml water, and 10 ml oil.
Use a spoon to evenly spread the sauce over the chicken wings, especially into the cuts on the wingettes, for about 3 minutes.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap to prevent splattering while marinating, and place it in the refrigerator for 120 minutes.
Remove the chicken wings. Place 4 chicken wings in a foil tray and pour the remaining sauce from the bowl evenly over them.
Place the foil tray on the air fryer basket. Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Open the air fryer, carefully remove the foil tray, and flip the chicken wings.
Continue roasting at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Remove and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/田螺酿/田螺酿.name: Stuffed River Snails
howtocook:meat_dish/田螺酿/田螺酿.description: Stuffed river snails are the top of Yangshuo's 'Eighteen Stuffed Dishes'. The snail meat is chewy and firm, the pork is tender and smooth, and the aroma of perilla and mint is refreshing. It was featured on 'A Bite of China 2'. Rich in protein and trace elements, the combination of snail and pork provides complementary nutrition. This dish has many steps and is a challenge for beginners. Snails need to be purged for half a day in advance. Actual cooking takes about 40 minutes. Suitable for family gatherings on holidays.
steps: Buy snails in the morning, put them in a basin of water, and let them sit for the afternoon to purge sand.
You can add a few drops of sesame oil (or wasabi~) to the water to speed up purging.
Scrub the snail shells clean with a brush (this step can take some time).
Use pliers or a cleaver to cut off the tail of the snail (about 1/3 to half).
Bring a large pot of water to a boil, blanch the snails for 1-2 minutes (do not overdo it, or the meat will become tough).
Remove and use a toothpick to pick out the snail meat, discard the operculum and the black mud intestine.
Rinse the snail shells clean and shake off water; set aside.
Wash the snail meat with salt, then chop into small pieces (do not mash; keep a grainy texture for chewiness).
Mince the pork.
Finely chop mint leaves and perilla leaves, mince ginger, and slice scallion into rings.
Put the chopped snail meat, minced pork, chopped mint and perilla, minced ginger, and scallion in a bowl.
Add 15ml Sanhua liquor, 15ml light soy sauce, 3g salt.
Optional: add 10ml oyster sauce, 2g white pepper powder, 10g starch.
Stir in one direction until the filling is sticky and firm.
For convenience, you can put all the above ingredients directly into a meat grinder and grind.
Use chopsticks to stuff the filling into the snail shells, filling them as tightly as possible without breaking the shell.
Repeat until all snails are stuffed.
Heat the wok, pour in 30ml cooking oil. Optionally add pickled bamboo shoots and dried chili peppers and stir-fry until fragrant.
Pour in about 300ml water or broth, add 15ml light soy sauce and 2g salt. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Put the stuffed snails into the pot, cover, and simmer over medium-low heat for 10 minutes.
Do not stir frequently during simmering; wait until the filling is about 60-70% cooked and set before gently stirring.
Uncover, add the remaining fresh mint and perilla leaves, and simmer for another 2-3 minutes.
Increase heat to reduce the sauce until thickened, then remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/番茄红酱.name: Tomato Red Sauce
howtocook:meat_dish/番茄红酱.description: This Italian-style sauce is rich and delicious, with a combination of vegetables and beef that provides a rich texture. It is high in protein and various vitamins, nutritionally balanced. The cooking process is a bit involved and requires some basic cooking skills, such as browning the beef until granular and controlling the heat for slow simmering. From prep to finish, it takes about an hour. You can make a larger batch and freeze it for later use, which is very convenient.
steps: Finely chop the carrots, celery, and onion; slice the garlic cloves.
Add 10ml olive oil, heat the oil and add the vegetables. Stir-fry over high heat until they start to change color, then remove from the pan.
Add another 10ml oil to the pan, add garlic and stir-fry for 10 seconds. Add the ground beef, sugar, salt, pepper, and spices. Fry until the beef is crispy (with a granular texture).
Add the cooked vegetables and tomato paste, continue to stir-fry, and mix well.
Slowly pour in the milk in batches, and cook over medium-low heat for 30 minutes. Done.

howtocook:meat_dish/瘦肉土豆片/瘦肉土豆片.name: Lean Pork and Potato Slices
howtocook:meat_dish/瘦肉土豆片/瘦肉土豆片.description: A quick home-style stir-fry, with tender pork slices and soft yet chewy potato slices infused with the fragrance of garlic sprouts, typical of home cooking. Lean pork provides high-quality protein and iron, while potatoes supply carbohydrates and dietary fiber, making a balanced dish. The process is simple, requiring attention to knife skills and heat control, suitable for beginners with some experience. From preparation to serving, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Peel the potatoes, cut in half, then slice into about 2mm thin pieces; set aside.
Wash the garlic sprouts and cut into about 1cm sections; set aside.
Wash the lean pork and slice into about 2mm thin pieces. Place in a bowl, add 5g cornstarch, 5g light soy sauce, and 3g dark soy sauce. Marinate for ten minutes; set aside.
While the pork is marinating, bring a pot of water to a boil. Add the potato slices and blanch for about 5 minutes.
Heat the wok and add 10ml-15ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add the marinated pork, stir-fry until it changes color. Then add the garlic sprouts and stir-fry together for about 20 seconds.
Add the potato slices, keep stir-frying. Add 2g salt and 5g light soy sauce.
Stir-fry for about 3 minutes, then plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/白菜猪肉炖粉条.name: Cabbage and Pork Braised with Vermicelli
howtocook:meat_dish/白菜猪肉炖粉条.description: Pork, cabbage and vermicelli braised is a classic home-style stew from Northeast China. The broth is rich and savory, the pork belly is tender, the vermicelli absorbs the meat flavor and becomes smooth and chewy, and the cabbage is sweet and refreshing. The dish is balanced with meat and vegetables, providing protein, vitamins and carbohydrates, nutritious and filling. The cooking process requires some patience, especially handling the vermicelli and controlling the heat, which may be challenging for beginners, but following the steps makes it hard to fail. From start to finish, it takes about 45 minutes to serve.
steps: Bring water to a boil in a pot. Add dried vermicelli and cook for 5 minutes. Pour out together with water into a container, cover and continue to soak. Set aside. (Do this first; proceed with the following steps during soaking)
Slice pork belly into 3mm thick pieces. Set aside.
Separate cabbage leaves from the stalks. Cut into two portions of slices. Set aside.
Heat the wok, add 10-15ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add pork belly and stir-fry until the meat changes color.
Add dark soy sauce and stir-fry for 1 minute to color the meat.
Add cabbage stalks, salt, and light soy sauce. Stir-fry for 1 minute (if sticking, add 10ml water).
Add water to submerge all ingredients. Add chicken bouillon and thirteen-spice. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Drain the vermicelli, cut into short sections, and place in a bowl. Set aside.
Add cabbage leaves, stir well. Place vermicelli on top of the vegetables, cover and cook for another 5 minutes.
Taste, turn off heat, and reduce sauce.

howtocook:meat_dish/空气炸锅脆皮现腌炸鸡/空气炸锅脆皮现腌炸.name: Air Fryer Crispy Marinated Fried Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/空气炸锅脆皮现腌炸鸡/空气炸锅脆皮现腌炸.description: Air fryer fried chicken is a home-cooked meat dish that is first marinated and then fried. The crust is crispy and the inside is juicy. The seasoning can be adjusted according to taste. Chicken wings are rich in high-quality protein and fat, making them suitable as a main dish or snack. Actual cooking time is about 55-70 minutes (including one coating; if choosing double coating, about 70-90 minutes). For best results, you can refrigerate and marinate overnight (prepare the day before and fry the next day).
steps: After washing the chicken wings, pat them dry. Make 2 cuts on each side of each wing to allow flavor to penetrate.
Prepare marinade: mix 15ml light soy sauce, 8g garlic powder, 5g paprika, 3g onion powder, 3g black pepper, and 5ml milk or Greek yogurt (optional, for tenderness). Stir well.
Place the marinade and chicken wings in a large zip-lock bag, massage thoroughly, and let sit in the refrigerator overnight.
Coating powder: mix 60g all-purpose flour, 30g starch, 5g garlic powder, 5g paprika, 2g onion powder, 2g black pepper, and 1g baking powder (optional, for extra crispiness). Stir well.
Batter (optional): beat 1 egg with 5ml milk or water until well combined.
Coating: shake off excess moisture from the chicken wings, place them in the coating powder, press gently, shake off excess powder, and let rest for 10 minutes.
Batter (optional): For a thicker coating, dip in batter, then repeat the coating step.
Dip the rested coated chicken wings into the batter, then gently shake off excess.
Place back into the coating powder, press gently, and shake off excess.
Let rest for 20 minutes (longer if possible, for a more stable crust).
Preheat the air fryer to 180°C for 5 minutes.
Brush the basket with a thin layer of oil. Place the chicken wings skin-side down, and spray or brush a thin layer of oil on top.
Set air fryer to 180°C and cook for 10 minutes.
Open the basket, flip the wings, spray or brush with more oil, then cook at 180°C for another 6 minutes.
Check the color of the skin and poke the thickest part with a toothpick to see if any blood comes out.
If needed, cook at 200°C for 1-2 minutes to crisp and brown (you can do this in increments, cooking 1 minute at a time and checking).

howtocook:meat_dish/粉蒸肉.name: Steamed Pork with Rice Flour
howtocook:meat_dish/粉蒸肉.description: Steamed Pork with Rice Flour is a classic Chinese steamed dish, rich in aroma and soft in texture, widely popular as home-style cuisine. Pork belly provides high-quality protein and fat, paired with root vegetables like potatoes for balanced nutrition. This dish is suitable for those with some cooking experience, taking about 90 minutes from preparation to completion.
steps: Wash the pork belly and cut into slices about 5cm long, 3cm wide, and 0.5cm thick.
Finely mince ginger and garlic into particles no larger than 1mm in diameter.

In a large bowl, combine the sliced pork belly, 15ml light soy sauce, 10ml dark soy sauce, 15ml cooking wine, 10g Pixian doubanjiang (broad bean paste), 10g minced ginger, 10g minced garlic, and 5g white sugar.
Stir well with chopsticks, cover with plastic wrap, and marinate at room temperature (20°C-25°C) for 30 minutes.
After marinating, add 100g steamed pork rice flour and continue stirring for 2 minutes until each slice is evenly coated.
Peel potato and slice into pieces about 0.8cm thick, each piece roughly 5cm x 5cm, total weight 300g.
Line the bottom of a 20cm diameter deep bowl with potato slices, minimizing overlap.
Evenly arrange the coated pork slices on top of the potatoes, pressing down firmly.
Add 2000ml water to a steamer, heat until it boils steadily (100°C).
Place the bowl into the steamer, cover with lid.
Steam over medium heat for 60 minutes (keep water boiling steadily, about 600W heat power).
After time, insert a chopstick into the center of a meat piece; if it goes through easily without resistance, it's done.
If not, continue steaming for another 10-15 minutes until meat is tender and fat has rendered.
Remove from steamer, plate, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/糖醋排骨/糖醋排骨.name: Sweet and Sour Pork Ribs
howtocook:meat_dish/糖醋排骨/糖醋排骨.description: Sweet and Sour Pork Ribs is a classic dish known for its tangy and sweet flavor, crispy exterior, and tender interior. It is widely loved. Pork ribs are rich in high-quality protein and calcium, providing good nourishment. The dish involves multiple steps such as blanching, deep-frying, caramelizing sugar, and reducing sauce, requiring some skill in heat control and seasoning. It may not be for beginners, but following this optimized process ensures a high success rate. Estimated cooking time: about 45 minutes.
steps: Put the ribs and ginger slices in cold water. Heat over high heat until the water boils and a lot of foam appears, then reduce to medium heat. When the water is continuously boiling, reduce to low heat and blanch for 2–3 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Rinse the ribs repeatedly with boiling water 2-3 times to thoroughly remove blood foam.

Pour enough cooking oil into the pot for deep-frying (amount depends on pot size; for a typical household, about 300 ml is recommended). Heat the oil to about 170°C, then add the ribs and fry for 3–5 minutes until the surface is lightly golden. Remove and drain oil.

Take another clean pot, heat 50 ml hot water over low heat, add 30 g white sugar, and gently stir until the sugar is completely dissolved and turns slightly pale yellow. The key is to observe the sugar dissolving; do not rely too much on color change.

Pour the fried ribs into the pot with the sugar syrup. Stir-fry quickly for 30 seconds, then add in order: 5 ml rice vinegar, 5 ml light soy sauce, 5 ml oyster sauce, 2 g chicken bouillon, 10 g ketchup, 0.5 g five-spice powder. Stir-fry for another 30 seconds to evenly coat the ribs. Then add boiling water just enough to cover the ribs.

Bring the liquid in the pot to a boil over high heat, then add 5 ml dark soy sauce for color and quickly reduce the sauce. If the ribs are large, you can turn to low heat and simmer for 5–10 minutes to allow better flavor absorption. Do not use medium heat for a long simmer of 20 minutes, as it will harm the texture.
Transfer to a plate, sprinkle with 2 g sesame seeds, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/糖醋里脊.name: Sweet and Sour Pork Tenderloin
howtocook:meat_dish/糖醋里脊.description: Sweet and sour pork tenderloin with a rich sweet and tangy flavor, crispy outside and tender inside, is a beloved home-style dish common in many regional cuisines. Pork tenderloin provides high-quality protein, but the coating and frying plus the sugar-vinegar sauce make it relatively high in calories. The key is mastering marination, double-frying, and sauce preparation—a bit challenging for beginners. It's recommended to prep all ingredients in advance and cook continuously. From prep to serving, it takes about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
steps: Marinate the meat: First slice the pork tenderloin into thick pieces, then use the back of a knife to pound it a bit to loosen the meat. Cut into finger-thick strips. Add cooking wine, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, salt, white pepper powder, and one egg. Mix well with your hands and marinate for at least 20 minutes.
Prepare the sauce: Mix ketchup, 10g vinegar, 30g white sugar, and 150ml water. Stir until the sugar dissolves. Set aside.
Coat with starch: It's better to coat all the strips first before frying, so you won't be in a rush. Prepare a large bowl with cornstarch, and coat each meat strip thoroughly with cornstarch.
Frying: Heat oil to 160°C and add the pork strips. You can test with a dry chopstick – if small bubbles form around it, the oil is ready. Fry over medium heat.

Fry until the surface turns light yellow, then remove. Maintain medium heat throughout. Then, heat the oil to 200°C and deep-fry the strips again for only 40 seconds until the skin becomes crispy. Remove immediately.
Coat with sauce: In a separate pan, add a little oil, pour in the prepared sauce, and bring to a boil. Add the fried pork strips and stir-fry until each piece is evenly coated with the sauce.
Put the fried pork strips in and stir-fry. Turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/红烧猪蹄/红烧猪蹄.name: Braised Pork Trotters
howtocook:meat_dish/红烧猪蹄/红烧猪蹄.description: Braised pork trotters is a classic home-cooked dish with rich sauce and tender, glutinous meat. It is savory, sweet, and extremely appetizing. Pork trotters are rich in collagen and protein, making them nutritious. This dish is somewhat challenging for beginners, but using a pressure cooker simplifies the process; it's easier for those with some cooking experience. From preparation to completion takes about 1 hour.
steps: Put the chopped pork trotters (kindly done by the vendor) into a pot of cold water, add 20 ml cooking wine and scallion and ginger, boil for 15 minutes to remove the bloodiness.
Heat the wok with cold oil, pour in 30 ml cooking oil, add 7-8 rock sugar pieces, cook on low heat until caramelized, using a spatula to crush the rock sugar, about 2 minutes.
Once caramelized, add the blanched pork trotters, continue on low heat, stir-fry until all trotters are lightly browned on both sides.
Add 2 bay leaves, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 star anise, 20 ml light soy sauce, 20 ml dark soy sauce, 10 ml cooking wine, 3 slices ginger, 8 g salt, turn to medium heat, continue stir-frying for 1 minute.
Add boiling water or cold water, enough to cover the trotters, cover the lid, bring to a boil over high heat, then turn off the heat.
Pour all the contents from the wok into a pressure cooker, pressure cook for 15 minutes (if you don't have a pressure cooker, you can simmer in the wok over low heat after boiling).
After 15 minutes, pour the pressure cooker contents back into the wok, turn to high heat to reduce the sauce. At this point, you can taste with chopsticks; if bland, add 2-3 g salt.
The time for reducing sauce over high heat depends on the amount of liquid; usually 30 seconds is enough. It's fine to leave a bit more sauce; the braised trotter sauce is also great with rice.

howtocook:meat_dish/红烧肉/南派红烧肉.name: Southern-Style Red-Braised Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/红烧肉/南派红烧肉.description: This red-braised pork belly dish has a bright red color, melt-in-your-mouth fat, tender and not dry lean meat, with a savory-sweet taste—a classic southern sweet flavor. Pork belly provides high-quality protein and rich collagen, while spices help remove any gamey taste and warm the stomach. Although it requires caramelizing sugar and slow simmering, each step is broken down clearly and easy to follow, so even beginners can succeed. It takes about 1.5 hours from start to table.
steps: Do not cut the pork belly into small pieces yet; cut into large chunks that can fit in the pot (about 5–6 cm wide).
Slice ginger (each slice about 3 mm thick).
Bring water to a boil.
Use tap water for cold water.
Cut the white parts of scallions into small sections (preferably scallions, but green onions are also fine).
Cut garlic in half lengthwise, do not smash, otherwise it will be hard to remove and affect the flavor during final reduction.
Divide the aromatics into two 'prep packs':
Pack A (for removing odor): 2 slices ginger, 2 scallion sections.
Pack B (for simmering): remaining 4 slices ginger, scallion whites, garlic cloves, Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, bay leaves, all placed in a small bowl for later use.

Place the large pork belly chunks in a pot with cold water, add 20 ml cooking wine and Pack A (2 slices ginger, 2 scallion sections), bring to a boil and cook for 15 minutes to remove bloodiness. Remove and rinse clean.

Remove the blanched pork belly, let cool slightly (until not too hot to handle). Cut into cubes of about 3 cm x 3 cm x 3 cm (after blanching, the meat tightens, making it easier to cut than raw meat, and the cubes are more uniform. The final size after cooking will be about 2.5 cm, ideal for eating and cooking through).

In a separate pot, caramelize sugar. Note: In this recipe, use 'Method 2' mentioned: when the sugar syrup forms dense small bubbles, quickly add Pack B (ginger, scallion whites, garlic, Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, bay leaves) to the pot and stir-fry until fragrant.

After the aromatics become fragrant (within 5-10 seconds), do not add boiling water yet. Immediately add the blanched pork belly and stir-fry over high heat to evenly coat the meat with caramelized sugar and render fat. When you smell the meat fragrance, pour in boiling water until the meat is fully submerged, cover the pot, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer for 50-60 minutes (40 minutes if using a pressure cooker).
Add 10 ml cooking wine.
After covering and bringing to a boil, every 25 minutes open the lid and skim off any oil and foam floating on the surface.
When the liquid level reduces to three-fifths of the height of the meat and the pot bottom, turn to medium heat, remove all aromatics except the meat and liquid, and start reducing the sauce.
Open the lid, and when the sauce is nearly gone and thick, remove from heat (be careful not to dry it out completely).

howtocook:meat_dish/红烧肉/简易红烧肉.name: Easy Braised Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/红烧肉/简易红烧肉.description: This home-style dish has a glossy red-brown color, tender and soft texture, rich sauce without being greasy, and a deep soy flavor. It's especially satisfying with rice. Pork belly provides high-quality protein and essential fatty acids, and adding quail eggs or tofu skin makes it more nutritious. The method is quite beginner-friendly, taking about 1.5 hours from prep to serving.
steps: Cut pork belly into large chunks (about 4.5 cm; freezing for half an hour to an hour makes cutting easier)
Cut tofu skin into 2 cm wide strips
Slice ginger (about 3 mm thick per slice)
Bring water to a boil
Boil quail eggs and poke holes with a fork or toothpick (as many as possible for better flavor)
Scallion, white part only
Place the cut pork belly in a pot of cold water, add cooking wine, scallion, and ginger, boil for 15 minutes to remove bloodiness
Put two slices of ginger in the pot for flavor
Over medium-low heat, directly add the pork belly without oil. Sear all six sides of each piece until oil renders.
Pour out the rendered oil and set aside. Push the pork belly to one side, add 15g rock sugar, and stir-fry until the sugar melts.
Once melted, stir-fry the pork belly with the sugar until evenly coated, then add
10ml light soy sauce
15ml dark soy sauce
5ml cooking wine
Stir-fry until well colored.
Add boiling water and braise for 40 minutes (if your knife skills are poor and chunks are too large, extend the time accordingly). Then add
2 slices ginger
3 bay leaves
2 star anise
Cover and bring to a boil, then add the boiled and poked quail eggs and tofu skin. Reduce to medium-low heat and simmer for 40 minutes (stir occasionally to prevent sticking).
Uncover the pot, and when the sauce is almost gone, increase heat to reduce the sauce (do not reduce completely).
Add 2-3g salt, stir once, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/红烧鸡翅.name: Braised Chicken Wings
howtocook:meat_dish/红烧鸡翅.description: Braised chicken wings is a homestyle dish that goes well with rice. It is savory with a hint of sweetness, glossy red in color, and the meat is tender and falls off the bone easily. Chicken wings are rich in high-quality protein and collagen, making them nutritious and easy to absorb. The difficulty is moderate; beginners can follow the steps and master it. From prep to serving, it takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Wash the chicken wings. Use a knife to make three slits on the thick part of each wing and one slit on the back to help the flavor penetrate.
If the chicken wings are not fresh, soak them in ginger water for 10-20 minutes after scoring.

Prepare 10g ginger slices (3-4 slices), 15g scallion sections (2-3 sections); smash 3-4 garlic cloves (about 15g) with the flat side of a knife (do not mince); set aside 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, and 2 dried chili peppers (optional).

Heat a pot and add enough cold water to submerge all the chicken wings. Place the chicken wings in the pot, add 20ml huangjiu or cooking wine, and 2 slices of ginger (about 5g). Bring to a boil over high heat, then continue boiling for 2-3 minutes, skimming off any foam.
After boiling, remove the chicken wings and rinse them twice with warm water to completely remove any blood foam. Drain well.
Heat another pot over high heat and add 10-15ml cooking oil.

When the oil reaches about 50% hot, add 15g rock sugar. Cook over high heat until the sugar melts completely. When the sugar melts and changes color, turn to low heat and continue stirring until the sugar turns date-red. Immediately add the drained chicken wings and stir-fry quickly for 1-2 minutes, coating each wing evenly with the caramel.

Add the prepared 1 bay leaf, 1 star anise, 2 dried chili peppers (optional), the remaining 2 ginger slices (about 5g), 15g scallion sections, and smashed garlic. Continue stir-frying for 30 seconds until the aromatics are fully fragrant.
Pour 15ml cooking wine or huangjiu along the edge of the pan, then 15ml light soy sauce. Stir-fry quickly for 10 seconds.
Add warm water (about 50-60°C) until it just barely covers the chicken wings.
Add 5ml dark soy sauce, 3g salt, 2g white sugar, and 1g white pepper. Gently stir to combine.
Cover the pot and cook over medium heat for 7-8 minutes.
Remove the lid. Use chopsticks to pick out and discard the bay leaf, star anise, ginger slices, scallion sections, and garlic from the pot.
Turn to high heat and keep stirring constantly until the sauce thickens (about 1-2 minutes).
Turn off the heat. Transfer the chicken wings to a plate and pour the remaining thickened sauce over them.
Finally, sprinkle 3g white sesame seeds and 5g chopped scallions on top. Done.

howtocook:meat_dish/羊排焖面/羊排焖面.name: Lamb Rib Braised Noodles
howtocook:meat_dish/羊排焖面/羊排焖面.description: Lamb rib braised noodles is a hearty Northern home-style dish with rich meat flavor and chewy noodles. The lamb ribs are tender, and the wide noodles absorb the savory broth, making every bite satisfying. This dish combines meat, noodles, and vegetables, providing a complete meal with protein, carbs, and fats. It requires braising the lamb first, then preparing the dough and braising the noodles - a bit more steps, suitable for experienced cooks. Estimated time: about 1.5 hours.
steps: Place lamb in cold water and blanch. When water boils, skim off the foam, then remove the lamb.
Slice ginger (4 slices). Set aside dried chilies and Sichuan peppercorns in a bowl.
Add oil to a wok. (A bit more is fine.)
Once oil is hot, add sugar and stir-fry lamb until caramelized.
After the lamb's moisture is evaporated, add salt, dark soy sauce, and the prepared seasonings.
Add enough water to cover lamb. Bring to a boil over high heat, let it boil for 10 minutes, then simmer on low heat for 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the dough. The amount and method are explained in the additional notes (Note 1).
Add green pepper, bell pepper, scallions, and the noodle sheets; stir-fry.
After stirring evenly, remove from heat and serve.

ingredients.name: Cooking oil
ingredients.note: to taste, a bit more is fine

howtocook:meat_dish/老妈蹄花/老妈蹄花.name: Mom's Pig Trotters
howtocook:meat_dish/老妈蹄花/老妈蹄花.description: Mom's Pig Trotters soup is milky white, the trotters are tender and fall off the bone, with a delicate texture. Paired with a sour and spicy dipping sauce, it is very appetizing. This is a classic Sichuan home-style dish. Pig trotters are rich in collagen, and white kidney beans add a creamy texture, making it nutritious. The preparation requires a pressure cooker and pre-soaking, which can be a bit challenging for beginners, but it's easy to follow the steps. Including soaking and pressure cooking, the whole process takes about 2 hours (excluding overnight soaking of white kidney beans).
steps: Soak 200g white kidney beans in water overnight in advance and set aside.
Prepare pig front trotters. When buying, ask the butcher to split them in half. Use a blowtorch to remove hair follicles, then wash them at home.
In a cold water pot, add pig trotters, scallion segments, ginger slices, and cooking wine. Blanch for 10 minutes, skim off foam, remove and wash clean, set aside.

In a pressure cooker, add pig trotters, angelica, dahurian angelica, white pepper powder, and ginger slices. After steam comes up, cook for 30 minutes. Add white kidney beans, then cook for another 10 minutes. At this point, if the broth is milky white, congratulations, it's correct (if you need to add water midway, only add hot water).
After removing the lid, add salt, chicken bouillon, and chopped scallions to season.

Prepare the soul dipping sauce: Add scallions, garlic, small red chili peppers, white pepper powder, light soy sauce, fragrant vinegar, chili oil, Sichuan pepper oil, and the original pig trotter broth.
Soul dipping sauce, pour it over.

howtocook:meat_dish/老式锅包肉/老式锅包肉.name: Old-Style Guo Bao Rou
howtocook:meat_dish/老式锅包肉/老式锅包肉.description: Guo Bao Rou is a classic dish from Northeast China, originating from the Daotai Mansion in Harbin. It features a bright sour aroma from white vinegar, a crispy shell, tender meat slices, and a sweet and sour taste. Pork provides high-quality protein, paired with scallions, ginger and other vegetables to stimulate appetite and cut the grease. This dish requires skills in battering, oil temperature control, and double frying. Cooking difficulty is above average; beginners need patience to practice. From marinating, making batter to frying and plating, estimated preparation time is about 90 minutes.
steps: Prepare pork:
Slice the pork loin into uniform pieces 8mm thick, removing white sinew.
Rinse the meat slices with clean water to remove blood.
Optional: Add 5g baking soda, mix well, and let sit for 5 minutes.
Rinse 1-2 times with clean water to remove excess baking soda.
Marinate the meat slices:
Add 3g salt and 5ml cooking wine to the meat, mix well, and marinate for 15 minutes.
Make the batter:
Method 1 (recommended):
Add 170g potato starch to an excess of water, stir, and let sit for 60 minutes until the starch settles.
Pour off all the clear water on top, retaining the starch paste at the bottom (like a non-Newtonian fluid).
Add the meat slices, 5ml water, and 3ml cooking oil, and mix well.
Method 2:
Mix 210g potato starch and 70g all-purpose flour.
Add water little by little, stirring until the batter is like yogurt, can be lifted into a thread, and piles up in the bowl.
Add 10ml cooking oil and 1g baking soda, and mix well.
Add the marinated meat slices to the batter and mix so each piece is evenly coated.
Prepare the sweet and sour sauce:
Mix 65g white sugar, 55g white vinegar, 4g light soy sauce, and 2g salt, stirring until dissolved. Set aside.
Optional: 4g water and 1.5g cornstarch to thicken the sauce further.
Prepare the garnishes:
Shred scallions, ginger, and carrot; cut cilantro into sections; slice garlic.
Fry the meat slices:
First fry:
Add cooking oil to a wok and heat to 170°C (350°F).
Fry the meat slices one by one until the surface sets, the batter no longer sticks to chopsticks, the meat floats, and turns light yellow, about 30 seconds (depending on size). Remove and set aside.
Second fry:
Raise oil temperature to 200°C (390°F).
Fry all meat slices again until golden and crispy, about 30 seconds. Remove and set aside.
Third fry (optional):
Raise oil temperature to 220°C (430°F).
Quickly re-fry the meat slices until they look yellow with a hint of red, about 30 seconds (depending on oil temperature retention), to enhance crispiness. Remove and drain oil.
Stir-fry:
Leave 20ml oil in the wok, pour in the prepared sweet and sour sauce, and bring to a boil over high heat. Cook until thickened, about 30-60 seconds.
Optional: Remove half of the sauce to make stirring easier later.
Add the fried meat slices, shredded scallions, garlic slices, shredded carrot, and cilantro. Pour the reserved sauce over everything, and quickly stir-fry 3 times to coat evenly.
Plate:
Transfer the Guo Bao Rou to a plate, sprinkle with white sesame seeds (optional), and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/肉饼炖蛋.name: Steamed Minced Pork with Egg
howtocook:meat_dish/肉饼炖蛋.description: Steamed minced pork with egg is a traditional home-style dish with savory and tender flavors. The pork patty absorbs the sauce, and the egg sits on top, creating a soft and juicy texture that pairs well with rice. It is rich in high-quality protein and various amino acids, helping to replenish energy. The recipe is very simple, beginner-friendly, and takes only about 20 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: In a bowl, combine minced pork, cooking wine, light soy sauce, white pepper powder, egg, and sesame oil. Mix well.
Spread the seasoned minced pork on a plate. Use a spoon to make a well in the center, then crack an egg into the well.
Add water to the pot to about 1/4 height. Bring the water to a boil, then place the plate in the pot, cover, and steam for 15 minutes.

howtocook:meat_dish/腐乳肉.name: Red Fermented Bean Curd Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/腐乳肉.description: This is a classic home-style dish from the Jiangnan region. Pork belly is braised with red fermented bean curd, resulting in a bright red, amber-like color. The meat is so tender it melts in your mouth, with layers of savory and sweet flavors mingling with the aroma of wine and beans. Rich in high-quality protein and B vitamins, it becomes easier to digest after slow simmering. The cooking process requires patience to control the heat and reduce the sauce, which can be a challenge for beginners. Estimated cooking time: about 1 hour.
steps: Cut the pork belly into small cubes of 2.5 cm on each side.
Place the pork belly in cold water with cooking wine and ginger slices, then bring to a boil over heat. Remove the pork when the water boils.
Mash the red fermented bean curd cubes and its liquid into a paste. Add rock sugar and dark soy sauce to make the sauce.
Heat oil in a wok, sauté scallions and ginger until fragrant, then add the pork cubes and stir-fry until golden brown.
Pour in the fermented bean curd sauce, stir well, then add hot water and simmer covered.
Simmer over low heat for 40 minutes.
Increase heat to reduce the sauce until it thickens and large bubbles appear, then turn off the heat.

howtocook:meat_dish/芥末罗氏虾/芥末罗氏虾.name: Wasabi Prawns
howtocook:meat_dish/芥末罗氏虾/芥末罗氏虾.description: This dish is savory with a slight kick, rich in wasabi flavor. The prawn meat is firm and bouncy. It's a quick home-style seafood dish. Prawns are rich in high-quality protein and calcium, low in fat, and paired with wasabi to stimulate appetite and cut richness. Moderate difficulty; as long as you prepare the sauce mixture and control the heat, beginners can quickly get the hang of it. It takes about 30 minutes in total.
steps: Prepare the prawns:
Cut the prawns along the back, remove the vein and sand sac. Alternatively, cut from the belly; when fried, they will open up and look nicer.
Rinse clean with water. After draining, you can dust with cornstarch or skip.
Prepare garlic and sauce mixture:
Finely chop 2 garlic cloves.
Prepare the sauce: mix light soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, white pepper, and salt. Squeeze in wasabi to taste. Add water to dilute, then add cornstarch and stir to dissolve.
Cooking process:
Heat oil in a wok, enough to coat the bottom.
Add the drained prawns and pan-fry slowly.
When the prawn oil is released (indicated by lots of bubbles), add the prepared garlic and bird's eye chilies.
Once you smell the garlic fragrance, add butter.
After the butter melts, stir well, then add the prepared sauce.
Cover and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens. Serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/茭白炒肉/茭白炒肉.name: Stir-fried Water Bamboo Shoots with Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/茭白炒肉/茭白炒肉.description: Stir-fried water bamboo shoots with pork is a quick home-style dish, fresh, tender, and crispy, with a savory taste. It's a typical Jiangnan home-style flavor. Water bamboo shoots are rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, paired with lean pork for quality protein, balancing nutrition. Medium difficulty, easy for beginners with a bit of practice. Estimated cooking time: about 20 minutes.
steps: Slice water bamboo shoots to 0.5 cm thickness each.
Cut lean pork into strips, 0.3-0.5 cm thick, mix with cooking wine, cornstarch, salt, and water.
Slice ginger, mince garlic.
Bring water to a boil in a pot, add water bamboo shoots, blanch for 60-90 seconds, then remove and drain.
Heat 15 ml oil in a wok, add pork strips, stir-fry for 60 seconds, then remove.
Heat 15 ml oil in the wok, add ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 30 seconds, add water bamboo shoots, stir-fry for another 30 seconds.
Add pork back, stir-fry for 60 seconds, add 20 ml water, add salt and chicken powder, stir-fry for 60 seconds, then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/荔枝肉/荔枝肉.name: Lychee Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/荔枝肉/荔枝肉.description: Lychee Pork is a traditional Fujian dish with sweet and sour flavor, crispy outside and tender inside. The pork pieces are coated in a glossy sauce, paired with fruit to add a refreshing fruity aroma. Pork tenderloin is rich in high-quality protein, and pineapple provides dietary fiber and vitamins, helping to stimulate appetite and cut through the richness. The cooking process involves cutting the meat, coating it in batter, and double-frying, which is a bit challenging but steps are clear. Suitable for home cooks with some experience, about 45 minutes to serve.
steps: Cut the lean meat into pieces (2-3 cm each). Place in a large bowl. Add 1 egg, 50g cornstarch, 3ml light soy sauce, 2g chicken bouillon.
Stir well until the cornstarch coats the meat. If too thin, add more cornstarch; if too dry, add water. Then add 5ml oil, stir well, and set aside.
In another bowl, combine ketchup, 3g chicken bouillon, 2ml light soy sauce, minced ginger, white sugar, 10g cornstarch, vinegar, and 200ml cold water. Stir well and set aside.
Cut one pineapple, prepare 6 pieces (each 1.5-2 cm).
Heat oil in a wok. Pour in 500ml oil. Heat until you hear the frying sound.
Add the meat pieces one by one into the oil (do not pour all at once). Make sure they don't stick together.
After all meat is added, flip the pieces every 30 seconds with a spatula until the surface turns golden.
Remove the meat. After one minute, fry again until the surface becomes crispy and golden. Remove and set aside in a large bowl.
Leave the wok, pour in the sauce. After 30 seconds, add the meat and pineapple pieces. Stir-fry well, then remove from heat.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds.

howtocook:meat_dish/荷兰豆炒腊肠/荷兰豆炒腊肠.name: Stir-fried Snow Peas with Chinese Sausage
howtocook:meat_dish/荷兰豆炒腊肠/荷兰豆炒腊肠.description: Stir-fried snow peas with Chinese sausage is a refreshing and savory home-style dish. The fat from the sausage coats the green snow peas, giving a crisp and tender texture without being greasy. Snow peas are rich in high-quality protein and various vitamins, helping to boost the body's disease resistance. The cooking method is simple, requiring almost no knife skills, and even beginners can quickly master it. It takes about 15 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Remove the stems from the snow peas; if you have time, you can also remove the tips.
Before buying the Chinese sausage, ask the seller if it is raw or cooked. If raw, steam it in advance; if cooked, it can be used directly.
Rinse the snow peas, then blanch them for about 45 seconds until they change color. Remove and drain, then rinse with cold water and set aside.
Heat the wok and add about 10ml of cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add the Chinese sausage and stir-fry until it slightly curls at the edges. Keep the heat very low to prevent burning.
Add the snow peas, turn the heat to medium-high, stir-fry for 30 seconds, then add light soy sauce. Stir-fry for another 20-30 seconds and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/萝卜炖羊排.name: Braised Lamb Ribs with Radish
howtocook:meat_dish/萝卜炖羊排.description: This soup is light yet flavorful, with tender lamb and sweet, soft radish that absorbs the broth—a common winter warmer in northern China. Lamb provides high-quality protein and iron, while radish offers vitamins and fiber; together they warm the spleen and stomach, dispel cold, and aid digestion. Although it requires blanching and two rounds of simmering, the steps are clear and easy to follow for beginners. Total prep takes about two hours, or about one hour with a pressure cooker.
steps: Peel the radish and cut into 3-5cm chunks using a rolling cut (oblique), set aside.
Ask the butcher to cut the lamb ribs when buying, as household knives are usually not strong enough; set aside.
Place lamb in cold water, add half the cooking wine and half the scallion and ginger, boil for 10 minutes to remove bloodiness. (Optional) Skim off any foam that appears during blanching.
In another pot with cold water, add the cut radish and half the rock sugar, bring to a boil and cook for 5 minutes to remove radish's spiciness.
Remove the blanched lamb ribs and place in a pressure cooker. Add water to cover all ingredients, then add about 300ml more water.
Add the remaining scallion, ginger, cooking wine, Sichuan peppercorns, rock sugar, angelica (optional), and salt. Cover, wait for steam, then cook on medium heat for about 15 minutes.
If using a regular pot, simmer for about 40 minutes to 1 hour.
Turn off heat, wait for pressure to release completely, open lid. Add the blanched radish, adjust seasoning, add 3-10g salt or water to taste.
Turn heat back on to medium. For pressure cooker, cook for another 10 minutes after steaming; for regular pot, cover and simmer for another 20 minutes.
Turn off heat, serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/蒜苔炒肉末.name: Stir-fried Garlic Scapes with Minced Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/蒜苔炒肉末.description: Stir-fried garlic scapes with minced pork is a northern Chinese home-style dish. It is savory, fresh, crispy, and tender with a rich garlic aroma. Garlic scapes are rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, while pork belly provides protein, making a balanced meat-and-vegetable combination. The recipe is simple, ingredients are easy to prepare, and even beginners can quickly master it. From cutting to serving, it takes only about 10 minutes.
steps: Cut garlic scapes into 5cm sections, set aside.
Cut pork belly into 5mm x 5cm strips, set aside.
Smash and mince garlic cloves, set aside.
Heat the wok, add 10ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add minced garlic, stir-fry over medium heat for 10 seconds until fragrant.
Add pork belly and 5ml light soy sauce, stir-fry over medium heat for 30 seconds until cooked and colored.
Add garlic scapes to the wok along with 10ml light soy sauce, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add 20g water to the wok, stir-fry over medium heat for 5 minutes until garlic scapes are slightly softened.
Finally, add 2g salt, stir-fry over medium heat for 30 seconds, then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/虎皮肘子.name: Tiger Skin Pork Elbow
howtocook:meat_dish/虎皮肘子.description: This dish is a classic Chinese banquet staple, with a glossy red color, attractive tiger-like skin pattern, and tender, flavorful meat that is rich but not greasy. The lean meat is soft and loose. Pork elbow is rich in collagen and high-quality protein, which becomes more absorbable after long braising, benefiting skin and joints. The process involves scorching, deep-frying, caramelizing, and pressure-cooking—quite challenging for beginners, so some cooking experience is recommended. Total time from prep to table is about 2.5 hours.
steps: Thaw the pork elbow and soak in water for 1 hour to remove blood.

If you have a flame torch, burn the skin of the pork elbow until it turns dark brown to remove hair and destroy sweat glands. Do not burn one spot too long to avoid charring. Stop when the skin is almost completely dark brown.

If no flame torch, heat a dry wok to above 200°C, place the pork elbow directly in the wok, and use a spatula or chopsticks to press the skin against the hot surface. When the contact area turns brown, reposition and continue until the entire skin is treated. Be careful not to let the wok get red-hot.
Use a scrubber under running water to clean the pork elbow, removing the burnt parts. After scrubbing, the skin should look as it did before scorching.

Place the pork elbow in a wok and add as much cold water as possible, depending on wok depth and elbow size. Ideally, the water should cover at least 3/4 of the elbow, as long as you can lift the wok and its contents.
Take the white part of 1 scallion, cut into 3 sections, and add to the wok.
Take 3 garlic cloves, smash each with the side of a knife, and add to the wok.
Add 3 grams of ginger to the wok.
Add 2 tablespoons of cooking wine to the wok.
Bring the water to a boil, wait 5 minutes, then remove the pork elbow and discard all aromatics from the wok. Reserve the broth in a separate container.
Add cold oil to the wok, enough to submerge about 3/5 of the elbow (use the previous water amount as reference). Heat over medium heat.
When the oil reaches 50% hot (about 150°C), reduce to low heat and add the pork elbow for deep-frying.
During deep-frying, be cautious for safety.
Use a spatula or other heat-resistant utensil to ladle hot oil over the parts of the elbow not submerged. If possible, flip the elbow every 3 minutes for even frying.
Deep-fry for about 20 minutes, until the skin is uniformly light brown and the lean meat edges are slightly charred. Remove and set aside.
The used oil can be reused for other frying, but not too many times.
Prepare 200 ml of caramelized sugar (for coloring) and set aside.

Add the pork elbow to a pressure cooker. Pour in all the reserved broth, caramelized sugar, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, cardamom, Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, and white vinegar. If you prefer a sweeter taste, add 2-3 grams of rock sugar.
Take the white part of 1 scallion, cut into 3 sections, and add to the pressure cooker.
Take 3 garlic cloves, smash with the side of a knife, and add to the pressure cooker.
Add 3 grams of ginger to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid and pressure cook for 40 minutes.
While cooking, make a cornstarch slurry: In a bowl, mix 1 tablespoon of cornstarch with 100 ml of water until it forms a white suspension.
After pressure cooking, open the lid and discard all aromatics, leaving only the pork elbow.
Transfer the remaining broth from the pressure cooker to a wok, and move the pork elbow to a plate or basin.

Place the wok over high heat to reduce the sauce. Dip a chopstick tip into the sauce to taste saltiness, and add salt as needed. Note that the sauce will taste less salty when more liquid is present, so adjust accordingly.
When the sauce boils, keep an eye on the remaining volume.
When the remaining broth is less than half of the original volume, stir the cornstarch slurry again and add half of it to the wok.
Wait for the sauce to boil again, then add the remaining slurry.
Let the sauce boil, then wait 1-2 minutes and turn off the heat. The sauce should now be thick and reddish-brown.
Ladle the sauce evenly over the pork elbow, trying to cover every part. If you run out of sauce before covering completely, serve as is; otherwise, serve with remaining sauce on the side.

howtocook:meat_dish/蚂蚁上树.name: Ants Climbing a Tree
howtocook:meat_dish/蚂蚁上树.description: Ants Climbing a Tree is a classic Sichuan home-style dish, savory and slightly spicy, with soft and slippery vermicelli that soaks up the sauce, and tender minced meat that is delicious. Made mainly from sweet potato vermicelli and minced pork, it is rich in carbohydrates and protein, providing a quick energy boost. The difficulty is moderate, the steps are clear, and it is beginner-friendly, taking about 20 minutes in total.
steps: Soak sweet potato vermicelli in advance for 20 minutes until soft, set aside
Finely chop garlic and ginger, set aside
Heat a wok, add 10ml cooking oil, add minced garlic and ginger, stir-fry until fragrant
Add minced pork, stir-fry until the meat turns white and slightly releases oil
Add Pixian broad bean paste, stir-fry until red oil appears
Add light soy sauce and dark soy sauce, stir well
Pour in 300ml water, bring to a boil
Add the soaked and drained vermicelli, gently stir with chopsticks to prevent sticking
Cook over medium-low heat for about 5 minutes, until the vermicelli fully absorbs the sauce and becomes slightly dry
Optionally sprinkle with chopped green onions, turn off the heat and serve

howtocook:meat_dish/血浆鸭/血浆鸭.name: Blood Duck
howtocook:meat_dish/血浆鸭/血浆鸭.description: Blood Duck is a traditional famous dish from Wugang, Hunan. The duck meat is crispy and tender, and after being soaked in vinegar blood, even the ginger and chili become sweet and not spicy. Duck meat and blood provide high-quality protein and iron, suitable for nourishing blood and body. There are many steps, requiring attention to heat and stirring, but following the steps, beginners can finish in about 2 hours.
steps: Cut fresh young duck meat into about 3cm pieces, add cooking wine and ginger slices to remove blood.
Heat a wok, add about 100ml cooking oil, heat on high until oil is hot, add the marinated duck meat and stir constantly.
When the duck meat has completely changed color (visibly white), add the liquor, then add 200ml boiling water, just enough to cover the duck meat, cover and cook on medium heat for 15 minutes.
After the water boils, uncover, add ginger and garlic, stir once, cover and continue heating for 10 minutes.

Uncover, add chili, stir constantly until the chili becomes soft, then add fresh duck blood. Stir constantly to ensure every piece of duck and chili is coated with blood (this is the essence of Blood Duck).
Stir until the duck blood turns black, add salt, chicken bouillon, and scallions. (If you like, you can also add 3-6 drops of wild pepper oil at this point.) Stir once or twice more.
Remove from heat, plate, and serve.

ingredients.name: Thai bird's eye chili (or facing-heaven chili)

howtocook:meat_dish/西红柿土豆炖牛肉/西红柿土豆炖牛肉.name: Tomato and Potato Braised Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/西红柿土豆炖牛肉/西红柿土豆炖牛肉.description: A home-style braised dish, sweet and sour appetizing, rich broth, tender beef, soft potatoes. Beef provides high-quality protein, tomatoes are rich in lycopene, combined with potatoes and onions for balanced nutrition. Simple operation, no complex skills needed, beginners can easily master. From prep to serving takes about 1 to 1.5 hours.
steps: Prep:
Peel potatoes, cut into 5cm chunks, set aside.
Score tomatoes with a cross, blanch in boiling water to remove skin, remove cores, cut into 3cm small pieces, set aside.
Slice 4g of scallion into chopped scallions, break the rest into 5-8cm sections.
Soak beef in cold water for half an hour to remove blood, or put beef in cold water and boil until surface turns white, then remove; skim off foam during boiling.
Dice onion into 0.5-1cm small pieces.
Cooking
Keep medium heat throughout.
Cover beef with cold water in a pressure cooker, add scallion sections, ginger slices, and 20g cooking wine. Pressure cook for 20 minutes after steam builds up.
Remove beef, cut into 5cm chunks, discard ginger, and pour the broth into a large bowl for later use.
Add oil; when oil is 40-50% hot, add Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, and bay leaves. Once fragrant (before they burn), remove and discard.
Add beef, scallion, and ginger; stir-fry until fragrant. It doesn't matter how long; if it looks like it's about to burn, add 10ml of the reserved broth. Same for the following steps.
After fragrant, add 15ml light soy sauce, 15ml cooking wine, pepper, 5-10g tomato paste/ketchup or one canned tomato, and diced onion; stir-fry until onion is translucent.
(Optional) Add a pinch of salt to help onion release water and prevent burning; don't add too much, adjust seasoning later.
Add tomatoes and stir-fry until they break down and become soft; pour in the remaining broth.
Simmering
After bringing to a boil over medium heat, reduce to low heat and simmer.
30-40 minutes before finishing, add potatoes and season.
Adjust sugar and salt to taste, adding gradually and tasting.
When beef is tender enough to be pierced with chopsticks, remove from heat!

ingredients.name: Cooking wine
ingredients.note: Add in two batches: for braising and stir-frying

howtocook:meat_dish/西红柿牛腩/西红柿牛腩.name: Tomato Beef Brisket
howtocook:meat_dish/西红柿牛腩/西红柿牛腩.description: Tomato beef brisket has a thick, tangy and sweet broth, the beef is tender and fragrant—a comforting home-cooked dish that whets the appetite. Rich in protein, vitamins, and lycopene, it's nutritionally balanced. A bit challenging for beginners, requiring skills in simmering meat and stir-frying tomato sauce. Typically takes about 90 minutes for a novice.
steps: Cut the beef brisket into strips, then into cubes about 2cm on each side. Place in cold water, bring to a boil, and cook for 2 minutes to remove blood. Drain and rinse clean.

In another pot, bring 2L of water to a boil. Add two 2cm scallion segments, two slices of ginger, star anise, and 5-10ml of cooking wine or Huangjiu. Add the blanched beef, cover, and simmer (1 hour in a clay pot, or 45 minutes on the stew setting of a pressure cooker). It's done when a chopstick can easily pierce the meat.

Peel the tomatoes: Score a cross on the top of each tomato down to the middle. Insert a chopstick or fork from the stem end, roast over low heat on a gas stove, turning constantly. Check frequently, peel off the skin when it blisters. Then cut into pieces, as small as possible.
Be careful not to burn yourself when peeling. The peeled tomatoes are very slippery, so cut slowly and safely.

Heat oil in a wok until 70% hot. Add 10g each of scallion and ginger. Add the tomatoes and stir-fry until they break down and turn red. Add the cooked beef and its broth, just enough to barely cover the beef.
Season with salt, sugar, and light soy sauce to taste. Cover with a lid.
After it comes to a boil, continue cooking on high heat for 3-5 minutes.
When the tomato sauce reaches a medium thick consistency, turn off the heat. Sprinkle with chopped scallions and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/豆豉鲮鱼油麦菜/豆豉鲮鱼油麦菜.name: Stir-fried Celtuce with Dace in Black Bean Sauce
howtocook:meat_dish/豆豉鲮鱼油麦菜/豆豉鲮鱼油麦菜.description: Stir-fried celtuce with dace in black bean sauce is a savory and flavorful quick home-style stir-fry, originating from Cantonese canned dishes. The celtuce is crisp, and the dace is crispy and aromatic. It combines meat and vegetables, rich in protein and dietary fiber. The method is simple and beginner-friendly, usually completed in 15 minutes.
steps: Ingredient preparation:
Wash celtuce and cut into sections.
Open the dace can, remove the main bones from the dace, cut into small pieces, and set aside.
Mince the garlic.
Stir-frying process:
Heat the wok, add cooking oil. When oil is hot, add the washed celtuce, stir-fry until wilted and water releases. Remove and set aside, discard the released liquid.
Reheat the wok, add the oil from the dace can, add minced garlic and stir until fragrant.
After the garlic is fragrant, add the black beans from the dace can, stir, then add the chopped dace pieces, stir again.
Once well mixed, add the celtuce, add light soy sauce and sugar to season.
After adding the seasoning, briefly stir-fry, then remove from wok and plate.

ingredients.name: Ganzhu brand dace in black bean sauce (canned)

howtocook:meat_dish/豉汁排骨.name: Steamed Spare Ribs with Black Bean Sauce
howtocook:meat_dish/豉汁排骨.description: A popular steamed dim sum from Cantonese teahouses, with rich fermented black bean aroma and tender, fall-off-the-bone ribs. Ribs provide high-quality protein and calcium, while fermented black beans add flavor and aid absorption. The process involves soaking, marinating, and precise steaming—a bit challenging for beginners, but clear steps make it easy to follow. From prep to serving, it takes about 1 hour. The leftover sauce is especially delicious over rice or noodles.
steps: Soak the ribs in cold water for 10 minutes to remove blood, change water and repeat twice, then pat dry with paper towels.
Soak the fermented black beans in water for 5 minutes.
Mix all ingredients (reserve 10 ml of cooking oil), marinate for 8-30 minutes.
Steam over boiling water for 18 minutes.
Let rest for 2 minutes.
Heat 10 ml of oil in a pan to 180-200°C.
Evenly sprinkle with scallions and white sesame seeds, then pour hot oil over the toppings.

howtocook:meat_dish/豉汁蒸白鱔/豉汁蒸白鱔.name: Steamed Eel with Black Bean Sauce
howtocook:meat_dish/豉汁蒸白鱔/豉汁蒸白鱔.description: Steamed eel with black bean sauce is a classic Cantonese steamed dish. The savory aroma of black bean and garlic penetrates the fish, giving it a tender and delicious texture. Eel is rich in high-quality protein and various unsaturated fatty acids, nourishing the body. The steps are clear, but handling raw fish and controlling the heat pose a slight challenge, suitable for cooks with some experience. It takes about 30 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Check and clean the eel:
When buying, ask the fishmonger to gut, remove slime, and cut into about 5 cm sections.
Check the eel at home, rinse thoroughly to ensure no residual slime or impurities.
Optional: Gently rub the eel pieces with 3g salt and 1 teaspoon cornstarch, marinate for 5 minutes, then rinse clean to remove fishy smell and enhance tenderness.
Prepare the sauce:

In a small bowl, mix 1 tablespoon black beans, 2 cloves minced garlic, 1.5 tablespoons light soy sauce, 0.5 teaspoon dark soy sauce, 0.5 teaspoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon sesame oil, and 1 tablespoon water. Stir well.
If you prefer a richer black bean sauce, add an extra 0.5 tablespoon of black beans.
Arrange on plate:
Place the eel pieces flat in a heatproof dish, overlapping as little as possible.
Evenly sprinkle ginger strips over the eel.
Evenly pour the prepared black bean sauce over the eel.
Steam:
Add water (500ml) to a steamer or electric steamer and bring to a boil.
Place the dish with eel into the steamer, cover with lid.
Steam over medium-high heat for about 8-10 minutes (adjust time based on eel piece size) until the eel is cooked through but not overdone (flesh turns white, no blood).
Garnish and finish:
Remove from steamer, sprinkle with scallion strips and red chili strips (optional) for garnish.
You may drizzle hot sesame oil on top to enhance aroma.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

howtocook:meat_dish/贵州辣子鸡/贵州辣子鸡.name: Guizhou Spicy Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/贵州辣子鸡/贵州辣子鸡.description: Guizhou Spicy Chicken is a classic home-cooked dish from the Guizhou region, made with Ciba chili and various spices. The chicken is tender and sticky, the potatoes are soft and flavorful, and the overall taste is spicy and rich. Chicken is high in quality protein, paired with potatoes and other ingredients, making it a balanced meal. This dish has many steps, posing a challenge for beginners, but it can be done step by step. From preparation to serving, it takes about 1.5 hours.
steps: Add oil to the pot until it reaches half the height of the pot. First fry the potato strips until golden brown, then remove and set aside. When the oil is hot enough to feel warm on your hand, add the chicken pieces to the pot and fry, along with sliced ginger and Sichuan pepper.
At the beginning of frying the chicken, the oil will be cloudy due to the water in the chicken. When the oil becomes clear, the chicken is done. Remove and set aside.
Now you can scoop out one-third of the oil from the pot; you won't need that much oil for now.
Heat the remaining oil in the pot, add Ciba chili, Doubanjiang, and sliced ginger. Stir-fry until the oil turns red. Add the fried chicken pieces and stir-fry evenly.
When the chicken is evenly colored, add dark soy sauce and pour in beer. The beer must cover the chicken. Add bay leaves, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Then add the potato strips and garlic (whole cloves, not chopped). Simmer for another 20 minutes.
Finally, add fermented rice wine and stir well, then add chopped garlic sprouts. Remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/辣椒炒肉.name: Stir-fried Pork with Green Peppers
howtocook:meat_dish/辣椒炒肉.description: Stir-fried pork with green peppers is a home-style dish with rich Hunan flavor. The peppers are dry-fried until slightly charred, and the pork slices are tender and flavorful. It's salty, savory, and mildly spicy, making it a great accompaniment to rice. Pork is rich in high-quality protein and B vitamins, while green peppers provide abundant vitamin C. This dish requires mastery of dry-frying and quick-stirring techniques, suitable for those with some cooking experience. From preparation to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Wash the green peppers, remove the stems and seeds, then cut into bite-sized pieces using a rolling cut (cut on the bias, turning the pepper as you go).
Smash the garlic cloves with the side of a knife, then slice crosswise into pieces. Finely chop the ginger into mince.
Slice the pork into pieces along the grain (i.e., the knife should be parallel to the meat fibers, resulting in slices with the grain in a '川' shape).
Rinse the sliced pork, place in a bowl, then add the measured light soy sauce, oyster sauce, and salt. Mix well and marinate for 10 minutes.

Heat the wok without oil, add the cut green peppers. Dry-fry over high heat until they develop a 'tiger skin' appearance (blistered and slightly charred). Then add 2g salt and stir-fry for another minute. Remove and set aside.
Without cleaning the wok, heat it over high heat, add 8ml oil. After 30 seconds, add the garlic and ginger, stir-fry for 15 seconds.
Add the marinated pork to the wok, stir-fry for 2 minutes, then add the dry-fried green peppers and stir-fry for another minute.
Add fermented black beans (douchi) to taste, then add dark soy sauce. Continue stir-frying for 30 seconds.
Remove from heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/酱排骨/酱排骨.name: Braised Pork Ribs
howtocook:meat_dish/酱排骨/酱排骨.description: Braised pork ribs are glossy red, tender and flavorful, with a rich bone aroma and a savory-sweet sauce. This classic home-cooked dish is rich in high-quality protein and calcium, offering balanced nutrition. The process involves caramelizing sugar and a longer simmer, which may be challenging for beginners, but following the steps carefully will yield good results. Preparation to serving takes about 1 hour.
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil, add ribs, ginger slices, and cooking wine. After boiling, skim off the white foam with a spoon. Remove ribs after 2-3 minutes.
Rinse ribs under cold water, washing 2-3 times.
Heat a small amount of oil in a pan over low heat. Add white sugar and stir gently until the sugar melts and turns yellow.
Add ribs and stir-fry for 30 seconds. Then add light soy sauce, oyster sauce, five-spice powder, garlic, and bird's eye chilies. Stir-fry for another 30 seconds. Add enough water to cover the ribs.
Boil over high heat for 30 minutes. Add dark soy sauce for color, then cook for another 10 minutes.
Plate and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/酱牛肉/酱牛肉.name: Soy-Marinated Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/酱牛肉/酱牛肉.description: Soy-marinated beef is a Northern-style home-style cold dish, rich in sauce aroma, firm and not loose in texture, chewy. Beef shank is rich in high-quality protein and various amino acids, and after slow cooking, it's easier to digest. The process is not too complicated, but includes marinating, slow cooking, and long refrigeration; it requires some patience for beginners, taking about 10 hours from start to finish.
steps: Clean the beef shank, soak in water for 1 hour to remove blood.

Use a toothpick or fork to poke holes on the surface of the beef shank, add cooking wine, Sichuan peppercorns, and ginger slices, then tightly wrap the container with plastic wrap and marinate for 4-6 hours.
Cut the beef shank into pieces of 8 cm, not exceeding 10 cm.
Put the beef shank in a pot, add cold water until just covered. Bring to a boil, start timing, and skim off the foam. After 3 minutes, turn off the heat, remove the beef, and wash with warm water.
Place the washed beef shank in a clay pot or stew pot, add water to cover the beef, turn on high heat, and add all other unused ingredients except salt.
After the water boils, reduce heat to low and cook for 90 minutes, then add salt.
After adding salt, continue on low heat for another 90 minutes (note: check the water level every 30 minutes, ensuring it reaches at least 80% of the beef's height).
After a total of 180 minutes of cooking, remove the beef shank, remove the beef shank, tightly wrap with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for several hours.
After taking it out, slice against the grain and serve. It is recommended to store the remaining beef shank unsliced in the refrigerator.

howtocook:meat_dish/醉排骨/醉排骨.name: Drunken Spare Ribs
howtocook:meat_dish/醉排骨/醉排骨.description: Drunken Spare Ribs is a traditional Fuzhou dish with a crispy outside and tender inside, sweet and sour, and a unique fish sauce aroma. The ribs are rich in protein and calcium, making them nutritious and satisfying. This dish requires skills in battering and double frying, so it's better for those with some cooking experience. It takes about 40 minutes in total.
steps: Add 5 g sweet potato starch and water to the ribs, stir, rinse 2-3 times, and put in a large bowl for later use.
Add fish sauce, sweet potato starch, and egg yolk to the ribs and stir well.
Place the ribs one by one into the pot (do not pour them all in at once), making sure they don't stick together.
After all are in, flip the meat pieces every 30 seconds with a spatula until the ribs are golden brown on the surface.
Remove the ribs, and after one minute, put them back into the oil to continue frying until the surface turns dark golden. Remove and set aside in a large bowl.
Prepare a small bowl, add minced garlic, black vinegar, white sugar, fish sauce, ketchup, chopped scallions, and sesame seeds, stir well, then pour in 5 ml hot oil.
Pour the sauce over the ribs, stir thoroughly, and transfer to a serving plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/青椒土豆炒肉/青椒土豆炒肉.name: Green Pepper, Potato, and Pork Stir-fry
howtocook:meat_dish/青椒土豆炒肉/青椒土豆炒肉.description: Green Pepper, Potato, and Pork Stir-fry is a home-style stir-fry that combines meat and vegetables. It tastes fresh, crispy, and tender, with a rich soy sauce flavor that goes well with rice. Potatoes are rich in carbohydrates and vitamin C, green peppers provide plenty of vitamin C and dietary fiber, and pork adds high-quality protein for a balanced meal. This dish is not complicated to make and is beginner-friendly, taking about 1 hour from prep to serving.
steps: Remove the stems and cut the green pepper into small pieces, peel and slice the potato into 2mm thick slices, slice the pork into 4mm thick slices, cut the scallion into 3mm small sections crosswise and lengthwise, peel and smash the ginger and garlic and chop into fine mince; mix potato starch with about 15g water to form a slurry.
Heat oil in a wok until 70% hot, add the pork slices and gently stir-fry until no longer red, add about 3ml soy sauce and stir-fry until the meat is evenly colored, then add about 2g salt.

Lower the oil temperature to 50% hot, add scallion, ginger, and garlic and stir-fry for 5 seconds, then add the potato slices, increase heat to 70% hot and stir-fry evenly, add about 5ml soy sauce and 2g salt, stir-fry until the potatoes are just cooked through and slightly golden on the surface.

Increase heat to 80% hot, add the green pepper, stir-fry over high heat until you smell the aroma (white smoke appears), stir-fry evenly for 1 minute to color, then pour the starch slurry around the edges of the wok and reduce heat to 40% hot to thicken.
Turn off the heat when the sauce appears thick and glossy, then transfer to a serving plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/青椒酿/青椒酿.name: Stuffed Green Peppers
howtocook:meat_dish/青椒酿/青椒酿.description: This classic Guangxi home-style dish features green peppers stuffed with seasoned meat or shrimp paste. The peppers are pan-fried until the skin wrinkles and develops a tiger-skin pattern, then braised to infuse flavors. The fresh aroma of green peppers blends with the savory filling, creating a salty, slightly spicy taste that pairs perfectly with rice. Both pork and shrimp provide high-quality protein, while green peppers are rich in vitamin C, offering a balanced nutrition. It takes a bit of patience to stuff the peppers, but the process is straightforward, and you can finish it in about 30 minutes.
steps: Wash the pork and chop into minced meat (or use a meat grinder). Mince the ginger, and chop the green onion into scallions.
Add minced ginger, scallions, 15ml light soy sauce, 5ml dark soy sauce, and 3g salt to the minced meat. Mix well.
Optional: Add 10ml cooking wine, 10ml oyster sauce, 1 egg, 5g starch, 2g white pepper, and 5g sugar.
Stir the meat filling in one direction until it becomes sticky and firm. Marinate for 10 minutes.
Wash the green peppers, remove the stems, and carefully use chopsticks to remove the seeds and white pith, keeping the peppers intact.
Optional: Sprinkle about 5g of dry starch inside the peppers and shake evenly to help the filling adhere.
Use chopsticks or a piping bag to stuff the marinated meat filling into the peppers. Fill as much as possible but not too tightly to avoid splitting.
Set aside all stuffed peppers.
In a bowl, mix 15ml light soy sauce, 5ml dark soy sauce, and 100ml water. Stir well.
Optional: Add 5g starch to thicken the sauce.
Heat a pan and add 30ml cooking oil. When the oil is about 50-60% hot, place the stuffed peppers in the pan (meat side down first).
Fry over medium-low heat, turning to cook all sides until the skin wrinkles and develops a tiger-skin pattern. Remove and set aside.
Leave a little oil in the pan. Optional: add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant.
Pour in the prepared sauce. Bring to a boil, then add the fried peppers.
Reduce to medium-low heat, cover, and simmer for 5-8 minutes until the sauce thickens and the peppers soften.
Uncover, increase heat to high, and reduce the sauce until it glazes the peppers. Remove from heat and plate.
Devein 250g shrimp, then use the back of a knife to pound into a paste (keep some texture for better mouthfeel; alternatively, buy ready-made shrimp paste and directly stuff it using a piping bag).
Season the shrimp paste: 5g minced ginger, 2g salt, 5g starch, 10ml light soy sauce.
Optional: Add 5ml cooking wine, 1g white pepper, and half an egg white.
Stir in one direction until the shrimp paste becomes firm. Marinate for 10 minutes.
The subsequent steps of stuffing, frying, and braising are the same as the pork version.
Shrimp paste cooks quickly, so reduce the braising time to 4-5 minutes.

ingredients.name: Green peppers (pointed peppers, choose straight and thick-fleshed)

howtocook:meat_dish/香干肉丝.name: Stir-fried Shredded Pork with Smoked Tofu
howtocook:meat_dish/香干肉丝.description: Shredded pork with smoked tofu is a savory, home-style stir-fry that pairs well with rice. The pork is tender, the smoked tofu is chewy, and the green peppers are crisp. It provides both animal and plant proteins, with green peppers adding vitamins. Moderate difficulty, achievable for beginners with some practice. Preparation time: about 30 minutes.
steps: Mix shredded pork (if not pre-cut, cut yourself) with light soy sauce (3ml) and cornstarch until well combined. Set aside.
Wash the green pepper, then cut into strips using a rolling cut technique. Set aside.
Slice the garlic crosswise into thin slices. Shred the smoked tofu.
Mix cornstarch with water (10ml) until smooth.
In a clean pan, heat 15ml oil. Add the shredded pork before the oil is hot, gently separate it with a spatula. Once the pork is cooked, immediately remove it, leaving the oil in the pan.
Add the garlic slices and shredded smoked tofu to the pan. Add 2ml light soy sauce and stir-fry evenly.
Depending on the heat, after 2-3 minutes, add the green pepper strips and stir-fry together.
After 1 minute, add the cooked pork back in and mix well.
Pour in the cornstarch-water mixture to thicken. Add 3g salt and 2g chicken bouillon. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes, then serve.
Finished product.

howtocook:meat_dish/香干芹菜炒肉/香干芹菜炒肉.name: Stir-fried Pork with Dried Tofu and Celery
howtocook:meat_dish/香干芹菜炒肉/香干芹菜炒肉.description: Stir-fried Pork with Dried Tofu and Celery is a home-style stir-fry. The dried tofu is chewy, the celery is crisp, and the pork is tender, with a savory and slightly spicy flavor. Celery is rich in dietary fiber, while dried tofu and pork provide high-quality plant and animal protein. The method is simple and beginner-friendly. From prep to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Remove leaves from celery, cut into segments, then cut into strips no longer than 4 cm. Set aside.
Cut dried tofu into strips about the width of your pinky finger. Set aside.
Slice garlic or mince it. Set aside.
Cut chili into rings or slice diagonally. Set aside.
Heat the wok, add 10-15 ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add Sichuan peppercorns and garlic and stir-fry until fragrant (if you eat ginger, you can also add some ginger slices or shreds).
Add pork and stir-fry until it changes color, then add 8 ml dark soy sauce for color and stir well (if you have chili bean paste, add 3 ml and stir-fry together).
Add dried tofu and stir-fry evenly (about 2 minutes).
Add chili and stir-fry evenly (about 1-2 minutes).
Add celery, add 5 g salt and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add 3 g chicken bouillon, 5 ml oyster sauce, and stir well. Then serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/香煎五花肉/香煎五花肉.name: Pan-Fried Pork Belly
howtocook:meat_dish/香煎五花肉/香煎五花肉.description: Pan-fried pork belly is a quick home-style meat dish. The pork belly slices are fried until crispy and fragrant, fatty but not greasy. Paired with crisp lettuce leaves and savory broad bean paste, it's rich in sauce flavor and refreshing. Pork is rich in high-quality protein and fat, quickly replenishing energy, while lettuce provides dietary fiber and vitamins for a more balanced nutrition. This dish is suitable for those with some basic cooking experience; beginners can also handle it easily by paying attention to heat and preventing oil splatter. From preparation to serving, it takes about half an hour.
steps: Slice the pork belly strip lengthwise into pieces about 1mm-1.5mm thick, set aside.
Place the sliced pork belly in a bowl, add 8g soy sauce, 1g salt, 1g MSG, 10g cooking wine, 2 slices of ginger, and 2 smashed heads of garlic, marinate for 10 minutes.
Tear off the lettuce leaves by hand, wash them, and set aside.
Heat the pan, pour in 5ml cooking oil. Once the oil is slightly smoking, add the pork belly. Fry one side until golden brown, then flip and fry the other side the same way.
Remove the pork belly from the pan and plate it.
Spread the broad bean paste on the lettuce leaves, wrap the pork belly, and enjoy.

howtocook:meat_dish/香菇滑鸡/香菇滑鸡.name: Braised Chicken with Shiitake Mushrooms
howtocook:meat_dish/香菇滑鸡/香菇滑鸡.description: Braised Chicken with Shiitake Mushrooms is a home-style dish primarily made with chicken thighs and shiitake mushrooms. The chicken thighs are stir-fried and then braised, resulting in a tender and smooth texture. Combined with the unique aroma of shiitake mushrooms, the broth is rich and savory. It is a Cantonese-style dish suitable for all ages. Chicken is rich in high-quality protein and various amino acids, while shiitake mushrooms contain lentinan and dietary fiber, which help boost immunity and promote digestion. This dish requires some knife skills and heat control, posing a challenge for beginners, and takes about 1 hour to prepare.
steps: Soak dried shiitake mushrooms in warm water.
Cut ginger into small pieces, cut scallions into sections, cut garlic cloves in half and then into small pieces.
Debone chicken thighs (optional, but it slightly affects the efficiency of programmers eating). Cut into small pieces.
Cut each rehydrated mushroom into four pieces. Reserve the mushroom soaking water.
Blanch chicken thigh pieces in boiling water for 1 minute to remove blood foam and impurities.
Add 15ml cooking wine, 15ml light soy sauce, 1.5g salt, and 15ml dark soy sauce to the chicken. Mix well.
Heat oil to 30% hot. Add chicken and stir-fry until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
Leave a little oil in the wok. Add scallions, ginger, and garlic and stir-fry until fragrant. Add mushrooms and stir-fry over high heat to combine.

After about 20 seconds, when the mushroom aroma wafts out, add the stir-fried chicken, all of the mushroom soaking water (this is the soul of the dish, according to this programmer), 15ml sugar, and 30ml light soy sauce.
Reduce to medium heat, do not cover, and simmer for 2 minutes to reduce the sauce. Drizzle with 5ml sesame oil, sprinkle with chopped scallions, then turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/香辣鸡爪煲/香辣鸡爪煲.name: Spicy Chicken Feet Pot
howtocook:meat_dish/香辣鸡爪煲/香辣鸡爪煲.description: Spicy, rich flavor, and chewy texture. It's a common home-style dish that goes well with rice. Chicken feet are rich in collagen, which helps nourish skin and strengthen bones. You need to remove the toenails first. If using whole chicken feet, you also need to debone them. It's a bit challenging for beginners, but with patience and following the steps, you can succeed. From blanching to slow simmering and then stir-frying to infuse flavor, the whole process takes about 45 minutes.
steps: Cut off the toenails of the chicken feet. If you bought only the paw part, cut them in half. If using whole chicken feet, you need to debone them. Rinse clean with water.
Place chicken feet in a pot with cold water, add scallion, ginger, and cooking wine to blanch. Bring to a boil, skim off the foam.
Add bay leaves, star anise, light soy sauce, and dark soy sauce. Cover and simmer on low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken feet, reserve a bowl of the chicken broth.

Heat oil in a wok, stir-fry minced ginger, minced garlic, and bird's eye chilies on low heat until fragrant. Add more chili flakes if you can handle the heat. Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, five-spice powder, and salt, stir-fry until the sauce is aromatic.
Add the chicken feet, season with a little salt, stir-fry for 1-2 minutes, then pour in the chicken broth, stirring constantly.
