Add chicken bouillon to enhance flavor, sprinkle with scallion segments, stir well, and then remove from heat.

howtocook:meat_dish/鱼香肉丝.name: Fish-Fragrant Shredded Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/鱼香肉丝.description: Fish-Fragrant Shredded Pork is a classic Sichuan dish with a sweet, sour, and mildly spicy flavor. The pork is tender and smooth, accompanied by carrots, green peppers, and wood ear mushrooms, offering a rich texture. It's rich in protein and various vitamins, with a balanced mix of meat and vegetables. The preparation is somewhat challenging, requiring a well-balanced fish-fragrant sauce and proper stir-frying technique. Beginners are advised to follow the steps strictly. The estimated preparation time is about 40 minutes (excluding time for soaking wood ear mushrooms).
steps: Make the marinade: Mix the following ingredients:
Light soy sauce 5ml
Cooking wine 5ml
Starch 5g
Water 20ml
Egg white 1 piece
Make the sauce: Mix the following ingredients:
Light soy sauce 5ml
Vinegar 15ml
Sugar 10g
Salt 1g
Starch 5g
Water 20ml
Marinate the pork tenderloin with the marinade for 15-30 minutes. Make sure to mix well.
Soak dried wood ear mushrooms for 4 hours, wash, and cut into small pieces.
Wash green peppers, remove stems, and shred.
Wash carrots, shred, and blanch the carrot shreds.
Mince ginger and garlic.
Cut scallions into 5mm sections.
Heat the wok, add 15ml oil.
Add the marinated pork to the wok, quickly stir-fry until it turns white, then remove and set aside.
Heat the wok again, add 5ml oil.
Add all the scallions, ginger, garlic, and doubanjiang (chili bean paste).
Add all the carrots, stir-fry for 20 seconds, then add green peppers and wood ear mushrooms, stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the cooked pork. Quickly stir-fry until well combined. Do not stir-fry for more than 20 seconds.
Pour in the sauce. Quickly stir-fry until well combined. Do not stir-fry for more than 15 seconds.
Turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/鱼香茄子/鱼香茄子.name: Fish-Fragrant Eggplant
howtocook:meat_dish/鱼香茄子/鱼香茄子.description: Fish-fragrant eggplant is a classic Sichuan home-style dish, sweet, sour and mildly spicy, with soft and tender eggplant that absorbs the sauce, making it a great accompaniment to rice. Eggplant is rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, and the ground meat adds protein. The difficulty is moderate; you need to master frying eggplant and seasoning techniques, but with a bit of practice, beginners can succeed. From prep to serving takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Cut the eggplant into strips.
Chop the meat into mince; chop green onion, ginger, and garlic; dice the small chilies.
Make the fish-fragrant sauce: In a bowl, combine salt, MSG, sugar, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, vinegar, and water starch, and stir well.

Pour 300ml oil into the wok, heat on low (low heat is easier to control). When the oil is 70% hot (about 40 seconds on low, when smoke appears), fry the eggplant for two minutes until the edges turn slightly yellow, then remove. The excess oil can be saved for later cooking.
Leave 15-30ml oil in the wok, stir-fry the ground meat until it turns white, then remove.
Pour 15-30ml oil into the wok, add chili bean paste, white parts of green onion, ginger, and garlic, stir-fry until fragrant, then add the ground meat and stir-fry evenly.

Add 80-150ml water (enough to come about 80% up the eggplant), add the eggplant and the sauce, stir-fry vigorously to let the flavors meld and reduce the sauce. Finally, add the green onion, stir-fry evenly, and serve.

howtocook:meat_dish/麻婆豆腐/麻婆豆腐.name: Mapo Tofu
howtocook:meat_dish/麻婆豆腐/麻婆豆腐.description: This spicy and silky tofu dish replaces traditional doubanjiang with salted duck egg, offering a salty-savory flavor with a hint of numbing and spicy notes—perfect to whet the appetite. Rich in high-quality protein, calcium, iron, and other trace elements. Moderate difficulty, beginner-friendly, takes about 40 minutes in total.
steps: Mince garlic and ginger; set aside.
Cut bird's eye chilies into rings; set aside.
Finely chop the pork belly into ground pork (skip if already ground).
Add half the salt and light soy sauce to the ground pork; mix well; set aside.

Split the salted duck egg vertically with a knife (be careful), remove the yolk (must remove, otherwise it will be fishy), then mash the egg white into pieces about 2 mm x 2 mm—no need to be too fine; set aside.
Open the tofu package, use a fruit knife to cut the tofu in the box into pieces about 2.5 cm x 3 cm; set aside.
Heat the wok, add 10-15 ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Reduce to low heat, add garlic, ginger, chili rings, Sichuan peppercorns, salted duck egg, and garlic chili sauce; stir-fry for 20 seconds until fragrant.
Increase to medium heat, add ground pork, stir-fry for about 1 minute until the meat changes color.
Reduce to low heat, add tofu, sprinkle the remaining salt and light soy sauce evenly over the tofu.
Pour boiling water from the side of the wok (otherwise the tofu may break), just enough to submerge the tofu.
Turn to high heat, once the water boils, immediately reduce to medium heat; simmer for about 10 minutes.
When the liquid reduces to 1/5 and the tofu surface has taken on color, turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/麻辣香锅.name: Mala Spicy Pot
howtocook:meat_dish/麻辣香锅.description: A spicy and rich Sichuan home-style dish, combining vegetables, meat, and tofu products. The taste is numbing and spicy with rich layers. The combination of meat and vegetables provides high-quality protein and various vitamins, making it nutritious. The steps are clear; need to master blanching and stir-frying heat; it's somewhat challenging for beginners. Total preparation time is about 30 minutes.
steps: Bring a pot of water to a boil (enough water to submerge the ingredients in steps 2-4).
Add the greens to the boiling water, blanch for 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the boneless meat to the boiling water, blanch for 4 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Add the Beijing Mala instant noodles to the boiling water, cook for 2 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Discard the boiling water, dry the pot, add 105 grams of cooking oil, and heat over high heat for 30 seconds.
Add the Mala Xiang Guo seasoning, stir-fry for 20 seconds.
Add the dried chili peppers, stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add the blanched greens, reduce to medium heat, and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add the blanched boneless meat, stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add the cooked Beijing Mala instant noodles, toss with chopsticks for 1 minute.
Turn off the heat, serve.

ingredients.name: Boneless meat (pork, beef, chicken, fish balls, ham sausage)

howtocook:meat_dish/黄油鸡.name: Butter Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/黄油鸡.description: This is a classic Indian curry dish, featuring grilled then simmered chicken thighs in a sauce made with tomatoes, butter, and cream. The texture is rich and silky, with a sweet, sour, and mildly spicy flavor, and a unique blend of spices. Chicken, yogurt, and cashews provide ample protein and healthy fats, while tomatoes and spices offer vitamins and antioxidants. It's somewhat challenging to make, requiring control of heat and balance of spices. Including marinating time, it takes about 1 hour 45 minutes to complete.
steps: In a large bowl, mix together yogurt, ginger paste, garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, salt, and lemon juice.
Add chicken pieces and coat thoroughly with the marinade.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Heat 15ml of cooking oil in a pan over high heat.
Place the marinated chicken pieces into the pan (do not pour in the marinade).
Sear over high heat until both sides are browned (about 3 minutes per side); the chicken does not need to be fully cooked.
Remove and set aside.
Add tomato chunks, onion chunks, cashews, ginger, garlic, and 100ml of water to the pan.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium heat and cook for 15 minutes until all ingredients are completely soft.
Let cool slightly, then transfer to a blender and blend into a smooth puree.
Strain through a fine mesh sieve to remove solids, yielding a smooth sauce. Set aside.
In a wok or pan, melt 50g of butter over medium heat.
Pour in the strained tomato sauce.
Add 5g red chili powder, 5g coriander powder, 3g cumin powder, and 5g salt.
Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until the sauce darkens and oil separates.
Add the seared chicken pieces and toss to coat evenly.
Add 10g sugar (to balance the acidity of the tomatoes) and stir.
Pour in 100ml heavy cream and mix well.
Simmer over low heat for 10-12 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked and has absorbed the sauce.
Add 3g garam masala and 2g cardamom powder.
Sprinkle in 3g dried fenugreek leaves (crush them between your palms before adding).
Stir to combine, then turn off the heat.
Serve on a plate, optionally drizzling a little extra cream on top for decoration.

howtocook:meat_dish/黄焖鸡.name: Braised Chicken
howtocook:meat_dish/黄焖鸡.description: Braised Chicken is a home-style dish featuring chicken thighs, mushrooms, and green peppers. The broth is rich, the chicken is tender, with a savory and slightly sweet flavor that goes great with rice. It is nutritious, providing high-quality protein and mushroom polysaccharides. The caramelizing sugar step can be a bit challenging, but you can substitute with dark soy sauce. Overall, it's not difficult and beginners can handle it. It takes about 40 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Wash the chicken thighs and chop them into 4 cm pieces.
Slice the ginger and cut the dried chili peppers into small rings.
Slice the mushrooms. Cut the green peppers into long, thin horse-hoof shapes. If using dried mushrooms, rinse off dust, soak overnight, and reserve the mushroom soaking liquid.
If using potatoes, cut them into rolling cut chunks similar in size to the chicken.
Caramelize the sugar: Pour oil into the wok, add sugar to the cold oil (this is somewhat challenging; beginners can skip to stir-frying the chicken and substitute with dark soy sauce).
Heat slowly over low heat. As the oil temperature rises, the sugar will melt and turn a deep brown (stir constantly to prevent burning).
Quickly add the chicken pieces, turn up the heat, and stir-fry fast! Splash in the cooking wine and continue stir-frying for a moment.
Add the ginger slices and dried chili peppers.
Add the soy sauce and stir well.
Pour in the mushroom soaking liquid or water, just enough to cover the chicken.
Add the mushroom slices, white pepper, salt, and potatoes.
Stir everything together, then cover and simmer over medium-low heat for 15–20 minutes. If possible, transfer to a clay pot.
When the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened (don't reduce the sauce too much), finally add the green peppers.
Add MSG, toss to combine, then turn off the heat! The green peppers should be just cooked through—don't overcook.

howtocook:meat_dish/黄瓜炒肉.name: Cucumber Stir-fry with Pork
howtocook:meat_dish/黄瓜炒肉.description: This is a refreshing, crisp, and tender home-style quick dish. It is savory and slightly spicy with garlic aroma. Rich in protein and various vitamins, it helps cool and hydrate. It's friendly for beginners; just pay attention to the heat and you'll succeed. Estimated cooking time: about 25 minutes.
steps: Slice the lean pork into pieces, place in a bowl, add 10g cooking oil and light soy sauce, mix well, and marinate for 10 minutes.
Cut off the 5cm ends of the cucumber, and slice the rest diagonally into 0.5cm thin slices.
Put the cucumber slices into a bowl, sprinkle with 8g salt, mix well, and marinate for 5 minutes.
Peel the garlic cloves, flatten them, and mince into garlic paste; set aside.
Remove the stems from the bird's eye chilies and cut into even 0.5cm sections.
Heat the wok, add 40g oil, wait until the oil smokes, then add the minced garlic and bird's eye chilies and stir-fry 5 times.
Add the marinated lean pork and stir-fry until the meat is cooked and changes color.
Add the cucumber, add 2g salt, stir-fry over high heat evenly for half a minute, then remove from wok.
If you can't guarantee high heat, it might be a bit undercooked. Consider extending to 50 seconds.

howtocook:meat_dish/黑椒牛柳/黑椒牛柳.name: Black Pepper Beef
howtocook:meat_dish/黑椒牛柳/黑椒牛柳.description: Black Pepper Beef is a popular home-style stir-fry. It features a rich black pepper flavor, tender beef slices, and sweet crunchy onions and bell peppers, balancing meat and vegetables. The taste appeals to all ages. Beef is rich in high-quality protein and iron, while bell peppers and onions provide various vitamins. This dish is very beginner-friendly; from prep to serving, it usually takes under an hour.
steps: Cut the beef into strips, preferably no longer than 8 cm, about 5-10 mm thick, and about 1 cm wide (not strict).
Marinate the beef with the marinade. Mix well and let it sit. Refer to the product instructions for the amount and time. You can extend the marinating time but not shorten it.
If using a liquid marinade, sprinkle a layer of black pepper three minutes before the end of marinating, then add starch, mix well again, and let sit for 20 minutes.
Turn on the heat and heat the pan. Add peanut oil.
When you see a faint smoke rising from the oil, add the beef and stir-fry.
Stir-fry over medium-high heat for 2 minutes until the beef surface changes color (no obvious redness; any red spots indicate insufficient stir-frying). Be careful of oil splatter.
Add the onion and bell peppers, and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add salt, sprinkle another portion of black pepper, and stir-fry for 30 seconds until well combined.
Once the onion has softened, turn off the heat, remove from the pan, and plate.

howtocook:meat_dish/黔式腊肠娃娃菜/黔式腊肠娃娃菜.name: Guizhou-Style Sausage with Baby Cabbage
howtocook:meat_dish/黔式腊肠娃娃菜/黔式腊肠娃娃菜.description: This dish is savory and delicious, blending the rich aroma of Guizhou-style sausage with the sweetness of baby cabbage. It is a rare non-spicy flavor in Southwestern cuisine, suitable for all tastes. Baby cabbage is rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, paired with the protein and fat of sausage for balanced nutrition. The cooking process is extremely simple, requiring no mastery of heat or extra seasoning. It takes only 15 minutes from prep to serving, making it perfect for beginners and busy days.
steps: Add 750ml water to the pot and heat until boiling.
Add the sausage, set a timer for 13 minutes.
Add the baby cabbage, set a timer for 2 minutes.
Turn off the heat, remove the sausage and baby cabbage.
Cut the baby cabbage into segments and slice the sausage; arrange on a plate.

howtocook:semi-finished/凉皮.name: Liangpi
howtocook:semi-finished/凉皮.description: Liangpi is a homestyle snack originating from Shaanxi. It has a chewy and smooth texture, sour and spicy flavor that whets the appetite, and a rich sesame paste aroma. The sesame paste and gluten provide plant protein and calcium, while the crisp cucumber and bean sprouts add vitamins and dietary fiber. The difficulty is moderate; it requires simple blanching, shredding, and sauce mixing, so beginners can manage it. From prep to table takes about 40 minutes, perfect for summer meals.
steps: Add 500ml water to a pot. Bring to a boil.
Put the bean sprouts into the pot and cook over high heat for 60 seconds. Remove the bean sprouts, rinse under cold water, and set aside on a plate.
Shred the cucumber and set aside on a plate.
Peel 10g garlic cloves, put them in a mortar with 1g salt. Pound into garlic paste, add 10g tap water. Set aside.
Note: Store-bought liangpi usually has cooking oil on the surface; you can rinse with tap water. Same for gluten.
Note: After washing the gluten, squeeze out excess water by hand (no need to be too forceful).

Prepare a small bowl, add 3g salt, 2g chicken bouillon, 5g light soy sauce, 1g dark soy sauce, 1g sesame oil, 2g oyster sauce, 5g Zhenjiang vinegar (salt and vinegar can be adjusted to taste; these amounts are for average taste).
Add 25-35g warm water (depending on desired saltiness) to the above seasonings, stir with chopsticks to dissolve. Set aside to cool.
Note: The following measurements are for one serving. For n people, multiply accordingly.
Take out a small bowl and put the prepared sesame paste in it.
Add 4g salt, 3g chicken bouillon, 5g light soy sauce, 1g dark soy sauce, 3g oyster sauce.
Use chopsticks to mix the seasonings with the sesame paste.
Add 10g water and mix well.
Repeat the previous step 2-3 times (depending on desired thickness of sesame paste).
Take out the previously prepared basin and add the prepared liangpi.
Pour in the salted water mixture, mix with chopsticks. Then transfer to a small bowl (including the liquid).
Place bean sprouts on top of the liangpi, then add the gluten.
Pour the prepared sesame paste over the gluten.
Sprinkle shredded cucumber on top.
Add chili oil if desired.
Your flavorful homestyle liangpi is ready!

howtocook:semi-finished/半成品意面.name: Semi-finished Pasta
howtocook:semi-finished/半成品意面.description: Using store-bought semi-finished ingredients, this Western-style quick meal features springy, chewy pasta paired with a rich, ready-made sauce. It's primarily carbohydrates for quick energy, with some protein. Extremely easy, even beginners can't go wrong—from stove to plate in about 2 minutes.
steps: Heat the pan.
Pour 50 ml of water into the pan.
Add the pasta and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Pour in the sauce and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Plate and serve.
Place the pasta in a microwave-safe dish.
Pour the included sauce over the pasta.
Add 50 ml of water.
Microwave at 700W for 2 minutes.
Remove, stir, and serve.

howtocook:semi-finished/懒人蛋挞/懒人蛋挞.name: Lazy Egg Tarts
howtocook:semi-finished/懒人蛋挞/懒人蛋挞.description: This dessert uses ready-made tart shells and egg tart mixture. The crust is flaky and fragrant, the filling is silky smooth with caramel spots. It's the easiest version of classic Cantonese dim sum. The main ingredients, eggs and milk, provide high-quality protein and calcium, making it suitable as a family snack. The whole process only requires assembly and baking, and is friendly even for absolute beginners. From preheating the oven to finishing takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C for 10 minutes.
Place the tart shells on a baking tray. Pour about 10ml of egg tart mixture into each shell, adjusting based on shell size; usually fill up to 4/5 full.
Put the baking tray into the oven, bake at 190°C with both top and bottom heat for 10-20 minutes. To quickly get caramelized spots on the filling, increase the top heat to 200-210°C.
Done when the filling has caramelized spots and the tart shells are fully puffed and oily.

howtocook:semi-finished/炸薯条/炸薯条.name: French Fries
howtocook:semi-finished/炸薯条/炸薯条.description: French fries are crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, golden and savory, a popular fast food snack. Potatoes provide carbohydrates and dietary fiber, but frying or air frying makes them high in calories. Using semi-finished fries and an air fryer makes the process simple, even for beginners. From preheating to serving, it takes about 25 minutes.
steps: When opening a large package of semi-finished fries, make a small opening to effectively reduce frost on the fries during long-term storage.
Plug in, preheat at 200°C for 5 minutes.
The purpose of preheating is to ensure the temperature inside the basket is at the required cooking temperature when you add the food.
Note: take out the fries only after preheating; do not let them soften before frying.
Place the fries in the air fryer, cook at 200°C for 20 minutes.
When taking out the fries, note that semi-finished fries already have oil, so use tongs or a suitable tool to avoid contact.
At 5–10 minutes, shake the basket to ensure even heating and prevent sticking.
At 10–15 minutes, take out the basket and spray a layer of oil covering about 2/3 of the surface area of the fries.
For crispy fries, after removing, shake the basket to let air pass over the fries, then plate; for soft fries, plate directly. Best served with dipping sauce or drizzled with sauce.

howtocook:semi-finished/牛油火锅底料.name: Beef Tallow Hot Pot Base
howtocook:semi-finished/牛油火锅底料.description: This Chongqing hot pot base uses a large amount of beef tallow as the base, combined with Ciba chili and various spices. The resulting base is spicy, rich, and intensely aromatic, making it the soul for blanching tripe, duck intestines, and other ingredients. Beef tallow and lard provide rich energy; chili and Sichuan pepper warm the body, dispel cold, and stimulate appetite. However, it is high in fat, so consume in moderation. The process requires precise control of oil temperature and stir-frying heat, which is challenging for beginners. From prep to completion, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Place the pot over high heat, add beef tallow and heat to 80% hot (240±10°C). Add 100g each of old ginger, scallions, onion, and garlic. Fry until dry (to absorb the gamey smell of beef tallow), then remove and discard.
Add (salad oil or vegetable oil) and pure lard. Wait until the oil temperature drops to 50% hot (150±10°C), then add Ciba chili and stir-fry continuously for 5-8 minutes.
Add doubanjiang (fermented broad bean paste) and stir to break it up. Switch to medium-low heat and slowly stir-fry until the residue turns slightly white and sandy (making a rustling sound).
When the oil turns cherry red in appearance, add 150g sliced ginger and 100g garlic, stir-fry until fragrant, about 15 seconds.
Add fermented black beans and dòumǔzi (fermented soybean paste) and stir-fry until fragrant. Add red Sichuan pepper and cumin, stir-fry until fragrant.
(For aged oil) At this point, add the whole spices.
Add malt powder and stir to combine, then add baijiu (Chinese liquor) and stir to combine.
Remove from pot and place in a container. Let it sit in a low-temperature environment (10-20°C) for 5 days for best results before use.
Remove from pot and place in a container. Add dried chili flakes and stir to combine. Let it sit for 24 hours, waiting to make aged oil.
Pour the base into a pot, add 3/5 boiling water, and bring to a boil over high heat (base: 2/5, boiling water: 3/5).
After boiling, foam will appear on the surface. Skim off the foam.
Switch to medium-low heat and simmer to extract flavor (about 25-30 minutes), then strain out the solids.
After the oil and water separate in the container, skim off the oil on top (scoop out the oil) and place into another container.
Pour the skimmed oil back into a clean pot and heat until the moisture in the oil is evaporated. Remove from pot and place into a container. This is the aged hot pot oil.

howtocook:semi-finished/空气炸锅羊排/空气炸锅羊排.name: Air Fryer Lamb Chops
howtocook:semi-finished/空气炸锅羊排/空气炸锅羊排.description: This dish is super lazy-friendly. The air fryer renders excess fat, giving a crispy exterior and tender interior with rich garlic flavor. The taste mainly depends on the quality of the lamb chops. Lamb is rich in high-quality protein and trace elements like iron and zinc, which help warm and nourish the body and boost immunity. The cooking difficulty is moderate; beginners can easily handle it. The actual hands-on time is only 10 minutes, and including marinating, you can serve it in about 40 minutes.
steps: Place lamb chops in a bowl and rinse with water to remove blood.
Pat lamb chops dry with paper towels. Spread black pepper mixed seasoning and garlic sauce on both sides. Let marinate for 20 minutes.
Place a foil bowl on the rack, put lamb chops on the rack, sprinkle with dried basil, place butter or barbecue seasoning on top. Air fry at 180°C for 10 minutes.

Flip lamb chops, sprinkle with dried basil, add butter (scoop from the foil bowl) or barbecue seasoning on top. Air fry at 180°C for 5 minutes (you can add a bit more time if desired; this is the time to avoid burning).

howtocook:semi-finished/空气炸锅鸡翅中/空气炸锅鸡翅中.name: Air Fryer Chicken Wings (Middle Section)
howtocook:semi-finished/空气炸锅鸡翅中/空气炸锅鸡翅中.description: This is a convenient and easy home-style quick dish. The chicken wings are crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with their own fatty aroma, more satisfying than fast food. Chicken wings are rich in high-quality protein and fat, providing ample energy. Very friendly for beginners; actual preparation takes just a few minutes, and the whole process takes about 40 minutes to serve.
steps: Take chicken wings out of the fridge, place them skin side down in a foil-lined baking pan, sprinkle with dried basil, cover with plastic wrap, and let thaw naturally for 20 minutes.
Sprinkle with dried basil, air fry at 200°C for 10 minutes.
Flip, sprinkle with dried basil, air fry at 200°C for 7 minutes.

howtocook:semi-finished/速冻水饺.name: Frozen Dumplings
howtocook:semi-finished/速冻水饺.description: This is a home-style noodle dish from China, made quickly with store-bought frozen dumplings. Thin-skinned and generously filled, they offer a savory and fresh taste. Paired with dipping sauces like vinegar, ginger, and garlic, the flavors gain extra depth. As a meal, it provides ample protein and carbs, making it nutritious and filling. No difficulties in preparation, even beginners can handle it, and it takes only about 15 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Over medium heat, pour water into a pot and wait for it to boil.
Pour the dumplings into the pot.
Before pouring, you can briefly rinse the dumplings with water if desired.

After adding the dumplings, stir the water with a spatula or ladle, but be careful not to hit the dumplings to avoid tearing the wrappers on the pot or sticking together, which can cause undercooked areas where they stick.
Stirring frequency doesn't need to be high; average 3 seconds of stirring every 30 seconds. Once the dumplings float, this step is no longer needed.

After the dumplings float and the water boils again, scoop up one dumpling with a ladle to check. If the wrapper is undercooked, ladle in 80ml of cold water to lower the temperature, then continue cooking until boiling again. Repeat this checking and stirring process; at most two additions of water will cook them through.
Once all dumplings have floated (about 8 minutes after adding them), use a spatula or slotted spoon to transfer the dumplings to a plate or bowl. Serve immediately.

After eating, wait for the pot water to cool, then pour out the water and wash the pot promptly with dish soap; otherwise, the cooked flour from the dumplings will form a sticky residue on the pot walls.

howtocook:semi-finished/速冻汤圆/速冻汤圆.name: Frozen Tangyuan
howtocook:semi-finished/速冻汤圆/速冻汤圆.description: Frozen tangyuan is a quick and convenient microwave dessert with soft and chewy skin and sweet filling. It is a traditional Chinese snack. It mainly provides carbohydrates and can quickly replenish energy. The method is very easy, even beginners can do it easily. From preparation to serving takes about 6 minutes.
steps: Take out the frozen tangyuan and put them in a bowl.
Pour boiling water until the tangyuan are submerged.
Microwave on high for 4 minutes.
If the tangyuan have all absorbed water and expanded, they are cooked.
If not cooked, heat for another 1 minute.

howtocook:semi-finished/速冻馄饨.name: Frozen Wontons
howtocook:semi-finished/速冻馄饨.description: Frozen wontons have thin skins and juicy filling, with a clear and flavorful broth. They're a quick and easy home-style noodle dish that provides carbs and protein for a fast energy boost. The cooking method is simple and beginner-friendly—just use a rice cooker. From boiling water to serving, it takes about 55 minutes.
steps: Pour water into the rice cooker, set it to simmer or cook mode for 35 minutes. At this point, when you open the lid, the water should be boiling.
Carefully place the frozen wontons into the water, being careful not to burn yourself.
Before adding to the rice cooker, you can briefly rinse them with water.
If the wontons come with a seasoning packet, add it to the water at this point.
Close the lid, set to the same simmer or cook mode for 20 minutes.
Ladle all the wontons into a bowl, along with enough water to cover them.
If you didn't add the seasoning packet earlier, now you can add salt, chicken bouillon, white pepper, and sesame oil to taste.
You can also garnish with 5–8 cilantro leaves (only for those who are not sensitive to the taste of cilantro).

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.note: Add to taste if no seasoning packet

ingredients.name: Chicken bouillon
ingredients.note: Add to taste if no seasoning packet

ingredients.name: White pepper
ingredients.note: Add to taste if no seasoning packet

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.note: Add to taste if no seasoning packet

howtocook:soup/勾芡香菇汤/勾芡香菇汤.name: Thickened Mushroom Soup
howtocook:soup/勾芡香菇汤/勾芡香菇汤.description: Thickened mushroom soup is rich in flavor, smooth and pleasant, a quick home-style soup. Mushrooms are rich in amino acids and polysaccharides, which help boost immunity. The process requires some basic heat control and thickening skills, but is still beginner-friendly. From prep to finish, it takes about 25 minutes.
steps: Slice the mushrooms (each slice 0.5-1 cm thick; thicker slices have more chew), place in a large bowl, add 2g salt, and soak for 15 minutes.
Put cornstarch in a small bowl, add 50ml water, stir until dissolved and no lumps (i.e., cornstarch slurry).
Discard the salted water from the bowl, and squeeze out excess moisture from the mushrooms (to facilitate frying) [Optional].
Heat oil over low heat until small bubbles appear (about 30s on low, depending on your stove), add mushrooms, fry each side for 10s [Optional].
Add 300ml boiling water, increase to medium heat, and cook for another 3-5 minutes.
Pour in the cornstarch slurry, stir the soup briefly, then add 3g salt and 3g chicken bouillon, and finally sprinkle with chopped green onions before serving.

howtocook:soup/奶油蘑菇汤.name: Creamy Mushroom Soup
howtocook:soup/奶油蘑菇汤.description: This is a classic French creamy soup with a silky smooth texture, blending the flavors of mushrooms and milk. Mushrooms are rich in dietary fiber and trace elements, while milk provides high-quality protein and calcium. The recipe is very simple, perfect for beginners, and takes about 30 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Slice the white mushrooms and set aside. Mince the onion and set aside.
Place butter in a frying pan, melt over low heat, then add the onion and cook until translucent.
Add the white mushrooms and stir-fry until they release water and soften. Sprinkle in the flour and stir well.
Add the milk and water, stir well, then bring to a boil over low heat, stirring constantly to prevent sticking.
Reduce to low heat and cook for about 10 minutes until the soup thickens.
Pour in the heavy cream and heat for another minute. Season with salt and black pepper.
Turn off the heat. Optionally, use a blender to puree into a smooth soup.

howtocook:soup/小米粥.name: Millet Congee
howtocook:soup/小米粥.description: Millet congee is a common home-style porridge in northern China. It has a smooth texture and rich grain aroma, and the millet oil that forms on top is especially nourishing for the stomach. It is rich in protein, fat, carbohydrates, and carotene, with vitamin B1 content among the highest in grains. It is easy to digest and absorb. The method is very simple, even beginners can easily master it. From boiling water to serving, it takes about 30-35 minutes.
steps: 100 grams of millet, put in a bowl, gently rinse once with water (stir with your hand, pour off the water, just to remove surface dust, do not rub!!!)
Boil the water, make sure it's boiling!!!
When the water is boiling vigorously, pour the millet into the pot. (A very important step that is easily overlooked.)
Stir so the millet doesn't stick to the bottom, continue boiling over high heat for 6-10 minutes, stirring occasionally in between.
Reduce to medium-low heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes. Leave the lid slightly ajar to prevent the millet oil from escaping. Stir a few more times to prevent sticking to the bottom.

howtocook:soup/排骨山药玉米汤/排骨山药玉米汤.name: Spare Ribs, Chinese Yam and Corn Soup
howtocook:soup/排骨山药玉米汤/排骨山药玉米汤.description: This spare ribs, yam, and corn soup is sweet and rich, with soft yam and sweet corn infused with the savory flavor of ribs. It's a family-friendly soup for all ages, rich in protein, dietary fiber, and vitamins, beneficial for the spleen and stomach, and boosts immunity. The method is simple, with clear prep and simmering steps, easy for beginners. It takes about 1 hour.
steps: Ingredient prep:
Wash and peel the carrot, then cut into rolling cuts about 3 cm.
Wash the corn, chop into small cylindrical pieces about 4 cm.
Peel the Chinese yam (wear gloves to avoid skin irritation from the mucilage), cut into long sections about 4 cm, and set aside.
Blanch the spareribs:
Add enough cold water to a pot, then add the rinsed spareribs.
Add about 10g ginger slices, a scallion knot, and 15ml cooking wine.
Bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, wait for 3 minutes, skimming off any foam with a spoon.
Remove the spareribs, rinse with warm water (about 40°C) to remove impurities, drain, and set aside.
Stir-fry the spareribs:
Heat a wok, pour in 10ml cooking oil, wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Add the remaining ginger slices (about 10g) and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add all the prepared spareribs, stir-fry over medium heat for about 1 minute until the surface is lightly browned.
Simmering process:
Add 1500ml to 2000ml of drinking water all at once.
Add all the prepared carrot, corn, and Chinese yam.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes.
Seasoning and serving:
Add 6g to 8g salt, stir well with a spoon.
Keep on low heat and simmer for another 2 minutes to let the salt infuse.
Turn off the heat, sprinkle with chopped scallions to taste, and serve.

howtocook:soup/排骨苦瓜汤/排骨苦瓜汤.name: Pork Rib and Bitter Melon Soup
howtocook:soup/排骨苦瓜汤/排骨苦瓜汤.description: Pork rib and bitter melon soup is a clear, savory, and slightly sweet Cantonese home-style soup. The ribs are tender, and the bitter melon clears heat and cuts grease. Rich in protein and vitamins, the dried shrimp also add calcium. The process requires blanching to remove odor and carefully removing the bitter melon pith, which can be challenging for beginners, but an electric pressure cooker simplifies it greatly. Traditional simmering takes 2 to 4 hours, so plan ahead 4 hours; with a pressure cooker, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Wash the ribs, cut into small pieces about 4cm ±2cm by 3±2cm (if you don't have a cleaver, ask the butcher).
In a wok, add 700ml cold water and the ribs, heat to boiling, then turn off heat and drain the ribs.
Cut the bitter melon in half lengthwise, thoroughly remove the seeds and white pith, then slice into 0.5±0.3 cm strips, and wash.
In a soup pot, add 1100ml water and the ribs, bring to a boil over high heat.

Once boiling, add the bitter melon and dried shrimp, reduce to low heat, and simmer for 120–240 minutes (after 120 minutes, you can test a piece of meat; if it separates easily from the bone, it's done).
When done, add salt and white pepper. Recommended amount: 0.3 to 0.6g salt and 0.1 to 0.5g white pepper per 250ml of soup.
Pour 1000ml water, ribs, dried shrimp, and bitter melon into the pressure cooker.
Select the soup function and cook (usually pressure cook for 45 minutes).
When done, season with salt and pepper as with the stovetop method.

howtocook:soup/昂刺鱼豆腐汤/昂刺鱼豆腐汤.name: Yellow Catfish and Tofu Soup
howtocook:soup/昂刺鱼豆腐汤/昂刺鱼豆腐汤.description: Yellow catfish and tofu soup is a tender, milky and flavorful home-style soup. The fish has few bones and tender meat, the tofu is smooth, with a slight warmth from pepper, suitable for all ages. The fish is rich in high-quality protein, and paired with tofu it provides calcium, making it nutritious and easy to absorb. The cooking process is not complicated; just pay attention to removing the fishy smell and prevent sticking when frying. Beginners can handle it. Estimated cooking time: about 40 minutes.
steps: After cleaning the fish, (especially the blood inside the belly; if not cleaned, it will have a fishy smell), place in a large bowl, add cooking wine, 10g ginger slices, and 5g salt. Marinate for 15 minutes.
Cut the tofu into pieces, soak in cold water for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.

Before frying the fish, rub the pan with ginger slices to prevent sticking. Pour in oil (15ml per piece of fish), heat until hot, then add the fish and fry for 2-3 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally to prevent sticking, and flip once.

After all the fish is fried, pour in boiling water, 5ml cooking wine, and ginger slices. Switch from low to high heat, cover the pot, and boil on high heat for 10 minutes (add enough water as some will evaporate).
Once the soup turns white, add the prepared tofu, reduce to medium heat and cook for another 5 minutes. Add 10g salt and 3g pepper to season. Finally, sprinkle with chopped green onions and serve.

howtocook:soup/朱雀汤/朱雀汤.name: Zhuque Soup
howtocook:soup/朱雀汤/朱雀汤.description: Zhuque Soup is a quick home-cook soup from eastern Henan, with a smooth and sweet taste and the rich aroma of sesame oil. Eggs provide high-quality protein. It's very simple to make—just boil water and pour it over the eggs—and can be ready in under ten minutes. It also helps clear heat and reduce internal heat.
steps: Beat the eggs in a bowl, then add sesame oil.
Bring water to a boil, then quickly pour it into the bowl with the eggs while it's still boiling.
Add sugar.

howtocook:soup/玉米排骨汤/玉米排骨汤.name: Corn and Pork Rib Soup
howtocook:soup/玉米排骨汤/玉米排骨汤.description: Corn and pork rib soup is a delicious and sweet home-style soup. The ribs are tender, and the corn and carrots bring natural sweetness. It is rich in protein, vitamins, and dietary fiber, with balanced nutrition. Simple to make, beginner-friendly. Just blanch and sauté the ribs, then simmer for about an hour. From prep to finish, it takes about an hour and a half.
steps: Cut the scallions into 3-4 cm sections, and pound them with the flat side of the knife.
Chop the corn into small pieces.
Cut the carrot into rolling-cut chunks.
Peel the ginger and cut into large slices.
Chop the fresh ribs into small pieces.
Put the ribs in a pot with cold water, add scallions, ginger, and cooking wine to blanch. Bring to a boil over high heat, skim off the foam, then remove the ribs and drain.
Heat the pan with cold oil, add the sliced ginger and ribs, and stir-fry. When the ribs are slightly golden, add vinegar (to help tenderize the meat) and stir-fry for another minute.
Pour in boiling water all at once (enough for the whole process, do not add more later). Bring to a boil over high heat.
First add the corn and black pepper, cover and simmer over low heat for 20 minutes. Then add the carrots, cover and continue simmering over low heat for 40 minutes.
Seasoning is simple: three minutes before serving, add nothing but salt. Finally, sprinkle with a handful of chopped scallions.

howtocook:soup/生汆丸子汤.name: Fresh Poached Meatball Soup
howtocook:soup/生汆丸子汤.description: Fresh Poached Meatball Soup has a tender and springy texture, making it a home-style soup with authentic flavor. Pork provides high-quality protein, and with ingredients like wood ear mushrooms and daylily buds, it is nutritionally balanced. The process requires hand-chopping the meat and mastering the heat for shaping the meatballs, which is somewhat challenging and more suitable for cooks with some experience. It takes about 1 hour from preparation to serving.
steps: Cut the meat, separating fat and lean in a 3:7 ratio.
Chop with the knife, pound with the back of the knife to break and loosen the meat fibers.
Chop and pound until minced. For best flavor, chop by hand; machine-ground meat is too sticky.
Per 500g meat, 6g salt, 1g pepper.
Mix well with your hands.
Add the scallion-ginger-Sichuan peppercorn water in batches, stirring and kneading to let the meat absorb the water. Per 500g minced meat, 80g of the water.
Add egg whites and continue stirring in one direction.
Add 40g potato starch and mix well.
Add cooked soybean oil to maintain a tender and springy texture.
Bring water to a boil in a pot, then reduce to low heat, keeping it at a gentle simmer.
Using your hand, squeeze out meatballs.
Once they all float, cook on low heat for 1 minute.
Place vermicelli at the bottom of the bowl.
Add wood ear mushrooms, daylily buds, and chopped scallions. Season with salt, pepper, and chicken bouillon powder.
Pour the soup and meatballs into the bowl.
Drizzle 3-5 drops of sesame oil.
Add a small cilantro sprig.

howtocook:soup/番茄牛肉蛋花汤.name: Tomato Beef Egg Drop Soup
howtocook:soup/番茄牛肉蛋花汤.description: Tomato Beef Egg Drop Soup is a sweet and sour appetizing home-style soup with a tender and smooth texture. Beef is rich in high-quality protein and iron, tomatoes provide lycopene and vitamin C, and eggs supplement lecithin. The method is simple, even beginners can master it easily, and it can be served in about half an hour.
steps: Slice the beef thinly
Cut the tomatoes into small pieces
Chop the scallion into small pieces
Slice the ginger
Mince the garlic
Place the beef in a bowl
Add salt and pepper, marinate for 15-20 minutes
Bring water to a boil
Add ginger slices and beef slices, cook until beef changes color
Add tomato pieces, cook until tomatoes soften
Beat the eggs, slowly pour into the pot, stir with chopsticks to form egg flowers
Add salt and pepper to taste
Finally add scallion pieces, then serve

howtocook:soup/皮蛋瘦肉粥.name: Century Egg and Lean Pork Congee
howtocook:soup/皮蛋瘦肉粥.description: Century egg and lean pork congee is a classic Cantonese home-style porridge. The base is smooth and silky, with the rich flavor of century egg blending with the tenderness of lean pork, creating a warm and delicious taste. It is rich in carbohydrates and high-quality protein, and when paired with vegetables, it offers balanced nutrition and is easy to digest. Although there are many steps, the process is straightforward, and even beginners can follow along. The estimated cooking time is about 1.5 hours. Using the rice cooker's timer or keep-warm function makes it especially convenient as a breakfast option.
steps: Rice - wash - place into rice cooker inner pot - add 1 liter drinking water
Lean pork - wash - lightly pat dry - add 10 ml cooking oil - rub evenly - place into rice cooker inner pot
Century egg - remove shell - wash - cut in half - separate egg white and yolk - roughly chop egg white - crumble egg yolk - place into rice cooker inner pot
Ginger - wash - peel - remove any yellow or black inedible parts - julienne - place into rice cooker inner pot
Scallion - wash - remove root - remove any yellow or black inedible parts - chop finely - place into small bowl and set aside
Cilantro - wash - remove root - remove any yellow or black inedible parts - chop finely - place into small bowl and set aside
Lettuce - wash - remove root - remove any yellow or black inedible parts - chop finely - place into small bowl and set aside
Soy sauce + oyster sauce + salt + white pepper powder - mix well - place into small bowl and set aside
Main ingredients - cook using rice cooker's porridge mode until done

Toppings - after main ingredients are cooked, blanch the lettuce in hot water separately, then open the lid and add together with the remaining toppings (scallion, cilantro) to the main ingredients, stirring well
Sauce - after main ingredients are cooked, open the lid and add the sauce together with the other toppings, stirring well

howtocook:soup/米粥.name: Rice Porridge
howtocook:soup/米粥.description: Plain porridge is light and soft, a warm stomach-soothing staple for home cooking. The pure rice aroma is suitable for all ages. Rich in carbohydrates and a small amount of protein, it is easy to digest and absorb, with health benefits for the spleen, stomach, and lungs. Simple to make, just control the heat and water amount; even beginners can do it easily. Total time about 25 minutes.
steps: (Optional) Mix Mo ml of oil with the washed rice, ensuring each grain is lightly coated.
(Optional) Refrigerate the rice-oil mixture for Tc.
Add rice and water to the pot.
Turn heat to high and heat to T1.
Before T1, reduce the heat. If you forget, water may overflow and extinguish the flame. Very dangerous!
Heat to Tr. Turn off the heat at Tr.

howtocook:soup/紫菜蛋花汤.name: Seaweed Egg Drop Soup
howtocook:soup/紫菜蛋花汤.description: Seaweed egg drop soup is a light and delicious home-style soup, a simple Chinese soup dish. Seaweed is rich in iodine, calcium, and dietary fiber, while eggs provide high-quality protein, making a balanced combination. The method is very simple and beginner-friendly, taking only about 20 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Soak dried seaweed in water for 15 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Heat the pot, add 1.5 L water, 5 ml oil, and 2 g salt. Bring to a boil, then add the seaweed.
After the seaweed boils for 3 minutes, slowly pour in the beaten eggs and cook for 30 seconds before removing from heat.
Sprinkle with scallions and turn to low heat for 20 seconds.
Turn off the heat, add a few drops of sesame oil before serving. Some also add a bit of dried shrimp for extra flavor.

howtocook:soup/罗宋汤.name: Borscht
howtocook:soup/罗宋汤.description: A home-style soup that originated from Russian beet soup, adapted in Shanghai. It features tomato and beef as main flavors, with a sweet and sour, rich, and savory taste. Rich in high-quality protein, lycopene, and various vitamins, nutritionally balanced. Simple to make, beginner-friendly, takes about 2-3 hours total.
steps: Dice onion, carrot, and parsley into 1cm cubes.
Cut red sausage and potato into 2cm chunks.
Remove the core from cabbage, then tear into 2cm pieces.
Season beef with 3g salt and 3g black pepper, marinate for 5 minutes.
Heat a frying pan and add vegetable oil.
Sear beef until browned on all sides (can be rare inside, be careful not to burn), then set aside.
Heat a soup pot, add olive oil, diced onion, carrot, and parsley.
Cook until onion is translucent, then add tomato paste and canned tomatoes.
Add beef and potato chunks, stir to combine.
Add water to cover ingredients, cook over medium heat for 1 hour.
Uncover, add cabbage and red sausage, stir well.
Cook uncovered over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat, add remaining 15g salt, mix well, and serve.

howtocook:soup/羊肉汤/羊肉汤.name: Lamb Soup
howtocook:soup/羊肉汤/羊肉汤.description: Lamb soup is a home-style warming soup, with milky white broth, tender meat, and the fresh aroma and slight spiciness of scallions and white pepper. Rich in protein, calcium, iron and other nutrients, it warms the spleen and stomach and helps ward off cold. The steps are simple, even beginners can master it easily, and the whole cooking process takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Cut lamb into pieces 5cm long and 0.5cm wide
Cut scallions into small sections
Put lamb in pot, add 1000ml room temperature water, add cooking wine and scallions
After boiling for 2 minutes, remove lamb, rinse with room temperature water, and drain
Heat oil in pot, add lamb, stir-fry for 2 minutes until lamb surface turns slightly yellow
Add boiling water, turn to high heat
After 5 minutes, add white pepper powder and salt, continue boiling for 5 minutes
After removing from heat, add cilantro and cumin powder, stir well

howtocook:soup/腊八粥.name: Laba Porridge
howtocook:soup/腊八粥.description: Laba porridge is a traditional festive sweet porridge with a thick and sticky texture, blending the complex aromas of various grains, beans, and dried fruits. Rich in carbohydrates, minerals, and vitamins, lotus seeds also help calm the mind and promote sleep, making it suitable for those under work stress. Although it contains many ingredients, the steps are simple—just slow simmer patiently, beginner-friendly. Excluding soaking prep time, cooking takes about 3 hours.
steps: Wash the mung beans, red beans, peanuts, soybeans, peas, and kidney beans. Place them in a clean glass bowl and add enough drinking water to fill 3/4 of the bowl. Soak overnight (or at least 8 hours).
Wash the rice, glutinous rice, coix seeds, black rice, millet, and lotus seeds. Place them in a clean glass bowl and add enough drinking water to fill 3/4 of the bowl. Soak for 3 hours.

Drain the soaking water from the bowl in step 1 (with mung beans, red beans, peanuts, soybeans, peas, kidney beans). Transfer the drained ingredients to a porridge pot. Add 1 liter of drinking water (or enough to submerge the ingredients by one thumb's width). Bring to a boil over high heat, then cover and simmer over low heat for 30 minutes.

Drain the soaking water from the bowl in step 2 (with rice, glutinous rice, coix seeds, black rice, millet, lotus seeds). Add the drained ingredients to the pot. Cover and simmer over low heat for 60 minutes.
Wash the red dates, longan, chestnuts, walnuts, and raisins (cut the red dates into small pieces). Add them along with rock sugar to the pot. Cover and simmer over low heat for 60 minutes.
Once the porridge becomes thick and sticky, turn off the heat. Serve in bowls and enjoy.

ingredients.name: Rock sugar (or white sugar, for seasoning)

howtocook:soup/菌菇炖乳鸽/菌菇炖乳鸽.name: Mushroom and Squab Stew
howtocook:soup/菌菇炖乳鸽/菌菇炖乳鸽.description: Mushroom and squab stew has a clear broth, tender meat, and a harmonious blend of mushroom aroma and squab freshness. It is a home-style Cantonese stew. Squab is rich in high-quality protein and various amino acids, while mushrooms provide polysaccharides and dietary fiber, making it gently nourishing. The dish requires blanching the squab to remove gaminess, then simmering with corn and mushrooms. It demands some attention to heat and timing, but beginners can manage with care. From preparation to serving, it takes about 45 minutes using a pressure cooker or just over 1 hour with a clay pot.
steps: Rinse the squab that the helpful stallholder has prepared under cold water.
Place the cleaned squab in a pot of cold water, add 15ml cooking wine and ginger. Bring to a boil, then remove the squab immediately to avoid nutrient loss.
Put the squab into a pressure cooker or clay pot. Add enough water to cover the squab. Add 20g ginger, 200g corn, and 100g mushrooms.
When done, ladle into a bowl and add 3-5g salt.

howtocook:soup/西红柿鸡蛋汤.name: Tomato Egg Drop Soup
howtocook:soup/西红柿鸡蛋汤.description: Tomato Egg Drop Soup is a sweet and sour, appetizing, and smooth home-style soup. It is extremely simple to make and suitable for all ages. Rich in lycopene, vitamin C, and high-quality protein, it is nutritious and easy to absorb. Very beginner-friendly, quick and time-saving, estimated cooking time about 15 minutes.
steps: Wash the tomatoes and cut into pieces.
Chop the green onion, ginger, and garlic.
Crack the eggs into a bowl and beat well with chopsticks (or a whisk).
Heat the pan and add 15 ml of oil. When a wisp of smoke rises from the oil, add the green onion, ginger, and garlic, and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the tomatoes and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Pour in water, about 1.2 times the height of the ingredients in the pan, and add salt.
When the soup boils, pour in the beaten eggs and use chopsticks to stir the eggs into ribbons. Add MSG and sesame oil.
Wait 30 seconds, then turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:soup/金针菇汤.name: Enoki Mushroom Soup
howtocook:soup/金针菇汤.description: This is a light and refreshing home-style soup that is delicious and easy to make. Enoki mushrooms are rich in amino acids and dietary fiber, and adding eggs provides high-quality protein. The recipe is very simple, even beginners can easily handle it. It takes about 10 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Tear the enoki mushrooms apart by hand, the more separated the better, otherwise they can hide in the teeth. Rinse and set aside.
Using a kitchen knife or fruit knife, cut the enoki mushrooms into sections. Adjust to personal preference, but the length should not exceed 5 cm.
Put the enoki mushrooms into a pot, add water until it is about 1.1 times the total height of the ingredients. After boiling, wait for 3 minutes.
Add MSG and salt, then stir.
Continue heating for about 30 seconds, then turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:soup/银耳莲子粥/银耳莲子粥.name: Tremella and Lotus Seed Porridge
howtocook:soup/银耳莲子粥/银耳莲子粥.description: Tremella and lotus seed porridge is sweet, smooth, with soft and sticky tremella and dense lotus seeds. It is a common nourishing congee. Rich in natural colloid, dietary fiber, and various trace elements, it helps to calm the mind and nourish the heart. The preparation requires soaking and slow simmering, testing a beginner's patience, and takes about 3.5 hours in total.
steps: Soak the tremella and lotus seeds in water for 2 hours, soak the red dates for 10-20 minutes, wash the goji berries, and set aside.
Pour 600ml water into a pot, bring to a boil, then add the tremella, lotus seeds, and red dates in order.
Wait for the water to boil again, cover with lid, and switch to medium heat to continue simmering.
After simmering for about 1 hour, add 5g-10g rock sugar and 5g-6g goji berries, then switch to low heat.
Continue simmering on low heat for 30 minutes; the tremella will start to become sticky.
Add another 5g-10g rock sugar, stir with a spoon for 5-10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and ladle out.

howtocook:soup/陈皮排骨汤.name: Tangerine Peel Pork Rib Soup
howtocook:soup/陈皮排骨汤.description: A clear and nourishing Cantonese slow-cooked soup with a bright broth, sweet taste, and unique fragrance of tangerine peel. Pork ribs provide quality protein, combined with American ginseng, dendrobium, and other ingredients to moisten the lungs, strengthen the spleen, promote fluid production, and quench thirst. Suitable for those who often stay up late. Medium difficulty; simply prepare the ingredients, place them in a stewing pot, and simmer for about two hours.
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in hot water to remove blood
Rinse tangerine peel, dwarf lilyturf, Solomon's seal, dendrobium, and American ginseng
Clean the stewing pot
Open the stewing pot, put the pork ribs at the bottom, then add tangerine peel, dwarf lilyturf, Solomon's seal, dendrobium, and American ginseng in order
Add hot water to the stewing pot, not too full
Add water to the steaming container, then stew for 1.5 hours
Add salt, serve hot

howtocook:soup/陈皮排骨汤/陈皮排骨汤.name: Tangerine Peel Pork Rib Soup
howtocook:soup/陈皮排骨汤/陈皮排骨汤.description: This soup is a classic Cantonese slow-cooked soup. The broth is clear, sweet, and aromatic with the unique aged fragrance of tangerine peel. Pork ribs provide high-quality protein and calcium. Combined with tangerine peel, American ginseng, dendrobium, and other ingredients, it helps regulate qi, strengthen the spleen, nourish yin, and moisten the lungs. It is suitable for those who stay up late or have throat discomfort. The process is not difficult; just prepare the ingredients and control the cooking time. Even beginners can do it easily. From prep to serving, it takes about two hours.
steps: Blanch the pork ribs in hot water to remove blood.
Rinse the tangerine peel, ophiopogon, polygonatum, dendrobium, and American ginseng.
Clean the soup pot.
Open the soup pot, place the ribs at the bottom, then add the tangerine peel, ophiopogon, polygonatum, dendrobium, and American ginseng in order.
Add hot water to the soup pot, not too full.
Add water to the cooking vessel and simmer for 1.5 hours.
Add salt and serve hot.

howtocook:soup/黄瓜皮蛋汤.name: Cucumber and Century Egg Soup
howtocook:soup/黄瓜皮蛋汤.description: Cucumber and century egg soup is a quick home-style soup. It is light in flavor, with the aromatic scent of century eggs and garlic cloves stir-fried until slightly charred. It warms the heart and stomach without being greasy. Cucumber is cooling and hydrating, while century eggs provide some protein, making it low-fat and light. The method requires almost no skill, even a novice can easily make it. From prep to serving, it takes only about ten minutes.
steps: Wash the cucumber, slice into thin rounds about 0.5-1.2 mm thick.
Wash and mince the scallion. Smash the garlic cloves with a knife, peel, and cut in half.
Peel the century eggs, cut each into 6-8 pieces. Pour oil into the pot, add the cut century eggs and garlic.
Stir-fry over low heat until the surface of century eggs and garlic is slightly browned. Add water, turn to high heat and bring to a boil.
Add the cucumber slices. As soon as the water boils again, turn off the heat immediately. Season with salt and chicken bouillon (optional), then serve.
Pour into a bowl and sprinkle with scallions.

howtocook:staple/中式馅饼/中式馅饼.name: Chinese-style Pie
howtocook:staple/中式馅饼/中式馅饼.description: This pancake has a slightly crispy exterior and a soft interior, with a savory filling. It's a home-style flour-based dish. Rich in carbohydrates and protein, with the addition of carrot and egg for extra nutrition. Medium difficulty; beginners need to master the heat, but the steps are clear and easy to follow. It takes about 1 hour from preparation to completion.
steps: Take the minced meat (thawed), add half of all the above seasonings (oil, salt, sugar, soy sauce, flavor seasoning) and all of the cornstarch, mix well, and marinate for 30 minutes.

Add the flour to a bowl, add the egg, add the remaining half of all the above seasonings, add water equivalent to half the amount of flour (so the batter is relatively thick but still pourable), and mix well.
Mince the garlic.
Cut the scallion into sections.
Finely dice the carrot (for the filling, so it should be as fine as possible, you can use a chopping motion).
Heat the pan with cold oil, use a generous amount of oil.
When the oil is hot, add the minced garlic and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the marinated minced meat, stir-fry until no longer pink.
Add the diced carrot to the meat and stir-fry together until the oil turns golden (this is to extract the carrot's flavor).
Turn off the heat. Let cool for 2 minutes.
Pour the stir-fried meat into the raw batter and mix well.
Turn on the heat again, coat the bottom of a non-stick pan with oil.
Reduce to low heat, pour the batter into the pan and spread evenly. Make sure it's not too thick. You can tilt the pan to let the batter flow across the bottom.
While the surface of the pancake is still liquid, sprinkle the scallion sections on top.
Keep on low heat until the bottom is set.
Flip the pancake and continue to cook on low heat until the other side is set.
Then, cook each side for an additional 20 seconds.
Turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:staple/凉粉/凉粉.name: Liangfen (Bean Jelly)
howtocook:staple/凉粉/凉粉.description: Liangfen is a traditional snack originating from Sichuan. It has a cool, smooth texture and a sour-spicy flavor that whets the appetite, making it perfect for summer. The main ingredient is pea starch, which is rich in carbohydrates and provides a quick energy boost. The process is not complicated, but requires patience in stirring and a long time for refrigeration to set, making it moderately challenging for beginners. From cooking to completion, it takes about 3-4 hours, most of which is waiting for refrigeration; the hands-on time is only about half an hour.
steps: Prepare the ingredients.
Mix 100g of pea starch and 100g of water together.
Pour 600g of water into a pot, bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat.
Pour in the starch water while continuously stirring until it becomes thick and evenly colored.
Find a container and brush a thin layer of cooking oil on it.
Pour the cooked starch into the container and refrigerate for 2-4 hours.
After refrigeration, remove, unmold, and cut into strips.
Mince garlic and bird's eye chili, add 10g chili powder, 5g crushed peanuts, and stir with hot oil.
Then add 10ml soy sauce, 10ml vinegar, 5ml sugar, 3g chicken bouillon, 3g salt, and stir well.
Pour the seasoning over the liangfen, then sprinkle with cilantro and serve.

howtocook:staple/利提巧卡.name: Litti Chokha
howtocook:staple/利提巧卡.description: Litti Chokha is a traditional staple from Bihar, India, consisting of golden-brown wheat balls and smoky vegetable mash. The texture is crispy outside and soft inside, with a rich aroma. The roasted chickpea flour filling is packed with plant protein and dietary fiber, paired with vegetables like eggplant, tomato, and potato, making it nutritious. The process involves kneading dough, stuffing, baking, and preparing the vegetables. It has several steps and is somewhat challenging for beginners, taking about 60 minutes in total.
steps: In a bowl, mix 120g roasted chickpea flour, chopped onion, green chilies, and ginger.
Add 3g cumin seeds, 5g sesame seeds, 3g red chili powder, 3g black salt, and 3g salt.
Add 15ml mustard oil, 10ml lemon juice, and chopped cilantro leaves.
Mix thoroughly until the filling is loose but can be shaped into a ball; set aside.
Place 200g whole wheat flour in a large bowl.
Gradually add warm water while kneading to form a smooth, soft dough.
Let the dough rest for 15 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions.
Press each portion into a round disc about 8cm in diameter, slightly thicker in the center.
Place about 15g of filling in the center of each disc.
Bring the edges together to enclose the filling, pinch to seal, and roll into a ball.
Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Place the littis on a baking sheet and bake for 25-30 minutes, turning once, until golden brown and cracked.
If using an open flame: place the littis directly over charcoal or a low gas flame, turning constantly, until evenly charred.
While still hot, brush each litti with ghee so it absorbs.
Roast the eggplant, tomato, and potato directly over an open flame, or in a 200°C oven for 20-25 minutes, until the skins are charred and the insides are completely soft.
Peel the roasted vegetables, then mash the eggplant, tomato, and potato separately with a fork or by hand.
Mix the mashed vegetables together.
Add chopped garlic, green chilies, 4g salt, and 15ml mustard oil.
Stir until well combined.
Garnish with cilantro leaves.
Arrange the littis on a plate with the chokha (vegetable mash) on the side.
Serve hot, breaking the litti with your hands and dipping it into the chokha.

ingredients.name: Roasted chickpea flour (Sattu, can substitute with roasted gram flour)
Mustard oil (can substitute with other cooking oil)
Mustard oil (can substitute with other cooking oil)
Black salt (Kala Namak, can substitute with regular salt)

howtocook:staple/印度烤饼.name: Indian Flatbread
howtocook:staple/印度烤饼.description: This is an Indian-style fermented flatbread, slightly charred and crispy on the outside, soft and chewy inside, perfect with curries. Rich in carbs and protein, yogurt makes it easy to digest. Moderately difficult, need to watch dough consistency and heat, but can be made with a skillet. Allow 1 hour for proofing, total about 1.5 hours.
steps: Dissolve 3g dry yeast and 10g sugar in 100ml warm water (about 40°C, not too hot).
Let stand for 5 minutes until frothy on surface (yeast is activated).
In a large bowl, mix 300g all-purpose flour and 5g salt evenly.
Add the yeast water, 60ml yogurt, and 15ml cooking oil.
Knead into a smooth, soft dough (slightly softer than usual, a little sticky is normal).
Knead for 8-10 minutes.
Brush 3ml oil on dough surface to prevent drying.
Cover bowl with a damp cloth or plastic wrap.
Place in a warm spot to rise for at least 1 hour until doubled in size.
Remove dough, gently knead to release air.
Divide into 6 equal pieces (about 80g each).
Take one piece, dust hands with 3ml flour or oil.
Roll into a long oval or teardrop shape, about 4-5mm thick.
If making garlic naan: sprinkle chopped garlic and cilantro on top, gently roll to embed.
Optionally sprinkle sesame seeds on top.
Heat a skillet (cast iron best) over high heat until very hot (water droplets evaporate instantly).
Brush one side of the flatbread with a thin layer of water.
Place water-side down into the skillet.
Cook for 1-2 minutes until bottom has golden brown spots and bread starts to puff.
Flip and cook another 1-2 minutes.
If you have a gas stove: after flipping, use tongs to hold the bread directly over an open flame for a few seconds; it will puff up quickly.
Remove and immediately brush with ghee or butter.
Preheat oven to highest temperature (usually 250°C).
Place flatbread on a baking sheet.
Bake for 3-5 minutes until golden and puffed.
Remove and brush with ghee or butter.
Serve hot.
Pairs well with any Indian curry like butter chicken or red kidney bean curry.

howtocook:staple/印度焖饭.name: Indian Biryani
howtocook:staple/印度焖饭.description: Indian Biryani is a royal dish originating from the Mughal Empire period. The rice grains are long and slender, with rich spices; the chicken is tender, the rice is distinct, and the layers are rich. Rich in protein and various spice essences, it can stimulate appetite. The preparation steps are relatively complicated and not friendly to beginners. The whole process takes about 90 minutes (chicken needs to be marinated for 1 hour in advance).
steps: In a large bowl, mix yogurt, grated ginger and garlic, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice
Add chicken pieces and coat evenly with the marinade
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate to marinate for at least 1 hour (overnight is better)
Soak 0.5g saffron in 30ml warm milk for 15 minutes; set aside
Pour 50ml cooking oil into a pot and heat over medium heat
Add onion slices and fry until deep golden brown and crispy (about 12-15 minutes, be patient)
Remove fried onions with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels; set aside
Drain the soaked Basmati rice
Bring 2000ml water to a boil in a large pot
Add bay leaves, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon stick, star anise, and 10g salt
Add rice and cook over high heat for 5-6 minutes until about 70% done (soft outside with a firm center)
Drain immediately, discard whole spices, and set aside
Grease the bottom of a heavy-bottomed pot with 15g ghee
Layer 1: Spread the marinated chicken evenly on the bottom of the pot
Layer 2: Spread half of the parboiled rice over the chicken
Sprinkle half of the fried onions, mint leaves, cilantro leaves, and green chilies
Layer 3: Spread the remaining rice on top
Sprinkle the remaining fried onions, mint leaves, and cilantro leaves
Evenly drizzle the saffron milk over the top layer of rice
Place the remaining 15g ghee (cut into small pieces and scattered) on top
Seal the lid: Mix flour with water to form a dough strip and seal the edge of the pot (traditional method), or wrap the pot mouth with aluminum foil and then cover with the lid
First, heat over high heat for 3 minutes until steam begins to escape
Then reduce to the lowest heat and cook for 25-30 minutes
Turn off the heat and let it rest without opening the lid for another 5 minutes
Remove the lid, gently fluff the rice from the bottom with a large spoon to mix the layers evenly
Serve, accompanied by yogurt (Raita)

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, can substitute with lamb)
Saffron (can substitute with yellow food coloring)

howtocook:staple/可乐炒饭.name: Cola Fried Rice
howtocook:staple/可乐炒饭.description: This dish uses cola as a substitute for sugar, giving it a unique caramelized and slightly sweet flavor. The rich sauce coats every grain of rice, making it a creative home-style fried rice. Eggs and ham sausage provide high-quality protein and energy, balanced with the staple rice for a filling meal. The steps are straightforward, suitable for beginners, and it can be on the table in about 20 minutes.
steps: Heat the pan, add 25 ml oil, crack two eggs, and fry until the bottom is completely set.
Flip and fry until both sides are completely set.
Turn off the heat, remove the eggs, cut into 2-5 cm² pieces, and return to the pan (or break them up directly with a spatula).
Turn the heat back on, add cola, light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, chili bean paste, and oyster sauce, stir to combine.
Heat until the liquid reduces to 1/3, then add the rice and ham sausage, stirring well.
After stirring evenly, reduce to low heat, make a well in the center of the rice, crack in one egg, cover, and steam for 2 minutes.
Uncover and stir-fry until the third egg is cooked through, sprinkle with chopped green onions and white pepper, and serve.

howtocook:staple/咸肉菜饭.name: Salted Pork and Vegetable Rice
howtocook:staple/咸肉菜饭.description: This is a home-style staple full of Shanghai alleyway charm. The richness of salted pork and the sweetness of greens are melted into every grain of rice with the help of lard. It offers both crispy rice crust and tender-crisp vegetable stems, with distinct layers. Greens are rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, while salted pork provides protein and energy—a balanced mix of meat and vegetables. The method is not complicated, but requires the trick of adding leaves in batches, which is a bit challenging for beginners. From prep to serving, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: If adding winter bamboo shoots, slice thinly and boil in cold water for 10 minutes to remove astringency.
Cut salted pork into 1 cm cubes.
Place salted pork and winter bamboo shoots in a cold pan with 10 g lard, cooking wine, and sugar. Stir-fry over medium-low heat until translucent and bubbling.
Chop the greens, separating stems and leaves. Cut stems into 0.5 cm square pieces, and leaves into 2-3 cm long by 1-1.5 cm wide rectangular pieces.
Stir-fry the stems until emerald green.
Rinse the rice and put it in the rice cooker, add water.
Spread the cooked pork and stems over the rice.
Start the normal cooking cycle. In the last 10 minutes, quickly open the lid and layer the leaves on top.
After cooking, let it rest for 5 minutes, then drizzle with 5 g lard and white pepper, and mix vigorously.

howtocook:staple/基础牛奶面包/基础牛奶面包.name: Basic Milk Bread
howtocook:staple/基础牛奶面包/基础牛奶面包.description: This basic milk bread simplifies traditional bread-making with a pre-fermented starter. The result is rich in milk flavor and soft in texture. Rich in carbohydrates and protein, it provides energy for the body. Moderate difficulty, suitable for baking beginners, takes about 200 minutes in total.
steps: First, mix the yeast and warm water (30°C) evenly using a spatula or kitchen utensil, and let stand for 5 minutes. Then mix with flour and stir until well combined. The starter should be a very sticky batter at this point.
Cover the batter with a cloth and place it in a warm place to ferment for 45 to 60 minutes.
The final batter should have many bubbles on the surface and have visibly increased in volume.

ingredients.name: Bread flour or all-purpose flour (for starter)
Bread flour or all-purpose flour (for dough)
Milk, light cream, condensed milk, or milk powder

howtocook:staple/微波炉腊肠煲仔饭/微波炉腊肠煲仔饭.name: Microwave Sausage Claypot Rice
howtocook:staple/微波炉腊肠煲仔饭/微波炉腊肠煲仔饭.description: This is a simple and quick Cantonese-style staple. The rice absorbs the fat from the sausage, becoming rich and savory. Paired with crisp vegetables and carrots, it offers a rich texture. Rich in carbohydrates, protein, and various vitamins, it's nutritionally balanced. Cooked in the microwave, it's very beginner-friendly, and it's estimated to be ready in just 15 minutes.
steps: Wash the rice and put it in a bowl. Add 400ml of water and cover with a lid.
Microwave on high for 6 minutes. While cooking, prepare the ingredients.
Slice the sausage.
Wash the greens.
Slice the carrot.
Chop the green onion.
Put the greens and carrot slices in a vegetable bowl. Add 10ml oil and 5g salt.
In a small bowl, mix 10ml light soy sauce and 5ml oil.
After 6 minutes, remove the bowl with a towel or heat-resistant gloves. The rice should be about 80% cooked.
Arrange the sliced sausage on top of the rice, then microwave on high for another 2 minutes.
Remove the sausage rice bowl. Put the vegetable bowl in the microwave and cook on high for 4-5 minutes.
Arrange the cooked vegetables on the sausage rice. Crack an egg on top. Depending on preference, microwave on high for 40-60 seconds.
Remove the sausage rice. It is basically ready.
Put the small bowl with soy sauce mixture in and microwave on high for 30 seconds.
Drizzle the heated soy sauce mixture over the rice and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
Extra vegetables can be dipped in soy sauce.

howtocook:staple/意式肉酱面/意式肉酱面.name: Spaghetti Bolognese
howtocook:staple/意式肉酱面/意式肉酱面.description: Spaghetti Bolognese is a simple and quick home-style Western dish with rich sauce and chewy noodles, perfect for busy days. Minced meat and onion paired with ready-made pasta sauce provide a fast boost of protein and carbs, and the onion adds dietary fiber. The recipe is very beginner-friendly; just basic chopping and stir-frying, and once you're used to it, you can serve it in 15 minutes—more satisfying than instant noodles.
steps: Add water to a pot, bring to a boil, then add the spaghetti (wait 6-12 minutes)
While the water is boiling, you can do the following steps, but keep track of the pasta cooking time
Dice the white onion
Heat oil in an empty pot, add the diced onion over medium heat
Stir constantly to prevent burning, until the onion becomes translucent
Add the minced meat, continue stirring (break it apart) until the meat turns brown
Add the pasta sauce, stir briefly
Drain the cooked spaghetti and mix it with the meat sauce (or simply pour the meat sauce over the spaghetti)

howtocook:staple/手工水饺.name: Handmade Dumplings
howtocook:staple/手工水饺.description: Handmade dumplings are a classic Chinese staple food, with thin wrappers and generous filling, savory and juicy. They are filling and can be seasoned according to personal taste. The pork filling provides high-quality protein and fat, and paired with chives, it also adds vitamins and dietary fiber. This dish is challenging for beginners, requiring skills in kneading dough, rolling wrappers, preparing filling, and cooking dumplings. Estimated preparation time is about 3 hours.
steps: Add all the flour to a bowl
Add sesame oil
Make a small well in the center of the flour
Add water in 4-5 batches, stirring, until the mixture becomes crumbly and slightly dry
Stop adding water, press the dough together with your hands
Press the dough until it incorporates all the flour from the bowl; the bowl should be clean
Place the dough on the table, cover with the bowl upside down, at 25°C, let it rest for about 45 minutes
After resting, roll the dough into a log, gather it into a ball, and roll again; repeat 3 times
Roll into a log, cut into 20 equal pieces, and roll each into a ball about 3-3.5 cm in diameter
Flatten each ball, dust hands, table, rolling pin, and dough with flour to prevent sticking
Roll out each piece into a circle about 8 cm in diameter and 2 mm thick, with the center slightly thicker than the edges by about 1 mm
Remove skin from pork, keep some fat, cut into small pieces
Use a cleaver (preferably two) to chop the pork into mince, put in a bowl
Finely chop green onions and ginger, add to the bowl and mix well
Wash chives, cut into pieces shorter than 3 mm
Mix chives with the minced pork, add 2 ml each of oyster sauce, light soy sauce, and sesame oil, add the egg white from one egg, mix well with your hands
Let it sit for 30 minutes, then start wrapping dumplings
Place a wrapper on your left hand; avoid getting flour on the side where the filling goes, to prevent sealing issues
With chopsticks in your right hand, take about half the diameter of the wrapper worth of filling
Seal along the edge of the wrapper, pinch firmly; for personal consumption, no decorative pleats needed, just ensure it's sealed
Use a pot that can hold 20 dumplings, or cook in batches
Boil water, filling the pot to about 3/4 full
Bring water to a boil over high heat, add dumplings, then reduce to medium heat
After adding dumplings and water bubbles, add 50 ml cold water along the edge (repeat this step twice)
After the third boil, add 50 ml cold water, bring to a boil again, then reduce to low heat and wait 60 seconds before removing

howtocook:staple/扬州炒饭/扬州炒饭.name: Yangzhou Fried Rice
howtocook:staple/扬州炒饭/扬州炒饭.description: This is an upgraded fried rice with rich ingredients. The rice grains are distinct, the eggs are tender, paired with shrimp, luncheon meat, and vegetable dices, creating distinct layers of texture. It is a classic staple of Huaiyang cuisine. Rich in high-quality protein, carbohydrates, and various vitamins, it is nutritionally balanced. Although there are many steps, the operations are intuitive and friendly for beginners, requiring only patient preparation. Estimated cooking time is about 1 hour.
steps: Cut carrot into 0.2 cm cubes, set aside.
Cut luncheon meat into 0.2 cm cubes, set aside.
Separate scallion white and green parts, cut each into 0.25-0.5 cm pieces, set aside separately.
Crack eggs into a bowl, beat evenly, set aside.
Boil carrot, green peas, and corn kernels until cooked, drain and set aside (do not discard the water).
Boil shrimp until cooked, drain and set aside (water can be discarded).
Heat the wok and oil, refer to 'hot wok, cold oil' technique from stir-frying and pan-frying.
Add 20-30 ml oil for the second pour. When the oil is hot, slowly pour in the egg (control the stream diameter from bowl to oil to about 0.5 cm). Do not stir.
Once the egg sets, immediately remove and set aside.
Add luncheon meat, green peas, carrot, corn kernels, and shrimp to the wok and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.
Rinse the wok with water to clean off any residues, ensuring the wok is clean (oil may remain but no impurities).
Heat the wok and add 10 ml oil. Add scallion white and sauté until fragrant.
Reduce to low heat (if oil is too hot, turn off heat for 1-2 minutes). Add rice, quickly chop and stir-fry with a spatula, ensuring each grain is coated with oil and separated.
Add the cooked egg, continue chopping to break the egg into small pieces and mix thoroughly with the rice.
Turn up to high heat, add all other reserved ingredients, and quickly stir-fry for 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle in salt and stir-fry until evenly mixed.
Sprinkle in scallion green and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Turn off heat and plate.

howtocook:staple/披萨饼皮/披萨饼皮.name: Pizza Dough
howtocook:staple/披萨饼皮/披萨饼皮.description: Pizza dough uses low-temperature overnight fermentation, resulting in a soft yet slightly chewy crust. It's a home-style Western pastry. Mainly provides carbohydrates and a small amount of healthy fats. The process is simple but requires patience for dough fermentation, and is beginner-friendly. From preparation to baking, including overnight refrigeration, it takes about 10-14 hours, so it's suitable to prepare a day in advance.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in the prepared warm water, stir a little, and set aside.
Take the prepared flour, add salt, olive oil, and sugar in order.
Prepare to mix water and flour, add water while stirring until all water is added.
Stir until no dry flour is visible.
Put it in a container about three times the size of the dough, seal, and refrigerate at 4°C for 8-12 hours. Usually make it at night and use it the next day.
Observe that the dough has finished proofing; it should be about twice its original size.
Take the proofed dough, divide evenly into four portions, cover each with plastic wrap, and set aside.
Sprinkle a little more flour on the work surface, ready to knead.
Since the dough is quite wet, coating it with flour will make it less sticky. Don't knead too much; just until the surface is slightly smooth.
