Stretch by hand or roll out with a rolling pin; it doesn't have to be perfectly round, just even thickness and fit in the oven.
Line with parchment paper, place the crust, add your preferred toppings, and sprinkle shredded cheese.
For fruit pizza, bake at top 180°C, bottom 220°C for 16 minutes.
For meat and vegetable pizza, bake at top 200°C, bottom 230°C for 18 minutes.
Drizzle with mayonnaise or your favorite sauce and enjoy~

howtocook:staple/日式咖喱饭/日式咖喱饭.name: Japanese Curry Rice
howtocook:staple/日式咖喱饭/日式咖喱饭.description: Japanese curry rice is a rich and hearty home-style Japanese dish, seasoned with curry roux, paired with potatoes, carrots, onions, and meat, smooth and creamy, mildly spicy and sweet. It is rich in protein, carbohydrates, and various vitamins, providing ample energy. The cooking process is not too complicated, but requires control of heat and stirring skills, suitable for those with some cooking experience. From preparation to serving takes about 1 hour, and it tastes even better after refrigeration.
steps: Cut off both ends of carrots, peel, and cut into rolling chunks
Peel the outer layer of onion, remove core, and cut into crescent shapes
Peel potatoes and cut into large chunks
Cut meat into chunks
Peel garlic, flatten, and mince
Chop curry roux to increase surface area for faster dissolving
Heat oil in pan, add garlic and meat, stir-fry quickly until meat surface turns white
Add carrots, stir-fry quickly until evenly heated
Add onions, stir-fry quickly until onions become translucent
Add potatoes, continue stir-frying until potatoes soften (test with chopsticks)
Add water to cover all ingredients, wait 15 minutes after boiling
Turn off heat, add curry roux and stir
Wait for curry to melt, then turn on heat again, stir slowly for 10 minutes to prevent burning
Turn off heat when the appearance becomes thick and sticky, finish
Microwave: high heat for 2-3 minutes per serving
Stovetop: add extra 50ml water, keep stirring while heating

howtocook:staple/日式肥牛丼饭/日式肥牛丼饭.name: Japanese Beef Rice Bowl
howtocook:staple/日式肥牛丼饭/日式肥牛丼饭.description: A classic Japanese home-style rice bowl, beef slices and white onion simmered in a savory-sweet sauce, tender meat, sweet onion, rich flavor. Beef provides high-quality protein and heme iron, onion is rich in dietary fiber and various antioxidants. Not complicated to make, even beginners can easily follow the steps, cooking process takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Peel the onion, remove the core, and cut into crescent-shaped slices.
Wash the green onions and cut into 0.5cm pieces.
Heat a pan and add white sesame seeds directly, shake the pan back and forth to evenly heat until lightly golden.
Blanch the beef slices in boiling water for 1 minute, then remove.

Mix 40g mirin (or 30g cooking wine), 30g soy sauce, 20g oyster sauce, 5g sugar, and 5g dark soy sauce (optional, for color) in a bowl to make the seasoning. (You can place the bowl on a kitchen scale for this step.)
Heat oil in a pan, add the onion, and stir-fry quickly until translucent.
Turn to low heat, add 250g water (or dashi), then return to high heat and wait for 3 minutes.
Add the beef and seasoning.
Continuously stir all ingredients for 10 minutes to prevent sticking.
Turn off the heat.
Serve the beef bowl over rice (make sure to drizzle some sauce over the rice).
Sprinkle with chopped green onions and white sesame seeds. Done.
Microwave: high heat for 2-3 minutes for a single serving.
Stovetop: add an extra 50ml water and stir continuously while heating.

howtocook:staple/汤面.name: Soup Noodles
howtocook:staple/汤面.description: Soup noodles are a freely customizable home-style staple. The broth is light and refreshing, the noodles are soft and smooth, and it can accommodate various meat and vegetable ingredients, with a flavor that can be savory or mild. It is rich in carbohydrates and high-quality protein, and with vegetables it adds vitamins and dietary fiber, making it nutritious and filling. The preparation is very simple, requiring no culinary skills, and even beginners can easily handle it. From preparing ingredients to serving, it usually takes about 30 minutes.
steps: First, cut the vegetable ingredients into pieces no larger than 4 cm on each side, to ensure even cooking.
If using raw meat, place it in cold water, cover with lid, bring to a boil. Skim off the scum that rises to the surface, then turn off the heat. Remove the partially cooked meat and set aside.
First, heat the water over high heat until boiling, then reduce to medium heat.

Add ingredients that take longer to cook (like partially cooked meat, mushrooms, etc.) into the pot first. To ensure they are cooked through, set a timer for 10 minutes after boiling resumes; add another 5 minutes for particularly large or tough pieces.

Add the noodles to the pot, stirring gently to ensure they are well submerged in the broth. Keep the liquid at a gentle boil and cook for 5 minutes. After adding noodles, white foam may appear; you can lift the lid slightly to release steam or remove the lid.
Add quick-cooking ingredients like leafy greens to the pot, stirring to submerge them. Cook for 2-5 minutes.
Turn off the heat. Add seasonings like salt, pepper, and sesame oil to taste. Stir well, then serve.

ingredients.name: Vegetables
ingredients.note: about the same volume as the noodles

howtocook:staple/河南蒸面条/河南蒸面条.name: Henan Steamed Noodles
howtocook:staple/河南蒸面条/河南蒸面条.description: Henan Steamed Noodles is a homestyle snack from Henan. The noodles are steamed, then stir-fried, and steamed again, resulting in a rich, flavorful, and chewy texture. Pork belly and garlic scapes provide protein and fiber, balanced with the staple. The steps are a bit many, but if followed in order, even beginners can succeed. From prep to finish takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Add water to a wok until 70% full, bring to a boil, and place a steamer basket on top.
Evenly spread the dried noodles in the basket, drizzle with 5ml oil and rub to coat. Steam for 15 minutes.
Remove the noodles and steamer basket, set aside, and discard the water in the wok.
Slice the pork belly into 2mm thick pieces.
Cut garlic scapes into 3cm segments.
Slice scallions into 0.2cm thin rounds.
Cut ginger into 1mm x 1mm x 3cm fine strips.
Smash garlic on a cutting board, then chop into 1mm pieces.
Heat the wok until dry, then add 3ml cooking oil.
Hold the handle and swirl the wok, coating 2/3 of the surface with oil.
Over medium heat, add the pork slices and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add scallions, ginger, garlic, and cooking wine. Continue stir-frying for 1 minute.
Add the garlic scapes and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Season with dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, salt, chicken bouillon, and thirteen spice. Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add 500ml water, just enough to submerge the vegetables. Simmer for 1 minute.
Add the steamed noodles, stirring constantly for 3 minutes until evenly colored. Turn off the heat.
Transfer the noodle-vegetable mixture to a separate basin.
Add cold water to the wok until 70% full, place the steamer basket, and spread the mixture evenly on top.
Once the water boils, steam over high heat for 15 minutes. Remove from heat.
Drizzle with 10g sesame oil and serve.

howtocook:staple/火腿饭团/火腿饭团.name: Ham Rice Balls
howtocook:staple/火腿饭团/火腿饭团.description: A simple and quick Japanese-style rice ball, savory ham paired with mayonnaise and seaweed, rich in taste and satisfying. Rich in carbohydrates, protein, and vitamins, it quickly replenishes energy. Extremely low difficulty, even beginners can easily make it. From cooking rice to finishing, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Put the rice and water into the rice cooker, press the rice mode, and wait for it to finish.
Put frozen corn kernels and green peas into a pot, add water to cover all ingredients, bring to a boil, wait 2 minutes, then drain.
Cut the ham into 1cm cubes.
Meanwhile, add 10ml of cooking oil, stir-fry the ham until browned.
Put rice, ham, shredded seaweed, green peas, corn kernels, and mayonnaise into a bowl, mix well.
Plate (if available).

howtocook:staple/炒凉粉/炒凉粉.name: Stir-fried Bean Jelly
howtocook:staple/炒凉粉/炒凉粉.description: Stir-fried bean jelly is a specialty snack from the Shanxi and Shaanxi regions. It is smooth and tender, savory with a slight spiciness, and carries the aroma of garlic and sauce. Bean jelly is mainly made from starch, providing rich carbohydrates for quick energy. The cooking difficulty is moderate, suitable for beginners with some basic skills. The whole process takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Cut the bean jelly into mahjong-sized cubes.
Turn the heat to low, heat oil in a wok. When the wok is slightly hot, add minced garlic and stir-fry until fragrant, then add broad bean paste and stir-fry until red oil appears.
Add the bean jelly cubes to the wok and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add light soy sauce for flavor and dark soy sauce for color, stir-fry evenly, then add chili flakes and continue stir-frying until well combined.
Add salt and thirteen-spice powder and stir-fry for another 10 seconds.
Add the prepared mineral water, stir-fry again for 10 seconds. When the sauce thickens, turn off the heat and transfer to a plate.
Sprinkle with chopped scallions and serve.

howtocook:staple/炒年糕.name: Stir-fried Rice Cakes
howtocook:staple/炒年糕.description: This is a staple dish in the Minnan style. The rice cakes are soft, chewy, and infused with the rich aroma of scallion oil. It's homestyle and filling. Made from rice flour, the rice cakes provide plenty of carbohydrates, while the eggs and greens add protein and vitamins for balanced nutrition. The recipe is simple, beginner-friendly, and takes about 30 minutes from prep to plate.
steps: Bring water to a boil in a pot, cook the rice cakes until done. Place them in a bowl with water to prevent sticking, then drain and set aside.
Slice the scallions into pieces (separate the white parts from the green parts). Cut the greens into small sections and set aside.
(Optional) Make scrambled eggs, refer to the recipe for Tomato and Egg Stir-fry.
Heat the wok, add 30ml cooking oil.
Add the white parts of the scallions to the wok, cook until most of them turn golden brown and fragrant. Pour out the scallion oil and set aside.
Reheat the wok, add 20ml cooking oil.
Add all the side ingredients (eggs, greens, etc.) and stir-fry until well combined.
Drain the water from the rice cakes, then add them to the wok.
Add soy sauce and salt, stir-fry until evenly coated.
Turn off the heat, add the scallion oil, stir-fry to combine, and transfer to a serving plate.

howtocook:staple/炒意大利面/炒意大利面.name: Stir-fried Spaghetti
howtocook:staple/炒意大利面/炒意大利面.description: This stir-fried spaghetti is soft and tasty, paired with beef slices and sauce, rich in sauce aroma, a home-style staple with Western flavor. Rich in carbs and protein, quickly replenishes energy. Easy to make, even beginners can master it, takes about 30 minutes total.
steps: Add 250g water per person
Bring water to a boil, add spaghetti, cook over medium heat for 15-20 minutes (this spaghetti is usually hard, best to taste before taking out; if the center is a bit hard, continue cooking)
Remove spaghetti, place on plate for later use
Heat oil in pan, when the oil is medium hot, add spaghetti and stir-fry for one minute (if too dry, add a little water)
Add 10g ketchup, beef slices, add 2g salt, continue stir-frying for one minute
Remove from pan

howtocook:staple/炒方便面/炒方便面.name: Stir-Fried Instant Noodles
howtocook:staple/炒方便面/炒方便面.description: This stir-fried instant noodle dish has a chewy, springy texture and a savory flavor. It's a quick and easy staple adapted from traditional boiled noodles. With eggs and ham sausage, it provides protein and energy. Simple to make, just rinse noodles with cold water to keep the texture. Perfect for beginners, ready in about 15 minutes.
steps: Remove the ham sausage from its packaging and cut into 1cm wide pieces.
Add 1200ml water to a pot, enough to submerge the noodle cake. Bring to a full boil; do not add noodles before boiling.
Add the instant noodle cake and boil for 45 seconds. Gently stir to separate the noodles.
Turn off the heat immediately once noodles are separated. Do not overcook!
Scoop out 80ml of the hot noodle water and set aside.
Drain the noodles and rinse with cold water.
Drain the noodles thoroughly. Do not soak in water for long.
Prepare a small bowl and squeeze in the seasoning packet from the instant noodles.
Add all the vegetable packet.
Add all the sauce packet.
Add 50% to 80% of the powder packet. (Using the whole packet will make it too salty.)
Pour the reserved 80ml hot noodle water into the bowl and stir well to make the sauce.
Take the required number of eggs and crack them into a small bowl. Do not add salt! (The eggs will absorb salt from other ingredients later.)
Heat the pan for 20 seconds, then add 8ml of oil.
Add the beaten eggs and scramble for about 20 seconds until set.
Remove the scrambled eggs and set aside. No need to wash the pan; there won't be much oil left.
Reheat the pan for 20 seconds and add 10ml of oil.
Add the ham sausage pieces from step 1 and stir-fry for 10 seconds.
Add the noodles from step 2 and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the sauce from step 3 and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the scrambled eggs from step 4 and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:staple/炒河粉.name: Stir-Fried Rice Noodles
howtocook:staple/炒河粉.description: Stir-fried rice noodles is a classic snack popular in the Guangdong region. It emphasizes wok hei (breath of the wok), dry and fragrant, with smooth and chewy noodles. Paired with shredded meat, bean sprouts, and cucumber strips, it offers a rich texture. It mainly provides carbohydrates and protein, and the vegetables add vitamins and fiber. This recipe is moderately difficult; even beginners can try it with all ingredients ready. The whole process takes about 30 minutes to serve.
steps: Finely chop the scallions (separate white and green parts), and crush the garlic cloves. Set aside on the cutting board.
Crack the egg. Transfer a little egg white to a small bowl, and put the rest into another bowl. Set aside.
Place the mung bean sprouts in a pot and boil for 60 seconds. Remove the sprouts, rinse under cold water, and put them in a plate. Set aside.
Cut the cucumber into strips and put them in a plate. They can be mixed with the bean sprouts.
Handle the gluten. Place it in a separate plate.
Cut the meat into thin strips. Add starch, the egg white from the small bowl, and ground pepper. Stir clockwise to mix well.
Note: The ready-made liangpi (cold skin noodles) from the supermarket usually have cooking oil on the surface. You can rinse them with tap water. Do the same for the gluten.
Note: After rinsing the gluten, squeeze out the excess water with your hands (no need to squeeze too hard).
Add cooking oil. Heat the wok, then pour out the oil.
Add the prepared meat. Stir-fry until it changes color. Transfer to a bowl and set aside.
While the wok is still hot, add 20g cooking oil (reduce for those with high blood pressure). Add the white parts of scallions and garlic. Stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the rice noodles. Drizzle with dark soy sauce for color. Stir-fry evenly, then add the rice noodle seasoning. Continue stir-frying.
When the noodles are almost translucent, add the cooked shredded meat and gluten. Add light soy sauce for freshness. Stir-fry a couple of times.
Add the bean sprouts and cucumber strips. Stir-fry until the noodles are fully translucent.
Turn off the heat!
Sprinkle the green parts of scallions on top. Lift the wok.
Transfer to a plate. Start eating!

howtocook:staple/炒馍.name: Stir-fried Steamed Bun
howtocook:staple/炒馍.description: Stir-fried steamed bun is a salty, crispy home-style flour dish, typical of northern Chinese cuisine. The bun cubes are fried until golden, crispy on the outside and soft inside. The addition of cumin and five-spice powder enhances the flavor. It can be paired with eggs for protein and scallions for freshness, making it a balanced meal. The difficulty is moderate, and even beginners can quickly master it. It takes about 15 minutes from start to serving.
steps: Cut the steamed bun into small cubes or slices.
If using eggs, crack the eggs into a bowl and beat them (you can add 1g salt and 1g five-spice powder now, or add them later during stir-frying).
Pour the eggs over the bun pieces and mix well. Do not use too much egg.
Heat the pan over high heat, then add cooking oil. (If you're worried about damaging a stainless steel pan, you can add the oil first and heat until hot.)
Add the bun pieces and stir-fry, mixing evenly.
Reduce the heat to low and stir-fry until the bun pieces turn golden brown.
Add salt, pepper, and five-spice powder.
Finally, add the chopped scallions and stir-fry a few times.
Turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:staple/炸酱面.name: Zhajiangmian (Fried Sauce Noodles)
howtocook:staple/炸酱面.description: Zhajiangmian is a traditional Beijing home-style noodle dish with rich sauce and chewy noodles, paired with refreshing vegetable toppings, balancing salty and sweet. The main nutrition comes from protein in lean meat and vitamins from vegetables, providing sufficient energy. It's beginner-friendly, taking about 30 minutes. With practice, you can shorten time by preparing toppings and stir-frying sauce in parallel.
steps: Cut the vegetable toppings into shreds and set aside.
Chop the green onion. Heat oil in a pan, add green onion and meat, stir-fry until meat is fully cooked (no pink).
Add bean paste and sweet bean paste, continue to stir-fry until slightly thickened. Remove from heat to get the fried sauce.
Take a large bowl, add cold water and set aside.
Cook noodles until just done (no white core), then transfer to the bowl with cold water from step 4.
Immediately drain and transfer to a clean bowl.
Take the fried sauce from step 3, pour into the bowl, and mix well. Then take the vegetable toppings from step 1, add to the bowl, and mix well.

howtocook:staple/烙饼/烙饼.name: Pan-fried Flatbread
howtocook:staple/烙饼/烙饼.description: This home-style flatbread has distinct layers, crispy on the outside and soft inside, with the savory aroma of oil pastry. It is a common staple on northern Chinese tables. It mainly provides carbohydrates and energy, suitable to accompany dishes. Making it requires some technique, especially the rolling of the pastry and the process of rolling out the dough, but following the steps is not difficult, and beginners can master it. From mixing the dough to finishing the flatbread, it takes about 1 hour.
steps: Pour 400g flour into a bowl. Use cold water for half and hot water for the other half to form dough. Stir into flocculent, then knead into a ball by hand. Cover with plastic wrap and let rest for 40 minutes.
10 minutes before the resting time is up, check the tips for making oil pastry (hot oil pastry works better).
Do not knead the rested dough. Press it slightly and divide it into four portions with one horizontal and one vertical cut.
Roll each portion into a ball, then roll out into a circle roughly the size of your electric pan. Take 1/4 of the oil pastry and spread it evenly over the surface.
Cut along one radius of the circle, then roll from the outer edge into a cone shape. Pinch the tip closed to prevent the oil pastry from leaking.
Press down on the tip of the cone to flatten it, then roll out again into a circle similar in size to the electric pan (about 3mm thick).
Preheat the electric pan, brush with cold oil (hot pan, cold oil). Place the rolled flatbread in the pan, brush the top with oil to seal in moisture, cover with lid.
Cook on high heat for one minute, open lid, flip the flatbread, and cook for another minute.
Repeat the above process to finish cooking all flatbreads.

howtocook:staple/烧饼/芝麻烧饼.name: Sesame Flatbread
howtocook:staple/烧饼/芝麻烧饼.description: Sesame flatbread is a well-known pastry in northern China. The crust is covered with sesame seeds, crisp and fragrant, while the inside is layered and soft. Flour provides ample carbohydrates, and sesame seeds are rich in calcium and unsaturated fats. The process involves kneading dough and making oil pastry, but it's not complicated—even beginners can follow. From preparation to serving takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Dough: 300g flour, 3g yeast, 3g sugar, 180g warm water, 20g cooking oil, rest for 10 minutes
Oil pastry: In a small bowl, combine 30g flour, 2g salt, 4g five-spice powder, 20g cooking oil, mix well, and set aside

Shape the flatbread: Roll the dough into a rectangle, spread the oil pastry on top, roll it up from one end, cut into 7 pieces, fold each in half, seal with your thumb and index finger, dip in water then white sesame seeds, roll into small rounds

Cook the flatbread: Preheat the electric griddle, pour in cold oil (cover the bottom with oil), place the rolled flatbreads in, brush some oil on top, spread evenly, cover, select the large bread setting, and remove when you hear the ding sound

howtocook:staple/热干面.name: Hot Dry Noodles
howtocook:staple/热干面.description: Hot Dry Noodles is one of the most representative snacks of Wuhan, belonging to Hubei cuisine. The alkaline noodles are chewy and smooth, coated with thick sesame paste, paired with pickled beans, dried radish, and minced meat, salty and slightly spicy, with a rich flavor. It mainly provides carbohydrates and protein, and the sesame paste is also rich in calcium. Simple and quick to make, even beginners can easily master it, taking about ten minutes from boiling to mixing.
steps: Boil water, add the alkaline noodles, blanch for 25 seconds and remove
Sprinkle with salt, chicken powder, and pepper
Dilute sesame paste with 90ml water, stir well, then add
Add 5ml soy sauce, add 30ml meat broth and garlic water
Add dried radish, minced meat, pickled beans, chopped green onions
Mix well and enjoy

ingredients.name: Alkaline noodles for Hot Dry Noodles

howtocook:staple/煮泡面加蛋.name: Instant Noodles with Egg
howtocook:staple/煮泡面加蛋.description: Instant noodles with egg is a quick and convenient staple dish. The texture is smooth and the soup is rich, perfect for filling your stomach anytime. It mainly provides carbohydrates and protein, quickly replenishing energy. The method is extremely simple: just boil water, cook the noodles, and crack in an egg. Even beginners can master it easily, and it takes about 10 minutes from start to finish.
steps: First, heat the water until boiling (no strict requirement for heat level; using hot water is faster)
Put the removed noodle cake into the pot
Add the seasoning packets from the instant noodles into the pot
Use chopsticks to gently stir the noodles, allowing the seasoning to fully dissolve and the noodle cake to be evenly soaked and heated
Cover with lid and wait about 1 minute until the water boils again
Crack the egg and add it to the pot
Wait about 3 to 4 minutes, then it's ready

howtocook:staple/照烧鸡腿饭.name: Teriyaki Chicken Leg Rice
howtocook:staple/照烧鸡腿饭.description: Teriyaki Chicken Leg Rice is a savory and sweet dish that goes well with rice. The chicken legs are pan-fried until crispy and then simmered in a rich sauce, resulting in tender and flavorful meat. Chicken leg meat is rich in high-quality protein, and paired with broccoli and carrots, it provides dietary fiber and vitamins. It requires some deboning skills, but overall it's not complicated; beginners can follow the steps and succeed. From preparation to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Take one chicken leg, place skin-side down on a cutting board.
Using the tip of a knife, carefully cut along the outline of the bone from one end to the other.
Use your fingers or the back of a knife to slowly push the meat away to expose the bone. If you encounter connective tissue, cut it with the knife tip.
When the meat is fully separated, hold one end of the bone, twist gently, and pull it out.
Repeat the above steps to debone all chicken legs.
Add ground black pepper, crushed black pepper, salt, and ginger slices to the deboned chicken legs. Marinate for 5 minutes.
In a bowl, mix cooking wine, light soy sauce, honey, dark soy sauce, and water.
Blanch the vegetables in boiling water for 1-2 minutes until cooked, then remove.
Heat a pan, add 15 ml oil, and sauté crushed garlic until fragrant.
Add the chicken legs and pan-fry over medium-low heat until golden on both sides. If the pan seems too dry, add 5-10 ml oil.
Pour in the prepared sauce, cover, and simmer over medium-low heat (low heat on a gas stove) for 5-10 minutes until cooked through and the sauce thickens and bubbles.
Slice the chicken, arrange with vegetables over rice, drizzle with the pan sauce, and enjoy slowly.

howtocook:staple/蒸卤面.name: Steamed Noodles with Braised Sauce
howtocook:staple/蒸卤面.description: This is a classic home-style noodle dish from southern Henan. It combines meat and vegetables, with noodles absorbing the rich sauce, resulting in a chewy and savory flavor. Pork belly provides high-quality protein and fat, while celery is rich in dietary fiber. The method is simple and easy to learn, even for beginners. The entire preparation takes about an hour.
steps: Remove skin from pork belly and slice into 2 cm by 6 cm by 0.5 cm pieces. Set aside.
Remove leaves from celery, cut off 2 cm from the root, then halve lengthwise and cut into 2 cm segments. Set aside.
Peel garlic and cut into small pieces. Slice scallion into 0.2 cm thin rounds. Cut ginger into thin strips. Set aside.
Heat a wok until smoking. Add 3 ml cooking oil to coat the wok, then discard the oil.
Add cooking oil again. Add the pork slices, scallion, ginger, garlic, and dried red chili peppers. Stir-fry for 1 minute, stirring constantly and evenly.
Add cooking wine, light soy sauce, and dark soy sauce. Stir-fry for another 1 minute.
Add 500 ml hot water. Cover and simmer for 3 minutes.
Add celery and green bell pepper to the wok. Season with salt and five-spice powder. Cover and continue simmering for 3 minutes, then turn off the heat.
Add 1000 ml water to a steamer. Bring to a boil. Spread the noodles evenly on a steamer tray and place into the steamer. Steam for 15 minutes.
Remove the steamed noodles. Use chopsticks and your hands to separate them on a cutting board. Let cool to room temperature.

Add the noodles to the wok with the vegetables. Mix by holding chopsticks in one hand and a spatula in the other, lifting the vegetables over the noodles until all noodles are evenly coated with sauce.
Place the mixed noodles back on the steamer tray. Steam again for 10 minutes, then turn off heat.

howtocook:staple/猪油拌饭.name: Lard Rice
howtocook:staple/猪油拌饭.description: This is a simple rice dish full of southern home-style flavor. The rice grains are coated in melted lard, with the savory saltiness of soy sauce and the fragrance of scallions. It's a comforting choice for children with poor appetite. It mainly provides quick carbs and fat energy, but is high in cholesterol, so not for frequent consumption. There's no threshold for making it; even someone who can't cook can easily make it. Just a few minutes with cooked rice yields a fragrant bowl of staple food.
steps: Spread lard at the bottom of a bowl
Add rice
Drizzle soy sauce and oyster sauce
Sprinkle scallions and lard cracklings
Mix well

ingredients.name: Light soy sauce (Lee Kum Kee or Haitian recommended)

howtocook:staple/电饭煲三文鱼炊饭/电饭煲三文鱼炊饭.name: Salmon Rice in Rice Cooker
howtocook:staple/电饭煲三文鱼炊饭/电饭煲三文鱼炊饭.description: This is a home-style dish that combines the Japanese cooking method of takikomi gohan. The rice soaks up the aroma of butter and salmon, resulting in a rich and savory flavor. Salmon provides high-quality protein and unsaturated fatty acids, and the addition of mushrooms adds dietary fiber. The operation is extremely simple, just one click on the rice cooker. From preparation to serving, it takes about 45 minutes, making it perfect for beginners.
steps: Remove scales and bones from salmon
Finely chop enoki mushrooms and shiitake mushrooms
Rinse rice three times
Put salmon, rice, and butter into the rice cooker
For a richer taste, add one more tablespoon of butter
Add water according to the rice cooker's markings
Set the rice cooker to cook rice mode and wait about 30-45 minutes

howtocook:staple/空气炸锅照烧鸡饭/空气炸锅照烧鸡饭.name: Air Fryer Teriyaki Chicken Rice
howtocook:staple/空气炸锅照烧鸡饭/空气炸锅照烧鸡饭.description: This Japanese-style teriyaki chicken rice has a rich sweet and savory sauce, with a slightly crispy surface and tender, juicy inside. It goes great with rice. Chicken and eggs provide high-quality protein, but the sauce uses quite a bit of soy sauce and sugar, so it's better for occasional indulgence. The method is simple: just mix the ingredients and let the air fryer do the work. Even beginners can handle it easily. It takes about 1 hour from prep to finish.
steps: Mix the soy sauce, sugar, and vinegar together, stir well to make the sauce and set aside.
In another bowl, add the chicken, egg, 1/2 of the sauce, and crushed Ritz crackers. Stir to combine.
Line the air fryer basket with a foil bowl, add the meat mixture, and pour the remaining sauce evenly over the top.
Air fry at 350°F (177°C) for 40 minutes. Best served over rice.
When golden and crispy, remove from the air fryer, cut into pieces, and serve on a plate.

howtocook:staple/米饭/煮锅蒸米饭.name: Pot-Steamed Rice
howtocook:staple/米饭/煮锅蒸米饭.description: This is a humble home-style staple. With an ordinary pot, you can make soft and fluffy rice. White rice mainly provides carbohydrates, quickly replenishing energy. The method is extremely simple; beginners only need to pay attention to the heat throughout. From adding rice to serving takes about 25 minutes.
steps: Rinse the rice
Add rice and water to the pot
Bring to a boil over high heat
Stir the bottom to prevent sticking
Cover, reduce heat to low, and cook for 10-15 minutes (depending on desired softness). Do not open the lid during cooking.
Turn off heat and let it rest for 5 minutes
Enjoy :)

howtocook:staple/米饭/电饭煲蒸米饭.name: Rice Cooker Steamed Rice
howtocook:staple/米饭/电饭煲蒸米饭.description: A staple base dish known in every household, with pure rice fragrance and soft, sticky texture. The amount of water can make it firmer or more moist. Rich in carbohydrates, quickly replenishes energy, and pairs well with various dishes. Extremely simple to make, even beginners can easily master it. It takes about 40 minutes from washing rice to steaming.
steps: Wash the rice.
Add the rice and water together into the rice cooker.
Plug in the rice cooker and set to cooking mode. Wait about 30 minutes.
Wait until the rice cooker automatically switches to keep-warm mode.
Let the rice sit in the cooker for 10-15 minutes.
Scoop out the rice.

howtocook:staple/红芸豆拌饭.name: Red Kidney Bean Rice Bowl
howtocook:staple/红芸豆拌饭.description: This North Indian homestyle dish is a traditional favorite from Punjab and Delhi. The tomato-onion gravy is rich and aromatic, and the red kidney beans become tender and flavorful after simmering. Mixed with hot rice, it's a warm and filling meal. The beans are packed with protein, dietary fiber, and various micronutrients, offering balanced nutrition. The recipe is simple and beginner-friendly, taking about 40 minutes to cook, but the dried beans need to be soaked for 8 hours ahead. It's a comforting home-cooked flavor, just like mom's.
steps: Soak dried red kidney beans in water for at least 8 hours (overnight); they will double in volume.
Drain the soaked red kidney beans.

Using a pressure cooker: Add the red kidney beans and 600ml water, close the lid, bring to high pressure over high heat then reduce to medium heat, cook for 15-20 minutes until beans can be easily crushed with fingers.
Using a regular pot: Add the red kidney beans and enough water, bring to a boil over high heat then reduce to medium heat, cook for 60-90 minutes until beans are completely soft.
After cooking, reserve the bean cooking liquid and set aside.
In a frying pan, heat 30ml vegetable oil or ghee over medium heat.
Add 2 bay leaves and fry until fragrant (about 10 seconds).
Add chopped onion and sauté until golden brown (about 6-8 minutes).
Add grated ginger and garlic, sauté for 1 minute.
Add chopped tomatoes and sauté until completely soft and oil separates (about 5 minutes).
Add 3g turmeric powder, 5g red chili powder, 5g coriander powder, 3g cumin powder, and 6g salt.
Sauté for 2 minutes to blend the spices with the tomatoes.
Pour the cooked kidney beans along with their cooking liquid into the pan.
Add green chili and mix well.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
During simmering, mash some of the beans with the back of a spatula to thicken the gravy.
Add 3g garam masala and stir well.
Turn off the heat and sprinkle fresh cilantro leaves.
Wash the rice thoroughly.
Place the rice in a rice cooker and add 400ml water.
Cook as usual.
Scoop the cooked rice onto a plate.
Spoon the red kidney bean curry over or alongside the rice.
Serve with onion salad and lemon wedges.

howtocook:staple/老友猪肉粉/老友猪肉粉.name: Old Friend Pork Rice Noodles
howtocook:staple/老友猪肉粉/老友猪肉粉.description: Old Friend Pork Rice Noodles are sour, spicy, fresh, and aromatic. The noodles are smooth and slippery, with the rich flavors of fermented black beans and sour bamboo shoots, a typical Nanning home-cooked taste. Pork provides high-quality protein, while rice noodles quickly replenish carbs. Overall difficulty is moderate; be sure to soak the rice noodles in advance. Once you get the hang of it, it takes about forty minutes to serve.
steps: Season all the pork with cooking wine, salt, light soy sauce, cornstarch, and pepper in a bowl; set aside.
Heat the wok without oil, add all the sour bamboo shoots and stir-fry until dry. Make a little space in the middle of the dried bamboo shoots.
Add 10-15 ml cooking oil, all the garlic, chopped chili, and fermented black beans into the center of the dried bamboo shoots. Push everything to the center and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add all the seasoned pork, then add 10 ml light soy sauce and stir-fry for one minute.
Add 5 ml rice vinegar, 10 ml light soy sauce, and 450 ml water. Bring to a boil.
After boiling, add the rice noodles that have been soaked in warm water, and cook for another 3 minutes. Then serve.

howtocook:staple/老干妈拌面.name: Laoganma Noodles
howtocook:staple/老干妈拌面.description: A spicy and appetizing home-style quick noodle dish. The chewy noodles are coated with the fermented bean chili flavor of Laoganma, with soy sauce enhancing the umami. Simple yet full of flavor. As a staple, it quickly replenishes carbohydrates and energy, and capsaicin helps stimulate appetite. No cooking skills required, easy for beginners. From boiling water to serving takes only about 10 minutes.
steps: Pour water into a pot and bring to a boil.
Evenly add noodles to the pot.
Stir occasionally during cooking to prevent noodles from clumping.
When you can pick up a noodle with chopsticks and it naturally slides off, wait another 30 seconds then turn off the heat.
Transfer noodles to a bowl.
Add Laoganma and soy sauce according to the measurements above.
Mix the noodles, Laoganma, and soy sauce evenly with chopsticks.
Enjoy.

howtocook:staple/肉蛋盖饭.name: Meat and Egg Rice Bowl
howtocook:staple/肉蛋盖饭.description: This dish is rich in sauce aroma, savory and sweet, with lightly charred ground meat and tender eggs, satisfying on hot rice. It's a home-style quick dish. Rich in high-quality protein and essential amino acids, it quickly replenishes energy. The method is simple and straightforward, even beginners can easily handle it. From prep to serving, it takes about fifteen minutes.
steps: Cook the rice. Typically use the measuring cup that came with the rice, one cup of rice is 240g.
Add 30ml oil to the pan.
Add the ground meat, cook over medium heat until both sides are lightly browned.
Crack the eggs into the pan, do not scramble, cover the pan.
Prepare a sauce: in a bowl, add the corresponding amounts of dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, vinegar, sugar, and red onion oil, stir to combine.
Uncover the pan, pour the sauce in, wait for three minutes.
Turn off the heat, spoon the meat and eggs over the rice.
Safety check, then start eating.

howtocook:staple/茄子肉煎饼/茄子肉煎饼.name: Eggplant and Preserved Meat Pancake
howtocook:staple/茄子肉煎饼/茄子肉煎饼.description: This home-style pancake combines the savory flavor of preserved meat with the soft texture of eggplant. It has a crispy outer layer and a tender interior, making it a rustic rice flour main dish. Rich in carbohydrates and high-quality protein, eggplant also provides dietary fiber and vitamins, offering a balanced mix of meat and vegetables. The difficulty is moderate; you need to control the batter consistency and frying heat, but beginners can succeed by following the steps. It takes about 30 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Peel the eggplant and slice it. Slice the preserved meat. Set aside.
Add 250g rice flour (ground from rice), 50g wheat flour, and 1 egg to a bowl in order.

While stirring with chopsticks, add water (to adjust consistency) to mix the rice flour, wheat flour, and egg into a batter. Stop adding water when the batter can coat the eggplant and meat slices without dripping. Then put all slices into the batter and coat them thoroughly.
Add 10-30ml cooking oil to a flat-bottomed pan over low heat.
Use chopsticks or a spoon to place the battered slices into the pan. Fry until golden on both sides, then cook for another 3-6 minutes (add more oil if needed during frying).
Sprinkle with salt, stir-fry evenly, then remove from heat and plate.

howtocook:staple/葱油拌面.name: Scallion Oil Noodles
howtocook:staple/葱油拌面.description: Scallion oil noodles is a classic Shanghai home-style noodle dish, known for its unique scallion oil aroma. It's simple and quick to make, rich in carbohydrates and fats, providing a quick energy boost. Beginners can finish it in about 20 minutes, making it an ideal quick weekday dinner.
steps: Wash the scallions and cut into long sections (about 5-7 cm). The white and green parts can be separated.
Add 100 ml cooking oil to the pan and heat over medium heat. First add the white parts of the scallions and stir-fry until slightly yellow.
Add the green parts of the scallions, turn to low heat, and continue stir-frying.
Keep on low heat and patiently stir-fry for about 15-20 minutes until the scallion sections become crispy and dark golden.
Remove the crispy scallions (keep the oil in the pan).

To the scallion oil in the pan, add 60 ml light soy sauce, 20 ml dark soy sauce, and 15 g sugar. Heat over low heat and stir for about 1 minute until the sugar dissolves and the sauce is well combined. Immediately turn off the heat. Pour the prepared scallion oil sauce into a container, let cool, and seal for storage.
Take 80 g dried noodles.
Add 1000 ml water to the pan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the noodles and cook according to the package instructions until done (usually 3-8 minutes, follow package instructions).
Remove the cooked noodles, drain well, and place in a bowl.
Add 15 ml of the prepared scallion oil sauce to the bowl with the noodles.
You can add the previously fried scallion sections (optional).
Mix quickly and evenly with chopsticks, and serve.

howtocook:staple/蛋包饭.name: Omurice (Egg-Wrapped Rice)
howtocook:staple/蛋包饭.description: Tender egg wrap filled with ketchup-flavored fried rice. Rich flavor, sweet and sour, appetizing. A classic Japanese home-style dish. Rich in protein, carbs, and vitamins, nutritionally balanced. Wrapping the egg requires some skill, but overall moderate difficulty, suitable for beginners. Takes about 25 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Dice the onion, carrot, and ham sausage or chicken breast into small cubes; set aside.
Heat a pan, add 10ml cooking oil, wait 10 seconds to heat.
First stir-fry the diced onion for 1 minute, then add carrot, corn kernels, and green peas (if using) and stir-fry for another 2 minutes.
Add the ham sausage or chicken breast cubes, stir-fry until color changes.
Add the rice and stir-fry to break up clumps, then add 20ml ketchup, stir well. The fried rice is done; remove and set aside.
Beat the eggs, add 10ml milk and mix well.
Add 5ml cooking oil to the pan, pour in the egg mixture, gently tilt the pan to spread evenly.
Heat on low heat. When the egg surface is half-cooked, place the fried rice in the center of the egg.
Use a spatula to fold the egg over the rice to form an oval shape.
Gently slide onto a plate, shape it, and optionally drizzle a little ketchup on top for decoration.

howtocook:staple/蛋炒饭.name: Egg Fried Rice
howtocook:staple/蛋炒饭.description: A home-style egg fried rice, with golden egg threads wrapping each grain of rice, paired with ham, carrot, and cucumber. Rich texture, savory and tasty. Eggs provide quality protein, carrots add vitamins, and carbohydrates supply energy. The steps are a bit many, but beginners can succeed by following the process. It takes about 25 minutes in total.
steps: Break cold rice into small pieces with a spatula beforehand.
Slice or dice ham, carrot, cucumber, etc. as desired.
If you have cooked meat at home, prepare it for better flavor.
Separate egg whites and yolks into two large bowls, beat each separately. Note: do not add salt at this step.
Heat the wok over high heat. When it starts to smoke, add cooking oil. Add egg whites, wait until mostly set, then remove and set aside.
If there's enough oil, add egg yolks directly. If not, add more oil and heat over high heat again.
When the yolks are mostly set, reduce heat to medium-low. Add ham, cooked meat, carrot, cucumber, etc. Stir-fry for 10 seconds until fragrant.
Add back the egg whites, stir-fry for 5 seconds, then quickly add rice and stir-fry over high heat, making sure each grain is coated with egg.
During stir-frying, break up any clumps of rice. This step may take a while. Once all rice is broken up and evenly mixed, it's done.
Reduce heat to low. Add salt, pepper, and light soy sauce.
Continue stir-frying evenly. When you see some rice grains 'jumping' in the wok, it's almost ready.
Finally, add chopped green onions and stir-fry for another 10 seconds.
Turn off heat and transfer to a bowl.

ingredients.name: Shredded beef jerky/luncheon meat/Chinese sausage/braised meat, etc. (optional)

howtocook:staple/螺蛳粉.name: Snail Noodles
howtocook:staple/螺蛳粉.description: Snail Noodles is a specialty snack from Liuzhou, Guangxi. The soup is savory and rich, with a sour and spicy kick. The rice noodles are soft, smooth, and chewy. The flavor combines the umami of snail soup, the unique fermented aroma of sour bamboo shoots, and the heat of chili oil, creating distinct layers. As a staple, the rice noodles provide ample carbohydrates, while the snail meat packet and toppings add protein. The sour bamboo shoots help stimulate appetite and aid digestion. This dish is simple to prepare: just cook the noodles and seasonings in order. It's very beginner-friendly. From boiling water to serving, it takes about ten minutes, perfect for a quick meal.
steps: Add water to a pot and bring to a boil.
Add the rice noodles and cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring occasionally with chopsticks to prevent sticking.
Add the soup base packet, adjusting to taste.
Add some of the topping packets, such as wood ear mushrooms, peanuts, and snails (these need to cook for a while to infuse flavor).
Add the seasoning packet, adjusting to taste.
Stir, then remove and place in a bowl.
Add the remaining topping packets, such as sour bamboo shoots and tofu skin (these should not be soaked in soup for too long).
Enjoy your meal.

howtocook:staple/西红柿鸡蛋挂面/西红柿鸡蛋挂面.name: Tomato Egg Noodle Soup
howtocook:staple/西红柿鸡蛋挂面/西红柿鸡蛋挂面.description: Tomato Egg Noodle Soup is a quick and easy home-style noodle dish. It has a sweet and sour, savory flavor with rich broth and chewy noodles. It's a daily staple with northern Chinese influences. Eggs provide high-quality protein, tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamins, and together with carbs, it's a balanced meal. Simple steps, beginner-friendly, takes about 20 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Wash and finely chop the scallions.
Cut the tomatoes into chunks. If you're not confident, you can look it up online first~
Cut the green pepper into diamond-shaped pieces.
Crack the eggs into a small bowl and beat them. If the eggs have a slight smell, add 2g of white vinegar to remove the odor.
Heat the wok over high heat, add 15-20g of cooking oil. Use a bit more oil for tender scrambled eggs, and leave some in the pan for later sautéing tomatoes.
When the oil is about 70% hot (you can feel the heat when your palm is about 10cm above), pour in the beaten eggs and quickly stir to scramble.
Once the eggs are fully set (no liquid egg remains), transfer them to a small bowl, leaving some oil in the pan.
With the remaining oil, add the white parts of the scallions and minced garlic, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the tomato chunks and green pepper pieces, and stir until the tomatoes release some juice.
Quickly add 5g of soy sauce and 2g of sugar.
Stir-fry for about ten seconds, then add a bowl of water (just enough to barely cover the tomatoes).
After boiling, add the scrambled eggs and 5g of oyster sauce (or 2g of chicken bouillon) to enhance the flavor.

Simmer over medium-low heat to reduce the sauce, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking. When the sauce has thickened, add some chopped scallion greens and a drizzle of sesame oil (optional). The topping is ready.
You can skip washing the pan; directly add 500ml of water.
Bring to a boil, add the dried noodles. When the noodles soften, add 100ml of cold water.
After it boils again, if the noodles float, add another 100ml of cold water.
After boiling, check if the noodles look translucent on the edges; if so, they are cooked.
Lift the noodles into the bowl with the topping, mix well, and enjoy~

howtocook:staple/豆角焖面/豆角焖面.name: Green Bean Braised Noodles with Pork Belly
howtocook:staple/豆角焖面/豆角焖面.description: Green bean braised noodles is a Northern Chinese home-style noodle dish. The noodles soak up the savory broth, becoming soft and chewy with a rich flavor, complemented by the freshness of green beans and the oiliness of pork belly. Pork belly provides protein and fat, while green beans are rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, making a balanced meal. The process is not difficult but requires attention to heat and timing for adding broth. Suitable for cooks with some experience, it takes about 30 minutes to serve.
steps: Cut the green beans into 5-6 cm segments.
Cut the scallions into 1-2 cm pieces.
Cut the ginger into 1mm x 1mm x 3cm strips.
Crush the garlic cloves on a cutting board, then mince to 1mm granules.
Slice the pork belly into 2mm thick slices.
First, heat the wok until all moisture evaporates; hold your hand 10 cm above the wok bottom, you should feel a noticeable heat.
Add the specified amount of cooking oil, hold the wok handle 5 cm above the stove, and swirl the wok to coat 2/3 of the wok surface (from bottom up).
Add all the ginger and scallion pieces, stir-fry for 5 seconds to release fragrance (Note: oil may splatter, wear gloves or protect yourself).
Add all the pork slices; do not stir immediately. Let them sit for 5 seconds, then stir-fry until all slices are coated with oil.
Keep stir-frying until all pork slices change color, then drizzle the prepared light soy sauce along the wok edge and stir well.
Add in order: salt, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, thirteen-spice powder, chicken bouillon powder, and all the green beans. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the prepared hot water.
When the water boils, use a spoon to scoop out half of the broth (be careful not to scoop out the vegetables).
Lay all the fresh noodles evenly on top of the vegetables.
Cover with a lid and simmer over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Remove the lid, and use a spoon to drizzle the reserved broth over the noodles, one spoonful at a time, evenly.
Cover again and simmer over medium heat for 3 minutes.
Remove the lid, and evenly sprinkle all the minced garlic and MSG over the dish.
Use chopsticks to toss and stir everything together until well mixed.
Turn off the heat.

howtocook:staple/酱拌荞麦面/酱拌荞麦面.name: Buckwheat Noodles with Sauce
howtocook:staple/酱拌荞麦面/酱拌荞麦面.description: Buckwheat noodles with sauce are sweet, sour, refreshing, and smooth. This is a simple and quick home-style cold noodle dish. Buckwheat noodles are rich in dietary fiber and protein, paired with cucumber and carrot for a refreshing texture and vitamins, making it nutritionally balanced. Just boil the noodles, cut the vegetables, and mix with Lao Gan Ma—easy steps that are very beginner-friendly, taking about 20 minutes. It tastes even better after chilling.
steps: Cook buckwheat noodles in cold water until done, about 8-10 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Cut cucumber and carrot into small strips.
Place buckwheat noodles, cucumber, and carrot in a plate, add Lao Gan Ma, and mix.

howtocook:staple/酸辣蕨根粉.name: Hot and Sour Fern Root Noodles
howtocook:staple/酸辣蕨根粉.description: Hot and sour fern root noodles are smooth and chewy, with a tangy and spicy flavor that whets the appetite. They are a home-style cold dish and can also be enjoyed as a main course. Fern root powder is rich in starch and dietary fiber, providing energy for the body. This dish is extremely simple to make and very beginner-friendly, taking only 10 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Fill a pot with water to about 3/5 of its depth and bring to a boil.
Once the water is boiling, add the fern root noodles and cook over medium-low heat for 8 minutes.
Remove from heat and drain.
According to the ratio, add soy sauce, vinegar, and chili oil.
Dip a chopstick and taste a bit.
If the soy sauce flavor is too strong, add the prepared salt.
If it's not savory enough, add the prepared sugar.
Stir well until most of the granular seasonings dissolve.
Take a bowl.
Add the sauce prepared in the previous step.
Rinse the fern root noodles under cold water, then add them to the sauce.
Stir well.
Chop the prepared scallions, garlic, and bird's eye chili peppers and sprinkle over the noodles.
Done! (｡･∀･)ﾉﾞ

howtocook:staple/醪糟小汤圆.name: Small Tangyuan in Fermented Rice Wine
howtocook:staple/醪糟小汤圆.description: Small tangyuan in fermented rice wine is a traditional Chinese dessert that is sweet, soft, and glutinous. The slight acidity of the fermented rice blends with the smoothness of the glutinous rice, warming the stomach and comforting the heart. It is rich in carbohydrates and contains a small amount of B vitamins, quickly replenishing energy and helping to promote blood circulation. The method is easy, especially friendly for beginners. From boiling water to serving, it takes only about 15 minutes. It's a home-style sweet soup that can be easily made anytime.
steps: Pour water into a pot and bring to a boil.
Add the small glutinous rice balls and cook for 8 minutes.
Add the fermented rice and goji berries, then cook for another 2 minutes.
Ladle into a bowl, add sugar to taste and stir well.
Enjoy.

howtocook:staple/陕西油泼面/陕西油泼面.name: Shaanxi Oil-Splashed Noodles
howtocook:staple/陕西油泼面/陕西油泼面.description: This traditional Northwestern noodle dish is famous for its chewy hand-pulled noodles and the spicy aroma from splashing hot oil. It is a well-known daily staple in Shaanxi. The noodles are rich in carbohydrates, and with greens and bean sprouts, they quickly replenish energy with a balanced diet. The whole process is straightforward and beginner-friendly, taking about 30 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: For hand-pulled noodles, use a rolling pin (or your hands if you don't have one) to lightly press the dough from top to bottom, then hold the left and right ends and gently pull apart.
Finely chop the green onion, mince the garlic, and set aside.
Wash the greens and bean sprouts, and set aside (if using).
Add enough water to a pot, bring to a boil over high heat, then add 1g salt.
Add the noodles, gently stir with chopsticks to prevent sticking, and cook until they float, then cook for 1-2 more minutes.
In the last minute of cooking, blanch the greens and bean sprouts.
Remove the cooked noodles, greens, and bean sprouts, drain well, and place in a bowl.
On top of the noodles, add light soy sauce, black vinegar, chopped green onion, minced garlic, and dried chili flakes. You can also add some sesame seeds.
Pour cooking oil into a wok, add Sichuan peppercorns, star anise, cinnamon, and bay leaves (if using). Fry over low heat until fragrant, then remove the spices.
When the oil reaches about 70% hot (starting to smoke slightly), quickly and evenly pour it over the chili flakes in the bowl. You should hear a sizzling sound.
Use chopsticks to thoroughly mix all the seasonings with the noodles, and serve.

ingredients.name: Dried chili flakes (or Qin pepper powder)

howtocook:staple/韩式拌饭/韩式拌饭.name: Korean Bibimbap
howtocook:staple/韩式拌饭/韩式拌饭.description: Korean bibimbap is a colorful and flavorful Korean home-style dish, with sweet and spicy sauce coating rice, vegetables, beef, and a sunny-side-up egg. Mix it all together for a satisfying bite. The variety of vegetables provides vitamins and dietary fiber, while beef and eggs supply high-quality protein, making it nutritionally balanced. It's simple to make, though the prep steps are a bit many. Even beginners can handle it with ease. It takes about 45 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Wash vegetables, cut into strips, stir-fry in a pan until softened, then set aside.
Boil water, when it boils, blanch the beef rolls until cooked through, about three minutes, then remove.
Fry a sunny-side-up egg.
Place rice in a bowl and then invert onto a large bowl.
Arrange the prepared vegetables and beef rolls in a circle on top of the rice, and place the fried egg in the center.
Prepare the sauce.
10ml Korean chili paste (sweet and spicy) + 5ml soy sauce + two bottle caps of Sprite (use Sprite Zero if dieting) + 10g sesame seeds + 5ml sesame oil.
Stir the above sauce, you can add more soy sauce and salt to taste.
Pour the prepared sauce over the arranged bowl.

howtocook:staple/韭菜盒子.name: Chive Boxes
howtocook:staple/韭菜盒子.description: Chive boxes are a traditional Chinese pastry, with a golden and crispy outer crust and a savory, juicy filling. Chives paired with shrimp and eggs are rich in vitamins and high-quality protein. The recipe is simple and beginner-friendly, taking about 2.5 hours from kneading to serving.
steps: Place the flour in a large bowl, add water, and mix into a smooth dough. Let it rest for 30 minutes.
Wash and chop the chives, add beaten eggs and 5g salt, mix well.
Divide the dough into small pieces, roll into thin rounds, and wrap with chive, shrimp, and egg mixture.
Heat a pan, add cooking oil, place the wrapped chive boxes, and fry until both sides are golden, about 3-4 minutes.
Transfer to a plate, let cool slightly, and enjoy.

howtocook:staple/鲜肉烧卖.name: Pork Siu Mai
howtocook:staple/鲜肉烧卖.description: This is a delicate Jiangnan-style dim sum with paper-thin wrappers and juicy, generous filling. When bitten into, the flavors of meat, bamboo shoots, and melted skin jelly combine in a savory-sweet broth, with a tender and springy texture. Pork and shrimp provide high-quality protein, while winter bamboo shoots and shiitake mushrooms add dietary fiber and trace elements. The low-fat version further reduces saturated fat. Although wrapping requires some patience, the steps are clear and beginners can master it with practice. From mixing the filling to serving takes about an hour.
steps: (Low-fat version) Take 10 mL cold water, add composite food thickener, and mix into a paste.
(Low-fat version) Add 25 mL boiling water, stir until a translucent gel forms.
(Low-fat version) After cooling, mix in 280 g lean ground meat + 15 g melted lard (or chicken fat).
Mix ground pork with minced ginger and scallion. Add light soy sauce, cooking wine, salt, sugar, and white pepper. Stir in one direction until the mixture becomes sticky.
If using bouillon cube, dissolve it in 15 mL hot water, stir until the emulsion is fully dispersed, then add 15 mL room-temperature water.
Gradually add water or broth while stirring until the filling absorbs liquid and becomes thick. If too thick, add 5 mL more water and continue stirring.
Add optional ingredients (bamboo shoots, skin jelly, mushrooms). Finally, drizzle with sesame oil and mix well. Refrigerate for 30 minutes to let flavors meld.
Take one siu mai wrapper and place about 20-25 g of filling (don't overfill, or it won't close).
Gently gather the wrapper edges with your thumb and index finger to form a 'vase' shape, leaving an opening at the top.
Gently press the bottom to ensure it stands stable.
If using shrimp, add one shrimp on top.
Repeat the wrapping steps until all are done.
Place in steamer basket, spaced apart to avoid sticking.
Steam over high heat for 8-10 minutes.

ingredients.name: Pork tenderloin or front leg (for low-fat version)

howtocook:staple/鲣鱼海苔玉米饭/鲣鱼海苔玉米饭.name: Bonito Flakes, Seaweed, and Corn Rice
howtocook:staple/鲣鱼海苔玉米饭/鲣鱼海苔玉米饭.description: This is a savory and delicious Japanese quick meal. The rice grains are distinct, blending the salty umami of bonito flakes, the crispy aroma of seaweed, and the sweet crunch of corn. Bonito flakes are rich in high-quality protein and DHA, paired with the minerals in seaweed and dietary fiber in corn, offering balanced nutrition. The method is very simple, easily completed by beginners. Actual hands-on time is only 5 minutes, plus cooking rice, about 40 minutes total to enjoy.
steps: Scoop the rice, add corn kernels, and mix well.
Add bonito flakes and seaweed sprinkle.

howtocook:staple/鹰嘴豆炸饼.name: Chickpea Curry with Fried Flatbread
howtocook:staple/鹰嘴豆炸饼.description: Chickpea curry with fried flatbread is a classic North Indian street snack. The soft fried bread paired with spiced chickpeas offers rich textures and sour, spicy, salty flavors. Chickpeas are high in plant protein and dietary fiber, making it filling and nutritious. The steps are a bit many, but following the process is not difficult, suitable for home cooking with some basic skills. About 50 minutes to complete (chickpeas need to be soaked for 8 hours in advance).
steps: Soak the dried chickpeas in water overnight (at least 8 hours); the volume will roughly double after soaking.
Drain the soaked chickpeas.
Put them in a pot, add 500ml water and 1 black tea bag (for coloring).
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium heat and cook for 25-30 minutes, until the chickpeas can be easily crushed with fingers.
Remove the tea bag and reserve the cooking liquid; set aside.
In a wok, heat 30ml cooking oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until golden brown (about 6-8 minutes).
Add the grated ginger and garlic, stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomato and stir-fry until the tomato is completely soft and oil separates (about 5 minutes).
Add 3g turmeric powder, 5g red chili powder, 8g coriander powder, 5g cumin powder, and 6g salt.
Stir-fry for 2 minutes to let the spices release their fragrance.
Pour the cooked chickpeas along with the reserved cooking liquid into the wok.
Add the green chili and stir well.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes, occasionally crushing some chickpeas with the back of a spoon to thicken the gravy.
Add 5g garam masala and 3g pomegranate powder (or 15ml lemon juice).
Stir well, then turn off the heat and set aside.
In a large bowl, mix 250g all-purpose flour, 2g baking soda, and 3g salt.
Add 60ml yogurt and 15ml cooking oil.
Gradually add warm water while kneading to form a smooth, soft dough (the dough should be slightly softer than regular dough).
Cover the dough with a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal portions.
Take one portion, lightly oil your hands, and roll it into a circle about 15cm in diameter and 3mm thick.
In a deep pot, heat 500ml cooking oil over medium-high heat until it reaches about 180°C.
Gently slide the flatbread into the hot oil and use a slotted spoon to gently press it down so it puffs up.
Fry for 1-2 minutes, then turn over and fry until both sides are golden brown.
Remove and drain on paper towels to absorb excess oil.
Reheat the chickpea curry.
Serve the fried flatbread hot with the chickpea curry.
It can be accompanied by sliced onions, green chilies, and lemon wedges.

howtocook:staple/麻油拌面.name: Sesame Oil Noodles
howtocook:staple/麻油拌面.description: Rich aroma of sesame oil, simply seasoned with dark soy sauce and white pepper, savory and satisfying. A time-saving and budget-friendly homemade noodle dish. Noodles provide ample carbohydrates, sesame oil contains unsaturated fatty acids and vitamin E, replenishing energy. Super easy to make, even beginners can handle it. From boiling water to mixing, it takes only about ten minutes.
steps: Pour water into a pot and bring to a boil (If you like chewy noodles, add 30g salt to the water; noodles boiled in salted water will be more chewy).
Add the instant noodles to the pot and cook for 3 minutes (or follow the cooking time on the package).
When the noodles start to loosen, stir to ensure even heating.
Drain the noodles and transfer to a bowl.
Add sesame oil, dark soy sauce, white pepper, and light soy sauce (optional) according to the measurements above.
Stir well with chopsticks.
A simple and budget-friendly lazy sesame oil noodle is done!

howtocook:staple/麻辣减脂荞麦面.name: Spicy Weight-Loss Buckwheat Noodles
howtocook:staple/麻辣减脂荞麦面.description: The spicy and numbing broth combines hot pot base with peanut butter and milk, creating a rich and smooth texture with bold Sichuan flavors. Paired with high-fiber buckwheat noodles and fresh vegetables, it's low-calorie and filling, perfect for a weight-loss diet. The method is foolproof, no cooking skills required, and the whole process takes about 20 minutes from start to eating straight from the pot.
steps: Wash the baby bok choy and lettuce, set aside.
Pour 500ml water into the pot, turn to high heat, add the buckwheat noodles and baby bok choy, wait for the water to boil.
After the water boils, reduce to low heat, add the hot pot base, peanut butter, milk, light soy sauce, and chili oil. Once it boils again, cook for 5 minutes.
Add the lettuce, cook for another 2 minutes.
Add vinegar and Sichuan peppercorn oil, turn off the heat, and eat directly from the pot.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/上汤娃娃菜/上汤娃娃菜.name: Baby Cabbage in Broth
howtocook:vegetable_dish/上汤娃娃菜/上汤娃娃菜.description: This baby cabbage in broth is sweet, fresh, and tender. It's a home-style vegetable dish, especially suitable during dieting. Baby cabbage is rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, low in fat and calories. Combined with century eggs and luncheon meat, it adds flavor without too much burden. The difficulty is moderate; beginners can try it. From prep to serving takes about 20 minutes.
steps: Wash the baby cabbage, cut vertically into sections.
Cut 3g scallions into small segments. Slice 10g garlic. Slice 10g ginger into small pieces.
Dice the century eggs, dice the ham sausage or luncheon meat into 1cm cubes.
Wash and shred the enoki mushrooms.
Boil water, blanch the baby cabbage for ten seconds, then remove.
Heat the pan with cold oil, swirl to coat, then pour out, and add a little fresh oil.
Reduce to low heat, add scallions, ginger, garlic, and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add about 300g water (enough to submerge the baby cabbage), then add baby cabbage, enoki mushrooms, and luncheon meat.
Add seasonings: oyster sauce, sugar, salt, MSG, and bring to a boil.
Cook for 3 minutes. After boiling, plate: arrange baby cabbage, top with century eggs, and pour the broth over.
Baby Cabbage in Broth

howtocook:vegetable_dish/乾隆白菜/乾隆白菜.name: Qianlong Cabbage
howtocook:vegetable_dish/乾隆白菜/乾隆白菜.description: Qianlong Cabbage is a classic Beijing-style cold dish. Hand-torn cabbage hearts are coated in a thick sesame paste dressing, resulting in a sweet, sour, crisp, and refreshing flavor that is rich and appetizing. Cabbage is rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, while sesame paste provides quality fats and calcium. No cooking or stir-frying is required—just mix the dressing and toss. It's beginner-friendly and can be served in about 10 minutes.
steps: Take only the inner tender leaves (yellow part) of the Napa cabbage, discard the stems, and tear into palm-sized pieces by hand. Do not use a knife (the rough torn surface holds the sauce better).
Rinse the torn cabbage leaves thoroughly and drain well. Any residual water will prevent the sauce from adhering.

In a bowl, add 30g sesame paste. First add 45ml black vinegar. Stir in one direction to gradually thin the paste until it drips in a thin stream (do not stir back and forth, as it may cause the sesame paste to separate oil).

Add 5g white sugar to the sesame paste dressing and stir well. Then add 1g salt and stir well. Finally, add 5ml sesame oil and stir well (add each seasoning separately and stir each time, checking the consistency as you go).
Evenly pour the prepared dressing over the cabbage leaves. Gently toss with your hands to coat every leaf.
Sprinkle with toasted white sesame seeds, plate, and serve. If time allows, let it rest for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌木耳/凉拌木耳.name: Cold Mixed Wood Ear Mushrooms
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌木耳/凉拌木耳.description: Cold Mixed Wood Ear Mushrooms: Since the distributed supplies include many dried goods, wood ear mushrooms are a relatively healthy food. Also, the cooking method for cold mixed wood ear mushrooms is relatively simple.
steps: Soak dried wood ear mushrooms in about 400ml water for about 45 minutes. (Skip this step if using fresh wood ear mushrooms.)
Remove the roots from the soaked wood ear mushrooms (as shown in pictures 4, 5, 6), and wash thoroughly.
Bring water to a boil in a pot, add the wood ear mushrooms, and cook on high heat for 1.5-2 minutes.

Mince the garlic cloves and Xiaomi chili peppers, put them in a bowl (medium or large), then add salt, sugar, light soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, and wasabi in order, using the amounts listed above.
Remove the wood ear mushrooms, drain well, and add them to the bowl from the previous step.
Mix thoroughly and serve on a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌油麦菜.name: Cold Mixed Celtuce
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌油麦菜.description: A refreshing and non-greasy home-style cold dish. The rich sesame paste coats the crispy and tender celtuce, blending sweet, sour, salty, and aromatic flavors, making it very appetizing. Celtuce is rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, while sesame paste provides calcium and healthy fats, offering balanced nutrition. The method is effortless: just cut and mix. From preparation to serving, it takes only about ten minutes, making it especially suitable for beginners or quick meals on busy days.
steps: Smash garlic cloves and mince them.
Put vinegar, soy sauce, sesame paste, sesame oil, sugar, oyster sauce, and minced garlic into a bowl and stir well.
Cut celtuce into sections, each no longer than 4 cm.
Put the celtuce into a larger bowl, pour in the sauce from the bowl, and mix thoroughly.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌莴笋/凉拌莴笋.name: Cold Mixed Celtuce
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌莴笋/凉拌莴笋.description: A crisp and refreshing cold mixed appetizer. After salting, the celtuce becomes extra tender and crunchy, with a slight spiciness that is very appetizing. Rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, low in calories. Simple to make, beginner-friendly. From prep to table in about 20 minutes; tastes even better chilled.
steps: Peel the celtuce and cut into small strips. Cut the radish into strips, put both in a large bowl, add salt, mix, and let sit for 10 minutes.
Rinse the celtuce with water 1-2 times.
Bring water to a boil in a pot, add celtuce, blanch for 1 minute, then drain and put into a large bowl.
Heat oil in a pan, add ginger slices, garlic cloves, and small red chilies, stir-fry for 30-45 seconds, then pour over the celtuce.
Mix well and serve on a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌豆腐.name: Cold Tofu Salad
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌豆腐.description: This cold dish is refreshing and delicious, rich in plant protein and calcium, low-fat and healthy. It's quick and easy to make, and beginners can finish it in 10 minutes.
steps: Cut the tofu into 2 cm cubes and set aside.
Add 500 ml drinking water to a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Put the tofu cubes in and cook for 1-2 minutes to remove the beany flavor and make the tofu firmer.
Remove the cooked tofu cubes, drain excess water, and place in a bowl.
Wash the scallions and cut into chopped scallions.
Peel the garlic and mince it.
In a clean small bowl, add 15 ml light soy sauce, 5 ml sesame oil, 5 ml vinegar (optional), 2 g sugar (optional).
Add the minced garlic.
Stir well until the sugar is fully dissolved and the sauce is well combined.
Pour the prepared sauce evenly over the tofu cubes.
Sprinkle the chopped scallions on top.
Drizzle 5 ml chili oil (optional) according to personal preference.
Gently mix with chopsticks or a spoon and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌金针菇.name: Cold Mixed Enoki Mushrooms
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌金针菇.description: Cold mixed enoki mushrooms is a simple and quick home-style appetizer salad, with a crisp, tender, and smooth texture, savory with a hint of sourness. Enoki mushrooms are rich in dietary fiber and various vitamins, and low in calories. The entire process only requires blanching and seasoning, very beginner-friendly, and can be prepared quickly even when busy. From prep to finish, it takes about 10 minutes.
steps: Cut off the roots of the enoki mushrooms, rinse thoroughly under clean water, and set aside.
Wash the scallions, then cut into chopped scallions, and set aside.
Peel the garlic cloves, then mince into garlic paste, and set aside.
Add 1000 ml of drinking water to a pot, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the enoki mushrooms, and cook for 1-2 minutes until they become soft.
Remove the cooked enoki mushrooms, drain well, and place in a large bowl. Set aside.
In another clean small bowl, add 15 ml soy sauce, 10 ml vinegar, 3 g sugar (optional), and 5 ml sesame oil (optional).
Add the minced garlic.
Stir well until the sugar is fully dissolved and the sauce is evenly mixed.
Evenly pour the prepared sauce over the enoki mushrooms.
Sprinkle the chopped scallions on top.
According to personal preference, drizzle with 5 ml chili oil (optional).
Gently toss with chopsticks until well combined, then serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌黄瓜.name: Cucumber Salad
howtocook:vegetable_dish/凉拌黄瓜.description: This is a refreshing and appetizing home-style cold dish with a crisp texture and a tangy, savory flavor. Cucumbers are rich in water and vitamins, helping to clear heat and relieve summer heat, as well as beautifying the skin. The recipe is very simple, beginner-friendly: just crush, season, and marinate for 15 minutes. The whole process takes about 20 minutes.
steps: Using a kitchen knife, smash the cucumber flat, then chop into 3 cm pieces.
Place the chopped cucumber into a bowl.
Smash the garlic and chop into fine pieces.
Add 5 grams of sugar to the bowl, mix well, and marinate for 15 minutes (marinating with sugar makes it tastier and keeps the color fresh, preventing wilting).
Add vinegar, soy sauce, salt, oyster sauce, and garlic to the bowl in order, and mix well.
Pour sesame oil into the bowl and stir evenly.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/包菜炒鸡蛋粉丝/包菜炒鸡蛋粉丝.name: Stir-fried Cabbage with Eggs and Vermicelli
howtocook:vegetable_dish/包菜炒鸡蛋粉丝/包菜炒鸡蛋粉丝.description: Cabbage stir-fried with eggs and vermicelli is a savory and delicious home-style quick dish. The crispy sweet cabbage shreds, soft and chewy vermicelli, and tender scrambled eggs blend together, creating rich layers of texture. Cabbage is rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, eggs provide high-quality protein, and carrots add nutrition, making a balanced combination of vegetables and protein. Preparation requires soaking vermicelli in advance; the overall process has several steps but is not complicated, offering some challenge for beginners. From prep to serving, it takes about 1 hour and 20 minutes.
steps: Shred carrots and cabbage; set aside.
First soak vermicelli in cold water for 1 hour, then put vermicelli in a pot, add boiling water, cook until softened, then drain and set aside.
Crack eggs into a bowl, add salt, and stir for 15 seconds.
Finely chop scallions, garlic, and dried chili peppers; set aside.
Heat oil in a wok, pour in eggs, scramble until cooked, then remove and set aside.
Add more oil, then add scallions, garlic, and dried chili peppers; stir-fry for 8 seconds.
Add shredded carrots and cabbage; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the vermicelli.
Add seasonings: light soy sauce 15 ml, dark soy sauce 10 ml, oyster sauce 10 ml, salt 2 g.
Add the previously scrambled eggs; stir-fry for about 15 seconds.
Serve and plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/印度土豆花菜.name: Indian Potato Cauliflower
howtocook:vegetable_dish/印度土豆花菜.description: This Indian home-style vegetarian dish features dry, golden, and aromatic spices, with tender potatoes and slightly charred cauliflower edges, typical of North Indian cuisine. Potatoes and cauliflower are rich in dietary fiber and vitamin C, making it a balanced dish. Simple to make, beginner-friendly, takes about 25 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut into about 2 cm cubes.
Break the cauliflower into uniform small florets; cut large ones smaller with a knife.
Pour 30 ml cooking oil into a wok and heat over medium heat.
When the oil is hot, add 3 g cumin seeds and fry until they darken and become fragrant (about 10 seconds; be careful not to burn).
Add the chopped onion and stir-fry until translucent and slightly golden (about 3-4 minutes).
Add ginger, garlic, and green chili, and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomato and stir-fry until soft and mushy (about 2-3 minutes).
Add 3 g turmeric powder, 3 g red chili powder, 5 g coriander powder, and 5 g salt.
Stir-fry for 1 minute to combine the spices with the vegetables.
Add the potato cubes and stir-fry for 2 minutes to coat them with spices.
Add the cauliflower florets and gently toss to combine.
Cover the wok, reduce heat to medium-low, and simmer for 12-15 minutes, opening and stirring every 3-4 minutes to prevent sticking.
When the potatoes can be easily pierced with a chopstick and the cauliflower has slight charred edges, sprinkle in 3 g garam masala.
Stir-fry for 1 minute.
Turn off the heat, garnish with coriander leaves, and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/印度葫芦丸子.name: Indian Bottle Gourd Kofta
howtocook:vegetable_dish/印度葫芦丸子.description: This North Indian vegetarian dish grates bottle gourd and mixes it with chickpea flour to form dumplings, which are deep-fried and then simmered in a tomato-onion curry. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, the rich and spicy sauce is flavorful. Bottle gourd is refreshing, and chickpea flour provides plant protein and dietary fiber, making it a filling and balanced vegetarian meal. The preparation involves frying and making the sauce; the steps are a bit many but easy to follow in order. The whole process takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Peel the bottle gourd and grate it into fine shreds using a grater.
Squeeze out the water from the grated bottle gourd with your hands, as dry as possible, otherwise the dumplings won't hold shape when fried.
Add 80g chickpea flour, 3g salt, 3g red chili powder, and 3g coriander powder to the squeezed bottle gourd.
Mix thoroughly with your hands and knead into a dough that doesn't stick to your hands.
Roll the mixture into small balls about 3 cm in diameter, and set aside.
Pour 500ml cooking oil into a deep pot and heat over medium heat to about 170°C (test with a small piece of dough; it should quickly float and bubble).
Fry the balls in batches for 4-5 minutes until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the fried balls on paper towels to remove excess oil, and set aside.
Pour 30ml cooking oil into a wok or pan and heat over medium heat for 10 seconds.
Add chopped onion and stir-fry until golden brown (about 5-7 minutes).
Add grated ginger and garlic, and stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chopped tomatoes and stir-fry until fully softened and oil separates (about 5 minutes).
Add 3g turmeric powder, 5g red chili powder, 5g coriander powder, 3g cumin powder, and 5g salt.
Stir-fry for 2 minutes to blend the spices with the tomatoes.
Add 200ml water, stir well, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Gently place the fried balls into the sauce and simmer on low heat for 3-5 minutes to let them absorb the sauce.
Sprinkle 3g garam masala and chopped green chilies.
Turn off the heat, garnish with coriander leaves, and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/地三鲜/地三鲜.name: Three Delicacies from the Earth
howtocook:vegetable_dish/地三鲜/地三鲜.description: Three Delicacies from the Earth is a classic home-style dish from Northeast China. The eggplant is soft and sticky, the potato is fluffy and fragrant, and the green pepper is crisp. The savory sauce with a hint of saltiness makes it a great accompaniment to rice. The combination of ingredients provides rich vitamins and dietary fiber, and the potato offers satisfying carbohydrate energy. This dish requires deep-frying and quick stir-frying, which demands some skill. Beginners may need a bit of practice. The entire process takes about 40 minutes.
steps: Wash the potato, eggplant, and green pepper.
Peel the potato.
Cut the eggplant and potato into irregular pieces of about 15g each.
Tear the green pepper along the grain into pieces about 25% larger than the potato and eggplant pieces.
Remove as much moisture from the green pepper surface as possible to avoid splattering during frying. Consider using a kitchen towel or air-drying for half an hour.
Cut the scallion into 0.5cm sections, totaling 5g. Mince the garlic into a paste, taking 15g, and divide into two portions of 7.5g each. Grate the ginger to make 5g.
In an empty bowl, combine 10ml light soy sauce, 10g sugar, 8g cornstarch, and 80ml water. Stir thoroughly to make the sauce mixture. Set aside.
Heat the wok and add 150ml oil.
Add the potato pieces and fry for about 3-4 minutes until golden brown and easily pierced with a chopstick.
Remove the potato pieces, leaving the oil in the wok.
Add the eggplant pieces and fry for about 2-3 minutes until they are about 70% done, soft, edges slightly charred, and excess oil rendered out.
Remove the eggplant pieces, leaving the oil (there may be little oil left).
If there is no flowing oil left in the wok, consider adding 15ml oil.
Add the green pepper pieces and fry for about 15 seconds until the edges start to blister, then remove immediately. Pour out the oil from the wok.
Reheat the wok and add 15ml fresh oil.
Add 5g scallion, 5g ginger, and 7.5g garlic. Stir-fry over medium-low heat until fragrant.
Add 15ml broad bean paste. Turn to low heat and stir-fry until the oil turns red and the paste is aromatic.
Pour in the sauce mixture. Immediately turn to high heat and stir rapidly to prevent burning. After about 10 seconds, the sauce will start to bubble and thicken.
Immediately add all the prepared potato, eggplant, and green pepper.
Stir-fry for 20 seconds to evenly coat the ingredients with the thick sauce.
Turn off the heat, sprinkle in 7.5g garlic. Toss to combine, then plate.
