howtocook:vegetable_dish/家常日本豆腐.name: Homestyle Japanese Tofu
howtocook:vegetable_dish/家常日本豆腐.description: Homestyle Japanese Tofu is a quick and easy home-cooked dish with a crispy outside and tender inside. Golden crust wraps silky egg tofu, paired with green pepper, carrot, and other vegetables. It can be seasoned with savory or sweet and sour flavors, crispy and tender. Japanese tofu is mainly made from eggs, rich in high-quality protein, plus wood ear mushrooms and vegetables for balanced nutrition. It requires a bit of pan-frying skill, moderate difficulty, but beginners can try. It takes about 30 minutes to serve.
steps: Slice the carrot, cut the green pepper into thin pieces, mince the green onion and garlic.
Open the packaged Japanese tofu, cut into cylinders about 1 cm thick.
Put cornstarch in a flat plate, ready to coat the tofu.
Gently place the tofu on the cornstarch, coat both sides and edges, being careful not to coat too thickly.
Add 150 ml oil to a flat pan, enough to submerge most of the tofu. When the edges turn golden, flip and fry the other side.
When both sides are fried, remove and set aside on a clean plate.
Pour 10-15 ml oil into a wok, add green onion and garlic, stir-fry until fragrant, then add green pepper, carrot, ham sausage, and black wood ear mushrooms.
Add oyster sauce, light soy sauce, salt, chicken bouillon, white sugar, and ketchup.
Gently stir-fry until the colors are evenly distributed.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/小炒藕丁/小炒藕丁.name: Stir-fried Diced Lotus Root
howtocook:vegetable_dish/小炒藕丁/小炒藕丁.description: Stir-fried Diced Lotus Root is a quick home-style vegetarian dish. The lotus root cubes are crispy and tender, with a savory and slightly spicy flavor that goes great with rice. Lotus root is rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, helping to clear heat and moisten dryness. It's easy to make; just pay attention to blanching the lotus root cubes and seasoning. Even a kitchen novice can do it. It takes about 20 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Cut the scallion and millet pepper into small sections and set aside.
Peel the lotus root and cut into pieces no larger than 3 cm. Place in water to prevent oxidation (darkening).
Take a wok, add 500 ml of cold water, and bring to a boil.
Blanch the lotus root cubes in the boiling water for 2 minutes, then remove and place in a plate, set aside.
Pour out the water from the wok, heat the wok until dry, then add 10-15 ml of cooking oil.
When the oil is hot, add the scallion sections and millet pepper, stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the blanched lotus root cubes and stir-fry over high heat.
Add light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and oyster sauce.
Stir-fry for 2 minutes, then serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/干锅花菜/干锅花菜.name: Dry Pot Cauliflower
howtocook:vegetable_dish/干锅花菜/干锅花菜.description: Dry Pot Cauliflower is a Hunan-style home-cooking dish. The cauliflower is tender and crisp, and the pork belly is rich and fragrant, with a slight spiciness. Cauliflower is rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, while pork belly provides protein and energy. The cooking difficulty is moderate. Just make sure to drain the cauliflower thoroughly after blanching and render the fat from the pork belly. Suitable for beginners with some cooking experience. It takes about 40 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Submerge cauliflower florets stem-side down in lightly salted water for 20 minutes. Then rinse and break into small florets with a small knife.
Blanch in boiling water for 1 minute, remove and immediately rinse under cold water until completely cooled. Drain and set aside.
Slice pork belly thinly. Cut off the white part of garlic and smash with the flat side of a knife. Cut small red chili peppers into sections.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Add the white parts of scallion and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add pork belly slices and stir-fry over medium heat until the surface changes color. Continue stir-frying for a while to render some fat from the fatty parts.
Add red chili pepper sections and cauliflower. Stir-fry a few times.
Add 10 ml light soy sauce.
Then add 5 g sugar, turn to high heat and stir-fry continuously for 1 minute.
Cut the garlic leaves into sections, add to the wok. Stir-fry a few times, then turn off the heat, cover and let sit for 1 minute. Serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/手撕包菜/手撕包菜.name: Hand-Torn Cabbage
howtocook:vegetable_dish/手撕包菜/手撕包菜.description: Hand-Torn Cabbage is a classic Hunan dish with a sour, spicy, and crispy taste. The cabbage is tender and flavorful, and the pork belly is dry-fried without being greasy. It's a great appetizer that goes well with rice. Cabbage is high in vitamin C and fiber, and the pork belly provides protein and energy. The key is to stir-fry over high heat quickly. It might be a bit challenging for new cooks, but following the steps will ensure success. It takes about 20 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Cut the cabbage in half, remove the white core in the middle 【see Figure 1】
Tear the cabbage into pieces by hand, put 2 g salt in a bowl, wash the cabbage and drain, set aside 【see Figure 2】
Prepare ginger slices, garlic cloves, small chili peppers, and garlic sprouts, set aside 【see Figure 3】
Slice the pork belly, wash with clean water, set aside
Add 30 ml cooking oil to the wok, add the cabbage and stir-fry over high heat for 1 minute, then add 3 g salt, continue stir-frying for 2 minutes, remove and set aside
Add 30 ml cooking oil to the wok, add the pork belly, stir-fry over high heat for 1 minute
Add the ginger slices and other ingredients, stir-fry for 1 minute
Add the cabbage and stir-fry, then add vinegar, cooking wine, chicken bouillon, cooking wine, continue stir-frying over high heat, remove from heat after 2 minutes

howtocook:vegetable_dish/拔丝土豆/拔丝土豆.name: Candied Potatoes
howtocook:vegetable_dish/拔丝土豆/拔丝土豆.description: Candied Potatoes is a classic Shandong dish. The finished dish is golden, with long sugar threads, crispy outside and chewy inside, sweet and tasty. Potatoes are rich in carbs and vitamin C, giving a quick energy boost. This dish is a bit tricky; beginners should watch the sugar syrup temperature to avoid seizing or scorching. Prep to plate takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Peel potatoes and cut into even small pieces. Add starch (without water) and toss to coat the potato surface.
Heat oil in a wok, add potato pieces, and gently fry and tumble for 5-7 minutes until a chopstick can pierce through.
Remove potatoes and set aside in a large bowl.
Add water and white sugar to the wok, stirring slowly in one direction until the sugar turns brown.
Pour the potatoes back in, stir-fry for 30 seconds, then remove from heat.
Transfer potatoes to a plate and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/松仁玉米.name: Pine Nut Corn
howtocook:vegetable_dish/松仁玉米.description: Pine Nut Corn is a quick and easy home-style dish with a sweet and crisp texture. The pine nuts are fragrant and crunchy, while the corn is sweet and juicy. It's a beloved Northern Chinese home-style flavor. The combination of corn and pine nuts provides rich dietary fiber, unsaturated fatty acids, and various vitamins, making it nutritious and balanced. The method is simple, even beginners can handle it easily. It takes only about fifteen minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Blanch corn kernels and carrot cubes for 1 minute, then drain and set aside.
Heat oil in a pan, add carrot cubes and stir-fry briefly, then add corn kernels and stir-fry.
Add white sugar and salt, stir well.
Mix water and starch to make a slurry, pour into the pan and stir-fry quickly to slightly thicken the sauce.
Add cooked pine nuts and stir-fry evenly.
Transfer to a plate and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/椒盐玉米/椒盐玉米.name: Pepper Salt Corn
howtocook:vegetable_dish/椒盐玉米/椒盐玉米.description: A flavorful snack with crispy outside and tender inside, salty and fragrant, with the dry-fragrant texture common in Sichuan restaurants. When bitten, the corn's natural sweetness emerges. Corn provides abundant carbohydrates and dietary fiber, and with sesame seeds, it also adds high-quality fats and vitamin E. This dish has a few more steps but is simple to make, requiring patience to shake off water and coat with starch. It's beginner-friendly. From thawing to serving takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Corn kernels are already shelled, just thaw them directly. Soak in warm water for 15 minutes or boil in hot water for 5 minutes.
Take out a colander, consider it as Box A, line with oil-absorbing paper, and pour in the thawed corn kernels.
shaking shaking shaking! - until the oil-absorbing paper is completely wet.
Take out a second colander Box B, line with oil-absorbing paper, and pour all the corn from Box A into Box B.
shaking shaking shaking! - until the oil-absorbing paper is completely wet.
Repeat the above operations multiple times until there are no visible water droplets on the corn surface but it remains moist.
Pour in a large amount of starch, enough to completely cover the corn kernels.
shaking shaking shaking! - until the starch coats the corn kernels.
Turn on the stove - place the pan - pour in oil, try to cover the bottom but not too much.
Oil at 80% heat, pour in the starch-coated corn kernels.
Fry on medium heat first for 30 seconds, do not stir, otherwise the starch will fall off.
Lightly stir-fry for 3 minutes, then remove from the pan.
The most important step: sprinkle 3g pepper salt, sprinkle sesame seeds!
Fragrant pepper salt corn is ready!

howtocook:vegetable_dish/榄菜肉末四季豆/榄菜肉末四季豆.name: Green Beans with Minced Pork and Olive Vegetables
howtocook:vegetable_dish/榄菜肉末四季豆/榄菜肉末四季豆.description: Green beans with minced pork and olive vegetables is a home-style stir-fry with strong Chaoshan flavor. It is salty, fragrant, crisp, and tender, making it a great appetizer. Green beans are rich in dietary fiber and vitamins, paired with minced pork for quality protein, while olive vegetables bring a unique savory flavor. The recipe steps are clear, and even beginners can quickly master it. Just ensure the green beans are thoroughly cooked. From preparation to serving, it takes about thirty minutes.
steps: Wash the green beans, remove the strings, and cut into evenly sized pieces. Set aside.
Smash the garlic and chop into mince. Set aside.
Cut the bird's eye chilies into evenly sized pieces. Set aside.
Remove the skin from the pork belly and mince it. Set aside.
Heat the wok, then add 20ml oil to coat the wok. After coating, pour out the hot oil, then add 10ml cold oil. This is the hot-wok-cold-oil technique, mainly to prevent the minced pork from sticking.
If you don't have a grease container at home, you can skip the oil coating step. Add oil directly, then put in the minced pork and stir-fry over low heat for two minutes until the pork fat renders.
Once the minced pork is fragrant, add the garlic, olive vegetables, and bird's eye chilies. Stir-fry until aromatic.
Add the green beans and stir-fry over medium heat for at least 5 minutes. Make sure the green beans are thoroughly cooked, otherwise they may cause food poisoning.
Once the green beans are cooked, drizzle 2ml of soy sauce along the side of the wok. Then add 2g salt, 1g chicken bouillon, 1g white pepper, and 0.5g sugar.
Stir-fry everything evenly to combine the seasonings.
Remove from heat and plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/水油焖蔬菜.name: Water-Oil Braised Greens
howtocook:vegetable_dish/水油焖蔬菜.description: Water-oil braised greens is a simple and quick home-style side dish. By adding a few drops of oil and seasonings to boiling water, the leafy greens become tender and glossy, refreshing yet with a light oily aroma. This method helps improve the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins, making it more nutritious than eating raw vegetables. The cooking difficulty is low, very beginner-friendly, and it generally takes only 10 minutes from washing to serving.
steps: Wash the greens
Add 150ml water to the pot and bring to a boil. (The water does not need to completely submerge the greens.)
Add 3g salt
(Optional) Add 3ml oyster sauce
Add 2ml cooking oil
Add the greens, toss briefly, then cover and simmer for 1 minute
Plate and serve

howtocook:vegetable_dish/油醋爆蛋.name: Oil and Vinegar Scrambled Eggs
howtocook:vegetable_dish/油醋爆蛋.description: This is a home-style quick dish full of Hunan flavor. Sour, spicy, fresh, and aromatic. The eggs are fluffy and golden, coated in a rich sauce that's very appetizing. Eggs provide high-quality protein and lecithin, and the rice vinegar helps stimulate appetite. The method is extremely simple and beginner-friendly, taking only about ten minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Do not beat the eggs; crack them directly into a bowl and set aside.
Cut scallions into 3 cm sections.
Put garlic cloves and bird's eye chilies into a garlic press and mince.
Add rice vinegar, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, and water to a small bowl, stir well to make the sweet and sour sauce.
Heat oil, pour in the eggs. Once set, break into large pieces with a spatula. Add minced garlic and chilies, then pour in the sweet and sour sauce.
Reduce the sauce over high heat. Just before serving, add the scallion sections.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/洋葱炒鸡蛋/洋葱炒鸡蛋.name: Scrambled Eggs with Onion
howtocook:vegetable_dish/洋葱炒鸡蛋/洋葱炒鸡蛋.description: Scrambled Eggs with Onion is a savory and fragrant home-style dish with tender eggs and sweet onion. It's a classic Chinese comfort food. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and vitamins, while onions provide dietary fiber and antioxidants, making this a nutritious and quick dish. The preparation is extremely simple and beginner-friendly, taking only about 5 minutes from start to finish.
steps: Crack eggs into a large bowl, add sliced onion and salt, then stir for 60 seconds.
Heat oil in a pan, pour in the eggs. Fry one side for 30-45 seconds, flip and continue to stir-fry. Repeat for 2-3 minutes, then sprinkle in cooking wine and remove from heat.
Plate the eggs and garnish with chopped green onions.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/清炒花菜.name: Stir-fried Cauliflower
howtocook:vegetable_dish/清炒花菜.description: A common home-style vegetable dish, crisp and refreshing. Rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber. Simple and quick to make, a beginner can finish in just 15 minutes.
steps: Wash the cauliflower, cut or break into small florets, slice the thick stems, and set aside.
Peel the garlic and slice into pieces, set aside.
Add 1000 ml drinking water to the pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the cauliflower florets, cook for 2-3 minutes until the color lightens and they become slightly tender.
Remove the cooked cauliflower, drain well, and set aside.
Heat the wok, add 15 ml cooking oil, and heat over high heat.
Add the garlic slices and stir-fry quickly until fragrant.
Add the blanched cauliflower florets, turn to medium-high heat, and stir-fry quickly for about 2 minutes to heat evenly.
Add 3 g salt and continue to stir-fry evenly.
Pour 50 ml drinking water along the edge of the wok, cover with lid, and simmer for 1 minute to help the cauliflower cook through and absorb flavor.
Uncover, stir-fry quickly and evenly, then serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/清蒸南瓜.name: Steamed Pumpkin
howtocook:vegetable_dish/清蒸南瓜.description: A simple home-style steamed dish that retains the natural sweetness and nutrition of pumpkin. The texture is soft, sweet, and fragrant. It can be served as a dessert or a main course. Pumpkin is rich in beta-carotene and dietary fiber, benefiting vision and digestive health. Extremely easy to make; even beginners can handle it. From prep to serving, it only takes 15-20 minutes.
steps: Wash the outside of the pumpkin and remove the pulp and seeds.
Cut the pumpkin into slices about 2 cm thick. Set aside.
Add 1000 ml of drinking water to the steamer pot.
Arrange the sliced pumpkin evenly on a plate.
Once the water in the steamer boils, place the plate with pumpkin into the steamer.
Cover the lid and steam over high heat for 15-20 minutes, until the pumpkin is soft and can be easily pierced with chopsticks.
Turn off the heat and carefully remove the plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒滑蛋/炒滑蛋.name: Silky Scrambled Eggs
howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒滑蛋/炒滑蛋.description: Silky scrambled eggs is a classic home-cooked dish with a tender, smooth texture and rich egg aroma, with a hint of milkiness. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and various vitamins, nutritious and easy to absorb. The method is very friendly, even beginners can master it easily. From prep to serving takes only about 5 minutes, making it an ideal choice for a quick energy boost when busy.
steps: Add milk and 5ml cooking oil to the eggs, mix well, and set aside.
Heat a non-stick pan over high heat for about 30 seconds, then add 5ml cooking oil.
After 30 seconds, reduce heat to low and pour in the egg mixture.
Let it sit for 5 seconds, then slowly push the egg mixture from the edges toward the center using a spatula.
Stir-fry until the eggs are mostly set, then turn off the heat and plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒茄子.name: Stir-fried Eggplant
howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒茄子.description: This home-style stir-fried eggplant is tender and flavorful, with a rich sauce. Adding star anise enhances the flavor. Eggplant is rich in dietary fiber, vitamin P, and potassium, which help protect the cardiovascular system. The recipe is simple and beginner-friendly. Cook over high heat throughout; from prep to finish takes about 20 minutes.
steps: Wash the eggplant, cut it into two sections lengthwise (vertical cut). Cut each section into diamond-shaped pieces. Place the cut eggplant in a bowl and set aside.
Wash the scallions, cut into chopped scallions, and place on the cutting board to set aside.
Cut the star anise and set aside on the cutting board.
If you plan to add ground meat, first stir-fry it over medium heat with 10 ml (if lean meat, use 15 ml) of oil until it changes color (about 1 minute), then set aside.
Heat the pan over high heat until there is no water in the pan.
Add cooking oil to the pan, enough to cover the bottom twice over (you can add more oil, but not less).
When the oil is about 60% hot, add the optional star anise, dried shrimp, and scallions.
If you do not have the optional ingredients like star anise, heat the oil until 90% hot.
When the oil in the pan is 90% hot, pour the eggplant from the bowl into the pan and stir-fry with a spatula.

After stir-frying for about 40 seconds, hold the spatula horizontally, parallel to the pan, and pour soy sauce onto the spatula. About 2.5 spatulafuls per person (you can add less soy sauce, but not more, as it will be salty).
When the eggplant becomes half-soft (about 1 minute later), add back the pre-fried ground meat and stir quickly to combine.
If you plan to add sugar and vinegar, add them.
Continue stir-frying.
When all the eggplant has changed color and become soft, remove from the pan.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒青菜.name: Stir-fried Greens
howtocook:vegetable_dish/炒青菜.description: This is a refreshing and delicious home-style vegetable dish, with a crisp and tender texture, savory and slightly sweet. Greens are rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, which aid digestion. The method is very simple, even beginners can easily handle it, from prep to serving takes only about 10 minutes.
steps: Break the greens into small pieces, wash them in clean water, and set aside.
Mince the garlic cloves and set aside.
Heat the wok over medium-high or high heat, then add 10-15ml of cooking oil. Wait 30 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Pour the prepared greens into the wok and stir-fry until they soften (about 1 minute).
Add the minced garlic cloves.
Pour in the measured clean water, the water level should fully cover or just about cover the greens. Add salt (2g portions), and continue stir-frying for about 1 minute.
Finally, add sugar, reduce heat to low, cover with lid, and heat for 2 minutes.
Transfer to a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/烤茄子/烤茄子.name: Roasted Eggplant
howtocook:vegetable_dish/烤茄子/烤茄子.description: Roasted eggplant is fragrant with a crispy outside and tender inside. The spicy aroma of cumin and minced garlic perfectly blends into the soft and sticky eggplant flesh. This dish brings street barbecue flavor easily at home. Eggplant is rich in vitamin P and anthocyanins, which help protect cardiovascular health and provide antioxidant benefits. The recipe requires an oven, but the steps are clear and beginners can finish it in about half an hour. It's a perfect quick and tasty dish for family gatherings.
steps: Place the soy sauce, cumin, salt, minced garlic, and chopped bird's eye chili in a bowl, mix evenly and set aside.
Wash the eggplant and dry its surface with a paper towel.
Use a fork to poke 4-8 holes on one side of the eggplant.
Use 15-25ml of cooking oil to coat the entire surface of the eggplant.
Preheat the oven to 200°C (with fan on high heat), preheat for 2 minutes.
Place the eggplant on the middle or upper rack, roast for 12-15 minutes (until the eggplant skin wrinkles and you can press it down 0.3-0.5cm).
Remove the eggplant and use a knife to make a vertical slit in the center, leaving 1-1.5cm at both ends.
Insert a small knife or fork into the slit and cut the inside of the eggplant vertically.
Gently open the slit and pour in the sauce prepared in step 1.
Return the eggplant to the oven, set temperature to 200°C, and roast for 4-7 minutes.
Remove and turn off the oven.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/白灼菜心/白灼菜心.name: Blanched Choy Sum
howtocook:vegetable_dish/白灼菜心/白灼菜心.description: This classic Cantonese dish uses the blanching technique—quickly boiling in water to retain the sweetness and crunch of choy sum, with particularly crisp stems. It is light and delicious, rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, low in fat and calories, making it ideal for health-conscious diners. The method is very simple, with an estimated difficulty of only two stars; beginners can finish in just 15 minutes. It's an excellent choice for a quick green vegetable fix.
steps: Wash the choy sum, remove the tougher or older parts of the roots. Use a knife to scrape the stems, removing the tough outer layer so the inner part is more tender. But note the blanching time for stems—if too long, they won't be crisp.
Mince the garlic into small pieces. If you have onion, add a bit of chopped onion as well.
Prepare the soul sauce: 5g light soy sauce, 5g oyster sauce, add 3g sugar and 100g water (half a bowl to a full bowl of sauce).
Bring 500ml water to a boil with 5g salt and 10g cooking oil.
Blanch the choy sum stems in the boiling water for 1 minute, until they turn dark green. Then put the whole choy sum in the pot and cook for another 1 minute. Remove and arrange on a plate.

In another small pot, pour in the prepared sauce. Bring to a simmer over low heat. Add half the minced garlic, a little shredded ginger, and minced bird's eye chili. While cooking, add a little chopped onion and garlic. First, pour oil into the pot, heat to 50% hot, then add the garlic and onion. Slightly fragrant, then add the sauce and bird's eye chili. Bring to a boil.
Reduce the sauce slightly—after boiling, wait about ten seconds. Then pour directly over the choy sum. Don't use too much sauce, but be generous with the garlic—don't skimp.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/皮蛋豆腐.name: Century Egg Tofu
howtocook:vegetable_dish/皮蛋豆腐.description: Century Egg Tofu is a refreshing and appetizing cold vegetarian dish. The century eggs are springy and smooth, while the silken tofu melts in your mouth. The savory and slightly sour flavor goes great with rice. Tofu is rich in plant protein and calcium, while century eggs provide iron, vitamin A, and other nutrients. Together, they are easy to digest. The process is almost zero-effort, even beginners can do it; from prep to serving takes only about 5 minutes.
steps: First, peel the century eggs and cut each into quarters.
Cut the silken tofu into pieces or slices and place in the middle.
Mix light soy sauce, white sugar, vinegar, sesame oil, and chili oil into a dressing, then pour over.
Finally, optionally sprinkle with crushed peanuts, chopped scallions, and cilantro.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/糖拌西红柿/糖拌西红柿.name: Tomato Salad with Sugar
howtocook:vegetable_dish/糖拌西红柿/糖拌西红柿.description: This is a home-style cold dish suitable for summer. It's sweet and sour, refreshing, and easy to make. Tomatoes are rich in vitamin C, appetizing and refreshing. Beginners can easily do it. The whole process (including refrigeration) takes about 15 minutes to serve.
steps: Use a knife to score the tomato skin in a cross pattern.
Insert a chopstick into the bottom of the tomato, rotate over a gas flame for 10 seconds (or pour boiling water over it for 30 seconds) until the skin curls.
Peel off the skin completely.
Cut the tomato into eight pieces (cut along the lines to retain more juice), remove the stem and core, and set aside.
After all are cut, arrange a layer of tomatoes evenly on a plate.
Sprinkle with sugar, repeat the previous step until all tomatoes are placed.
Refrigerate for 10 minutes.
A plate of tomato salad with sugar is ready. Nutritious, delicious, sweet and sour, perfect for cooling down and refreshing in summer.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/红烧冬瓜/红烧冬瓜.name: Braised Winter Melon
howtocook:vegetable_dish/红烧冬瓜/红烧冬瓜.description: Braised Winter Melon is a home-style vegetarian dish with bright red color, savory and delicious taste, soft and glutinous texture that absorbs flavors well. It is a popular Chinese braised dish. Winter melon is rich in water, dietary fiber, and vitamin C. It has the effects of clearing heat, relieving summer heat, diuresis, and reducing swelling. With simple seasonings, it is nutritious and easy to digest. This dish is beginner-friendly with clear steps; just master the heat and seasoning. Estimated cooking time: about 25 minutes.
steps: Peel the winter melon, cut into small pieces no larger than 2cm per side.
Heat oil in a pan, add winter melon, gently fry and toss for 2 minutes until the surface turns golden.
Remove the winter melon, set aside in a large bowl.
Using the remaining oil in the pan, add minced ginger, light soy sauce, and oyster sauce in order, stir-fry for 15 seconds.
Return the winter melon to the pan, stir-fry for 30 seconds, then add boiling water to cover the winter melon, cook on high heat for 10 minutes.
Add dark soy sauce for color, continue cooking until the winter melon is soft and tender (a chopstick can easily pierce through it).
Add chicken bouillon, cooking wine, and scallions, stir-fry for 30 seconds, then remove the winter melon to a large bowl.
Keep the remaining sauce in the pan, add water starch, bring to a boil, then pour the sauce over the winter melon.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/红烧茄子.name: Braised Eggplant
howtocook:vegetable_dish/红烧茄子.description: Braised eggplant is a savory and rich home-style dish that goes great with rice. The soft eggplant pieces soak up the sauce, paired with bell peppers and tomatoes for a tangy and slightly spicy flavor. Eggplant is rich in dietary fiber and vitamin P, and the fried pieces are fragrant without being greasy. The process involves coating and deep-frying, then slow-braising to infuse flavor. There are quite a few steps, but beginners can try it if they pay attention to details. From prep to serving takes about 45 minutes.
steps: Wash the green eggplants, green chili peppers, tomatoes, onion, and scallions.
Cut the scallion into 5 mm wide rings. Peel and crush the garlic. Cut the tomato into 6 cm³ cubes. Cut the green chili peppers and onion into 5 g pieces.
Cut the eggplant into diamond-shaped pieces (first cut into 2 cm thick slices, then cut the slices into 2 cm strips, then cut diagonally into pieces...).
Pour the flour into a bowl. Gradually add a small amount of water, stirring until it forms a thick batter.
Add the starch and 30 g of water, stir until well combined.
Crack the egg into the bowl, add (flour/20) grams of salt, and stir until smooth.
Pour the eggplant pieces into the batter, stirring to coat each piece evenly.

Heat the wok over high heat, add 500 ml of oil. When the oil starts to shimmer (a wisp of smoke), reduce to low heat. Use chopsticks to carefully place the eggplant pieces into the oil. Once all pieces are in, turn to medium heat. Fry until golden brown, then remove and drain the oil.
Add (servings 5) g of oil, add the garlic and scallion, stir-fry for 15 seconds. Add the green chili pepper pieces and stir-fry for 30 seconds. Add the tomato and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the fried eggplant pieces. Add water until it reaches 0.8 times the height of the ingredients in the wok.
Add soy sauce and (servings 3) g of salt.
Wait until the sauce thickens (the water level is about 0.2-0.3 times the height of the remaining ingredients). Remove the lid, plate the dish, and turn off the heat.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/素炒豆角.name: Stir-fried Green Beans
howtocook:vegetable_dish/素炒豆角.description: This is a home-style stir-fry with rich savory sauce. The green beans are tender and flavorful, with a hint of spiciness that pairs well with rice. Rich in dietary fiber and various vitamins, it aids digestion. Simple to make, even beginners can handle it easily. From prep to finish, it takes about 20 minutes.
steps: Chop the spring onion into rings and mince the garlic. Set aside.
Mix light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, and salt to make the seasoning sauce. Set aside.
Slice the chili peppers into rings. Set aside.
Remove the strings from the green beans, cut diagonally at a 45° angle into 4-10 cm pieces. Set aside.
Heat oil (10-15 ml) in a wok until smoking, then add the spring onion and chili peppers. Stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the green beans and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the seasoning sauce and stir-fry over high heat for 2 minutes.
Pour in 150 ml of water.
Reduce to medium-low heat, cover, and simmer for 8-10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, then remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/脆皮豆腐.name: Crispy Tofu
howtocook:vegetable_dish/脆皮豆腐.description: Crispy tofu is crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, with a savory-sweet sauce that makes it a quick and satisfying dish. Tofu is rich in plant-based protein and calcium, while eggs provide high-quality protein, creating a balanced nutritional profile. Making it requires patience to achieve a golden crust, but the steps are clear and easy for beginners to follow. The entire process takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Beat the eggs to form an egg wash and set aside.
Prepare the sauce: (20 g light soy sauce + 10 g oyster sauce + 5 g dark soy sauce + 10 g sugar + 10 g cornstarch + 200 ml water)
Slice the old tofu (personal suggestion, for reference: 5 slices per person, thickness 1.2 cm)
Pour cornstarch into a plate. Coat the tofu slices with cornstarch, then with egg wash, and set aside.
Heat the pan, add 18 ml cooking oil. Wait 10 seconds for the oil to heat up.
Place the egg-washed tofu slices evenly in the pan, covering the bottom. Fry over low heat until golden brown, then flip.
After both sides are golden, pour in the sauce, ensuring each piece is coated. Cook over high heat for 3 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Turn off the heat.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/芹菜拌茶树菇/芹菜拌茶树菇.name: Celery with Tea Tree Mushroom Salad
howtocook:vegetable_dish/芹菜拌茶树菇/芹菜拌茶树菇.description: This is a refreshing and crisp cold dish. The crunchiness of celery complements the chewy texture of tea tree mushrooms, creating a rich mouthfeel. It is rich in various vitamins and minerals, nutritious and appetizing. The recipe is very simple, even beginners can master it easily. From prep to finish, it takes only about 30 minutes.
steps: (Skip if using celery sprouts) Boil a kettle of hot water and set aside.
Remove yellow leaves from fresh celery sprouts or celery, wash, and set aside.

(Skip if using celery sprouts) Remove the leaves from the celery and place them in a separate bowl. Cut the celery stalks into strips about 2-3 mm wide and set aside. This step is to ensure the celery blanches faster and more evenly, resulting in a crisper texture.
Cut celery sprouts into 4 cm segments and set aside.
(Skip if using celery sprouts) Place a pot on the stove, pour the hot water from the kettle into the pot, and bring to a boil.

(Skip if using celery sprouts) Add the celery strips to the pot and blanch for about 20 seconds. Then add the celery leaves, and after 5 seconds, turn off the heat. Remove everything and rinse under cold water. Set aside.
Sprinkle the blanched celery or celery sprouts with the prepared salt, sesame oil, oyster sauce, and premium light soy sauce. Mix well.
Add the tea tree mushrooms to the bowl and mix well.
Arrange on a plate.
Enjoy!

howtocook:vegetable_dish/茄子炖土豆.name: Braised Eggplant with Potatoes
howtocook:vegetable_dish/茄子炖土豆.description: Braised Eggplant with Potatoes is a home-style stew from Northeast China. It has a soft and savory flavor, with a slight spiciness from chili and garlic aroma, making it a great dish to go with rice. Eggplant is rich in anthocyanins and dietary fiber, potatoes provide carbohydrates and vitamin C, and pork adds protein, making it nutritionally balanced and filling. The cooking process is simple but requires control of stir-frying and reducing the sauce, which may be a bit challenging for beginners. Estimated difficulty: medium. It takes about 45 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Wash the eggplant, potatoes, and chili peppers. Peel and crush the garlic.
Cut the eggplant and potatoes into cubes of about 6 cubic centimeters. Cut the chili peppers into small pieces of 5 grams. Cut the meat into shreds of 3 centimeters.
Turn on the heat, heat the wok, and add 13 ml of oil.
When you see a wisp of smoke from the oil in the wok, add the chili peppers.
After sautéing until fragrant, immediately add the meat and stir-fry with a spatula for 30 seconds.
Add the potatoes and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the eggplant and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add soy sauce and salt, and continue stir-frying for 5 minutes.
Add water until the water level is 0.9 times the height of the ingredients in the wok, and cover with a lid.
Wait until the water level is 0.1 times the height of the ingredients, then uncover, add the garlic, stir well, and turn off the heat.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/莴笋叶煎饼/莴笋叶煎饼.name: Lettuce Leaf Pancake
howtocook:vegetable_dish/莴笋叶煎饼/莴笋叶煎饼.description: A simple and quick home-style snack, crispy outside and tender inside, with the refreshing flavor of lettuce leaves. Lettuce leaves are rich in vitamins and fiber, paired with eggs for protein. No complicated skills – just mix and fry both sides. Ready in about 10 minutes.
steps: Chop the lettuce leaves, add eggs, starch, soy sauce, chicken bouillon, and mix well. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan, pour in the lettuce leaf mixture, and spread evenly over the pan surface.
Fry the first side for 120 seconds, then flip and fry the other side for 60 seconds. Remove from pan.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/菠菜炒鸡蛋/菠菜炒鸡蛋.name: Spinach and Egg Stir-fry
howtocook:vegetable_dish/菠菜炒鸡蛋/菠菜炒鸡蛋.description: Spinach and egg stir-fry is a light and refreshing home-style quick dish. The spinach is tender, and the eggs are savory. It's a popular flavor suitable for both northern and southern tastes. Spinach is rich in iron, folic acid, and vitamins, while eggs provide high-quality protein and lecithin. Eating them together helps nourish blood, boost energy, and strengthen the body. The whole dish is simple to make, perfect for cooking beginners. It takes only about 15 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Trim the roots of the spinach, wash it, place it in a colander, and blanch in boiling water.
Crack the eggs into a bowl and beat until smooth.
Heat the wok, then add 10ml oil.
When the oil is hot, pour in the egg mixture. Stir-fry over medium heat for 15 seconds, first cooking into an omelet, then break into small pieces with a spatula.
Turn off the heat, transfer the egg pieces to a plate. Do not wash the wok.
Reheat the wok, add 5ml oil. When hot, add the spinach and stir-fry over high heat for 15 seconds. Then add the egg pieces and stir-fry until well combined.
Add 5g salt and 100ml drinking water. Stir-fry over high heat for 10 seconds.
Turn off the heat and transfer to a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/葱煎豆腐.name: Scallion Pan-Fried Tofu
howtocook:vegetable_dish/葱煎豆腐.description: Crispy on the outside, tender inside, rich scallion aroma, savory with a hint of spiciness, a home-style dish. Tofu is rich in plant protein and calcium, paired with green peppers and scallions, nutritionally balanced. Moderate difficulty, requires slow frying over low heat. Beginners can succeed with patience, about 30 minutes total.
steps: Wash the tofu. Cut into slices about 5 mm thick, place on a plate.
Wash the scallions, remove roots, cut into chopped scallions, set aside.
Wash the peppers, cut open, remove seeds, cut into 1 cm pieces, set aside.
Heat the pan, add 9 ml of oil.
After the oil is in the pan, spread it evenly over the bottom, place tofu pieces evenly, fry over low heat until golden brown, then flip.
When both sides are golden, transfer to a plate and set aside.
Add more oil to cover the bottom of the pan, add the peppers, stir-fry over high heat, and press them with a spatula for 3 minutes.
Add the tofu back, stir-fry, add salt and chicken bouillon, stir-fry over medium heat for 1 minute, then add 10 ml water, and reduce the sauce over high heat.
Before removing from heat, sprinkle with the prepared chopped scallions, then serve on a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒜蓉空心菜/蒜蓉空心菜.name: Garlic Water Spinach
howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒜蓉空心菜/蒜蓉空心菜.description: Garlic water spinach is a crisp, refreshing, and garlicky home-style quick dish, typical of Sichuan-style stir-fry. Water spinach is rich in dietary fiber, vitamins, and calcium, which helps promote intestinal motility. The method is very simple and friendly to beginners, from preparation to serving takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Wash water spinach, remove bad leaves or tough stems, cut into 2 or 3 equal segments (to prevent them from being too long to stir-fry).

First, pour a small amount of oil into the pan, heat until it's just smoking. Then, tilt the pan to spread the hot oil evenly over the bottom, then pour in the remaining oil (hot pan, cold oil method).
Add minced garlic, stir-fry over low heat for 10-15 seconds until fragrant.
Quickly and evenly add water spinach, turn the heat to high. Use a spatula in your left hand and chopsticks in your right, stirring constantly until the leaves soften and turn green.
Then, without using chopsticks, use a spatula to quickly stir-fry the softened water spinach for 15-20 seconds for even heating. Sprinkle in 2g salt, 3g sugar, and 8ml light soy sauce.
Continue stir-frying over high heat for 10 seconds, then serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒜蓉西兰花.name: Garlic Broccoli
howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒜蓉西兰花.description: This home-style vegetable dish is known for its rich garlic flavor and simple seasoning. After blanching, the broccoli is tender or slightly crisp, light and delicious. Rich in vitamin C, dietary fiber, and various antioxidants, it's a quick and nutritious vegetable dish. The process is easy, even beginners can master it, from prep to finish in about 15 minutes.
steps: Cut the broccoli into small florets and wash thoroughly.
Peel the garlic and mince it; set aside.
Add 1000 ml of drinking water to a pot and bring to a boil over high heat.
Add the broccoli and blanch for 2-3 minutes over high heat until it turns bright green and becomes tender.
Remove the broccoli, drain well, and arrange on a plate; set aside.
Heat a pan and add 10 ml of cooking oil. Once hot, add the minced garlic and stir-fry over low heat until fragrant.
Add 10 ml of light soy sauce, 5 ml of oyster sauce, 2 g of sugar, and 30 ml of drinking water.
Bring the sauce to a boil.
Evenly pour the cooked garlic sauce over the broccoli on the plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒲烧茄子.name: Unagi-Style Eggplant
howtocook:vegetable_dish/蒲烧茄子.description: Unagi-style eggplant is a home-style dish with a sweet-salty flavor and soft, sticky texture. It draws inspiration from Japanese unagi cooking but uses Chinese pan-frying and braising techniques. Eggplant is rich in dietary fiber and various vitamins, helping to clear heat and promote blood circulation. The difficulty is moderate; beginners need a bit of patience. Estimated cooking time: about 30 minutes.
steps: Peel the eggplant, cut it horizontally into two sections.
Steam for 5 minutes.
Cut lengthwise without cutting through, then make 2-3 slashes on each cut side so it can be flattened.
Pan-fry until both sides are golden brown.
Pour the unagi sauce over the eggplant until it reaches half the height of the eggplant.
Fry until the bottom side is colored, then flip.
Meanwhile, reduce the sauce. If it doesn't thicken, add a cornstarch slurry (cornstarch to water ratio between 1:4 and 1:10 depending on the sauce consistency).
Pour the remaining unagi sauce over the eggplant.
Remove from the pan. A serving of eggplant braised in unagi sauce is ready.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/虎皮青椒/虎皮青椒.name: Tiger Skin Green Peppers
howtocook:vegetable_dish/虎皮青椒/虎皮青椒.description: Tiger Skin Green Peppers is a home-style side dish. The skin is slightly charred and wrinkled, with a tender yet crisp texture. It's savory with a hint of sweet and sour, very appetizing and goes well with rice. Green peppers are rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, which help boost immunity and aid digestion. The cooking process is simple and straightforward; you just need to master pressing and heat control. It's beginner-friendly and takes about 15 minutes to complete.
steps: Remove the stems of the green peppers and rinse thoroughly under tap water.
Cut the peppers into long slices; one pepper can be cut lengthwise into 3-4 slices.
Peel the garlic and mince into pieces about 2mm x 2mm x 2mm in size.
Sauce 1: Take a small bowl, pour in 20ml oil, and add the minced garlic.
Sauce 2: Put sugar, soy sauce, vinegar, and salt all into a bowl (or similar container) and stir.
Pour Sauce 1 into the pan, heat to medium heat, then add the pepper slices. Do not overlap; place each slice separately in the pan.
Constantly press the peppers with a spatula, flipping at the right time.
Stir-fry for about 2 minutes. When the pepper skin becomes wrinkled, pour in Sauce 2.
Increase heat and continue stir-frying for 30 seconds, then serve on a plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/蚝油三鲜菇/蚝油三鲜菇.name: Oyster Sauce Mixed Mushrooms
howtocook:vegetable_dish/蚝油三鲜菇/蚝油三鲜菇.description: Oyster Sauce Mixed Mushrooms is a delicious, tender, and quick home-style dish. The mushrooms soak up the oyster sauce, resulting in a soft yet chewy texture with a slight spiciness, refreshing and not greasy. Rich in plant protein, dietary fiber, and various vitamins, paired with broccoli for balanced nutrition. Simple to make, even beginners can master it easily. From prep to finish, it takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Remove the root ends of the shimeji and white beech mushrooms, then break apart the clusters.
Slice the shiitake mushrooms (each slice 0.5-1 cm thick; thicker slices have more chewiness).
Pour cornstarch into a small bowl, add 50ml water, and stir until dissolved and no lumps remain (this is the slurry). Set aside.
Bring water to a boil, add broccoli, boil for 3 minutes, then transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Clean the pot, bring fresh water to a boil, add 5g salt, then add the shimeji, white beech, and shiitake mushrooms. Boil for 1 minute.
After 1 minute, drain the mushrooms well.
Heat oil in a pan. When small bubbles appear, add ginger, Thai chili, and bell pepper. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add the three mushrooms, then add light soy sauce, oyster sauce, and chicken bouillon. Stir-fry evenly, then add the slurry.
Cook over medium heat until the sauce thickens. Add cooking wine and chopped green onions, then remove from heat.
Arrange the broccoli around the dish.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/蚝油生菜.name: Oyster Sauce Lettuce
howtocook:vegetable_dish/蚝油生菜.description: Oyster Sauce Lettuce is a refreshing and savory home-cooked dish. Crisp lettuce is drizzled with a garlicky oyster sauce, savory with a hint of sweetness. It's a quick vegetarian dish commonly found in both Cantonese and Sichuan restaurants. Rich in vitamins and dietary fiber, it aids digestion. The recipe is very beginner-friendly, taking only about 15 minutes from washing to serving.
steps: Wash the lettuce and remove any spoiled leaves.
Heat the pan, add 1 L cold water, then add 2-3 ml cooking oil and 0.5 g salt. Wait for the water to boil.
Once boiling, add the lettuce and blanch each leaf for 10 seconds.
Remove the lettuce, drain, and arrange on a plate.
Make the sauce: Mix 10 ml light soy sauce, 6-8 ml oyster sauce, 0.5 g salt, and 1 g sugar in a bowl. Add 10-15 ml cold water and stir well.
Reheat the pan over fire, add 5-8 ml cooking oil. When hot, add minced garlic.
Wait for the garlic aroma, then pour in the prepared sauce. Bring to a boil and immediately turn off the heat.
Pour the sauce evenly over the lettuce.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/西红柿炒鸡蛋.name: Scrambled Eggs with Tomatoes
howtocook:vegetable_dish/西红柿炒鸡蛋.description: A sweet and sour home-style dish that is appetizing. Tender scrambled eggs wrapped around soft, juicy tomatoes—a classic flavor on Chinese family tables. Eggs provide high-quality protein, while tomatoes are rich in vitamin C and lycopene, making it nutritious and easy to absorb. Simple steps and easily available ingredients, especially suitable for beginners. From prep to serving, it takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Wash the tomatoes
Optional: Remove the tomato skin
Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water, then transfer to cold water and peel off the skin
Remove the stem and cut into small pieces no larger than 4 cm on each side; these are the tomato pieces
Crack the eggs into a bowl, add 1g salt, and beat well; this is the egg mixture
You may consider adding 1ml vinegar to the eggs; this removes any fishy smell and makes the eggs fluffier
Heat the wok and add cooking oil
When the oil is hot, pour in the egg mixture. Stir-fry until the eggs are set and slightly golden; these are the half-cooked eggs
Turn off the heat. Transfer the half-cooked eggs to a plate, then turn the heat back on
Note: Do not wash the wok
Add the tomato pieces, press and stir-fry with a spatula for 20 seconds, or until the tomatoes are soft
Add the half-cooked eggs back to the wok and stir-fry evenly
You may consider adding 10ml tomato paste and 50ml water for more sauce
You can also add some other cooked meat and ingredients
Add the remaining salt, sugar (optional, if you prefer a sweet version), and chopped scallions (optional), and stir-fry evenly
Turn off the heat and serve

howtocook:vegetable_dish/西红柿豆腐汤羹/西红柿豆腐汤羹.name: Tomato and Tofu Soup
howtocook:vegetable_dish/西红柿豆腐汤羹/西红柿豆腐汤羹.description: A light and delicious home-style soup, sweet and sour, appetizing, with a smooth texture. Tomatoes are rich in vitamin C and lycopene, paired with eggs and tofu, providing high-quality protein, balanced nutrition and easy absorption. Simple to make, just chop, stir-fry and boil, even beginners can master it easily, from preparation to serving in about 15 minutes.
steps: Cut tomatoes into small cubes, beat eggs in a bowl, cut tofu into cubes and set aside.
Heat oil in a pot, add ginger slices, after 5 seconds add tomatoes and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add boiling water to the pot, bring to a boil, after 60 seconds add beaten eggs and tofu cubes.
After the soup boils again, add water starch, stir in one direction for 2 circles.
Add chicken bouillon, salt, and scallions, after 30 seconds remove from heat.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/西葫芦炒鸡蛋/西葫芦炒鸡蛋.name: Stir-fried Zucchini with Eggs
howtocook:vegetable_dish/西葫芦炒鸡蛋/西葫芦炒鸡蛋.description: Stir-fried Zucchini with Eggs is a refreshing and delicious home-style quick dish. The zucchini is tender and juicy, the eggs are fluffy and savory, and adding tomatoes brings a sweet and sour flavor that pairs perfectly with rice. Eggs provide high-quality protein and lecithin, while zucchini is rich in vitamin C and dietary fiber, making the combination nutritionally balanced. The cooking process is simple and beginner-friendly; from prep to serving, it usually takes only 15 minutes for novices.
steps: Wash the tomatoes, cut into small pieces, and set aside.
Wash the zucchini, cut into diamond shapes about 4cm on each side, and set aside.
Crack three eggs into a bowl, beat until well mixed, and set aside.
Heat the pan and add 5ml - 10ml of cooking oil.
Pour in the eggs, keep stirring until the eggs are set, break into small pieces with a spatula, then transfer to a bowl and set aside.
Add 5ml - 10ml of cooking oil to the pan, pour in the tomatoes, and stir-fry until soft.
Add the zucchini and stir-fry together. Add 6g of salt, reduce the heat to low, and wait 4 - 5 minutes.
Add the reserved eggs, stir-fry over medium heat for 15 seconds.
Turn off the heat and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/话梅煮毛豆/话梅煮毛豆.name: Plum-Boiled Edamame
howtocook:vegetable_dish/话梅煮毛豆/话梅煮毛豆.description: Plum-boiled edamame is a sweet and sour appetizing home-style side dish. The texture is refreshing, soft, and sticky, with the natural fruity aroma of plums. Edamame is rich in plant protein and dietary fiber, which helps replenish energy and promote digestion. The recipe is very simple, even beginners can easily master it. It takes only 35 to 45 minutes to complete.
steps: Add salt to water, soak edamame for 15 minutes
Add boiling water, add edamame and plums, boil for 20-30 minutes
Remove from heat and enjoy

howtocook:vegetable_dish/酸辣土豆丝.name: Sour and Spicy Shredded Potatoes
howtocook:vegetable_dish/酸辣土豆丝.description: A classic home-cooked dish with a sour and spicy flavor that whets the appetite. The texture is crisp and refreshing, and the color is bright. It's a popular quick stir-fry. Potatoes provide carbohydrates and potassium, while chili peppers are rich in vitamin C, helping to stimulate appetite. The steps are simple and clear, friendly for beginners, taking about 20 minutes from prep to serving.
steps: Peel the potatoes and cut into shreds (or use a grater).
Wash the shredded potatoes in clean water to remove excess starch, then blanch them in boiling water for 10 seconds. Drain and set aside.
Chop the scallion into small pieces; mince the garlic (divide into two equal portions for later); cut dried chilies into small sections; shred the green and red bell peppers.
Heat a wok over low heat, add oil, then stir-fry half of the scallion (the white part), half of the garlic, and the dried chilies until fragrant.
Add the shredded green and red peppers and stir-fry a few times, then add the shredded potatoes and stir-fry until they change color.
Add 5ml light soy sauce, 10ml aged vinegar, pour in the remaining half of the minced garlic and 2g salt, and quickly stir-fry until well combined.
Before serving, sprinkle the remaining scallion over the dish, toss well, and plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/金针菇日本豆腐煲.name: Enoki Mushroom and Japanese Tofu Pot
howtocook:vegetable_dish/金针菇日本豆腐煲.description: Enoki Mushroom and Japanese Tofu Pot is a home-style dish with rich savory sauce. The tofu is crispy outside and tender inside, while the enoki mushrooms are smooth and flavorful. Enoki mushrooms are rich in amino acids and dietary fiber, and Japanese tofu provides high-quality protein, making it nutritious and appetizing. The steps are simple, easy for beginners, and takes about 30 minutes from preparation to serving.
steps: Slice the tofu, pan-fry over low heat until both sides are golden brown, then remove and set aside.
Mince the garlic; mix light soy sauce, oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar, and 100ml water to make the sauce.

Heat oil in a pot, add chopped chili peppers and minced garlic, stir-fry until fragrant. Add enoki mushrooms first and cook until soft. Place the fried tofu on top of the mushrooms, pour in the sauce from step 2, cover and simmer for 5 minutes, then reduce the sauce over high heat.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/金钱蛋.name: Golden Coin Eggs
howtocook:vegetable_dish/金钱蛋.description: Golden Coin Eggs is a home-style Hunan dish with crispy exterior and tender interior, savory and slightly spicy. The golden fried egg slices resemble copper coins, symbolizing good fortune. Eggs paired with green and red peppers provide high-quality protein and various vitamins, offering balanced nutrition. The difficulty is moderate; beginners can master it with a bit of practice. It takes about 30 minutes to complete this rice-pairing dish.
steps: Put eggs in cold water, bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to low heat and cook for 8 minutes
Rinse under tap water to cool, making them easy to peel
Mince garlic (particle size no more than 1 mm), dice line peppers and bird's eye chilies into small pieces (about 2-3 mm)
Cut each cooked egg along the short axis into 4 roughly equal pieces
If using starch, coat each egg slice with starch, then shake off excess
Add 25 ml oil to the pan (20 ml if not using starch), add the egg slices
After frying until slightly golden, add another 10 ml of oil (if not using starch, add 10 ml), and flip
When the second side is golden, add line peppers, bird's eye chilies, and minced garlic; fry for about one minute, then flip
Then add doubanjiang (chili bean paste) and fry for about a few tens of seconds, then flip
Mix light soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar into a sauce. Gently toss the pan, then serve

howtocook:vegetable_dish/陕北熬豆角.name: Northern Shaanxi Braised Green Beans
howtocook:vegetable_dish/陕北熬豆角.description: This is a rustic farm-style stew from Northwest China. The green beans are soft, the potatoes are creamy, and the tomato sauce is rich and tangy, making it appetizing. It's rich in dietary fiber, vitamins, and carbohydrates, offering balanced nutrition. The method is extremely simple, ingredient amounts can be adjusted to taste, and it's almost foolproof—very beginner-friendly. From prep to serving, it takes about 30 minutes.
steps: Chop scallion into rings, mince garlic, shred ginger, and set aside.
Remove strings from green beans, cut into 2-10 cm sections, and set aside.
Peel potato, cut into 1 cm cubes, and set aside.
Peel tomato, cut into 1 cm cubes, and set aside.
Remove seeds from pepper, cut into 0.15 cm wide strips, and set aside.
Heat oil (10-15 ml) in a wok until smoking, add scallion, ginger, and garlic, stir-fry until fragrant.
Add green beans, stir-fry until color changes (from light green to bright green).
Add potato cubes, stir-fry for 30 seconds.
Add hot water (just enough to cover the ingredients), cover and braise until potatoes are soft (check with chopsticks).
Add tomato cubes, salt, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, five-spice powder, and pepper. Braise until tomatoes break down into sauce (stir occasionally to prevent sticking).
Add chopped cilantro, then remove from heat and serve.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/雷椒皮蛋.name: Smashed Century Egg with Green Peppers
howtocook:vegetable_dish/雷椒皮蛋.description: Smashed Century Egg with Green Peppers is a humble-looking but flavorful home-style cold dish. The charred aroma of green peppers blends with the smooth richness of century eggs, creating a tangy and spicy appetizer, perfect for summer meals. Century eggs are rich in protein and minerals, green peppers provide abundant vitamin C, and garlic helps stimulate appetite. The whole dish is simple to make, easy for beginners, and takes about 15 minutes from prep to finish.
steps: Wash the green peppers, remove the stems, cut them open lengthwise, remove the seeds, and flatten them on the cutting board. Set aside. (Be sure to remove the seeds, or they will pop in the pan.)
Cut the scallion into 0.5 cm pieces. Set aside.
Peel the garlic and mince finely. Set aside.
Peel the century eggs. Set aside.
Cut the Thai chilies into 5-10 mm pieces. Set aside.
Heat the pan and add 10-20 ml of cooking oil.
Add all the green peppers. Reduce the heat to low and fry until the peppers are soft. (Test with a chopstick – it should pierce through easily.)
Turn off the heat. Transfer the century eggs and green peppers to a small metal bowl.

Method 1: If you have a rolling pin and smashing things won't disturb the neighbors, use one end of the rolling pin to smash the century eggs and green peppers in the bowl until they are combined. (Optional)
Method 2: Tear the green peppers by hand into strips about 0.5 cm wide, and use a fork to mash the century eggs. (Optional)
Add the Thai chilies.
Add light soy sauce, aged vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, and any other reserved ingredients not yet used.
Stir evenly.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋火腿炒黄瓜.name: Stir-fried Cucumber with Egg and Ham
howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋火腿炒黄瓜.description: This is a refreshing and quick home-style stir-fry, salty and savory, with crisp cucumber, soft scrambled eggs, and flavorful ham. It's a popular Chinese home-cooked dish. Eggs provide high-quality protein, cucumbers are rich in vitamins and water, and ham adds flavor, making it nutritionally balanced. The cooking steps are simple and straightforward, very low difficulty, and friendly for kitchen beginners. From prep to plating, it takes only about 10 minutes.
steps: Wash the cucumber, cut into half-round slices, set aside.
Cut the ham into half-round slices, set aside.
Finely chop the red chili pepper (optional), set aside.
Crack the eggs into a bowl, beat well, this is the egg mixture.
Pour 5ml cooking oil into a hot pan.
When the oil is hot, reduce to low heat, pour in the beaten eggs, stir with chopsticks to scramble until the eggs are set and slightly golden, this is the half-cooked eggs. Remove and set aside.
Without washing the pan, pour 5ml cooking oil into the pan, add cucumber slices and stir-fry over high heat for 1 minute.
Add the half-cooked eggs to the pan, season with 2g salt and 3ml light soy sauce, immediately add ham slices and chopped chili (optional), stir-fry evenly.
Turn off the heat, plate.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/微波炉鸡蛋羹.name: Microwave Steamed Egg Custard
howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/微波炉鸡蛋羹.description: Microwave steamed egg custard is a simple and quick home-style steamed egg dish with a tender and smooth texture, especially suitable as a late-night snack or quick meal. Eggs are rich in high-quality protein and various vitamins, providing essential nutrients for the body, and the dried shrimp adds calcium. This dish is very beginner-friendly, requiring no mastery of heat control; from beating the eggs to serving, it only takes about 10 minutes.
steps: Crack the eggs into a microwave-safe ceramic bowl and beat them with chopsticks.
Add water and salt, and stir well.
Add the dried shrimp into the bowl and stir evenly, making sure the shrimp are not clumped together.
Finely chop the scallions into pieces about 0.6 to 3 mm in size, then add to the bowl and stir well.

Place the bowl and its contents into the microwave, cover the surface with plastic wrap or a microwave-safe ceramic lid (note: do not seal; must leave room for expansion), and heat for 2 minutes (500W).
Carefully remove the plastic wrap or other cover, then continue heating for 2 minutes.
If the microwave does not have a rotating turntable, slowly rotate the bowl horizontally by 180 degrees to ensure even heating.
Add sesame oil.
Carefully remove the bowl from the microwave (it's really hot).
If you choose to add soy sauce, make sure it flows over the surface of the egg custard and then coats it in a thin layer.
Enjoy the egg custard happily.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/蒸箱鸡蛋羹.name: Steamed Egg Custard
howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/蒸箱鸡蛋羹.description: This is a tender and smooth home-style egg custard quickly made using a steamer. The texture can be adjusted: less water makes it firmer, more water makes it tenderer. Rich in high-quality protein and various nutrients, easy to digest and absorb. Moderate difficulty, beginners can master it with a little care. From preparation to serving, it takes only about 15 minutes.
steps: Beat one egg in a bowl until smooth.
Add purified water at 60°C, 1.0 to 1.5 times the volume of the egg, and stir well.
Within the correct range, less water gives a firmer texture, more water gives a tenderer texture.
Note: Outside the range, too little water makes it too firm, too much water makes it too thin.
Add 1g of salt.
Salt plays an important role in enhancing the base flavor and coagulating.
Add 5ml of cooking oil.
Strain the egg mixture to remove foam (optional; without straining, the steamed custard may have bubbles and look less appealing).
Make sure the steamer's water supply is sufficient (if unsure, fill the water tank).
Place the bowl with the fully stirred egg mixture into the steamer.
Set to 100°C and steam for 10 minutes.
Open the steamer (Note: When opening the steamer, steam will burst out instantly; open slowly and carefully).
Remove from steamer (can add light soy sauce for seasoning).
Enjoy!

howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/鸡蛋羹.name: Egg Custard
howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋羹/鸡蛋羹.description: This dish is a simple and quick home-style steamed dish, with a tender texture like jelly, savory and suitable. It often appears on tables as breakfast or a side dish. Eggs provide high-quality protein and lecithin, easy to digest and absorb. Very beginner-friendly, just a few steps to steam a smooth and delicate egg custard, the whole process takes about 15 minutes.
steps: Crack two eggs into a bowl and beat them.
Add 3g of salt.
Salt plays an important role in adding flavor and helping coagulation.
Add 2ml of white vinegar to remove the eggy smell (optional).
Rice vinegar also works, but it may give the eggs a slightly darker color.
You can also add cooking wine, which also removes the smell without the acidity of vinegar.
Add purified water at 70°C, about 1-1.5 times the volume of the eggs, and stir well.
With 1x water, the egg is firmer; with 1.5x water, it is more tender.
Strain the egg mixture to remove foam (optional; if not strained, the custard may have bubbles and look less appealing).
Add 300-500ml of water to a pot, bring to a boil, then place the bowl with the egg mixture inside.
Cover the bowl with a lid, invert a grooved metal plate over it, or use plastic wrap to prevent steam from dripping in, to avoid a honeycomb texture.
Steaming steps (choose one):
Steam over medium heat for 10 minutes, then turn off the heat and let it rest for 5 minutes.

Steam over high heat for 5 minutes, keeping the water boiling; otherwise, extend the time. Open the lid every 2 minutes to release steam. Opening too late may cause honeycomb texture; opening too early or too often requires longer steaming.
How to tell if it's done?
Shake the bowl; if the custard is no longer liquid and has a jelly-like wobble, it is ready.
Remove from the pot.
Add sesame oil and light soy sauce, and enjoy.
Alternatively, replace sesame oil with Sichuan pepper oil for a numbing sensation.

howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋花/鸡蛋花.name: Egg Flower Soup
howtocook:vegetable_dish/鸡蛋花/鸡蛋花.description: A simple home-style therapeutic soup drink with both sweet and savory flavors. The texture is smooth and tender. It's a folk remedy for soothing the throat. Rich in high-quality protein and lecithin, easy to digest and absorb, it can relieve dry and itchy throat discomfort. Extremely simple to make; beginners can pick it up after one look. It takes only about 5 minutes from boiling water to completion.
steps: Crack the eggs into a bowl.
Using chopsticks or a whisk, beat the eggs in one direction until the whites and yolks are fully combined and the color is uniform. (This takes about 1-2 minutes.)
Add sugar or salt and other seasonings to the egg mixture, and stir lightly.
Prepare freshly boiled water at 100°C.
While quickly stirring the egg mixture in the bowl with chopsticks, pour the boiling water in a thin stream into the egg mixture. Ensure the boiling water is fully mixed with the egg mixture.
Continue stirring for a moment until the egg mixture is completely cooked by the hot water, forming uniform pale yellow egg flowers.
